But when millions of Malaysians need to fly home for Hari Raya and airlines are about to hit them with surcharges — or cancel flights entirely — your Transport Minister’s solution is to give airlines permission to charge more. Where is the emergency aviation fund? Where is the temporary jet fuel subsidy? Where is the fare cap for domestic routes during festive periods? You had time to ban Raya open houses for “fiscal discipline” but no time to protect the rakyat from being gouged at the airport. Reformasi, apparently, does not extend to airline pricing.

Sources: Bernama | FMT | Malay Mail | The Sun | The Star

item.title: Ramanan Says Government 'Studying' WFH for Public Sector — Thailand Acted Immediately, Malaysia Still Forming a Study
item.description:
Said by: R. Ramanan, Human Resources Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-12

📌 What Happened

On March 12, 2026, Human Resources Minister Ramanan Ramakrishnan told reporters in Putrajaya that possible cost-saving measures — including work-from-home for public sector employees and the private sector — are being “studied” and will be presented at a special Cabinet meeting on Friday, March 14. “We have seen what Thailand has done, and we are currently conducting studies on this,” Ramanan said. He added that he would receive a briefing from deputy secretaries-general and then present findings to Cabinet. He acknowledged the prolonged Middle East conflict and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz as drivers of the crisis.

🔥 Why It Matters

Thailand’s Cabinet approved mandatory work-from-home for government officials immediately upon the escalation of the energy crisis. Malaysia’s government — facing the same crisis, with RON97 up 18%, diesel up 26%, and oil past $100 a barrel — is still “conducting studies.” The contrast is damning. Every day Malaysian workers commute to offices they could work from remotely is a day the government is forcing the rakyat to absorb fuel costs that could be avoided with a single directive. Ramanan’s own framing gives the game away: “If we ourselves do not practise savings but ask the people to do so, that would not be fair.” Correct. So why are you still studying instead of acting?

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The COVID-19 pandemic proved — three years ago — that Malaysian public servants and private sector workers can work from home effectively. The infrastructure exists. The precedent exists. The legal framework for flexible work arrangements already exists under the amended Employment Act. There is nothing left to “study.” What Ramanan is really saying is that the government does not want to issue a directive without political cover from a Cabinet meeting that hasn’t happened yet. This is not governance. This is a bureaucracy waiting for permission to do the obvious. Thailand acted in hours. Malaysia is taking days — and counting.

📜 Previous Cases

On March 11, this database logged an entry noting that Anwar’s crisis response to $100+ oil included solidarity tweets and “monitoring” — but no WFH mandate, no toll waiver, no food price caps. Now, one day later, the government has graduated from “monitoring” to “studying.” During the ASEAN Summit in May 2025, Fahmi Fadzil spent weeks saying the Cabinet was “deliberating” WFH before eventually implementing it. The pattern is consistent: Malaysia’s government treats every decision as requiring maximum process and minimum urgency, even when the cost of delay is borne entirely by ordinary workers.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians you would lead by example. Ramanan said it himself: it would be “unfair” to ask the people to save while the government doesn’t. So act. Issue the directive today. Thailand didn’t need a Friday Cabinet meeting to decide that government workers should stop burning expensive fuel to sit in offices with perfectly functional internet connections. Every day you “study” is a day you’re choosing process over people. The rakyat cannot afford your deliberation speed.

Sources: The Star | Bernama

item.title: RON97 Up 18%, Diesel Up 26% — Government Lets Unsubsidised Fuel Prices Explode While Claiming It's 'Cushioning the Rakyat'
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance / Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-12

📌 What Happened

The Ministry of Finance announced on March 11 that effective March 12-18, RON97 petrol will surge 60 sen per litre to RM3.85 — an 18% increase from RM3.25. Unsubsidised RON95 jumps 60 sen to RM3.27 — a 22% spike. Diesel in Peninsular Malaysia leaps 80 sen to RM3.92 — a staggering 26% increase. The government maintained the Budi95 subsidised rate at RM1.99 for eligible motorists and offered a RM100 top-up for diesel vehicle owners under existing schemes, raising interim cash assistance from RM200 to RM300 for March. The MOF said Malaysia’s prices “remain lower than neighbouring countries” and promised “strengthened enforcement” against fuel leakage.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is the sharpest single-week fuel price adjustment since the Middle East crisis escalated. Diesel at RM3.92 directly hits logistics, haulage, and food supply chains — costs that will be passed to consumers within days. The two-tier fuel system is now stretched to breaking point: subsidised RON95 sits at RM1.99 while unsubsidised RON95 is at RM3.27, a 64% gap that incentivises smuggling and gaming. The government’s entire fuel strategy is now a subsidy wall protecting one product while everything else burns. Businesses that run on diesel — fishermen, farmers, truckers, food manufacturers — are absorbing a 26% cost spike with no price controls downstream. This will feed directly into food inflation during Ramadan, when household spending is already elevated.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government’s framing is pure theatre. “The government will continue to bear part of the subsidy to cushion the impact” — they are subsidising one fuel grade and letting everything else rip. The RM100 diesel top-up is an insult: if you’re a lorry operator burning hundreds of litres a week, RM300 a month covers roughly one fill-up at the new price. Meanwhile, the MOF’s line about prices being “lower than neighbouring countries” is the oldest deflection in the subsidy playbook — it tells you nothing about whether Malaysian wages can absorb these costs. Anwar promised fuel subsidy rationalisation that would be “fair and gradual.” A 26% diesel spike in one week is neither fair nor gradual. It’s crisis management dressed up as policy.

📜 Previous Cases

This government has been kicking the fuel subsidy can since taking office. The targeted subsidy for RON95 (Budi95) was meant to replace blanket subsidies, but diesel rationalisation has been stop-start since June 2024 when diesel subsidies were first restructured. Every time global prices spike, the government scrambles with ad hoc cash handouts instead of structural reform. Just last week, Anwar admitted he could only hold the RON95 subsidy for “one or two months” — and now the prices around it are exploding. The pattern is clear: announce reform, delay implementation, throw cash when crisis hits, repeat.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians you would rationalise subsidies fairly. You told them the economy was strong and well-managed. You told them you had a plan. An 18% spike in RON97 and a 26% spike in diesel in a single week is not a plan — it’s a surrender to market forces with a press release attached. The rakyat who drive RON97 or run diesel-dependent businesses are not “cushioned” — they are exposed. And the fishermen, farmers, and truckers who feed this country are now subsidising your political survival with their margins. If the Budi95 wall cracks — and at these global prices, it will — you have no backup plan. Just more press statements about how prices are lower than Singapore.

Sources: The Edge Malaysia | MOF Official Statement

item.title: TikTok Removes Second MalaysiaNow Video on Azam-Gate — MCMC Chairman Who Licenses TikTok Also Sits on MACC's Advisory Board
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / MCMC (Mohamad Salim Fateh Din, Chairman)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-12

📌 What Happened

On March 12, 2026, a six-minute MalaysiaNow video was removed from TikTok without explanation. The clip featured a joint press conference by Muda leaders Ainie Haziqah and Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz alongside Pejuang’s Mukhriz Mahathir and Rafique Rashid, announcing a campaign highlighting Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s failure to act on MACC chief Azam Baki following two damning Bloomberg investigations. This is the second MalaysiaNow video on Azam-gate to be taken down — the first, a Malay-language summary of Bloomberg’s ‘corporate mafia’ exposé, was removed on February 13. Both videos remain available on YouTube and other platforms. TikTok is one of only three platforms — alongside WeChat and Telegram — that have agreed to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission’s (MCMC) controversial social media licensing regime, which came into effect in 2026.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not a one-off glitch. It is a pattern. The government introduced social media licensing ostensibly to protect children from online harm. Critics warned from day one that the real purpose was content control — and the Azam-gate takedowns are proving them right. Platforms that accepted MCMC licensing are now acting as censorship proxies for a government under pressure. The fact that only licensed platforms are removing the content, while YouTube and others still host it, confirms the licensing regime is functioning exactly as feared: as a lever to suppress journalism that embarrasses Putrajaya.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Here is the conflict of interest that makes this indefensible. The MCMC chairman, Mohamad Salim Fateh Din — the man who oversees TikTok’s licensing compliance — also chairs the Anti-Corruption Advisory Board, an oversight body under MACC itself. Salim has publicly defended Azam Baki, stating that the MACC chief ‘complied with the prevailing public service governance framework’ on the shareholding scandal. So the regulator who decides whether TikTok keeps its licence is also the man defending the subject of the censored videos. Pakatan Harapan promised press freedom, media reform, and an end to government interference in journalism. Instead, they built a licensing apparatus that lets a conflicted appointee pressure platforms into silencing critical coverage of the government’s own anti-corruption failures.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows a well-documented trajectory. In February 2026, TikTok removed MalaysiaNow’s first Azam-gate video summarising Bloomberg’s investigative report. Before that, Fahmi Fadzil’s MCMC had already requested the removal of over 300 TikTok videos between 2020 and 2023 under the previous government — and PH criticised those takedowns as authoritarian overreach. Now PH is using the same tools, with additional legal teeth via the licensing regime they introduced. The pattern is bipartisan: every Malaysian government, regardless of coalition, reaches for the censorship lever when the pressure builds. PH simply formalized it.

🎯 PH Callout

You campaigned on repealing the Sedition Act, reforming MCMC, and defending press freedom. You said Malaysians deserved a government that could withstand scrutiny. Instead, you appointed a conflicted corporate figure to run MCMC and the MACC advisory board simultaneously, introduced a licensing regime that turns social media platforms into compliance arms of the state, and used it to suppress coverage of your own anti-corruption scandal. If Azam Baki’s conduct is defensible, let the videos stand. If the investigation findings are transparent, release them. The fact that you are censoring opposition press conferences about your own failures tells the rakyat everything they need to know about what ‘Madani’ means in practice.

Sources: MalaysiaNow — Another TikTok video by MalaysiaNow on Azam-gate taken down | MalaysiaNow — TikTok blocks MalaysiaNow video on Bloomberg’s exposé | MalaysiaNow — Make the findings public, group holds Anwar to promise of transparency

item.title: [BM] Ahli Parlimen Belakang PH Desak Pilihan Raya Kilat Berselindung Di Sebalik 'Reformasi' — Gabung PRU16 Dengan Semua PRN, Jimat Duit Kata Mereka
item.description:
Said by: Azli Yusof (Amanah-Shah Alam) / Jimmy Puah (PKR-Tebrau)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac, dua ahli parlimen belakang Pakatan Harapan — Ahli Parlimen Shah Alam Azli Yusof (Amanah) dan Ahli Parlimen Tebrau Jimmy Puah (PKR) — menyeru agar semua pilihan raya dewan undangan negeri diadakan serentak dengan Pilihan Raya Umum ke-16. Bercakap kepada FMT, kedua-dua ahli parlimen berhujah bahawa pilihan raya berasingan membawa kepada peratusan keluar mengundi yang lebih rendah, membazir wang awam, dan menghasilkan kerajaan negeri dengan mandat lebih lemah. Azli berkata pilihan raya berasingan ’tidak adil kepada pengundi, terutamanya mereka yang tinggal jauh dari tempat mengundi.’ Putrajaya membelanjakan RM725 juta untuk PRU15 pada 2022 dan RM420 juta lagi untuk enam pilihan raya negeri seterusnya pada 2023.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan cadangan dasar neutral. Ia langkah terbaharu PH dalam isyarat terselaras — berikutan cadangan serupa oleh Setiausaha Agung DAP Anthony Loke tahun lepas dan desakan terbaharu pengerusi PH Johor Aminolhuda Hassan — untuk menyelaraskan kalendar politik bagi pilihan raya umum awal. Dengan menyeru pilihan raya serentak, ahli parlimen belakang PH melakukan kerja pesanan kerajaan: menormalisasi idea pembubaran awal sambil membungkusnya sebagai reformasi penjimatan kos.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH tidak mengambil peduli tentang ‘keletihan pengundi’ apabila ia sesuai bagi mereka. Pada 2022, enam negeri yang ditadbir PH memilih untuk menangguhkan pilihan raya negeri dan bukannya mengadakannya bersama PRU15, dengan alasan kebimbangan monsun. Kini, tiba-tiba, gabungan yang sama mahu semuanya diadakan bersama — tetapi hanya kerana masanya menguntungkan mereka. Pembangkang berpecah selepas perpecahan Bersatu-PAS, ekonomi bertahan, dan kelulusan Anwar masih utuh. Ini bukan reformasi. Ini kejuruteraan pilihan raya dengan cat reformis.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang pemasaan pilihan raya strategik. Mahathir memanggil pilihan raya kilat apabila keadaan memihak kepadanya. Najib membubarkan Parlimen awal pada 2013 dan 2018. Coraknya sentiasa sama: sesiapa yang mengawal masa mengawal naratif.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu menjanjikan reformasi institusi. Kamu menjanjikan tadbir urus tidak lagi mengenai kemudahan politik. Namun kamu menggunakan ahli parlimen belakang untuk melembutkan tapak bagi pilihan raya kilat sambil berlakon ia mengenai penjimatan wang rakyat. Jika kamu benar-benar percaya pada pilihan raya serentak, bentangkan pindaan perlembagaan untuk menetapkan tarikh pilihan raya — seperti yang dilakukan demokrasi matang. Tetapi kamu tidak akan, kerana seluruh tujuannya ialah mengekalkan kuasa membubarkan di dalam poket PM. Berhenti menyamarkan strategi pilihan raya sebagai reformasi.

Sumber: FMT | The Vibes | NST

item.title: [BM] Anwar Haramkan Rumah Terbuka Raya Atas Nama 'Disiplin Fiskal' — Selepas Baru Umumkan RM15 Bilion Bantuan Tunai Dan Bonus RM500
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengumumkan bahawa semua agensi kerajaan, GLC, dan GLIC dilarang mengadakan majlis rumah terbuka Aidilfitri tahun ini — keputusan yang dipersetujui dalam Kabinet pagi itu. Beliau membingkainya sebagai isyarat disiplin fiskal di tengah ketidaktentuan global akibat konflik Timur Tengah. Pada masa yang sama, Anwar juga mengumumkan sekatan lebih ketat terhadap perjalanan luar negara bagi menteri dan pegawai kerajaan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Persembahan berjimat cermat ini tiba hanya dua hari selepas kerajaan mengumumkan akan mengekalkan subsidi RON95 pada RM1.99 — dan kurang seminggu selepas Anwar mempercepatkan RM15 bilion bantuan tunai Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) serta Bantuan Kewangan Khas RM500 bagi kakitangan awam dan kakitangan masjid, semuanya tepat pada masanya sebelum Hari Raya. Kerajaan membelanjakan jumlah rekod untuk pemindahan tunai langsung — bentuk perbelanjaan paling berkuasa secara pilihan raya — sambil membatalkan sambutan rumah terbuka komuniti yang kosnya hanya sebahagian kecil. Ini bukan disiplin fiskal. Ini berjimat cermat terpilih.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kamu tidak boleh mengumumkan program pemindahan tunai terbesar dalam sejarah Malaysia satu minggu, kemudian membatalkan rumah terbuka Raya minggu berikutnya dan memanggilnya berhemah. Rumah terbuka ialah acara komuniti — kosnya berjuta-juta. Program STR berharga berbilion. Jika krisis Timur Tengah begitu teruk sehingga kakitangan awam tidak boleh makan ketupat di majlis kerajaan, mengapa ia tidak cukup teruk untuk menghentikan RM15 bilion tunai sebelum pilihan raya? Kerana rumah terbuka tidak membeli undi. Tunai dalam akaun bank sebelum hari mengundi boleh.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Ini ialah teater krisis Malaysia yang biasa. Mahathir melakukannya semasa krisis kewangan Asia 1997 — berjimat untuk rakyat, bailout untuk kroni. Najib bercakap disiplin fiskal sementara 1MDB menghembus berbilion. Anwar sendiri mengejek pemberian musim perayaan BN semasa beliau dalam pembangkang, memanggilnya pembelian undi. Kini beliau melakukan perkara yang sama, tetapi lebih besar.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu menjanjikan reformasi, bukan perbelanjaan pilihan raya era BN yang dibungkus semula dengan lapisan kesalehan. Jika kamu benar-benar bimbang tentang kedudukan fiskal, mulakan dengan RM15 bilion pemindahan tunai yang baru kamu umumkan, bukan nasi lemak di rumah terbuka kerajaan. Batalkan pelantikan politik yang menguras FSB. Bekukan perbelanjaan naungan. Jangan berkuliah kepada rakyat tentang kesederhanaan sementara buku cek kerajaan terbuka luas bagi setiap kumpulan pengundi yang penting sebelum PRU16.

Sumber: Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Anwar Kata Bekalan Petroleum 'Selamat Sampai Mei' — Sementara Selat Hormuz Terbakar Dan Kapal Diserang
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengadakan sidang akhbar secara langsung di Putrajaya untuk menangani krisis tenaga Timur Tengah yang semakin meningkat. Beliau mendakwa bekalan produk petroleum Malaysia ‘mencukupi sekurang-kurangnya sehingga Mei 2026,’ memetik semakan dengan Petronas. Beliau mengesahkan semula harga RON95 bersubsidi RM1.99 di bawah Budi Madani, menyebutnya ‘keputusan sukar,’ dan mengumumkan pasukan petugas khas menteri untuk mengkaji kesan ekonomi setiap hari.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini Perdana Menteri sebuah negara pengimport tenaga memberitahu orang ramai jangan bimbang tentang bekalan — sementara Selat Hormuz, yang dilalui kira-kira seperlima minyak dunia, mengalami gangguan ketenteraan aktif. Sebuah kapal kargo Thailand diserang berhampiran Selat pada hari yang sama. ‘Selamat sehingga Mei’ hanya dua bulan lagi.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

‘Selamat sehingga Mei’ bukan jaminan — ia pengakuan berselindung di sebalik keselesaan. Dua bulan bekalan di tengah-tengah peperangan berhampiran laluan minyak terpenting dunia bukan keselamatan tenaga. Ia undur detik. Baru beberapa hari lalu, pada 6 Mac, Anwar sendiri mengakui beliau hanya mampu mengekalkan subsidi RON95 untuk ‘satu atau dua bulan.’ Kini bekalan itu sendiri mempunyai jangka hayat dua bulan. Dan penyelesaiannya? Lebih banyak jawatankuasa.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Anwar mempunyai corak menggunakan Petronas sebagai perisai retorik yang didokumen dengan baik. Pada 2023, beliau menjanjikan rasionalisasi subsidi bahan api ‘dalam beberapa bulan’ — ia mengambil masa sehingga lewat 2024 untuk memperkenalkan Budi Madani. Buku permainan sentiasa sama: sebut Petronas, umumkan jawatankuasa, dan harap krisis berlalu sebelum sidang akhbar seterusnya.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu menjanjikan rakyat Malaysia kerajaan yang berterus terang. Jadi berterus teranglah: dua bulan bekalan bukan keselamatan — ia jam berdetik. Di mana pelan luar jangka untuk Jun? Di mana dasar rizab petroleum strategik yang tidak pernah kamu bina? Jawatankuasa tidak menghasilkan minyak. Jika Selat kekal tertutup dan rantaian bekalan retak, ‘kami telah semak dengan Petronas’ tidak akan memastikan lampu menyala atau lori bergerak.

Sumber: Malay Mail | The Sun | The Edge Malaysia | NST

item.title: [BM] Fahmi Kata Kabinet Mahu Undang-Undang 3R Lebih Ketat — PH Janji Mansuh Undang-Undang Sekat Kebebasan Bersuara, Kini Tulis Yang Baru
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac 2026, Menteri Komunikasi dan jurucakap kerajaan Fahmi Fadzil mengumumkan bahawa Kabinet telah memutuskan tindakan tegas mesti diambil terhadap isu 3R — kaum, agama, dan raja — termasuk di media sosial. Fahmi berkata kerajaan sedang mempertimbangkan untuk meminda undang-undang sedia ada atau memperkenalkan perundangan baru untuk menutup apa yang disifatkannya sebagai kelemahan yang membenarkan individu tertentu mengabaikan langkah penguatkuasaan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Manifesto Pakatan Harapan 2018 secara jelas berjanji memansuhkan atau memperbaharui Akta Hasutan 1948, Akta Mesin Cetak dan Penerbitan, dan Seksyen 233 Akta Komunikasi dan Multimedia — alat yang sama yang BN gunakan selama berdekad untuk mengawal ucapan berkaitan kaum, agama, dan raja. Lapan tahun kemudian, PH tidak memansuhkan satu pun. Sebaliknya, Fahmi kini mengumumkan kerajaan mahu menulis undang-undang baru yang menyekat topik yang sama. Ini bukan reformasi. Ini adalah kerajaan yang bertukar daripada berjanji membongkar mesin penapisan kepada mengembangkannya.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Setiap kerajaan autoritarian Malaysia telah menggunakan 3R sebagai justifikasi untuk membungkam pengkritik. Mahathir melakukannya. Najib melakukannya. Kini kerajaan Anwar melakukannya — pada minggu yang sama ia menggunakan Seksyen 124B untuk menyoal siasat aktivis antirasuah seperti Ketua Pegawai Eksekutif C4, dan pada bulan yang sama ia menggunakan naratif ‘komplot Zionis’ untuk mewajarkan penyiasatan terhadap masyarakat sivil. ‘Kelemahan’ yang Fahmi mahu tutup bukan kelemahan — ia adalah saki-baki kebebasan bersuara dalam sistem yang sudah sarat dengan undang-undang menindas.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Ramkarpal Singh dari DAP sendiri mempersoalkan penggunaan berterusan Akta Hasutan baru-baru ini pada Januari 2026. Pada 2018, Lim Guan Eng menyifatkan Akta Hasutan sebagai ‘drakonian.’ Semasa dalam pembangkang, Fahmi sendiri mengkritik penggunaan undang-undang ucapan oleh BN untuk membungkam perbezaan pendapat. Akta Hasutan kekal. Akta Mesin Cetak kekal. SOSMA kekal. Tiada satu pun dimansuhkan. Dan kini, bukannya menunaikan janji tersebut, PH mencadangkan untuk menambah senjata. Trajektorinya jelas: daripada reformis kepada penguatkuasa dalam satu penggal parlimen.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda memberitahu rakyat Malaysia anda akan meruntuhkan tembok yang BN bina di sekitar kebebasan bersuara. Sebaliknya, anda menambah dawai berduri. Jika anda tidak mampu menangani kritikan tentang kaum, agama, dan raja tanpa mencapai kuasa penahanan baru, anda telah menjadi apa yang anda katakan anda sedang lawan. Manifesto anda berkata mansuhkan. Rakyat masih menunggu.

Sumber: Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Fahmi Umum Jawatankuasa Khas 'Kaji' Kesan Ekonomi Konflik AS-Iran — Buku Panduan Krisis Kerajaan Adalah Satu Lagi Jawatankuasa
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil mengumumkan pada 11 Mac bahawa sebuah jawatankuasa khas yang diketuai pengerusi Petronas Amir Hamzah dan termasuk Menteri Ekonomi Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, akan menilai kesan ekonomi konflik AS-Iran terhadap Malaysia ‘secara harian.’ Penemuan jawatankuasa ini akan dibentangkan pada mesyuarat Kabinet khas yang dijadualkan pada Jumaat, 13 Mac. Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim berkata bekalan petroleum Malaysia ‘mencukupi sekurang-kurangnya sehingga Mei 2026,’ walaupun pasaran minyak global bergolak akibat gangguan di Selat Hormuz dan serangan balas antara Iran, AS dan Israel yang bermula pada 28 Februari.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Tindak balas naluri kerajaan terhadap krisis yang sudah pun menghentam dompet rakyat Malaysia — RON97 naik 60 sen, diesel naik 80 sen, inflasi makanan akan menyusul — adalah menubuhkan jawatankuasa untuk ‘mengkaji’ kerosakan. Bukan bertindak. Bukan membekukan tol, mengawal harga makanan, mengarahkan kerja dari rumah, atau memotong perbelanjaan politik yang membazir. Untuk mengkaji. Inilah intipati buku panduan pengurusan krisis Madani: umumkan jawatankuasa, jadualkan mesyuarat, janjikan laporan, dan suruh rakyat menunggu. Jawatankuasa ini tidak bermesyuarat sehingga 12 Mac, dua minggu penuh selepas konflik bermula. Harga minyak sudah melonjak. Rantaian bekalan sudah terganggu. Sebuah kapal kargo Thai diserang berhampiran Selat Hormuz. Masa untuk ‘penilaian’ adalah 28 Februari. Masa untuk bertindak adalah minggu lepas.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Ini bukan tadbir urus. Ini teater birokrasi yang berpakaian pengurusan krisis. Jawatankuasa ini diketuai pengerusi Petronas — entiti yang sama yang mendapat manfaat daripada harga minyak tinggi — dan termasuk Menteri Ekonomi yang baru beberapa hari lalu mengunjurkan pertumbuhan 4-4.5% seolah-olah rantau ini tidak bergolak. Pengumuman Fahmi dibuat pada hari yang sama dengan sederet keputusan Kabinet lain: mengharamkan rumah terbuka Raya atas nama ‘disiplin fiskal’ sambil meluluskan bantuan tunai RM15 bilion, meluluskan projek mega GLC 5,000 ekar, dan mengumumkan bonus RM500 untuk penjawat awam dan kakitangan masjid. Jawatankuasa ini adalah cara kerajaan kelihatan responsif tanpa benar-benar responsif. Ia adalah kenyataan akhbar yang berpura-pura menjadi dasar.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang dan memalukan tadbir urus melalui jawatankuasa. Pentadbiran Anwar telah melantik jawatankuasa khas untuk penyeludupan diesel, pembaharuan SPRM, isu pegangan saham Azam Baki, dan kini kesan ekonomi perang. Coraknya sentiasa sama: umumkan jawatankuasa, hasilkan tajuk berita, kemudian biarkan penemuan berkumpul debu atau serahkan keputusan kepada orang lain — seperti yang berlaku minggu ini apabila laporan jawatankuasa Azam Baki diserahkan kepada Ketua Setiausaha Negara tanpa pendedahan awam.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda berjanji reformasi. Anda berjanji kerajaan yang bertindak, bukan yang mengkaji masalah sementara rakyat kehabisan simpanan di pam minyak. Dua minggu selepas krisis tenaga geostrategik terburuk sejak Perang Teluk, jawapan anda adalah jawatankuasa yang belum bermesyuarat, laporan yang belum ditulis, dan mesyuarat Kabinet khas yang belum berlaku. Rakyat tidak perlu jawatankuasa untuk memberitahu mereka diesel lebih mahal. Mereka perlukan kerajaan yang faham kepentingan bertindak segera. Setiap hari anda ‘mengkaji,’ seseorang melepaskan makan, menyerap surcaj penghantaran, atau melihat margin mereka runtuh. Berhenti mengkaji kebakaran. Padamkannya.

Sumber: Malay Mail | The Star

item.title: [BM] Kabinet Akhirnya Bincang Laporan Azam Baki — Serah Keputusan Kepada KSN, Enggan Dedahkan Penemuan
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / Cabinet

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac 2026, Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil mengumumkan bahawa Kabinet telah menerima dan membincangkan penemuan jawatankuasa khas — dipengerusikan oleh Peguam Negara Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar — yang menyiasat kontroversi pegangan saham ketua pesuruhjaya SPRM Azam Baki. Keputusan Kabinet: serahkan kepada Ketua Setiausaha Negara Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar untuk menentukan ’langkah seterusnya.’ Fahmi enggan mendedahkan kandungan laporan. Apabila ditanya secara langsung sama ada Kabinet membincangkan pelanjutan kontrak Azam, Fahmi berkata: ‘Ia tidak dibincangkan.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah respons institusi rasmi pentadbiran Anwar terhadap berbulan-bulan krisis mengenai ketua SPRM — dan ia hanyalah penyerahan birokrasi. Pendedahan Bloomberg keluar berminggu-minggu lalu. DAP menuntut RCI. Ahli parlimen belakang PKR memberi amaran Anwar mempertaruhkan legitimasinya. Masyarakat sivil telah dipanggil dan disoal siasat. Dan selepas semua itu, langkah besar Kabinet ialah menyerahkan kepada penjawat awam dan enggan memberitahu orang ramai apa yang sebenarnya ditemui jawatankuasa Peguam Negara.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kabinet ialah badan pembuat keputusan eksekutif tertinggi di negara ini. Ia menerima laporan. Ia membincangkan penemuan. Dan kemudian ia berkata: bukan tanggungjawab kami. Keengganan mendedahkan penemuan amat menjatuhkan — jika laporan itu membersihkan Azam, mereka akan mengatakannya. Jika ia menghukumnya, mereka akan bertindak. Kesunyian memberitahu kamu segala-galanya. Sementara itu, arahan untuk menyiasat tuduhan ‘mafia korporat’ kedengaran tegas sehingga kamu mengingat SPRM diminta menyiasat… dirinya sendiri.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Skandal pegangan saham Azam Baki telah menjadi krisis perlahan sejak 2022. Anwar, selepas mengambil kuasa, memperbaharui kontrak Azam berkali-kali sambil menjanjikan reformasi. Straits Times melaporkan pada 9 Mac bahawa Anwar secara peribadi memberitahu Kabinet beliau tidak akan memperbaharui kontrak Azam apabila tamat 12 Mei — tetapi masih enggan menggantungnya sementara itu.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu menjanjikan Malaysia ‘reformasi institusi’ dan ’toleransi sifar terhadap rasuah.’ Sebaliknya, Kabinet kamu menerima laporan mengenai ketua antirasuah paling berkompromi dalam sejarah Malaysia dan menjawab: ‘Tanya Ketua Setiausaha Negara.’ Itu bukan reformasi. Itu bukan akauntabiliti. Itu bukan pun tadbir urus. Ia pengecut berselindung di sebalik prosedur.

Sumber: Malay Mail | FMT | Bloomberg

item.title: [BM] Kabinet Kekal RON95 Pada RM1.99 — Anwar Kata 'Keputusan Sukar' Sambil Mengelak Reformasi Subsidi Tiga Tahun Berturut-turut
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengumumkan pada 11 Mac 2026 — selepas mesyuarat Kabinet — bahawa harga petrol RON95 di bawah mekanisme subsidi bersasar Budi95 akan kekal pada RM1.99 seliter. Beliau menyebutnya ‘keputusan sukar,’ menyatakan bahawa harga pasaran RON95 telah meningkat kira-kira tiga kali ganda akibat konflik AS-Iran yang semakin meningkat.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan kemurahan hati — ia keruntuhan reformasi. Kerajaan Anwar menjanjikan penstrukturan semula subsidi bahan api bersasar sebagai tunjang disiplin fiskal. Mekanisme Budi95, yang dilancarkan sebagai kompromi yang dicairkan, sepatutnya menjadi langkah pertama ke arah memansuhkan subsidi menyeluruh. Sebaliknya, krisis geopolitik memberi kerajaan alasan sempurna untuk menunda reformasi selama-lamanya sambil membelanjakan berbilion untuk subsidi bahan api tanpa sasaran menjelang pilihan raya kilat yang dijangka secara meluas.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Anwar telah ‘hampir melakukan reformasi’ subsidi bahan api sejak memegang jawatan pada November 2022. Pada 2023, ia ’tahun depan.’ Pada 2024, ia ’tidak lama lagi.’ Baru lima hari lalu, Anwar mengakui beliau hanya mampu mengekalkan harga RM1.99 untuk ‘satu atau dua bulan.’ Kini, dengan Hari Raya menjelang dan pilihan raya dikhabarkan untuk pertengahan 2026, Kabinet ‘memutuskan’ untuk mengekalkannya — seolah-olah ada peluang mereka akan menaikkan harga bahan api beberapa minggu sebelum musim perayaan. Ini bukan keputusan sukar. Ia satu-satunya keputusan yang kerajaan yang takut pengundi akan buat.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Malaysia terperangkap dalam kitaran subsidi bahan api selama berdekad. Kerajaan Najib memansuhkan subsidi RON95 pada 2014 dengan apungan terurus, hanya untuk kerajaan PH 1.0 Mahathir memperkenalkan semula siling harga. Anwar berjanji akan memecahkan corak ini dengan subsidi bersasar. Sebaliknya, Budi95 mengekalkan harga RM1.99 yang sama bagi majoriti besar pemandu.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu memberitahu rakyat Malaysia kamu akan berbeza. Kamu berkata kamu akan membuat keputusan sukar yang Najib dan Muhyiddin tidak sanggup buat. Kini kamu membelanjakan berbilion untuk mengekalkan bahan api murah semasa Ramadan, menjelang Hari Raya, menjelang pilihan raya yang kamu sediakan secara senyap. Ini bukan reformasi. Ini helah tertua dalam politik Malaysia — dan kamu bersumpah tidak akan menggunakannya.

Sumber: The Star | Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Kabinet Lulus Terminal Ketiga Pelabuhan Klang Di Pulau Carey — Projek Mega 5,000 Ekar Penuh Cap Jari GLC
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac 2026, Menteri Pengangkutan Anthony Loke mengumumkan Kabinet telah meluluskan pembangunan Terminal Ketiga Pelabuhan Klang di Pulau Carey — projek mega yang terbengkalai bertahun-tahun. Projek ini melibatkan lebih 5,000 ekar tanah, dengan SD Guthrie (GLC di bawah PNB) dan Perbadanan Kemajuan Negeri Selangor (PKNS) sudah menuntut ribuan hektar tanah dan dasar laut. PKNS diperuntukkan 1,011 hektar tanah dasar laut dan 688 hektar plot Yayasan Selangor diletakkan di bawah pengurusan bersama PKNS. PM Anwar mengeluarkan arahan pada Disember 2025 untuk mempercepatkan projek ini.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah salah satu kelulusan infrastruktur terbesar pentadbiran Anwar — dan ia penuh dengan cap jari GLC dan perbadanan berkaitan negeri. Peruntukan tanah kepada PKNS dan perkongsian SD Guthrie telah dikunci sebelum sebarang tender kompetitif untuk pelabuhan itu sendiri. Pulau Carey adalah rumah kepada komuniti Orang Asli Mah Meri, dan kebimbangan alam sekitar serta perpindahan telah menghantui projek ini selama bertahun-tahun. Anwar berjanji pada Disember 2025 bahawa projek akan diteruskan “dengan perlindungan untuk Orang Asli dan alam sekitar,” tetapi tiada semakan alam sekitar bebas atau mekanisme persetujuan komuniti yang didedahkan kepada umum.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH bertanding atas platform ketelusan, tender terbuka, dan menamatkan penguasaan GLC terhadap aset awam. Apa yang kita dapat sebaliknya ialah projek mega 5,000 ekar yang dibahagikan antara gergasi perladangan berkaitan PNB dan GLC negeri Selangor sebelum orang awam sempat melihat pelan pembangunan. Pejabat MB Selangor mengakui peruntukan tanah “diputuskan beberapa minggu lalu” — sebelum Kabinet pun meluluskan projek tersebut. Itu bukan tadbir urus; itu pra-penempatan. Dan Orang Asli? Mereka mendapat “perlindungan” dalam ucapan dan perpindahan dalam amalan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Sejarah projek mega infrastruktur Malaysia penuh dengan kos berlebihan, perolehan tidak telus, dan pengayaan GLC. ECRL dirunding semula tetapi masih kurang ketelusan penuh. Pelabuhan Klang sendiri adalah tapak skandal PKFZ — bencana RM12.5 bilion yang melibatkan kos tanah melampau, naungan politik, dan pertuduhan jenayah.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda memberitahu pengundi anda akan menamatkan hubungan rapat GLC-negeri yang menukar tanah awam menjadi keuntungan swasta. Jadi di mana tender terbuka untuk Terminal Ketiga Pelabuhan Klang? Di mana penilaian kesan alam sekitar bebas? Di mana rundingan Orang Asli? Pembangunan 5,000 ekar yang melibatkan SD Guthrie, PKNS, dan tanah Yayasan Selangor — semuanya diperuntukkan sebelum Kabinet mengundi — bukan reformasi. Ia permainan lama dengan label Madani.

Sumber: Bernama | Malay Mail | The Edge Malaysia | NST

item.title: [BM] KPDN Kelam-Kabut Soal Penyeludupan Minyak Lepas Harga Cecah $100 — Armizan Janji Penguatkuasaan Yang Sepatutnya Sudah Lama Ada
item.description:
Said by: Armizan Mohd Ali, Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN)

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 10–11 Mac, Menteri KPDN Armizan Mohd Ali mengumumkan kementeriannya akan “memperkukuh penguatkuasaan” terhadap penyeludupan minyak dan mempergiatkan operasi Ops Tiris di sempadan negara, selepas harga minyak global melepasi AS$100 setong akibat konflik AS-Israel-Iran yang semakin memuncak. Armizan mengakui jurang yang melebar antara harga minyak bersubsidi Malaysia (RON95 pada RM1.99) dan harga pasaran di negara jiran mewujudkan “risiko tinggi” penyeludupan. Beliau memberitahu orang ramai tiada “keperluan untuk bimbang atau membeli panik” dan mendakwa pelan kontingensi sudah sedia. Secara berasingan, NST melaporkan Anwar melantik Timbalan Perdana Menteri Fadillah Yusof mengetuai jawatankuasa penguatkuasaan khas khusus menyasarkan penyeludupan diesel.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Malaysia telah tahu tentang penyeludupan minyak selama bertahun-tahun. Kerajaan menyusun semula subsidi diesel pada 2024 sebahagiannya kerana kebocoran rentas sempadan. Ops Tiris sudah berjalan sejak sekurang-kurangnya 2023. Namun apabila krisis tenaga terbesar dalam satu dekad melanda — dengan minyak melepasi $100 dan Selat Hormuz terancam — tindak balas kerajaan adalah mengumumkan ia akan “memperkukuh” penguatkuasaan yang sepatutnya sudah lama ada, dan menubuhkan satu lagi jawatankuasa.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Jaminan Armizan bahawa bekalan “mencukupi” dan “pelan kontingensi sudah sedia” adalah skrip sama yang dibaca setiap menteri KPDN apabila keadaan tidak kena. Tiada pelan kontingensi pernah didedahkan kepada umum. Tiada statistik penguatkuasaan Ops Tiris pernah dikongsi. Dan penubuhan jawatankuasa khas yang diketuai TPM — terhadap masalah yang sudah ada pasukan petugas — adalah teater birokrasi klasik. Jika penguatkuasaan anda memadai, anda tidak perlu jawatankuasa baru setiap kali harga minyak melonjak.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Corak ini bermula berdekad lalu. Pada 2022–2023, penyeludupan diesel melintasi sempadan Thai dan Singapura begitu berleluasa sehingga KPDN sendiri mengakui kerugian ratusan juta ringgit setahun. Subsidi diesel bersasar yang dilaksanakan pada Jun 2024 sepatutnya menyelesaikan masalah — tetapi pengakuan Armizan sendiri bahawa “risiko penyeludupan kekal tinggi” membuktikan ia tidak berhasil.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda berjanji reformasi subsidi akan bijak, bersasar, dan kedap bocor. Ia bukan satu pun daripada itu. Anda sudah tiga tahun untuk membina infrastruktur penguatkuasaan sempadan, mendigitalkan pengesanan pengedaran minyak, dan menutup kelemahan penyeludupan. Sebaliknya, apabila krisis melanda, anda tergesa-gesa mengumumkan “penguatkuasaan diperkukuh” yang sepatutnya sudah wujud. Berhenti menubuhkan jawatankuasa. Mulakan mentadbir.

Sumber: The Sun Malaysia | The Vibes | NST | SCMP | Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Minyak Lepas $100, Selat Hormuz Ditutup, Rakyat Bakar Minyak Pergi Kerja — Anwar Taknak WFH, Taknak Beku Tol, Taknak Kawal Harga Makanan
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Perang Iran-AS telah menolak harga minyak global melepasi $100 setong, menutup Selat Hormuz — laluan hampir 80% minyak Asia — dan menyebabkan FBM KLCI Malaysia menjunam 2.55% dalam satu sesi pada 9 Mac. RON97 naik ke RM3.25, diesel ke RM3.12, dan Anwar sendiri mengakui pada 7 Mac kerajaan hanya mampu mengekalkan RON95 pada RM1.99 untuk ‘satu atau dua bulan lagi.’ Kos pengangkutan melonjak, harga makanan akan menyusul, dan pakar memberi amaran tentang inflasi import yang akan menghentam meja makan rakyat. Tindak balas Anwar setakat ini: tweet solidariti, usul parlimen kecemasan mengutuk Israel, dan Zafrul berkata Malaysia ‘bersedia’ — tanpa menamakan satu pun langkah ekonomi domestik konkrit.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan krisis teori. Rakyat Malaysia sudah membayar lebih di pam untuk RON97 dan diesel. Kos pengangkutan terus mempengaruhi makanan, ubat-ubatan, dan barangan keperluan. Pasaran saham berdarah. Namun Perdana Menteri — yang juga Menteri Kewangan — tidak mengumumkan satu pun langkah ekonomi domestik kecemasan. Tiada campur tangan mod krisis. Tiada bantuan. Hanya ucapan tentang geopolitik sementara kos sara hidup rakyat meningkat secara langsung.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Empat perkara yang Anwar boleh lakukan esok dengan kuasa eksekutif sedia ada — tetapi tidak akan:

1. Arahkan kerja dari rumah untuk semua pekerjaan yang sesuai WFH. Malaysia sudah ada rangka kerja Pengaturan Kerja Fleksibel di bawah Akta Kerja. Semasa COVID, kerajaan mengarahkan WFH dan ia berkesan. Dengan minyak melepasi $100 dan pengguna membelanjakan RM300-500/bulan untuk minyak dan tol, arahan WFH sementara akan segera mengurangkan kos minyak isi rumah.

2. Beku atau mansuhkan tol lebuhraya semasa krisis. Kerajaan sudah melanggar janji pemansuhan tol. Pembebasan tol sementara semasa krisis tenaga akibat perang adalah bantuan ekonomi asas.

3. Kenakan kawalan harga kecemasan ke atas barangan makanan asas. Kos pengangkutan daripada gangguan Hormuz sudah mempengaruhi harga import. Akta Kawalan Harga dan Antipencatutan 2011 memberi kerajaan kuasa untuk menentukan harga maksimum.

4. Beku perbelanjaan GLC dan pelantikan politik yang tidak perlu. Laporan IDEAS mendapati 238 pelantikan politik ke FSB dan GLC di bawah Anwar. Setiap satu datang dengan gaji, elaun, dan faedah yang dibiayai wang awam.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Semasa COVID-19, kerajaan Muhyiddin melaksanakan arahan WFH menyeluruh, moratorium pembayaran pinjaman, bantuan tunai, dan subsidi gaji dalam beberapa minggu. Anwar, yang mengawal Pejabat PM dan Kementerian Kewangan, mempunyai lebih banyak kuasa fiskal tertumpu — dan menggunakannya kurang.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda memberitahu kami anda berbeza. Anda memberitahu kami Madani bermakna rakyat didahulukan. Minyak melepasi $100. Selat Hormuz terganggu. Tindak balas krisis anda adalah… usul parlimen tentang Iran dan tweet tentang solidariti? Arahkan WFH. Bekukan tol. Kawal harga makanan. Potong lemak GLC. Anda ada kuasa. Anda ada duluan. Anda ada alat. Gunakannya.

Sumber: Xinhua | SAYS | The Star | NST | CNBC | Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Mohamad Hasan Kata Agensi Pelancongan Mesti Bawa Pulang Jemaah Umrah Terperangkap — 'Tak Boleh Serah Kerajaan Sahaja'
item.description:
Said by: Mohamad Hasan, Foreign Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Menteri Luar Negeri Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, bercakap pada sidang akhbar di Putrajaya pada 11 Mac, mengisytiharkan bahawa syarikat pelancongan dan agensi yang menguruskan pakej umrah mesti bertanggungjawab sepenuhnya membawa pulang jemaah yang terperangkap akibat konflik Asia Barat. Beliau berkata syarikat penerbangan juga mesti menghormati tiket mereka: ‘Jika tiket dengan Malaysia Airlines, MAS mesti bawa pulang. Jika tiket AirAsia, AirAsia mesti buat demikian.’ Beliau menambah tegas: ‘Ia tidak boleh diserahkan kepada kerajaan Malaysia sahaja.’ Ini pada hari yang sama penerbangan pemindahan pertama kerajaan — pesawat sewa Malaysia Airlines A330-300 — mendarat di KLIA dengan hanya 163 rakyat Malaysia dari Arab Saudi, Qatar, Jordan dan Syria.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah kes klasik kerajaan membingkaikan kegagalan tindak balas krisis sebagai soal akauntabiliti sektor swasta. Pada 3 Mac, Mohamad Hasan memberitahu Dewan Negara bahawa keadaan di Teluk ’terkawal’ dan ’tiada keperluan untuk pelan pemindahan.’ Lapan hari kemudian, kementeriannya menyelaraskan penerbangan penghantaran pulang kecemasan sementara ratusan rakyat Malaysia — termasuk jemaah umrah yang terperangkap selama tiga hingga lima hari di lapangan terbang Dubai, Doha, Abu Dhabi dan Bahrain — menunggu seseorang untuk membawa mereka pulang.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kementerian Luar Negeri wujud tepat untuk saat seperti ini: apabila warganegara Malaysia terperangkap di luar negara semasa konflik dan memerlukan kerajaan bertindak. Menyuruh agensi pelancongan dan syarikat penerbangan menguruskannya bukan kepimpinan krisis — ia memindahkan bil. Ramai jemaah ini warga emas, mengembara dengan pakej bajet, dan tidak mempunyai daya tawar untuk menuntut penerbangan dijadualkan semula daripada syarikat penerbangan yang operasinya huru-hara. Kerajaan memindahkan 163 orang dengan satu penerbangan sewa dan kemudian berpaling ke mikrofon untuk berkata, pada dasarnya, ‘yang lain masalah orang lain.’

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang tindak balas pemindahan yang lambat atau tidak memadai. Semasa krisis Sudan 2023, rakyat Malaysia terperangkap berhari-hari sebelum Wisma Putra bergerak. Semasa COVID-19, penerbangan penghantaran pulang huru-hara. Coraknya biasa: tangguh, kemudian alihkan. Jaminan Mohamad Hasan ’terkawal’ pada 3 Mac menggemakan setiap menteri luar Malaysia yang pernah meremehkan krisis yang sedang berkembang sehingga ia tidak dapat dinafikan.

🎯 Teguran PH

PH berkuasa dengan janji kerajaan yang mengutamakan rakyat — ‘Kerajaan Madani,’ pentadbiran yang penyayang dan responsif. Apabila warganegara anda tidur di lantai lapangan terbang di Teluk kerana ruang udara ditutup dan peluru berpandu menembak, menyuruh agensi pelancongan menyelesaikannya bukan keprihatinan. Ia pengecut birokrasi. Pindahkan rakyat anda dulu. Selesaikan siapa bayar kemudian.

Sumber: NST | The Star | The Sun | FMT (kenyataan 3 Mac)

item.title: [BM] Polis Soal Siasat Ketua Pegawai Eksekutif C4 Antirasuah Bawah Seksyen 124B — Kerajaan Anwar Guna Siasatan 'Komplot Guling' Untuk Gertak Masyarakat Sivil
item.description:
Said by: Bukit Aman (Royal Malaysia Police) / C4 Center (Pushpan Murugiah, CEO)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 10 Mac 2026, Ketua Pegawai Eksekutif Pusat Membanteras Rasuah dan Kronisme (C4) Pushpan Murugiah dipanggil ke Bukit Aman dan disoal siasat selama kira-kira 90 minit di bawah Seksyen 124B Kanun Keseksaan — aktiviti yang memudaratkan demokrasi berparlimen. Soal siasat itu dikaitkan dengan siasatan polis terhadap dakwaan komplot menggulingkan kerajaan. Tetapi soal siasat melangkaui e-mel tersebut. Polis mempersoalkan sumber pembiayaan C4, sama ada organisasi itu menerima arahan untuk mengeluarkan kenyataan, dan — yang mengejutkan — mencabar hak C4 untuk mengulas hal ehwal tadbir urus negara. C4 mengesahkan beberapa pengulas politik dan ahli akademik lain turut dipanggil ke Bukit Aman di bawah siasatan yang sama.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan siasatan polis rutin. Ini adalah jentera negara — beroperasi di bawah kerajaan yang berkuasa atas mandat reformasi — dikerahkan untuk meneliti, menekan, dan menjejaskan kesahihan badan pemerhati antirasuah bebas. Seksyen 124B adalah pertuduhan berat. Menggunakannya untuk menyoal pemimpin masyarakat sivil tentang pembiayaan organisasi mereka, autonomi, dan kedudukan untuk mengulas hal ehwal awam adalah kesan menggerunkan yang klasik. Ia menghantar isyarat kepada setiap NGO, ahli akademik, dan pengulas politik di Malaysia: kritik pengendalian kerajaan terhadap skandal SPRM, dan anda mungkin mendapati diri anda di bilik soal siasat polis menjelaskan mengapa anda rasa anda berhak bersuara.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Anwar Ibrahim membina seluruh kerjaya politiknya atas dakwaan bahawa beliau dizalimi oleh kerajaan yang mempersenjatakan institusi terhadap pengkritik. Beliau menghabiskan bertahun-tahun di penjara. Beliau menghimpunkan jutaan orang dengan janji bahawa di bawah PH, polis tidak akan digunakan untuk membungkam perbezaan pendapat. Kini kerajaannya mengheret masuk Ketua Pegawai Eksekutif NGO antirasuah dan bertanya — dengan muka selamba — sama ada C4 mempunyai kedudukan untuk mengulas tentang rasuah. Kemunafikan ini sungguh mengejutkan. C4 telah bersikap tidak berpihak sejak 2014. Ia mengkritik BN, ia mengkritik PN, dan kini ia mengkritik PH — dan inilah pentadbiran yang menghantar polis.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Corak ini bukan baru dalam politik Malaysia — ia hanya baru untuk PH. Di bawah Najib, pemimpin masyarakat sivil dan wartawan rutin dipanggil di bawah Akta Hasutan, SOSMA, dan Akta Komunikasi dan Multimedia kerana mengkritik 1MDB dan rasuah kerajaan. Di bawah Muhyiddin, Ordinan Darurat digunakan untuk menggantung Parlimen dan membungkam perbezaan pendapat. Anwar sendiri adalah mangsa paling terkenal persenjataan institusi. Kini, di bawah kerajaannya sendiri, Seksyen 124B diregangkan untuk meliputi aktiviti NGO yang satu-satunya kesalahan mereka adalah menuntut akauntabiliti daripada SPRM.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda berjanji reformasi. Anda berjanji kebebasan institusi. Anda berjanji polis tidak akan lagi digunakan sebagai senjata politik. Kini polis anda menyoal Ketua Pegawai Eksekutif NGO antirasuah tentang hak mereka untuk bersuara — di bawah siasatan ‘komplot guling’ yang sama yang anda gunakan untuk memburukkan keluarga Daim, menyerang Bloomberg, dan mengalihkan perhatian daripada skandal Azam Baki. Setiap jam kerajaan Anwar mengugut masyarakat sivil dan bukannya memperbaharui SPRM adalah satu lagi jam yang membuktikan jenama Madani itu kosong.

Sumber: FMT | BFM News

item.title: [BM] Sabah Tolak PH Di Peti Undi — Kini Mustapha Sakmud Kata Dia Suara Mereka Di Putrajaya
item.description:
Said by: Mustapha Sakmud, Sabah PH Chairman / Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 11 Mac 2026, Pengerusi PH Sabah dan Menteri Hal Ehwal Sabah dan Sarawak Mustapha Sakmud memberitahu FMT bahawa PH Sabah akan berperanan sebagai ‘saluran’ untuk memastikan isu negeri disuarakan di peringkat persekutuan. Beliau berkata beliau berhasrat memanfaatkan pelantikan menteri — dibuat pada 15 Februari — untuk membawa kebimbangan rakyat Sabah ke mesyuarat Kabinet.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

PH dihancurkan dalam pilihan raya negeri Sabah pada 29 November 2025. PKR hanya berjaya dengan satu kerusi. DAP tidak menang langsung. GRS meraih 29 kerusi, Warisan meraih 25, dan rakyat Sabah menjelaskan dengan nyata mereka tidak mahu PH mewakili mereka di peringkat negeri. Namun tiga bulan kemudian, Anwar memberikan Mustapha portfolio menteri persekutuan — memasukkan PH ke dalam ruang politik Sabah melalui pintu belakang. Ini bukan perwakilan demokratik. Ini naungan sebagai pengganti mandat.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kamu tidak boleh kalah pilihan raya seteruk ini dan kemudian mendakwa kamu suara rakyat. PH menang satu kerusi di seluruh dewan undangan negeri Sabah. Itu bukan saluran — itu surat penolakan. Hujah Mustapha berbunyi: ‘Rakyat Sabah tidak mengundi kami, tetapi kami dapat jawatan Kabinet, jadi kami akan wakili mereka.’ Ini tepat jenis keistimewaan politik tanpa usaha yang PH pernah tuduh BN amalkan.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Ini mengikuti tradisi panjang Malaysia menggunakan pelantikan persekutuan untuk mengimbangi kekalahan pilihan raya di peringkat negeri. BN melakukannya selama berdekad-dekad di Sabah dan Sarawak. PH, sebagai pembangkang, mengecam amalan yang sama ini. Kini dalam kerajaan, mereka mengamalkan buku permainan yang sama.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu menjanjikan reformasi. Kamu menjanjikan kuasa datang daripada rakyat, bukan daripada Putrajaya. Dan kini kamu memberikan kerusi Kabinet kepada seorang yang gabungannya menang satu kerusi di seluruh pilihan raya negeri dan menyuruhnya mewakili rakyat yang menolaknya. Jika ‘rakyat Sabah akan menghargainya,’ seperti dakwaan Mustapha, maka ujilah — bertanding pilihan raya kecil di Sabah atas rekod PH.

Sumber: FMT

item.title: Anwar Bans Raya Open Houses for 'Fiscal Discipline' — Days After Announcing RM15 Billion in Cash Handouts and RM500 Bonuses
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced that all government agencies, GLCs, and GLICs are banned from holding Aidilfitri open house celebrations this year — a decision agreed upon in Cabinet that morning. He framed it as a signal of fiscal discipline amid global uncertainty caused by the Middle East conflict. In the same breath, Anwar also announced tighter restrictions on overseas travel for ministers and government officials, saying only pre-scheduled and essential foreign engagements would be approved. A special Cabinet meeting has been called for March 13 to assess the fiscal situation.

🔥 Why It Matters

This austerity performance arrives just two days after the government announced it would maintain the RON95 subsidy at RM1.99 — and less than a week after Anwar brought forward RM15 billion in Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) cash handouts plus RM500 Special Financial Assistance for civil servants and mosque personnel, all conveniently timed before Hari Raya. The government is spending record sums on direct cash transfers — the most electorally potent form of spending — while cancelling community open houses that cost a fraction of that. This is not fiscal discipline. This is selective austerity: cut the visible, communal celebrations while flooding individual bank accounts with cash right before a rumoured snap election.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

You cannot announce the largest cash transfer programme in Malaysian history one week, then cancel Raya open houses the next week and call it prudence. Open houses are community events — they cost millions. The STR programme costs billions. If the Middle East crisis is so dire that civil servants can’t eat ketupat at a government function, why is it not dire enough to pause the RM15 billion in pre-election cash? Because open houses don’t buy votes. Cash in bank accounts before polling day does. The travel restriction is window dressing — ’except for those already scheduled’ is a loophole wide enough to fly the entire Cabinet through.

📜 Previous Cases

This is textbook Malaysian crisis theatre. Mahathir did it during the 1997 Asian financial crisis — austerity for the rakyat, bailouts for cronies. Najib talked fiscal discipline while 1MDB bled billions. Anwar himself mocked BN’s festive-season handouts when he was in opposition, calling them vote-buying. Now he’s doing the same thing, but bigger: record STR payouts, record BKK bonuses, and a Raya ban for optics. The pattern is always the same — cut the things that don’t win elections, spend lavishly on the things that do.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised reformasi, not repackaged BN-era electoral spending with a piety overlay. If you’re genuinely worried about the fiscal position, start with the RM15 billion in cash transfers you just announced, not the nasi lemak at government open houses. Cancel the political appointments bleeding FSBs dry. Freeze the patronage spending. Don’t lecture the rakyat about moderation while your government’s chequebook is wide open for every vote bank that matters before GE16. This is not discipline — this is performance.

Sources: Malay Mail | The Rakyat Post

item.title: Anwar Says Petroleum Supply 'Secure Until May' — While Strait of Hormuz Burns and Ships Get Attacked
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim held a live-streamed press conference in Putrajaya to address the escalating Middle East energy crisis. He claimed that Malaysia’s petroleum product supplies are “sufficient at least until May 2026,” citing checks with Petronas. He reaffirmed the RM1.99 subsidised RON95 price under Budi Madani, called it a “difficult decision,” and announced a special task force of ministers to review economic impacts daily. He also tasked the Deputy Prime Minister and KPDN with cracking down on fuel smuggling.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is the Prime Minister of an energy-importing nation telling the public not to worry about supply — while the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly a fifth of the world’s oil transits, is under active military disruption. A Thai cargo ship was attacked near the Strait on the same day. Brent crude hit US$119 per barrel days earlier before easing to around US$90. Airlines across Asia are hiking fares. The IEA is recommending a record 400-million-barrel emergency reserve release. Anwar’s reassurance lands in a context where every major energy market signal is flashing red — and “secure until May” is only two months away.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

“Secure until May” is not reassurance — it’s a confession dressed up as comfort. Two months of supply in the middle of a shooting war near the world’s most important oil chokepoint is not energy security. It’s a countdown. Just days ago, on March 6, Anwar himself admitted he could only hold the RON95 subsidy for “one or two months.” Now the supply itself has a two-month shelf life. And the solution? More committees. A daily economic impact review panel. A smuggling task force. None of this addresses what happens in June if the Strait stays contested, if Petronas hedges unwind, or if global allocation priorities shift away from Southeast Asia. The press conference was a performance of control, not a demonstration of it.

📜 Previous Cases

Anwar has a well-documented pattern of using Petronas as a rhetorical shield. In 2023, he promised fuel subsidy rationalisation “within months” — it took until late 2024 to even introduce Budi Madani, and the targeted subsidy still doesn’t cover diesel properly. In 2024, he told Malaysians the economy was “on track” while inflation ate into household budgets. The “we checked with Petronas” line echoes previous administrations — Najib’s government used similar assurances during the 2014-2015 oil price crash while raiding Petronas dividends to plug fiscal holes. The playbook is always the same: invoke Petronas, announce a committee, and hope the crisis passes before the next press conference.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised Malaysians a government that tells the truth, even when it’s hard. So tell the truth: two months of supply is not security — it’s a ticking clock. Where is the contingency plan for June? Where is the strategic petroleum reserve policy you never built? Where is the long-term energy diversification strategy beyond “Petronas says we’re fine”? You banned Raya open houses for “fiscal discipline” the same week you announced RM15 billion in cash handouts. You formed three new committees in one press conference. Committees don’t produce oil. If the Strait stays shut and supply chains fracture, “we checked with Petronas” will not keep the lights on or the lorries moving. Stop performing calm and start building resilience.

Sources: Malay Mail | The Sun | The Edge Malaysia | NST

item.title: Cabinet Finally Discusses Azam Baki Report — Punts Decision to Chief Secretary, Refuses to Reveal Findings
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / Cabinet

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced that Cabinet had received and discussed the findings of the special committee — chaired by Attorney General Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar — investigating MACC chief commissioner Azam Baki’s shareholding controversy. Cabinet’s decision: hand the matter to Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar to determine “next steps.” Fahmi refused to disclose the report’s contents. When asked directly whether Cabinet discussed the extension of Azam’s contract, Fahmi said: “It was not discussed.” Separately, Cabinet instructed the police, Securities Commission, MACC itself, and the Inland Revenue Board to continue investigating Bloomberg’s “corporate mafia” allegations — including seizing proceeds believed to have criminal elements.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is the Anwar administration’s formal institutional response to months of crisis over the MACC chief — and it amounts to a bureaucratic handoff. The Bloomberg exposé dropped weeks ago. DAP demanded an RCI. PKR backbenchers warned Anwar risks his legitimacy. Civil society has been summoned and interrogated. And after all of that, Cabinet’s big move is to delegate to a civil servant and refuse to tell the public what the AG’s committee actually found. The message is clear: accountability will be managed, not delivered. By routing the decision through the Chief Secretary rather than making a political call, Anwar insulates himself from direct responsibility for whatever happens next — whether Azam stays, goes, or quietly expires in May.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The Cabinet is the highest executive decision-making body in the country. It received the report. It discussed the findings. And then it said: not our call. This is the equivalent of a CEO receiving an internal audit on a compromised compliance officer and telling HR to “handle it.” The Prime Minister has the power to act. He chose not to. The refusal to disclose the findings is especially damning — if the report cleared Azam, they would say so. If it condemned him, they would act. The silence tells you everything. Meanwhile, the instruction to probe “corporate mafia” allegations sounds tough until you remember MACC is being asked to investigate… itself. The fox is auditing the henhouse, and Cabinet just handed it the clipboard.

📜 Previous Cases

Azam Baki’s shareholding scandal has been a slow-rolling crisis since 2022, when the Securities Commission confirmed he held millions of shares in a public company — a potential breach of anti-corruption rules. Anwar, upon taking power, renewed Azam’s contract multiple times while promising reform. In February 2026, Bloomberg published detailed allegations of a “corporate mafia” operating within MACC. Anwar’s response has followed a consistent pattern: acknowledge the problem, set up a committee, delay action, and let the news cycle move on. The Straits Times reported on March 9 that Anwar had privately told Cabinet he would not renew Azam’s contract when it expires May 12 — but still refused to suspend him in the meantime.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised Malaysia “institutional reform” and “zero tolerance for corruption.” Your own manifesto said MACC would be made independent and accountable. Instead, your Cabinet received a report on the most compromised anti-corruption chief in Malaysian history and responded with: “Ask the Chief Secretary.” That is not reform. That is not accountability. That is not even governance. It is cowardice dressed in procedure. If you cannot fire the man running your anti-corruption agency while he is under investigation for the very corruption he is supposed to fight, then every word of your reform agenda is decoration. Act, or admit you never intended to.

Sources: Malay Mail | FMT | Bloomberg

item.title: Cabinet Greenlights Port Klang Third Terminal on Carey Island — A 5,000-Acre Megaproject With GLC Fingerprints All Over It
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Transport Minister Anthony Loke announced that Cabinet has approved the development of Port Klang’s Third Terminal on Carey Island — a megaproject that has been in limbo for years. The project involves over 5,000 acres of land, with SD Guthrie (a GLC under PNB) and the Selangor State Development Corporation (PKNS) already staking claims on thousands of hectares of land and seabed. PKNS was allocated 1,011 hectares of seabed land and a 688-hectare Yayasan Selangor plot was placed under joint PKNS management. PM Anwar issued a directive in December 2025 to accelerate the project, and now Cabinet has formally greenlit it.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is one of the largest infrastructure approvals of the Anwar administration — and it has GLC and state-linked corporation fingerprints all over it. The land allocation to PKNS and the SD Guthrie partnership were locked in before any competitive tender for the port itself. Carey Island is home to the Mah Meri Orang Asli community, and environmental and displacement concerns have dogged this project for years. Anwar promised in December 2025 that the project would proceed “with safeguards for Orang Asli and the environment,” but no independent environmental review or community consent mechanism has been made public. The approval comes amid a Middle East crisis, fiscal austerity theatre (banning Raya open houses), and pre-election positioning — making the timing politically convenient for announcing a mega-development that benefits Selangor’s PH-aligned state machinery.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH ran on transparency, open tenders, and ending GLC capture of public assets. What we got instead is a 5,000-acre megaproject carved up between a PNB-linked plantation giant and the Selangor state GLC before the public even saw a development plan. The Selangor MB’s office admitted the land allocation was “decided a few weeks ago” — before Cabinet even approved the project. That’s not governance; that’s pre-positioning. And the Orang Asli? They get “safeguards” in speeches and displacement in practice. This is exactly the kind of opaque, GLC-driven megaproject that PH promised to end when it was in opposition.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia’s infrastructure megaproject history is littered with cost overruns, opaque procurement, and GLC enrichment. The East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) was renegotiated but still lacks full transparency. The Penang Transport Master Plan has faced years of environmental and governance controversy. Port Klang itself was the site of the Port Klang Free Zone (PKFZ) scandal — a RM12.5 billion fiasco involving inflated land costs, political patronage, and criminal charges. PH’s own 2018-2020 government inherited that mess and promised never to repeat it. Now they’re greenlighting another massive port project on the same stretch of coast, with pre-allocated GLC land deals and no visible competitive process.

🎯 PH Callout

You told voters you’d end the cosy GLC-state nexus that turns public land into private profit. You said open tenders and institutional reform would replace backroom deals. So where is the open tender for Port Klang Terminal 3? Where is the independent environmental impact assessment? Where is the Orang Asli consultation? A 5,000-acre development involving SD Guthrie, PKNS, and Yayasan Selangor land — all allocated before Cabinet even voted — is not reform. It’s the same game with a Madani label. If you can ban Raya open houses for “fiscal discipline,” you can publish the full procurement framework for a multi-billion ringgit port before you approve it.

Sources: Bernama | Malay Mail | The Edge Malaysia | NST

item.title: Cabinet Maintains RON95 at RM1.99 — Anwar Calls It a 'Difficult Decision' While Dodging Subsidy Reform for the Third Year Running
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced on March 11, 2026 — following a Cabinet meeting — that the price of RON95 petrol under the Budi95 targeted subsidy mechanism will remain at RM1.99 per litre. He called it a “difficult decision,” noting that the market price of RON95 has risen roughly threefold due to the escalating US-Iran conflict disrupting oil markets and threatening Gulf shipping routes. RON97 had already risen to RM3.25, unsubsidised RON95 to RM2.67, and diesel to RM3.12. Anwar framed the decision as a sacrifice for the rakyat.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not generosity — it is the collapse of reform. Anwar’s government promised targeted fuel subsidy restructuring as a cornerstone of fiscal discipline. The Budi95 mechanism, launched as a watered-down compromise, was supposed to be the first step toward removing blanket subsidies. Instead, a geopolitical crisis has given the government the perfect excuse to shelve reform indefinitely while spending billions on untargeted fuel subsidies ahead of widely expected snap elections. Every week the subsidy is maintained at current market spreads, Malaysia hemorrhages fiscal resources that could go to healthcare, education, or infrastructure.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar has been “about to reform” fuel subsidies since he took office in November 2022. In 2023, it was “next year.” In 2024, it was “soon.” In 2025, the Budi95 mechanism finally launched — then immediately became a political tool rather than a fiscal one. Just five days ago, Anwar admitted he could only hold the RM1.99 price for “one or two months.” Now, with Hari Raya approaching and elections rumoured for mid-2026, the Cabinet “decides” to maintain it — as if there was ever a chance they’d raise fuel prices weeks before a festive season and a potential snap poll. This isn’t a difficult decision. It’s the only decision a government terrified of voters would make.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has been trapped in the fuel subsidy cycle for decades. Najib’s government removed RON95 subsidies in 2014 with a managed float, only for Mahathir’s PH 1.0 government to reintroduce price ceilings. Anwar promised to break the pattern with targeted subsidies — means-tested, fiscally responsible, reform-driven. Instead, Budi95 kept the same RM1.99 price for the vast majority of motorists and excluded only foreign-registered vehicles and luxury cars. The IMF, World Bank, and Malaysia’s own fiscal policy advisors have repeatedly warned that blanket fuel subsidies are regressive, benefiting the wealthy more than the poor. Anwar knows this. He cited these studies himself when he was in opposition.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians you’d be different. You said you’d make the hard calls Najib and Muhyiddin wouldn’t. You quoted fiscal responsibility in every speech, promised targeted subsidies in every manifesto, and lectured the opposition about populism from every podium. Now you’re spending billions to keep fuel cheap during Ramadan, ahead of Hari Raya, ahead of elections you’re quietly preparing for. This isn’t reform. This is the oldest trick in Malaysian politics — and you swore you’d never play it. Every ringgit spent on untargeted subsidies is a ringgit stolen from the structural reforms you promised the rakyat.

Sources: The Star — Cabinet decides to maintain subsidised RON95 price at RM1.99 | Malay Mail | The Star — PM: Fuel subsidy can last up to two months (March 7) | paultan.org — Budi95 petrol price maintained (March 6)

item.title: Fahmi Announces Special Committee to 'Assess' Economic Impact of US-Iran Conflict — Government's Crisis Playbook Is Another Committee
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced on March 11 that a special committee, led by Petronas chairman Amir Hamzah and including Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, will evaluate the economic impact of the US-Iran conflict on Malaysia ‘on a daily basis.’ The committee’s findings will be presented at a special Cabinet meeting scheduled for Friday, March 13. Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia’s petroleum supply is ‘sufficient at least until May 2026,’ even as global oil markets reel from the Strait of Hormuz disruption and retaliatory strikes between Iran, the US, and Israel that began on February 28.

🔥 Why It Matters

The government’s instinctive response to a crisis that is already hitting Malaysian wallets — RON97 up 60 sen, diesel up 80 sen, food inflation incoming — is to form a committee to ‘assess’ the damage. Not to act. Not to freeze tolls, cap food prices, mandate work-from-home, or cut wasteful political spending. To study. This is the Madani crisis management playbook distilled to its essence: announce a committee, schedule a meeting, promise a report, and tell the rakyat to wait. The committee doesn’t even meet until March 12, two full weeks after the conflict began. Fuel prices have already spiked. Supply chains are already disrupted. A Thai cargo ship was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz. The time for ‘assessment’ was February 28. The time for action was last week.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

This is not governance. This is bureaucratic theatre dressed up as crisis management. The committee is led by the Petronas chairman — the same entity that benefits from higher oil prices — and includes the Economy Minister, who was publicly projecting 4-4.5% growth just days ago as if the region wasn’t on fire. Fahmi’s announcement came on the same day as a cascade of other Cabinet decisions: banning Raya open houses for ‘fiscal discipline’ while approving RM15 billion in cash handouts, greenlighting a 5,000-acre GLC megaproject, and announcing RM500 bonuses for civil servants and mosque staff. The committee is the government’s way of looking responsive without being responsive. It is a press release pretending to be policy.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long and inglorious history of governance-by-committee. Anwar’s administration has appointed special committees for diesel smuggling, MACC reform, the Azam Baki shareholding issue, and now the economic impact of a war. The pattern is always the same: announce the committee, generate a headline, then quietly let the findings gather dust or punt the decision to someone else — as happened this same week when the Azam Baki committee report was handed to the Chief Secretary with no public disclosure. The committee reflex is not unique to PH, but PH was supposed to be different. They promised responsive, transparent, and accountable governance. Instead they’ve perfected the art of the bureaucratic stall.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised reformasi. You promised a government that acts, not one that studies the problem while the rakyat burns through their savings at the pump. Two weeks into the worst geopolitical energy crisis since the Gulf War, your response is a committee that hasn’t met yet, a report that hasn’t been written, and a special Cabinet meeting that hasn’t happened. The rakyat doesn’t need a committee to tell them diesel is more expensive. They need a government that acts like it understands urgency. Every day you ‘assess,’ someone skips a meal, absorbs a delivery surcharge, or watches their margins collapse. Stop studying the fire. Put it out.

Sources: Malay Mail | The Star | Borneo Post

item.title: Fahmi Says Cabinet Wants Stricter 3R Laws — PH Promised to Repeal Restrictive Speech Laws, Now They're Writing New Ones
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Communications Minister and government spokesperson Fahmi Fadzil announced that Cabinet has decided firm action must be taken on 3R issues — race, religion, and royalty — including on social media. Fahmi said the government is considering amending existing laws or introducing new legislation to close what it describes as loopholes that allow certain individuals to ignore enforcement measures. He said the Attorney General has been directed to act decisively, and that the review would examine gaps in current laws that prevent early enforcement actions such as detentions.

🔥 Why It Matters

Pakatan Harapan’s 2018 manifesto explicitly pledged to repeal or reform the Sedition Act 1948, the Printing Presses and Publications Act, and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act — the very tools BN used for decades to police speech on race, religion, and royalty. Eight years later, PH has not repealed a single one. Instead, Fahmi is now announcing the government wants to write new restrictive laws on the same topics. This is not reform. This is a government that went from promising to dismantle the censorship apparatus to expanding it — while using the language of ‘harmony’ and ’national unity’ as cover.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Every authoritarian Malaysian government has used 3R as the justification for silencing critics. Mahathir did it. Najib did it. Now Anwar’s government is doing it — in the same week it’s using Section 124B to interrogate anti-corruption activists like C4’s CEO, and in the same month it invoked a ‘Zionist plot’ narrative to justify investigating civil society. The ’loopholes’ Fahmi wants to close are not loopholes — they are what remains of free expression in a system already stacked with repressive laws. When PH says ‘stricter enforcement,’ they don’t mean protecting minorities. They mean giving the state more power to detain first and ask questions later.

📜 Previous Cases

DAP’s own Ramkarpal Singh questioned the continued use of the Sedition Act as recently as January 2026, noting it contradicts PH’s reform agenda. In 2018, Lim Guan Eng called the Sedition Act ‘draconian.’ In opposition, Fahmi himself criticised BN’s use of speech laws to silence dissent. The Sedition Act remains on the books. The Printing Presses Act remains on the books. SOSMA remains on the books. Not one has been repealed. And now, instead of delivering on those promises, PH is proposing to add to the arsenal. The trajectory is clear: from reformers to enforcers in one parliamentary term.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians you would tear down the walls BN built around free speech. Instead, you’re adding barbed wire. If you cannot handle criticism about race, religion, and royalty without reaching for new detention powers, you have become the thing you said you were fighting. Every new law you pass to silence 3R speech will be inherited by the next government — including the ones you call extremists. Think about that before you hand them a sharpened tool. Your manifesto said repeal. The rakyat is still waiting.

Sources: Malay Mail

item.title: KPDN Scrambles on Fuel Smuggling After Oil Tops $100 — Armizan Promises Enforcement That Should Have Been in Place for Years
item.description:
Said by: Armizan Mohd Ali, Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN)

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 10–11, KPDN Minister Armizan Mohd Ali announced that his ministry would “strengthen enforcement” against fuel smuggling and intensify Ops Tiris operations at national borders, after global oil prices topped US$100 per barrel due to the escalating US-Israel-Iran conflict. Armizan acknowledged that the widening gap between Malaysia’s subsidised fuel prices (RON95 at RM1.99) and market prices in neighbouring countries creates “high risk” of smuggling. He told the public there was “no need for concern or panic buying” and claimed contingency plans were in place. Separately, NST reported that Anwar appointed Deputy Prime Minister Fadillah Yusof to chair a special enforcement committee specifically targeting diesel smuggling (single-source: NST).

🔥 Why It Matters

Malaysia has known about fuel smuggling for years. The government restructured diesel subsidies in 2024 partly because of cross-border leakage. Op Tiris has been running since at least 2023. Yet when the biggest energy crisis in a decade hits — with oil past $100 and the Strait of Hormuz under threat — the government’s response is to announce that it will “strengthen” enforcement it should have had in place all along, and form yet another committee. This is reactive governance dressed up as decisive action. The gap between Malaysia’s RM1.99 RON95 and unsubsidised market prices of RM2.67+ makes Malaysia a fuel smuggling magnet, and every week of delay costs the rakyat.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Armizan’s reassurance that supply is “sufficient” and that “contingency plans are in place” is the same script every KPDN minister reads when things go sideways. No contingency plan has ever been made public. No enforcement statistics on Op Tiris outcomes have been shared. And the formation of a special committee chaired by the DPM — on a problem that already has an existing task force — is textbook bureaucratic theatre. If your enforcement was adequate, you wouldn’t need a new committee every time oil prices spike. The government is spending more time announcing what it will do than actually doing it.

📜 Previous Cases
