This pattern goes back decades. In 2022–2023, diesel smuggling across the Thai and Singapore borders was so rampant that KPDN itself admitted losses of hundreds of millions of ringgit annually. The targeted diesel subsidy implemented in June 2024 was supposed to solve the problem — but Armizan’s own admission that “the risk of smuggling remains high” proves it didn’t. In 2025, Anwar boasted about saving RM4 billion in subsidy leakage — a claim that was never independently verified. Now, with oil at $100+ and the subsidy gap wider than ever, the same enforcement gaps are back, and the government’s playbook hasn’t changed.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised subsidy reform would be smart, targeted, and leak-proof. It’s none of those things. You’ve had three years to build border enforcement infrastructure, digitise fuel distribution tracking, and close the smuggling loopholes that every analyst warned you about. Instead, the moment a crisis hits, you scramble to announce “strengthened enforcement” that should already exist and form a committee that duplicates existing task forces. Armizan telling Malaysians “don’t panic buy” while admitting smuggling risk is “high” is not reassurance — it’s a contradiction. Stop forming committees. Start governing.

Sources: The Sun Malaysia | The Vibes | NST | SCMP | Straits Times

item.title: Mohamad Hasan Says Travel Agencies Must Bring Home Stranded Umrah Pilgrims — 'It Cannot Be Left to the Government Alone'
item.description:
Said by: Mohamad Hasan, Foreign Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, speaking at a press conference in Putrajaya on March 11, declared that travel companies and agencies managing umrah packages must take full responsibility for bringing home pilgrims stranded by the West Asia conflict. He said airlines must also honour their tickets: ‘If the ticket is with Malaysia Airlines, MAS must bring them home. If it is an AirAsia ticket, AirAsia must do so.’ He added bluntly: ‘It cannot be left to the Malaysian government alone.’ This came the same day the government’s first evacuation flight — a chartered Malaysia Airlines A330-300 — landed at KLIA with just 163 Malaysians from Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Jordan and Syria, after days of airspace closures and flight disruptions across the Gulf.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is a textbook case of the government reframing a crisis-response failure as a question of private-sector accountability. On March 3, Mohamad Hasan told the Dewan Negara that the situation in the Gulf was ‘under control’ and there was ’no need for an evacuation plan.’ Eight days later, his ministry was coordinating emergency repatriation flights while hundreds of Malaysians — including umrah pilgrims who had been stranded for three to five days at airports in Dubai, Doha, Abu Dhabi and Bahrain — waited for someone to get them home. The pivot from ’no evacuation needed’ to ’travel agencies must handle it’ is not reassurance — it is damage control dressed as delegation.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The Foreign Ministry exists precisely for moments like this: when Malaysian citizens are stranded abroad during a conflict and need their government to act. Telling travel agencies and airlines to sort it out is not crisis leadership — it is passing the bill. Many of these pilgrims are elderly, travelling on budget packages, and do not have the leverage to demand rescheduled flights from airlines whose operations are in chaos. The government evacuated 163 people on one chartered flight and then turned to the microphone to say, essentially, ’the rest of you are someone else’s problem.’ Meanwhile, the National Security Council coordinated the evacuation under PM Anwar’s directive — so the government clearly has the machinery. It just chose to limit its use and outsource the rest.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long history of slow or inadequate evacuation responses. During the 2023 Sudan crisis, Malaysians were stranded for days before Wisma Putra mobilised. During COVID-19, repatriation flights were chaotic, with competing agencies and unclear responsibility chains leaving citizens in limbo. The playbook is familiar: delay, then deflect. Mohamad Hasan’s ‘under control’ assurance on March 3 echoes every Malaysian foreign minister who has ever downplayed a developing crisis until it became undeniable — then blamed someone else for the gaps.

🎯 PH Callout

PH came to power promising a government that puts rakyat first — ‘Kerajaan Madani,’ a compassionate, responsive administration. When your citizens are sleeping on airport floors in the Gulf because airspace is shut and missiles are flying, telling travel agencies to figure it out is not compassion. It is bureaucratic cowardice. Evacuate your people first. Sort out who pays later. That is what a government that means its slogans would do.

Sources: NST | The Star | The Sun | FMT (March 3 statement)

item.title: Oil Past $100, Strait of Hormuz Shut, Rakyat Burning Fuel to Commute — Anwar Won't Mandate WFH, Freeze Tolls, Cap Food Prices, or Cut GLC Fat
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

The Iran-US war has pushed global oil past $100 per barrel, effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz — through which nearly 80% of Asia’s oil transits — and sent Malaysia’s FBM KLCI plunging 2.55% in a single session on March 9. RON97 is up to RM3.25, diesel to RM3.12, and Anwar himself admitted on March 7 that the government can only hold RON95 at RM1.99 for ‘one or two more months.’ Freight costs are surging, food prices are next, and experts from NST to Hong Leong IB are warning of imported inflation hitting the rakyat’s kitchen table. Anwar’s response so far: a strongly worded tweet about solidarity, an emergency parliamentary motion condemning Israel, and Zafrul saying Malaysia is ‘prepared’ — without naming a single concrete domestic measure to protect workers and families from the economic fallout.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not a theoretical crisis. Malaysians are already paying more at the pump for RON97 and diesel. Freight costs feed directly into food, medicine, and essential goods. The stock market is bleeding. And yet the Prime Minister — who is also the Finance Minister — has not announced a single emergency domestic economic measure. No crisis-mode intervention. No relief. Just speeches about geopolitics while the rakyat’s cost of living climbs in real time. Malaysia proved during COVID that the government can act decisively on domestic economic protection when it wants to. The difference is political will — and right now, Anwar’s will is directed at Parliament speeches about Zionist plots, not at shielding the people he governs.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Four things Anwar could do tomorrow with existing executive power — and won’t:

1. Mandate work-from-home for all WFH-compatible jobs. Malaysia already has a Flexible Work Arrangement framework under the Employment Act. During COVID, the government mandated WFH and it worked. With oil past $100 and commuters burning RM300-500/month on fuel and tolls, a temporary WFH directive for office-based workers would immediately cut household fuel costs, reduce demand on subsidised petrol, and ease the fiscal pressure Anwar says he can only hold for two months. Instead, millions of KL workers sit in traffic burning fuel the government can barely afford to subsidise.

2. Freeze or waive highway tolls during the crisis. The government already broke its toll abolition promise — paying RM591 million just to freeze hikes rather than remove tolls. A temporary toll waiver during a war-driven energy crisis is basic economic relief. Singapore waived ERP during COVID disruptions. Malaysia won’t even pause toll collection while fuel prices spike.

3. Impose emergency price controls on essential food items. Freight costs from Hormuz disruptions are already feeding into import prices. Cooking oil, flour, rice, and protein prices will follow. The Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011 gives the government power to gazette maximum prices for essential goods. Anwar used this power selectively before. Now, when a global war is about to hit Malaysian dinner tables, silence.

4. Freeze non-essential GLC and political appointment spending. The IDEAS report found 238 political appointments to FSBs and GLCs under Anwar. Every one of those comes with salaries, allowances, and perks funded by public money. During a war-driven fiscal crunch — with the subsidy bill ballooning and oil revenue uncertain — a responsible government would freeze all discretionary patronage spending and redirect it to rakyat relief. Instead, the appointments continue.

📜 Previous Cases

During COVID-19, the Muhyiddin government — for all its democratic illegitimacy — implemented a blanket WFH directive, moratoriums on loan repayments, cash handouts (BPN), and wage subsidies within weeks of the crisis hitting. The Ismail Sabri government continued with hybrid WFH arrangements and extended moratoriums. These were imperfect governments acting under crisis pressure. Anwar, who controls both the PM’s office and the Finance Ministry, has more concentrated fiscal power than either predecessor — and has used less of it in response to a war that is already hitting Malaysian wallets harder than the early weeks of COVID hit fuel prices. The precedent exists. The tools exist. The power exists. The will does not.

🎯 PH Callout

You told us you were different. You told us Madani meant the people come first. You told us having the PM also serve as Finance Minister would mean faster, more decisive economic management. So where is it? Oil is past $100. The Strait of Hormuz is disrupted. Your own deputy economy minister admits growth projections are shaky. Your own mouth admitted the RON95 subsidy has weeks left. And your crisis response is… a parliamentary motion about Iran and a tweet about solidarity? Mandate WFH. Freeze tolls. Cap food prices. Cut the GLC fat. You have the power. You have the precedent. You have the tools. Use them — or admit that ‘Ekonomi Madani’ was never about protecting the rakyat. It was about protecting the coalition.

Sources: Xinhua — KLCI plunges 2.55% | SAYS — Oil spike impact on Malaysia | The Star — RON95 subsidy 1-2 months | NST — Oil price surge could hit Malaysia | CNBC — Strait of Hormuz impact | Straits Times — Malaysia subsidy bill pressure | Malaysiakini — 238 political appointments | Asia Media Centre — Hormuz buffer

item.title: PH Backbenchers Call for Snap Election Dressed Up as 'Reform' — Hold GE16 With All State Polls, Save Money, They Say
item.description:
Said by: Azli Yusof (Amanah-Shah Alam) / Jimmy Puah (PKR-Tebrau)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, two Pakatan Harapan backbenchers — Shah Alam MP Azli Yusof (Amanah) and Tebrau MP Jimmy Puah (PKR) — called for all state assembly elections to be held concurrently with the 16th General Election. Speaking to FMT, both MPs argued that separate elections lead to lower voter turnout, waste public money, and produce state governments with weaker mandates. Azli said separate polls are “unfair to voters, especially those who live far away from where they vote.” Puah called it “simply too tiring for people to come out and vote twice.” Putrajaya spent RM725 million on GE15 in 2022 and a further RM420 million on six subsequent state elections in 2023.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not a neutral policy suggestion. It is PH’s latest move in a coordinated signal — following DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke’s identical proposal last year and PH Johor chairman Aminolhuda Hassan’s recent push — to align the political calendar for a snap general election. By calling for simultaneous polls, PH backbenchers are doing the government’s messaging work: normalizing the idea of an early dissolution while packaging it as cost-saving reform. The real audience is not voters who are tired of going to polling stations — it is UMNO, GPS, and other coalition partners who need to be brought on board before the PM pulls the trigger.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH did not care about “voter fatigue” when it suited them. In 2022, six PH-governed states chose to delay their state elections rather than hold them with GE15, citing monsoon concerns. Now, suddenly, the same coalition wants everything held together — but only because the timing benefits them. The opposition is fractured after the Bersatu-PAS split, the economy is holding, and Anwar’s approval is still intact. This is not reform. This is election engineering with a reformist paint job. If PH were serious about alignment, they would legislate fixed election dates — not send backbenchers to float trial balloons in FMT.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long history of strategic election timing. Mahathir called snap elections when conditions favoured him. Najib dissolved Parliament early in 2013 and 2018. Even within the current cycle, the Sabah, Melaka, and Johor snap elections of 2020–2022 were all called for political advantage, not democratic principle. PH itself benefited from the chaos of misaligned polls — the August 2023 state elections became a de facto referendum on the unity government. The pattern is always the same: whoever controls the timing controls the narrative.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised institutional reform. You promised that governance would no longer be about political convenience. Yet here you are, using backbenchers to soften the ground for a snap election while pretending it is about saving the rakyat money. If you actually believe in synchronized elections, table a constitutional amendment to fix election dates — the way mature democracies do. But you will not, because the whole point is to keep the power to dissolve in the PM’s pocket. Stop dressing up electoral strategy as reform. Malaysians can tell the difference.

Sources: FMT — Hold GE16 with all state polls to ensure true representation, say MPs | The Vibes — Simultaneous polls could boost PH but test unity | NST — PH Johor proposes simultaneous elections

item.title: Police Question C4 Anti-Corruption CEO Under Section 124B — Anwar's Government Uses 'Plot to Topple' Probe to Interrogate Civil Society
item.description:
Said by: Bukit Aman (Royal Malaysia Police) / C4 Center (Pushpan Murugiah, CEO)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 10, 2026, the Center to Combat Corruption and Cronyism (C4) CEO Pushpan Murugiah was summoned to Bukit Aman and questioned for approximately 90 minutes under Section 124B of the Penal Code — activities detrimental to parliamentary democracy. The questioning was linked to the police investigation into an alleged plot to topple the government, reportedly because Pushpan’s name appeared in an email deemed relevant to the probe. But the interrogation went far beyond that email. Police questioned C4’s sources of funding, whether the organisation had received instructions to issue statements, and — remarkably — challenged C4’s right to comment on matters of national governance. C4 confirmed that several other political commentators and academics had also been summoned to Bukit Aman under the same investigation.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not a routine police inquiry. This is the machinery of state — operating under a government that came to power on a reform mandate — being deployed to scrutinise, pressure, and delegitimise an independent anti-corruption watchdog. Section 124B is a serious charge. Using it to question a civil society leader about their organisation’s funding, autonomy, and standing to comment on public affairs is a textbook chilling effect. It signals to every NGO, academic, and political commentator in Malaysia: criticise the government’s handling of the MACC scandal, and you may find yourself in a police interview room explaining why you think you have the right to speak. This is the same playbook that Malaysians rejected when BN did it. The fact that PH is now running it should alarm every voter who believed the reformasi promise.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar Ibrahim built his entire political career on the claim that he was persecuted by a government that weaponised institutions against critics. He spent years in prison. He rallied millions with the promise that under PH, the police would not be used to silence dissent. Now his government is hauling in the CEO of an anti-corruption NGO and asking — with a straight face — whether C4 has the standing to comment on corruption. The hypocrisy is breathtaking. C4 has been non-partisan since 2014. It criticised BN, it criticised PN, and now it criticises PH — and this is the administration that sends the police. The “plot to topple the government” narrative has become a catch-all justification for intimidating anyone who asks uncomfortable questions about Azam Baki, the MACC, and the corporate mafia allegations. If your anti-corruption credentials can’t survive scrutiny from an anti-corruption NGO, the problem is not the NGO.

📜 Previous Cases

This pattern is not new in Malaysian politics — it is simply new for PH. Under Najib, civil society leaders and journalists were routinely summoned under the Sedition Act, SOSMA, and the Communications and Multimedia Act for criticising 1MDB and government corruption. Under Muhyiddin, the Emergency Ordinance was used to suspend Parliament and silence dissent. Anwar himself was the most prominent victim of institutional weaponisation — twice jailed on charges widely seen as politically motivated. Now, under his own government, Section 124B is being stretched to cover the activities of NGOs whose only offence is demanding accountability from the MACC. Bersih was told to stop criticising MACC “during Ramadan.” DAP’s own youth wing warned of a “climate of fear.” And now C4’s CEO is sitting in Bukit Aman explaining why an anti-corruption organisation dares to talk about corruption.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised reformasi. You promised institutional independence. You promised that the police would never again be used as a political weapon. Now your police are questioning the CEO of an anti-corruption NGO about their right to speak — under the same “plot to topple” investigation that you’ve used to smear Daim’s family, attack Bloomberg, and deflect from the Azam Baki scandal. Every hour that Anwar’s government spends intimidating civil society instead of reforming the MACC is another hour that proves the Madani brand is hollow. If you want Malaysians to believe in your reform agenda, start by not behaving exactly like the governments you replaced.

Sources: FMT | BFM News

item.title: Sabah Rejected PH at the Ballot Box — Now Mustapha Sakmud Says He'll Be Their Voice in Putrajaya Anyway
item.description:
Said by: Mustapha Sakmud, Sabah PH Chairman / Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Sabah PH chairman and Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Minister Mustapha Sakmud told FMT that Sabah PH will serve as a “conduit” to ensure state issues are voiced at the federal level. He said he intends to leverage his ministerial appointment — made on February 15 — to bring Sabahan concerns to Cabinet meetings and government agencies. “This is the role we aim to play, and we hope the people of Sabah will appreciate it when their fundamental needs are addressed,” he said.

🔥 Why It Matters

PH was annihilated in the November 29, 2025 Sabah state election. PKR scraped through with a single seat. DAP won nothing. GRS took 29 seats, Warisan took 25, and Sabahans made it abundantly clear they did not want PH representing them at the state level. Yet barely three months later, Anwar handed Mustapha a federal ministerial portfolio — Sabah and Sarawak Affairs — effectively using the federal Cabinet to insert PH into Sabah’s political space through the back door. This is not democratic representation. This is patronage as a substitute for a mandate.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

You cannot lose an election this badly and then claim you are the people’s voice. PH won one seat in the entire Sabah state assembly. That is not a conduit — that is a rejection letter. Mustapha’s argument boils down to: “Sabahans didn’t vote for us, but we got a Cabinet post, so we’ll represent them anyway.” This is the exact kind of unearned political entitlement PH once accused BN of practising. The appointment rewards loyalty to Anwar, not service to Sabah. If Sabahans wanted PH to speak for them, they would have voted for PH. They didn’t.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows a long Malaysian tradition of using federal appointments to compensate for electoral losses at the state level. BN did it for decades in Sabah and Sarawak — parachuting federal figures into state politics through GLCs, agencies, and development corridors. PH, in opposition, railed against this exact practice. Now in government, they’ve adopted the same playbook. Anwar’s December 2025 Cabinet reshuffle — which created the Sabah and Sarawak Affairs portfolio — was widely seen as a damage-control exercise after PH’s Sabah wipeout. The pattern is clear: when voters reject you, buy your way back in through Putrajaya.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised reformasi. You promised that power would come from the people, not from Putrajaya. You told Sabahans for years that BN was using federal patronage to override their democratic choices. And now you’ve handed a Cabinet seat to a man whose coalition won one seat in an entire state election and told him to go represent the people who rejected him. If “the people of Sabah will appreciate it,” as Mustapha claims, then put it to a test — contest a by-election in Sabah on PH’s record. Otherwise, stop pretending a ministerial appointment is a mandate.

Sources: FMT — Sabah PH will bridge state needs with national policies, says Mustapha

item.title: [BM] DAP Akhirnya Desak RCI Terhadap SPRM — Lambat Berminggu, Dan PAS Betul Panggil Ia Teater Politik
item.description:
Said by: DAP Leadership / Ahmad Fadhli Shaari, PAS Information Chief

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 10 Mac, DAP — parti terbesar dalam gabungan Pakatan Harapan dengan 40 kerusi parlimen — secara rasmi menuntut Suruhanjaya Siasatan Diraja (RCI) untuk menyiasat Suruhanjaya Pencegahan Rasuah Malaysia atas dakwaan yang pertama kali dilaporkan oleh Bloomberg, termasuk bahawa ketua SPRM Azam Baki melanggar had pegangan saham penjawat awam dan agensi itu terlibat dalam ugutan korporat. Ketua penerangan PAS Ahmad Fadhli Shaari menyifatkan tuntutan itu sebagai “teater politik,” menyatakan DAP berdiam diri apabila laporan Bloomberg yang sama mendakwa PM Anwar Ibrahim secara peribadi campur tangan dalam siasatan SPRM.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah contoh paling jelas jurang kredibiliti DAP terhadap reformasi institusi. Parti itu menghabiskan berminggu-minggu selepas pendedahan Bloomberg enggan mencabar secara langsung pengendalian Anwar terhadap Azam Baki, walaupun lapan MP PKR yang diketuai Rafizi Ramli secara terbuka menuntut penggantungan Azam pada pertengahan Februari. Tuntutan RCI DAP yang lewat ini hanya datang selepas kekalahan teruk PRN Sabah dan kemarahan pengundi yang semakin memuncak — bukan atas keyakinan tulen.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

DAP mempunyai angka untuk memaksa isu ini dari hari pertama. Empat puluh kerusi. Blok terbesar dalam kerajaan. Sebaliknya, mereka duduk diam berminggu-minggu sementara laporan Bloomberg mendedahkan potensi rasuah di peringkat tertinggi agensi antirasuah. Kini mereka mahu pujian kerana menuntut RCI? Itu bukan akauntabiliti. Itu kawalan kerosakan berpakaian prinsip. Dan bahagian paling memalukan: DAP masih belum menangani dakwaan Bloomberg bahawa PM mereka sendiri campur tangan dalam hal ehwal SPRM.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Corak ini adalah DAP tulen di bawah kerajaan perpaduan. Selepas kekalahan teruk PRN Sabah pada November 2025, DAP berjanji “mempercepatkan agenda reformasi dalam enam bulan akan datang.” Beberapa bulan kemudian, rang undang-undang had tempoh PM gagal sebahagiannya kerana MP kerajaan tidak hadir, dan tindak balas DAP adalah menyalahkan pembangkang bukannya mempertanggungjawabkan gabungan mereka sendiri.

🎯 Teguran PH

DAP, anda berjanji reformasi. Anda berjanji akauntabiliti institusi. Tetapi apabila Bloomberg mendedahkan cerita antirasuah terbesar dalam politik Malaysia sejak 1MDB, naluri pertama anda adalah melindungi PM, bukan orang awam. Tuntutan RCI yang datang berminggu lewat dan sengaja mengelak peranan PM bukan reformasi — ia teater. Jika anda mahu buktikan PAS salah, mulakan dengan menangani dakwaan Bloomberg tentang campur tangan Anwar dalam SPRM.

Sumber: FMT | The Edge Malaysia | Reuters

item.title: [BM] Dzulkefly Janji — Sekali Lagi — Nak Banteras Buli Tempat Kerja Di Hospital Kerajaan
item.description:
Said by: Dzulkefly Ahmad, Health Minister

Category: Health

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Menteri Kesihatan Dzulkefly Ahmad mengumumkan pada 10 Mac 2026 bahawa Kementerian Kesihatan akan “memperkukuh penguatkuasaan” terhadap buli tempat kerja di kemudahan kesihatan kerajaan. Beliau berkata hospital awam sepatutnya menjadi “persekitaran kerja yang profesional dan beretika” dan merujuk kepada pekeliling Jabatan Perkhidmatan Awam (JPA) yang melarang bahasa kasar dan penderaan lisan di tempat kerja. Beliau berjanji untuk “mempertingkatkan pelaksanaan” pekeliling tersebut.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan pengumuman baru. Dzulkefly telah membuat janji yang hampir sama sejak sekurang-kurangnya Ogos 2025, apabila Kementerian Kesihatan merekodkan 430 aduan buli dengan hanya empat di bawah siasatan. Beliau melancarkan ikrar “toleransi sifar,” mengumumkan garis panduan baru yang mengklasifikasikan ancaman lisan dan ejekan sebagai buli (sepatutnya pada Oktober 2025), dan juga mengancam akan memecat pegawai kanan yang menyembunyikan kes. Beberapa bulan kemudian, beliau kembali ke podium mengatakan perkara yang sama — yang memberitahu anda segala-galanya tentang sama ada janji sebelum ini membuahkan hasil.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Apabila seorang menteri mengumumkan dasar yang sama tiga kali dalam setahun, pengumuman itu adalah dasarnya — bukan penguatkuasaan. Buku panduan Dzulkefly adalah tadbir urus PH klasik: kenal pasti masalah sebenar, adakan sidang akhbar, keluarkan pekeliling, kemudian tidak buat apa-apa sehingga pusingan akhbar buruk seterusnya memaksa sidang akhbar lain. Pekeliling yang dirujuknya sudah wujud. Garis panduan sudah dilancarkan. Pendirian toleransi sifar sudah diisytiharkan. Jika mana-mana langkah itu berkesan, beliau tidak perlu berdiri pada Mac 2026 berjanji “mempertingkatkan pelaksanaan.”

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Pada Ogos 2025, menteri yang sama menjadi tajuk berita kerana mengisytiharkan toleransi sifar terhadap buli selepas kes “Dr S,” seorang pegawai perubatan siswazah di Sabah yang didakwa dibuli sehingga terkurung di bilik selama bertahun-tahun sementara pelakunya berpindah ke luar negara menjalani latihan pakar. Pada Julai 2025, kementerian mengumumkan garis panduan baru mengklasifikasikan penghinaan, ancaman, dan ejekan sebagai buli tempat kerja.

🎯 Teguran PH

Kerajaan Madani Anwar berjanji reformasi institusi dan kerajaan yang berfungsi untuk rakyat. Dzulkefly adalah menteri kanan PH dalam portfolio kritikal, dan yang terbaik beliau boleh tawarkan selepas tiga tahun berkuasa ialah mengumumkan semula pekeliling yang sama dan memanggilnya kemajuan. Doktor junior — tulang belakang sistem kesihatan awam Malaysia — layak mendapat lebih daripada kenyataan akhbar kitar semula.

Sumber: Free Malaysia Today | The Malaysian Reserve (Ogos 2025)

item.title: [BM] Penduduk Kampung Lee Kong Chian 99 Tahun Disuruh Keluar — RM50K Sekepala, Hannah Yeoh 'Tak Dapat Hadir'
item.description:
Said by: Lo Su Fui, Deputy Minister (Federal Territories) / Hannah Yeoh, Minister (Federal Territories)

Category: Housing

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Lebih 150 penduduk Kampung Lee Kong Chian di Setapak — satu-satunya kampung Cina swasta yang masih tinggal di Kuala Lumpur, ditubuhkan pada 1927 sebagai kuarters pekerja Kumpulan Getah Lee — mengadakan perhimpunan aman pada 8 Mac 2026 selepas 146 isi rumah menerima notis pengusiran pada November 2025 yang mengarahkan mereka mengosongkan premis menjelang September. Tanah telah dijual kepada syarikat swasta. Penduduk ditawarkan pampasan RM50,000 setiap isi rumah, yang mereka tolak, menuntut unit Projek Perumahan Rakyat (PPR) sebagai ganti. Menteri Wilayah Persekutuan Hannah Yeoh tidak hadir pada sambutan Chap Goh Meh terakhir kampung pada 10 Mac; Timbalan Menteri Lo Su Fui muncul, menyerahkan RM10,000 untuk jamuan makan malam, dan berkata beliau akan “membawa perkara ini kembali ke Jabatan Wilayah Persekutuan.”

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah kes klasik perpindahan bandar yang berpakaian transaksi pasaran. Kampung Lee Kong Chian bukan penempatan setinggan — ketua kampung Tan Lai Kee menegaskan rumah-rumah dibina dengan kelulusan rasmi pemilik tanah asal. Ini adalah 230 isi rumah, ramai daripada mereka keturunan pekerja yang membina kehidupan mereka di sekitar kilang Getah Lee hampir satu abad lalu. Apabila kerajaan membenarkan penjualan swasta menghapuskan komuniti lebih 1,000 orang dengan notis enam bulan dan cek RM50,000 — lebih kurang harga Myvi terpakai — ia memberitahu anda dengan tepat di mana rakyat biasa berada dalam hierarki keutamaan pembangunan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH bertanding sebagai gabungan yang akan melindungi komuniti daripada pemaju rakus dan kronisme. Anwar sendiri berulang kali bercakap tentang kemampuan perumahan, tadbir urus mengutamakan rakyat, dan maruah rakyat. Jadi apabila kampung berusia 99 tahun — dengan perumahan berdokumen dan sah yang dibina di bawah kelulusan rasmi — diserahkan notis pengusiran enam bulan dan tawaran pampasan yang tidak mampu menampung deposit flat di Setapak, di mana kerajaan reformis? Hannah Yeoh menghantar timbalan dengan cek RM10,000 untuk jamuan makan malam. Itu bukan tadbir urus — itu corak menunggu.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Ini mengikuti corak yang sama seperti pengusiran Kampung Sungai Baru pada 2025. Kampung Baru KL — enklaf Melayu terakhir di pusat bandar — telah berdepan ancaman pembangunan semula selama berdekad. Kerajaan PH juga menolak Rang Undang-Undang Pembaharuan Bandar Nga Kor Ming, yang akan memberikan kuasa menteri yang luas ke atas pemerolehan harta — rang undang-undang yang begitu kontroversi sehingga ahli parlimen belakang PH sendiri memberontak.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda berjanji Malaysia Madani — Malaysia yang bertamadun dan penyayang. Kampung berusia 99 tahun dengan 230 keluarga, keturunan pekerja yang membina bandar ini, disuruh keluar dengan RM50,000 dan jabat tangan. Menteri Wilayah Persekutuan anda tidak pun hadir. Jika penduduk ini tidak layak mendapat perumahan ganti PPR — program yang kerajaan anda sendiri mentadbir — berhentilah berpura-pura anda mentadbir untuk rakyat.

Sumber: The Star | NST | NST

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Senyap-Senyap Tarik Balik Kes Bankrap NFCorp — Pejuang Antirasuah Berdamai Dengan Orang Yang Pernah Didedahkan
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Pandan MP / Former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Rafizi Ramli, MP Pandan dan bekas Menteri Ekonomi yang membina kerjaya politik atas pendedahan skandal Perbadanan Fidlot Nasional (NFCorp), telah secara senyap menarik balik prosiding kebankrapan dan penggulungannya terhadap pengarah NFCorp Datuk Seri Dr Mohamad Salleh Ismail dan syarikat itu sendiri. Notis Pemberhentian difailkan pada 3 Mac, dan Mahkamah Tinggi membatalkan kes pada 10 Mac tanpa perintah kos. Peguam Rafizi, Razlan Hadri Zulkifli, mengesahkan perkara itu “diselesaikan secara baik.” Kes ini berpunca daripada kos yang diperintahkan mahkamah kepada Rafizi selepas Mahkamah Persekutuan membenarkan rayuannya pada April 2022 — NFCorp dan Salleh membayar RM300,000 pada Disember 2022 tetapi berhenti membayar baki faedah tertunggak.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Seluruh jenama politik Rafizi dibina atas pendedahan NFCorp. Pada 2012, beliau mendedahkan pinjaman lembut RM250 juta kerajaan yang dimaksudkan untuk projek penternakan lembu negara yang sebaliknya dibelanjakan untuk kondominium mewah dan perbelanjaan peribadi. Beliau didakwa di bawah Akta Rahsia Rasmi. Beliau dipenjarakan. Mahkamah membenarkannya sehingga ke Mahkamah Persekutuan. Dan kini, pada Mac 2026, dengan siasatan SPRMnya sendiri tergantung atas perjanjian cip ARM Holdings bernilai RM1.11 bilion, Rafizi secara senyap berdamai dengan orang yang sama yang pernah diheret ke mahkamah. Masa yang mustahil untuk diabaikan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Inilah lelaki yang memberitahu rakyat Malaysia beliau tidak akan pernah berkompromi terhadap akauntabiliti. Lelaki yang berkata rasuah mesti dikejar tanpa henti. Dan kini beliau meninggalkan wang yang mahkamah katakan miliknya — bukan kerana beliau murah hati, tetapi kerana kalkulus politik telah berubah. Rafizi sedang disiasat SPRM. Bekas pembantunya telah melarikan diri ke UK. Kedudukan politiknya dalam PKR lemah selepas digugurkan daripada Kabinet. Apabila anda tidak lagi dalam kedudukan kuat, anda tidak mencari gaduh — anda berunding. Itu bukan prinsip. Itu kelangsungan hidup.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Rafizi mempunyai corak keagresifan terpilih. Beliau tidak kenal lelah menentang skandal era BN — NFCorp, 1MDB, Tabung Haji — apabila PH dalam pembangkang. Tetapi apabila PH berkuasa, semangat itu pudar. Masalah SPRM terbarunya atas perjanjian cip ARM Holdings, di mana beliau didakwa memudahkan pelaburan RM1.11 bilion dalam syarikat dengan asas yang dipersoalkan, menunjukkan seorang lelaki yang telah menyimpang jauh daripada akar pemberi maklumatnya.

🎯 Teguran PH

PH, anda memberitahu rakyat Malaysia bahawa Reformasi bermakna akauntabiliti tanpa pengecualian. Rafizi adalah anak poster anda — pemberi maklumat berani yang dipenjarakan kerana mendedahkan rasuah. Kini beliau berunding dengan orang yang didedahkan, menggugurkan hutang yang diperintahkan mahkamah, dan membuat perjanjian baik sementara siasatan SPRMnya sendiri berjalan. Jika pejuang antirasuah terbaik anda tidak mampu menyelesaikan kes yang sudah dimenangi, apa sebenarnya pendirian PH?

Sumber: Malay Mail | Free Malaysia Today | The Star | The Edge

item.title: [BM] Ramanan Dakwa 'Ejen Berbayar' Dalang Ketegangan Kaum — Janji Tindakan Kabinet Bukan Selesaikan Punca
item.description:
Said by: R. Ramanan, Human Resources Minister / Aaron Ago Dagang, National Unity Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Menteri Sumber Manusia dan Naib Presiden PKR Datuk Seri R. Ramanan mengadakan sidang akhbar khas di Putrajaya pada 9 Mac diiringi pegawai PKR, mendakwa “ejen provokasi” dibayar untuk mencetuskan ketegangan kaum dan agama di Malaysia. Beliau berkata perkara itu akan dibangkitkan di Kabinet pada 11 Mac, dan tangkapan sudah dibuat. Menteri Perpaduan Negara Aaron Ago Dagang menyokong seruan itu pada 10 Mac. Kedua-dua menteri tidak menamakan provokator yang didakwa atau pembiaya mereka.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah eskalasi terbaru dalam corak di mana menteri PH membingkaikan rasa tidak puas hati kaum yang sah dan kritikan media sosial sebagai kerja penyabot berbayar, bukannya gejala kegagalan dasar sebenar. Hanya seminggu selepas dakwaan Anwar tentang ‘komplot Zionis untuk menstabilkan kerajaan’ di Parlimen, sidang akhbar Ramanan — lengkap dengan entourage PKR — menandakan bahawa Kerajaan Perpaduan sedang membina naratif terselaras untuk mewajarkan tindakan keras terhadap perbezaan pendapat. Bahasa kabur tentang “ejen” dan “provokator” tanpa menamakan sesiapa mewujudkan kesan menggerunkan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Hujah Ramanan pada dasarnya: ekonomi bagus, ringgit kukuh, pengangguran rendah — jadi sesiapa yang mengadu tentang isu kaum mestilah ejen berbayar. Ini adalah pengelakan klasik. Ketegangan kaum di Malaysia bukan dihasilkan oleh provokator bayangan; ia adalah produk ketidaksamaan struktur selama berdekad dan kelas politik yang tidak pernah secara serius menangani rasa tidak puas hati antara etnik. Ramanan mengadakan sidang akhbar ini diiringi pegawai PKR, menjadikannya acara parti yang menyamar sebagai kenyataan menteri. Menteri Sumber Manusia tiada tanggungjawab portfolio terhadap keharmonian kaum — itu milik Aaron Ago Dagang. Jadi mengapa Ramanan memimpin? Kerana ini operasi politik PKR, bukan tindak balas tadbir urus.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Ini berikutan ucapan Parlimen Anwar pada 3 Mac di mana beliau menyalahkan “kumpulan Zionis terkemuka” dan Bloomberg kerana cuba menstabilkan kerajaannya. Sebelum itu, PH menggunakan Akta Hasutan dan Akta Komunikasi dan Multimedia terhadap pengguna media sosial yang mengkritik kerajaan — undang-undang sama yang pernah mereka janjikan untuk dimansuhkan. Naratif ’ejen berbayar’ adalah buku panduan BN yang dikitar semula.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda bertanding atas platform melindungi kebebasan sivil dan membina ‘Malaysia Madani’ di mana warganegara boleh bersuara secara bebas. Kini menteri anda mengadakan sidang akhbar memberi amaran kepada rakyat Malaysia supaya diam atau berdepan pendakwaan, sambil enggan menamakan orang yang mereka tuduh. Jika ’ejen’ ini benar wujud, namakan mereka, dakwa mereka, dan biarkan mahkamah memutuskan. Jika anda tidak mampu menamakan mereka, berhentilah menggunakan ancaman kabur untuk menyejukkan wacana awam.

Sumber: Bernama | NST | The Star | Malay Mail

item.title: 99-Year-Old Kampung Lee Kong Chian Residents Told to Get Out — RM50K Per Family, Hannah Yeoh 'Unable to Attend'
item.description:
Said by: Lo Su Fui, Deputy Minister (Federal Territories) / Hannah Yeoh, Minister (Federal Territories)

Category: Housing

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

More than 150 residents of Kampung Lee Kong Chian in Setapak — Kuala Lumpur’s sole surviving private Chinese village, established in 1927 as quarters for Lee Rubber Group factory workers — held a peaceful rally on 8 March 2026 after 146 households received eviction notices in November 2025 ordering them to vacate by September. The land has been sold to a private company. Residents were offered RM50,000 per household in compensation, which they rejected, demanding People’s Housing Project (PPR) units instead. Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh was absent from the village’s final Chap Goh Meh celebration on 10 March; Deputy Minister Lo Su Fui showed up, handed over RM10,000 for the dinner, and said he would “bring this matter back to the Federal Territories Department to explore how we can best provide assistance.” The government’s official position, per NST, is that it will “review the situation before deciding whether to intervene.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This is a textbook case of urban displacement dressed up as market transaction. Kampung Lee Kong Chian is not a squatter settlement — village chief Tan Lai Kee stressed the houses were built with formal approval from the original landowner. These are 230 households, many of them descendants of workers who built their lives around the Lee Rubber factory nearly a century ago. When the government allows a private sale to erase a community of over 1,000 people with six months’ notice and a RM50,000 cheque — roughly the price of a used Myvi — it tells you exactly where ordinary Malaysians sit in the hierarchy of development priorities. The Federal Territories Ministry, under PH’s Hannah Yeoh, is the body with direct jurisdiction over KL housing and land disputes. Yet the minister was absent, and the government’s posture is to “review” and “explore” — the bureaucratic language of stalling while the clock runs out on residents.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH campaigned as the coalition that would protect communities from rapacious developers and crony capitalism. Anwar himself has spoken repeatedly about housing affordability, people-first governance, and the dignity of the rakyat. So when a 99-year-old village — with documented, legitimate housing built under formal approval — is handed a six-month eviction notice and a compensation offer that wouldn’t cover a deposit on a flat in Setapak, where is the reformist government? Hannah Yeoh sent a deputy with a RM10,000 dinner cheque. The deputy said he’d “bring this back” to the department. That’s not governance — that’s a holding pattern. If PH cannot protect a century-old KL community with clear historical legitimacy from being bulldozed by a private developer, then every “people first” slogan they’ve ever uttered is decoration.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows the same pattern as the Kampung Sungai Baru evictions in 2025, where residents were forced out amid redevelopment pressure with inadequate compensation and minimal government intervention. KL’s Kampung Baru — the last Malay enclave in the city centre — has faced decades of similar redevelopment threats, with successive governments oscillating between grand masterplans and inaction. The PH government has also pushed Nga Kor Ming’s Urban Renewal Bill, which would grant sweeping ministerial powers over property acquisition — a bill so controversial that even PH’s own backbenchers revolted. The pattern is consistent: communities with deep roots but no political leverage get treated as obstacles to development, while the government oscillates between performative sympathy and institutional passivity.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised Malaysia Madani — a civilised, compassionate Malaysia. A 99-year-old village with 230 families, descendants of workers who built this city, is being told to leave with RM50,000 and a handshake. Your Federal Territories Minister didn’t even show up to the last Chap Goh Meh these families will ever celebrate in their homes. You sent a deputy with dinner money. If this is what “reviewing the situation” looks like, then your review is a eulogy. These residents are not asking for handouts — they’re asking for replacement housing under PPR, a programme your own government administers. Either use the power you have to intervene, or stop pretending you govern for the people.

Sources: The Star — Setapak Village Marks ‘Farewell’ Chap Goh Meh | NST — Over 150 Residents Rally Against Gombak Eviction Plan | NST — Govt to Review Gombak Eviction Dispute | BFM News (Threads)

item.title: DAP Finally Demands RCI on MACC — Weeks Late, and PAS Correctly Calls It Political Theatre
item.description:
Said by: DAP Leadership / Ahmad Fadhli Shaari, PAS Information Chief

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

On March 10, DAP — the largest party in the Pakatan Harapan coalition with 40 parliamentary seats — formally called for a Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) to investigate the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission over allegations first reported by Bloomberg, including that MACC chief Azam Baki breached civil servant shareholding limits and that the agency was involved in corporate intimidation. PAS information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari responded by calling the demand “political theatre,” noting that DAP had remained silent when the same Bloomberg reports alleged that Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim personally interfered in MACC investigations.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is the clearest example yet of DAP’s credibility gap on institutional reform. The party spent weeks after the Bloomberg exposé refusing to directly challenge Anwar’s handling of Azam Baki, even as eight PKR MPs led by Rafizi Ramli publicly demanded Azam’s suspension in mid-February. DAP’s belated RCI call comes only after its catastrophic Sabah election loss and growing voter fury — not from genuine conviction. When even PAS can accurately identify your reform posture as performative, something has gone deeply wrong with your party’s moral compass.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

DAP had the numbers to force the issue from day one. Forty seats. The largest bloc in government. Instead, they sat on their hands for weeks while Bloomberg’s reporting exposed potential corruption at the highest levels of the anti-graft agency — and Anwar’s alleged interference in it. Now they want credit for demanding an RCI? That’s not accountability. That’s damage control dressed up as principle. And the most damning part: DAP still hasn’t addressed the Bloomberg allegation that their own Prime Minister interfered in MACC affairs. They’re demanding an investigation into everyone except the man they answer to.

📜 Previous Cases

This pattern is pure DAP under the unity government. After the Sabah election wipeout in November 2025, DAP pledged to “accelerate the reform agenda over the next six months.” Months later, the PM tenure limit bill failed partly because government MPs were absent, and DAP’s response was to blame the opposition rather than hold their own coalition accountable. On the Azam Baki shareholding scandal — which first surfaced in 2021 when DAP was in opposition and demanded action — DAP is now the party in government protecting the same man they once wanted removed. The pattern is clear: loud in opposition, silent in power.

🎯 PH Callout

DAP, you promised reformasi. You promised institutional accountability. You promised you were different. But when Bloomberg dropped the biggest anti-corruption story in Malaysian politics since 1MDB, your first instinct was to protect the Prime Minister, not the public. An RCI demand that comes weeks late and deliberately avoids the PM’s role isn’t reform — it’s theatre. If you want to prove PAS wrong, start by addressing the Bloomberg allegations about Anwar’s interference in MACC. Until then, Ahmad Fadhli is right: you are not qualified to champion this.

Sources: FMT | The Edge Malaysia | Reuters | US News / Reuters

item.title: Dzulkefly Promises — Again — to Crack Down on Workplace Bullying in Government Hospitals
item.description:
Said by: Dzulkefly Ahmad, Health Minister

Category: Health

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad announced on March 10, 2026 that the Health Ministry will “bolster enforcement” against workplace bullying in government health facilities. He said public hospitals should be “professional and ethical workplace environments” and pointed to a Public Services Department (PSD) circular that bans coarse language and verbal abuse in the workplace. He pledged to “enhance implementation” of the circular to ensure “no ambiguity” in handling misconduct — language that strongly implies the existing rules are not being enforced.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not a new announcement. Dzulkefly has been making near-identical promises since at least August 2025, when the Health Ministry recorded 430 bullying complaints with only four under investigation. He launched “zero tolerance” pledges, announced new guidelines classifying verbal threats and mockery as bullying (due in October 2025), and even threatened to fire senior officials who conceal cases. Months later, he is back at the podium saying the same things — which tells you everything about whether the previous rounds of promises produced results. Housemen and junior doctors continue to report systemic harassment, and the ministry’s response remains performative announcements rather than structural accountability.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

When a minister announces the same policy three times in under a year, the announcement is the policy — not the enforcement. Dzulkefly’s playbook is textbook PH governance: identify a real problem, hold a press conference, issue a circular, then do nothing until the next round of bad press forces another press conference. The circular he cited already exists. The guidelines were already launched. The zero-tolerance stance was already declared. If any of those measures had worked, he would not be standing up in March 2026 promising to “enhance implementation.” The problem is not ambiguity in the rules — it is impunity for senior medical staff who bully juniors, and a ministry that treats announcements as action.

📜 Previous Cases

In August 2025, the same minister made headlines for declaring zero tolerance on bullying after the case of “Dr S,” a houseman in Sabah allegedly bullied so severely she was confined to her room for years while the perpetrator moved abroad to pursue specialist training. In July 2025, the ministry announced new guidelines classifying insults, threats, and mockery as workplace bullying — guidelines supposedly launching in October 2025. Pressure group Hartal Doktor Kontrak has repeatedly called for systemic reform, not just guidelines, pointing to research showing high prevalence of bullying among Malaysian junior doctors across multiple government hospitals.

🎯 PH Callout

Anwar’s Madani government promised institutional reform and a government that works for the people. Dzulkefly is a senior PH minister in a critical portfolio, and the best he can offer after three years in power is re-announcing the same circular and calling it progress. Junior doctors — the backbone of Malaysia’s public health system — deserve more than recycled press releases. Either enforce the rules you already have, fire the people responsible, and publish the outcomes — or stop pretending the announcements mean anything.

Sources: Free Malaysia Today | The Malaysian Reserve (Aug 2025) | The Sun

item.title: Rafizi Quietly Drops NFCorp Bankruptcy Bid — The Anti-Corruption Crusader Makes Nice With the People He Once Exposed
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Pandan MP / Former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

Rafizi Ramli, the Pandan MP and former Economy Minister who built his political career on exposing the National Feedlot Corporation (NFCorp) scandal, has quietly dropped his bankruptcy and winding-up proceedings against NFCorp director Datuk Seri Dr Mohamad Salleh Ismail and the company itself. A Notice of Discontinuance was filed on March 3, and the High Court struck out the case on March 10 without an order as to costs. Rafizi’s lawyer Razlan Hadri Zulkifli confirmed the matter was “amicably resolved.” The case stemmed from court-ordered costs owed to Rafizi after the Federal Court upheld his appeal in April 2022 — NFCorp and Salleh had paid RM300,000 in December 2022 but stopped paying the outstanding interest balance.

🔥 Why It Matters

Rafizi’s entire political brand was built on the NFCorp exposé. In 2012, he blew the lid off a RM250 million government soft loan meant for a national cattle-rearing project that instead went toward luxury condominiums and personal expenses — a scandal that brought down UMNO Wanita chief Shahrizat Abdul Jalil and became a symbol of BN-era corruption. He was charged under the Official Secrets Act for it. He went to jail for it. The courts vindicated him all the way to the Federal Court. And now, in March 2026, with his own MACC investigation hanging over his head over the RM1.11 billion ARM Holdings chip deal, Rafizi quietly makes nice with the very people he once dragged through the courts. The timing is impossible to ignore.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

This is the man who told Malaysians he would never compromise on accountability. The man who said corruption must be pursued relentlessly, regardless of political cost. And now he’s walking away from money the courts said he was owed — not because he’s being magnanimous, but because the political calculus has changed. Rafizi is under MACC investigation. His former aide has fled to the UK. His political position within PKR is weakened after being dropped from Cabinet. When you’re no longer in a position of strength, you don’t pick fights — you settle. That’s not principle. That’s survival.

📜 Previous Cases

Rafizi has a pattern of selective aggression. He was relentless against BN-era scandals — NFCorp, 1MDB, Tabung Haji — when PH was in opposition. But once PH came to power, the fire dimmed. As Economy Minister, he championed the Central Database Hub (PADU) that drew criticism for data privacy concerns, and oversaw investment policies that favoured headline numbers over structural reform. His recent MACC trouble over the ARM Holdings chip deal, where he allegedly facilitated a RM1.11 billion investment in a company with questionable fundamentals, shows a man who has drifted far from his whistleblower roots. The NFCorp settlement is just the latest chapter in a long arc from crusader to career politician.

🎯 PH Callout

PH, you told Malaysians that Reformasi meant accountability without exception. Rafizi was your poster boy — the brave whistleblower who went to prison for exposing corruption. Now he’s settling with the people he exposed, dropping court-ordered debts, and making amicable deals while his own MACC probe gathers steam. If your best anti-corruption champion can’t even follow through on a case he already won, what exactly does PH stand for anymore? You don’t get to claim the moral high ground when your own people are cutting quiet deals in courtrooms.

Sources: Malay Mail | Free Malaysia Today | The Star | The Edge

item.title: Ramanan Claims 'Paid Agents' Are Behind Racial Tensions — Promises Cabinet Crackdown Instead of Addressing Root Causes
item.description:
Said by: R. Ramanan, Human Resources Minister / Aaron Ago Dagang, National Unity Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

Human Resources Minister and PKR Vice-President Datuk Seri R. Ramanan held a special press conference in Putrajaya on March 9 flanked by PKR officials, claiming that “agents of provocation” are being paid to incite racial and religious tensions in Malaysia. He said the matter would be raised at Cabinet on March 11, and that arrests have already been made. National Unity Minister Aaron Ago Dagang echoed the call on March 10, urging police to “take stern action” against anyone disrupting harmony. Neither minister named the alleged provocateurs or their financiers — Ramanan specifically said he would not name them to avoid “making them famous.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This is the latest escalation in a pattern where PH ministers frame legitimate racial grievances and social media criticism as the work of paid saboteurs, rather than symptoms of real policy failures. Coming barely a week after Anwar’s “Zionist plot to destabilise the government” claims in Parliament, Ramanan’s press conference — complete with a PKR entourage — signals that the Unity Government is building a coordinated narrative to justify crackdowns on dissent. The vague language about “agents” and “provocateurs” without naming anyone creates a chilling effect: anyone who raises racial issues can now be framed as a paid agent. For a coalition that came to power promising civil liberties reform, this is a deeply authoritarian move.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Ramanan’s argument boils down to: the economy is doing well, the ringgit is strong, unemployment is low — so anyone complaining about racial issues must be a paid agent. This is textbook deflection. Racial tensions in Malaysia are not manufactured by shadowy provocateurs; they are the product of decades of structural inequality, uneven affirmative action, and a political class that has never seriously addressed inter-ethnic grievances. PH promised institutional reform. Instead, it is adopting the exact same “external enemies” narrative that BN and PN used for decades — blame outsiders, arrest critics, and call it national unity. Ramanan held this press conference flanked by PKR officials, making it a party event masquerading as a ministerial statement. The Human Resources Minister has no portfolio responsibility for racial harmony — that belongs to Aaron Ago Dagang. So why is Ramanan leading the charge? Because this is a PKR political operation, not a governance response.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows Anwar’s March 3 Parliament speech where he blamed a “prominent Zionist group” and Bloomberg for trying to destabilise his government — a claim Daim’s family has since sued him over, calling it an “outright lie.” Before that, PH used the Sedition Act and Communications and Multimedia Act against social media users who criticised the government — the same laws they once promised to repeal. The “paid agents” narrative is a recycled BN playbook: Najib’s government routinely blamed racial tensions on “DAP provocateurs” and “foreign-funded NGOs.” PH has now adopted the same framework, just with different villains. Every Malaysian government, from Mahathir 1.0 to Muhyiddin to Anwar, has discovered that blaming racial tensions on shadowy outsiders is easier than fixing the system.

🎯 PH Callout

You ran on a platform of protecting civil liberties, repealing oppressive laws, and building a “Malaysia Madani” where citizens could speak freely. Now your ministers are holding press conferences warning Malaysians to shut up or face prosecution, while refusing to name the people they accuse. If these “agents” are real, name them, charge them, and let the courts decide — you said it yourself, Ramanan. If you cannot name them, then stop using vague threats to chill public discourse. You are not protecting national unity. You are protecting yourselves from accountability.

Sources: Bernama | NST | The Star | The Vibes | Malay Mail | Bernama — Aaron Ago Dagang

item.title: [BM] Anwar Kutuk Serangan Iran Ke Atas Kuwait — Berminggu Selepas Usul Parlimen Mempertahankan Iran Terhadap Serangan AS-Israel
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 9 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim menelefon Perdana Menteri Kuwait, Sheikh Ahmad Abdullah Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah, untuk mengutuk serangan Iran yang menyasarkan Kuwait. Beliau menyatakan takziah atas korban dan mengesahkan sokongan Malaysia terhadap keselamatan dan integriti wilayah Kuwait. Panggilan telefon ini datang tepat seminggu selepas Anwar membentangkan usul tergempar di Parlimen pada 2 Mac mengutuk serangan AS-Israel ke atas Iran, yang mana beliau mengisytiharkan Malaysia ’tidak lagi mampu menelan’ kuliah hak asasi Barat dan ahli parlimen belakang PH Syahredzan Johan menyeru AP memboikot semua acara kedutaan AS.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Kejutan mendadak ini adalah intinya. Tujuh hari lalu, Anwar meletakkan Malaysia sebagai pembela Iran terhadap pencerobohan Barat, membingkai konflik dalam istilah anti-imperialis dan menggunakannya untuk meraih mata politik domestik dengan pangkalan sokongannya. Kini Iran adalah pencerob yang menyerang jiran Teluk, dan Anwar perlu mengutuk negara yang sama yang beliau pertahankan. Ini bukan dasar luar negeri berprinsip — ia penempatan reaksioner dan oportunis yang berubah mengikut kitaran berita.

💩 Kenapa Ia Karut

Anda tidak boleh membentangkan usul tergempar mempertahankan Iran pada hari Isnin dan mengutuk Iran pada Isnin berikutnya dan berpura-pura kedua-dua pendirian adalah sebahagian daripada rangka kerja dasar luar negeri yang koheren. Sama ada Malaysia mempunyai pendirian berprinsip tentang kedaulatan dan penggunaan kekerasan — yang mana ia sepatutnya lebih berhati-hati pada 2 Mac dan bukannya menjadikan Parlimen perhimpunan politik — atau ia mereka-reka sambil berjalan. Seruan boikot Syahredzan kelihatan sangat tidak masuk akal sekarang: patutkah AP turut memboikot acara kedutaan Iran?

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Dasar luar Malaysia di bawah Anwar ditandai penempatan reaksioner yang didorong tajuk berita. Kerajaan berayun antara menarik pelaburan Barat dan mengecam kemunafikan Barat, antara melibatkan China dalam perdagangan dan menolak isu Laut China Selatan, bergantung kepada penonton domestik. Mahathir melakukan perkara yang sama — ucapan terkenal ‘Yahudi memerintah dunia’ di OIC pada 2003 diikuti penglibatan senyap berterusan dengan institusi kewangan Barat.

🎯 Panggilan PH

PH, jika anda mahu Malaysia dipandang serius di pentas dunia, pilih satu laluan. Anda menjadikan Parlimen perhimpunan solidariti untuk Iran tujuh hari lalu. Ahli parlimen belakang anda sendiri menyeru boikot acara kedutaan AS. Kini anda menelefon Kuwait untuk mengutuk negara yang sama yang anda pertahankan. Apakah pendirian sebenar Malaysia? Rakyat layak mendapat dasar luar negeri, bukan papan mood.

Sumber: QNA | Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Anwar Lancar Pelan Induk Pasaran Modal, Kata 'Reformasi Tidak Boleh Dirunding' — Sementara Setiap Reformasi Sebenar Ditangguh Atau Dibunuh
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

PM Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim menyampaikan ucaptama di pelancaran Pelan Induk Pasaran Modal 2026–2030 (CMP4) Suruhanjaya Sekuriti pada 9 Mac 2026. Beliau mengisytiharkan bahawa “kerajaan telah menjadikan reformasi tidak boleh dirunding” dan pasaran modal Malaysia “mesti memacu kemakmuran bersama, bukan sekadar keuntungan.” CMP4 menyasarkan pertumbuhan pasaran modal Malaysia ke RM6.3 trilion menjelang 2030.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Frasa “reformasi tidak boleh dirunding” akan berkuasa jika ia datang daripada kerajaan yang sebenarnya menyampaikan reformasi. Sebaliknya, ia datang daripada PM yang sama yang kerajaannya, dalam dua minggu lepas sahaja:

  • Gagal meluluskan rang undang-undang had tempoh PM kerana 8 MPnya sendiri tidak hadir (3 Mac)
  • Menangguhkan rang undang-undang pemisahan AG-PP untuk semakan jawatankuasa 3 bulan (4 Mac)
  • Enggan menggantung ketua SPRM Azam Baki walaupun dakwaan semakin memuncak (9 Mac)
  • Menangguhkan reformasi RON95 berulang kali — Anwar sendiri mengakui beliau hanya mampu mengekalkan harga “satu atau dua bulan lagi” (7 Mac)

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Anwar menggunakan “reformasi” seperti jenama mewah menggunakan “eksklusif” — ia perkataan pemasaran yang terpisah daripada produk. Pelan Induk Pasaran Modal adalah dokumen Suruhanjaya Sekuriti. Ia peta jalan teknokratik untuk pertumbuhan pasaran. Tetapi Anwar tidak boleh berdiri di podium, mengisytiharkan “reformasi tidak boleh dirunding,” dan menerima pujian untuk reformasi apabila:

  • Peguam Negara masih merangkap Pendakwa Raya — konflik kepentingan yang PH berjanji perbaiki sejak 2018.
  • Ketua SPRM kekal di pejabat walaupun dakwaan.
  • Reformasi institusi yang memerlukan keberanian politik ditangguhkan secara sistematik. Pertumbuhan pasaran modal bukan reformasi. Menaikkan penilaian PLC bukan reformasi. Reformasi adalah apabila anda mengubah peraturan yang melindungi golongan berkuasa. Anwar tidak melakukan itu.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

  • 2018–2020 (PH 1.0): Kerajaan PH pertama juga berjanji pemisahan AG-PP, IPCMC, dan reformasi institusi. Tiada yang selesai sebelum Langkah Sheraton.
  • 4 Mac 2026: Fahmi umumkan rang undang-undang pemisahan AG-PP ditangguhkan untuk semakan jawatankuasa parlimen 3 bulan.
  • 3 Mac 2026: Pindaan perlembagaan had tempoh PM gagal kerana PH tidak mampu menghimpunkan kehadiran penuh.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda kata reformasi “tidak boleh dirunding.” Mengapa setiap reformasi akhirnya dirunding dan ditangguhkan? Pemisahan AG-PP — ditangguhkan. Had tempoh PM — gagal atas kehadiran anda sendiri. Azam Baki — masih di pejabat. RON95 — masih bersubsidi tanpa jadual. Anda ada majoriti parlimen. Anda ada mandat institusi. Yang anda tidak ada ialah kehendak politik untuk membelanjakan modal gabungan anda pada sesuatu yang tidak menguntungkan gabungan itu sendiri. Berhenti memberitahu penonton Suruhanjaya Sekuriti bahawa reformasi tidak boleh dirunding. Mulakan memberitahu Kabinet anda sendiri.

Sumber: The Star | NST | The Edge | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Anwar Percepatkan Bantuan Tunai STR RM15 Bilion — Perbelanjaan Rekod, Masa Hari Raya, Bau Pilihan Raya
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 9 Mac 2026, PM Anwar Ibrahim mengumumkan Fasa 2 program pemindahan tunai Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) akan dipercepatkan ke 10 Mac — berminggu-minggu lebih awal daripada jadual asal. Pembayaran awal ini memberi manfaat kepada 5.2 juta penerima. Anwar membingkaikan langkah itu sebagai membantu rakyat Malaysia bersedia untuk Hari Raya Aidilfitri. Jumlah peruntukan 2026 untuk STR dan SARA berjumlah RM15 bilion — tertinggi dalam sejarah program bantuan tunai langsung Kerajaan Persekutuan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Tiga perkara menjadikan ini penting:

  1. Perbelanjaan rekod bagi pemindahan tunai. RM15 bilion bukan jumlah kecil — dan ia datang daripada PM yang berkempen atas disiplin fiskal dan menamatkan budaya bantuan.
  2. Masa yang mencurigakan. Reuters melaporkan kerajaan mungkin memanggil PRU kilat seawal Julai 2026. Mempercepatkan bayaran tunai tepat sebelum Hari Raya adalah politik tunai pra-pilihan raya klasik.
  3. Reformasi struktur tiada di mana-mana. Pemindahan tunai bukan dasar ekonomi. Ia penawar kesakitan. Rasionalisasi subsidi RON95 telah ditangguhkan berulang kali.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Anwar menghabiskan bertahun-tahun — berdekad — menyerang program bantuan tunai BR1M BN sebagai pembelian undi, rasuah populis, dan ketidakbertanggungjawaban fiskal. Beliau memanggilnya “tongkat” yang membuatkan rakyat bergantung kepada kemurahan kerajaan. Kini beliau menjalankan buku panduan yang sama, kecuali beliau membelanjakan lebih banyak wang dan mempercepatkan bayaran untuk mendarat tepat sebelum Hari Raya. STR adalah BR1M dengan penjenamaan semula.

“Peruntukan tertinggi dalam sejarah” bukan satu kebanggaan — ia pengakuan. Jika ekonomi berprestasi seperti dakwaan kerajaan, mengapa kerajaan perlu membelanjakan RM15 bilion rekod untuk bantuan tunai? Sama ada pertumbuhan tidak sampai kepada rakyat biasa, atau ini tentang pilihan raya, bukan ekonomi.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

  • BR1M di bawah Najib (2012-2018): Anwar dan PH tanpa henti menyerang BR1M sebagai pembelian undi yang tidak mampan. Kini mereka menjalankan versi lebih besar program yang sama.
  • Bantuan Raya 2024: Anwar mempercepatkan bayaran STR sebelum Hari Raya 2024 juga. Ini kini corak tahunan.
  • Kelewatan subsidi RON95 (2024-2026): Kerajaan berulang kali menangguhkan reformasi subsidi minyak bersasar.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda berjanji “reformasi, bukan derma.” Anda memberitahu rakyat Malaysia PH akan membina ekonomi di mana orang tidak perlu bantuan. Anda mengejek Najib kerana BR1M. Kini anda membelanjakan RM15 bilion untuk plaster yang sama — dan anda bangga dengannya. Ini bukan keprihatinan. Ini perbelanjaan kempen yang difailkan di bawah baris bajet yang berbeza.

Sumber: Bernama | Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | Reuters (isyarat PRU kilat)

item.title: [BM] Anwar Taknak Gantung Azam Baki — Biar Saja Kontrak Tamat Mei Sementara Negara Terbakar
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Straits Times sources

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

The Straits Times melaporkan pada 9 Mac 2026 — memetik sumber kerajaan dan undang-undang peringkat tinggi — bahawa PM Anwar Ibrahim telah memutuskan tidak memperbaharui tempoh ketua pesuruhjaya SPRM Tan Sri Azam Baki apabila ia tamat pada 12 Mei 2026. Tetapi Anwar tidak menggantung Azam sekarang. Beliau tidak mengarahkan siasatan awam bebas. Beliau hanya akan membiarkan masa habis — dua bulan lagi ketua antirasuah yang dituduh mengetuai “mafia korporat.” Ini selepas Bloomberg melaporkan SPRM di bawah Azam menyalahguna kuasa untuk memeras ugut dan memanipulasi tokoh korporat; Azam didapati melanggar peraturan pegangan saham penjawat awam berkali-kali; Rafizi Ramli dan 8 MP PKR menuntut penggantungan segera Azam; Bersih menuntut peletakan jawatan; dan perhimpunan 15 Feb di KL menuntut tangkapan Azam.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan kemenangan. Ini kawalan kerosakan berpakaian keputusan. Anwar membiarkan Azam Baki berkhidmat dua bulan lagi sementara SPRM kekal di bawah awan dakwaan bahawa ia beroperasi sebagai sindiket peras ugut. Setiap siasatan yang SPRM jalankan antara sekarang dan 12 Mei tercemar. Setiap serbuan, setiap saman, setiap pendakwaan membawa bau agensi yang kredibilitinya telah runtuh.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

1. Anwar memperbaharui Azam tiga kali. Setiap tahun sejak 2023, Anwar secara peribadi melanjutkan tempoh seorang lelaki yang gabungannya sendiri pernah tuntut digantung pada 2021-2022. 2. PH berkempen atas penapisan parlimen ketua SPRM. Ia kini 2026. Itu tidak pernah berlaku. 3. “Tidak memperbaharui” bukan akauntabiliti. Penggantungan mencetuskan siasatan. Tidak memperbaharui hanya bermakna Azam bersara pada Mei dengan pencen dan maruah utuh. 4. Anwar menyifatkan dakwaan SPRM sebagai konspirasi Zionis. Bukannya menghadapi kerosakan institusi, Anwar memberitahu Parlimen pada 3 Mac bahawa kritikan terhadap SPRM adalah sebahagian daripada “komplot Zionis.” 5. Dua bulan lagi agensi terjejas. SPRM kini menyiasat keluarga Daim atas dakwaan “komplot guling kerajaan” dan menyiasat pengkritik Azam Baki di bawah undang-undang berkaitan hasutan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

2021 — Skandal pegangan saham pertama: Azam Baki didapati memiliki lebih sejuta saham. 2022 — Janji pilihan raya PH: Manifesto PRU15 PH memasukkan komitmen penapisan parlimen pelantikan ketua SPRM. 2023-2025 — Tiga pembaharuan: Anwar melanjutkan tempoh Azam setiap tahun. Februari 2026 — Pendedahan Bloomberg. November 2025 — Kekalahan teruk Sabah.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda berjanji pengawasan parlimen terhadap ketua SPRM. Anda tidak pernah menunaikannya. Anda memperbaharui Azam Baki tiga kali. Kini langkah besar anda adalah tidak memperbaharui kontraknya — minimum yang mungkin — sambil membiarkannya berkhidmat dua bulan lagi. Ini bukan reformasi. Ini menunggu masa habis dan memanggilnya kemajuan. Gantung Azam Baki sekarang. Tubuhkan suruhanjaya siasatan diraja. Berhenti bersembunyi di sebalik pasukan petugas dan teori konspirasi.

Sumber: The Straits Times | The Edge Malaysia | SCMP | CNA

item.title: [BM] Anwar Ulang 'Bantuan Berdasar Keperluan, Bukan Kaum' Di Dewan Negara — Skrip Sama, Sesi Lain, Sifar Perubahan Struktur
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Perdana Menteri Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu Dewan Negara pada 9 Mac 2026 bahawa bantuan kerajaan diperuntukkan berdasarkan keperluan, bukan kaum. Beliau menegaskan kerajaan Madani akan “kekal adil kepada semua negeri” dan bantuan menyasarkan mereka yang paling memerlukan tanpa mengira latar belakang. Ini bukan kali pertama Anwar menggunakan ayat yang sama. Pada Ogos 2025, beliau memberitahu Dewan Negara yang sama perkara yang sama — hampir perkataan demi perkataan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah salah satu slogan PH yang paling kerap diulang. Ia menjadi teras tawaran Ekonomi Madani, dan ia bergema dengan pengundi yang bosan dengan sistem pengagihan berasaskan kaum. Tetapi slogan bukan dasar. Soalannya mudah: adakah kerajaan sebenarnya menyusun semula mana-mana program peruntukan khusus Bumiputera utama sejak berkuasa? Adakah mana-mana kuota berasaskan kaum diperbaharui? Jawapannya, berdasarkan semua bukti yang ada, adalah tidak. Seni bina pengagihan berasaskan kaum tidak disentuh.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Berkata “bantuan berdasarkan keperluan, bukan kaum” sambil mengekalkan setiap struktur peruntukan berasaskan kaum bukan reformasi. Ia adalah penjenamaan. Anwar tidak berbohong — secara teknikal, beberapa program bantuan seperti SARA menyasarkan isi rumah berpendapatan rendah tanpa mengira kaum. Tetapi item besar — sasaran ekuiti Bumiputera, peruntukan MARA, perolehan khas Bumiputera, pengambilan pekerja keutamaan GLC — kekal tidak disentuh. Apa yang Anwar lakukan adalah pengelakan klasik: tunjuk program kebajikan kecil yang berasaskan keperluan, ulang slogan, dan harap tiada siapa bertanya tentang seni bina struktur.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

  • Ogos 2025: Anwar memberitahu Dewan Negara “peruntukan berdasarkan keperluan, bukan kaum.” Dewan sama, skrip sama.
  • Julai 2025: RMK-13 dilancarkan dengan pembingkaian peralihan daripada dasar berasaskan kaum kepada berasaskan keperluan.
  • Timbalan Menteri Ekonomi Hanifah (Disember 2025): Memberitahu Parlimen kerajaan “mengkaji” penggunaan pendapatan boleh guna sebagai penanda aras lebih adil — bermakna alat pengukuran asas pun belum diperbaharui.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda bertanding atas “Reformasi.” Anda berjanji Malaysia di mana kaum tidak menentukan akses kepada peluang. Tiga tahun kemudian, setiap program struktur berasaskan kaum kekal utuh. Tidak satu pun diperbaharui. Dan anda berdiri di Dewan Negara — buat kali kedua dalam setahun — mengatakan ayat yang sama. Jika itu benar, tunjukkan kepada kami kertas dasar. Tunjukkan program yang disusun semula. Sehingga itu, ini bukan dasar. Ini kenyataan akhbar.

Sumber: Bernama | The Star (Ogos 2025) | NST (Ogos 2025)

item.title: [BM] Anwar Umum Bantuan Tunai RM500 Untuk Penjawat Awam Dan Kakitangan Masjid — Musim Pilihan Raya Hari Raya Bermula
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 9 Mac 2026, Anwar Ibrahim mengumumkan Bantuan Kewangan Khas (BKK) 2026: RM500 untuk penjawat awam Gred 15 ke bawah, RM250 untuk pesara dan veteran, dan RM500 untuk kira-kira 70,000 kakitangan masjid dan agama — imam, bilal, siak, marbut, guru KAFA dan takmir. Bayaran masuk pada 13 Mac, beberapa hari sebelum Hari Raya Aidilfitri, apabila perbelanjaan isi rumah memuncak dan muhibah politik paling ketara. Anwar memegang kedua-dua portfolio PM dan Menteri Kewangan, memberikannya kuasa sepihak ke atas masa dan sasaran pengeluaran sedemikian.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Ini perbelanjaan kitaran pilihan raya yang jelas: disasarkan, populis, dan diwaktukan untuk impak emosi maksimum semasa cuti agama. Menggabungkan penjawat awam dan kakitangan masjid dalam pengumuman yang sama mengukuhkan dua bank undi kritikal serentak — birokrasi dan penubuhan agama. RM500 untuk kakitangan masjid adalah percubaan telus untuk memotong monopoli PAS terhadap undi agama, dibiayai bukan oleh reformasi institusi tetapi oleh pindahan tunai terus daripada wang awam.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Bantuan tunai sebelum musim perayaan adalah buku panduan BN yang PH kritik selama berdekad. BR1M Najib dipersendakan sebagai “beli undi” oleh PH ketika pembangkang. BKK Anwar adalah perkara yang sama dengan nama berbeza. Kemasukan kakitangan masjid amat sinis: peranan ini secara tradisinya dikaitkan dengan kawasan luar bandar dan separa bandar yang sejajar PAS, menjadikan ini bidaan terus untuk bersaing dengan PAS atas dasar agama menggunakan wang awam. Jika anda mahu menambah baik kehidupan pekerja masjid, anda akan memperbaharui syarat pekerjaan dan skala gaji mereka secara kekal — bukan melemparkan bonus pra-Raya apabila anda perlukan undi mereka.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: BR1M Najib (kemudian BSH) adalah program bantuan tunai perayaan asal, dikritik meluas oleh PH sebagai pembelian undi populis. BPN Muhyiddin semasa COVID turut diwaktukan untuk impak politik. Setiap PM Malaysia menemui kuasa pilihan raya pindahan tunai sebelum cuti besar. Anwar berjanji akan berbeza. Dia tidak.

🎯 PANGGILAN PH: Anda mempersendakan BR1M bertahun-tahun. Memanggilnya rasuah. Memanggilnya tidak mapan. Kini anda melakukan perkara yang sama, diwaktukan pada kalendar yang sama, menyasarkan bank undi yang sama — dengan bonus tambahan membeli undi masjid dengan wang pembayar cukai. Jika inilah rupa ‘Malaysia Madani’, ia sekadar 1Malaysia dengan penjenamaan lebih baik.

Sumber: Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] DAP Percepatkan Kongres Ke Julai — Undi Sama Ada Pemimpin Patut Keluar Kerajaan, Tapi Janji Kekal Juga
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, DAP Secretary-General / Transport Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

DAP, parti komponen terbesar dalam gabungan Pakatan Harapan Anwar mengikut kerusi parlimen, telah mempercepatkan kongres kebangsaannya dari September ke 12 Julai 2026. Di kongres, lebih 4,000 perwakilan akan mengundi dalam referendum dalaman sama ada pemimpin parti patut meletak jawatan daripada jawatan kerajaan. Setiausaha agung DAP dan Menteri Pengangkutan Anthony Loke memberitahu Malaysiakini bahawa “tindak balas kerajaan tidak memadai dan tidak memenuhi jangkaan rakyat” berhubung skandal SPRM.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Kongres DAP kini adalah acara dalaman paling penting dalam kalendar politik PH sebelum PRU akan datang. Jika majoriti besar perwakilan mengundi supaya pemimpin meletak jawatan, ia akan menstabilkan kerajaan Anwar pada masa paling teruk. Referendum ini juga undi proksi sama ada asas DAP masih percaya kerajaan perpaduan berbaloi untuk disokong.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Inilah petunjuknya: DAP berkata MP-nya akan terus menyokong Anwar tanpa mengira keputusan referendum. Jadi perwakilan berasa diberi kuasa dengan mengundi untuk gerak isyarat dramatik, sementara kepimpinan parti sudah pra-komited untuk tidak mengubah apa-apa. Ini teater akauntabiliti. Loke kata tindak balas kerajaan tidak memadai, kemudian kekal dalam kerajaan. DAP kata reformasi adalah garisan merah, kemudian melihat rang undang-undang had tempoh PM gagal dan ketua SPRM kekal di pejabat. Mempercepatkan kongres untuk mengancam keluar sambil berjanji kekal bukan politik tekanan. Ia persembahan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

DAP mempunyai corak membantah kuat dan mematuhi secara senyap. Pada 2023, parti itu memprotes pengaruh UMNO dalam kerajaan perpaduan tetapi kekal. Pada 2024, MP DAP membangkitkan kebimbangan mengenai perjanjian tahanan rumah Najib tetapi tidak mengambil tindakan. Kekalahan teruk Sabah November 2025 sepatutnya menjadi amaran. Sebaliknya, tindak balas DAP adalah kongres lima bulan lagi di mana hasilnya telah dipra-neutralkan.

🎯 Teguran PH

DAP, anda memberitahu pengundi anda akan menjadi suara hati kerajaan perpaduan. Tiga tahun kemudian, setiausaha agung anda sendiri mengakui tindak balas kerajaan terhadap rasuah tidak memadai. Akar umbi anda murka. Dan jawapan anda adalah kongres di mana perwakilan mengundi soalan yang anda sudah putuskan tidak penting? Sama ada referendum ini bermakna sesuatu atau tidak. Jika pemimpin anda kekal tanpa mengira hasilnya, berhenti berpura-pura ini demokrasi dalam parti. Ini kawalan kerosakan.

Sumber: Straits Times | CNA

item.title: [BM] Keluarga Daim Saman Kerajaan Anwar Atas Dakwaan 'Komplot Zionis' — Kata Siasatan Polis Tidak Sah Dan Bertentangan Perlembagaan
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Daim Zainuddin’s Family (Respondents)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Keluarga bekas menteri kewangan Daim Zainuddin — balunya Naimah Abdul Khalid, dua anak lelaki, dan dua peguam keluarga — memfailkan saman di Mahkamah Tinggi Kuala Lumpur pada 7 Mac 2026, menuntut pengisytiharan bahawa siasatan polis terhadap dakwaan komplot menjatuhkan kerajaan Anwar adalah tidak sah, bertentangan perlembagaan, dan dicemari niat jahat. Ini berikutan Anwar memberitahu Parlimen pada 3 Mac bahawa seorang suspek dalam konspirasi yang didakwa telah melantik firma PR antarabangsa untuk melancarkan serangan terselaras terhadap institusi negara, mendakwa komplot itu melibatkan ‘kumpulan Zionis terkemuka,’ pelobi asing, bankir, dan agensi media antarabangsa.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan lagi sekadar naratif politik — ia kini konfrontasi undang-undang. Anwar membina kesnya untuk ‘komplot Zionis’ berdasarkan keistimewaan parlimen dan laporan polis, tetapi cabaran mahkamah memaksa kerajaannya mengemukakan bukti sebenar atau menghadapi kemaluan siasatannya diisytiharkan bertentangan perlembagaan. Pengkritik sudah menunjukkan kemunafikan: kem politik Anwar sendiri pernah mencari sokongan asing, melibatkan media antarabangsa, dan melobi kerajaan luar negara secara meluas semasa beliau berdekad dalam pembangkang.

💩 Kenapa Ia Karut

Anwar menghabiskan bertahun-tahun sebagai pemimpin pembangkang memupuk hubungan dengan media antarabangsa, kerajaan asing, dan masyarakat sivil global untuk menekan kerajaan Malaysia. Sekutunya terbang ke Washington, London, dan Geneva untuk menyuarakan pendirian mereka. Itu advokasi.
