Where is the Madani government that talks about inclusivity? The Chinese farmers in Tanjong Sepat would like to know.

Sources: Straits Times | NST | CodeBlue | The Star

item.title: Tiong King Sing Pulls 'Do You Know Who I Am?' Card While Lecturing Staff About Rudeness
item.description:
Said by: Tiong King Sing, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-12

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 12 February 2026, Tourism Minister Tiong King Sing launched the ‘Mesra Malaysia’ (Friendly Malaysia) campaign at KL Sentral — a campaign about being welcoming and courteous to tourists. During the launch, Tiong complained about rudeness from public and private sector employees, saying: “Some officers think they have a lot of power. But when people don’t understand certain issues, we must explain.”

The South China Morning Post ran the story with the devastating headline: “Malaysian minister slams rude staff hindering tourism: ‘do you know who I am?’” — capturing the irony of a powerful politician lecturing others about arrogance while exuding precisely the same energy.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Visit Malaysia Year 2026 is supposed to be a national priority. The tourism sector is critical to Malaysia’s economy. And the minister responsible for making Malaysia welcoming to the world is making headlines for his own attitude problem.

This isn’t Tiong’s first controversy. In 2025, Anwar had to personally warn him after alcohol was served at a Tourism Malaysia gala dinner — a sensitivity failure in a Muslim-majority country. The pattern is a minister who generates more negative headlines than tourist arrivals.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

You’re launching a campaign called ‘Friendly Malaysia’ while acting like the least friendly person in the room. The cognitive dissonance is spectacular. A minister complaining about public servants who “think they have a lot of power” — while wielding the power of a federal ministry to publicly shame unnamed workers — is peak Malaysian political irony.

The ‘do you know who I am’ energy is exactly the attitude tourists and citizens hate about Malaysian bureaucracy. And the minister tasked with fixing it is the embodiment of it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Tiong King Sing is a businessman-politician from Sarawak’s GPS coalition. The alcohol controversy at the gala dinner in 2025 already showed a minister out of touch with sensitivities. Malaysia’s tourism sector has also been plagued by scam taxi drivers, overcharging at tourist spots, and visa problems — systemic issues that require systemic solutions, not press conferences about attitude.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You put Tiong King Sing in charge of tourism. He’s now making international headlines for arrogance while launching a friendliness campaign. The minister responsible for Malaysia’s image is damaging Malaysia’s image. If you want Visit Malaysia Year 2026 to succeed, start by ensuring your ministers don’t become the joke tourists tell when they get home.

Sources: SCMP | CNA

item.title: [BM] Projek MyKiosk Nga Kor Ming: Tiada Penilaian Keperluan, Tiada KPI, SPRM Panggil Kegagalan Tadbir Urus
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Housing

Date: 2026-02-10

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 10 Februari 2026, Suruhanjaya Pencegahan Rasuah Malaysia (SPRM) mendedahkan Kementerian Perumahan dan Kerajaan Tempatan (KPKT) di bawah Nga Kor Ming gagal menjalankan penilaian keperluan yang wajar sebelum melancarkan inisiatif MyKiosk — program seluruh negara untuk menyediakan kiosk moden kepada peniaga.

SPRM mengeluarkan lima cadangan penambahbaikan tadbir urus dan menyatakan: “Ketiadaan penilaian keperluan dan mekanisme pemantauan hasil yang menyeluruh, termasuk KPI, menghalang penilaian impak projek di peringkat pihak berkuasa tempatan.”

Bekas naib presiden MCA Ti Lian Ker segera menyeru peletakan jawatan Nga, berkata: “Apabila berbilion wang awam dipertaruhkan, gagal bertanya soalan asas — siapa memerlukannya, di mana patut diletakkan, dan bagaimana mengukur kejayaan — bukan sahaja cuai tetapi tidak bertanggungjawab.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Ini asas tadbir urus 101. Anda tidak melancarkan program seluruh negara dengan wang awam tanpa bertanya dahulu: Adakah sesiapa memerlukannya? Projek MyKiosk digambarkan sebagai gajah putih selepas gagal memenuhi objektif kerajaan, dengan kiosk yang ditempatkan secara buruk menyebabkan kesulitan awam berbanding membantu peniaga.

Ti Lian Ker mencatat ini menambah kepada “rekod pertimbangan yang dipersoal dan keutamaan yang salah letak yang semakin bertambah” — termasuk arahan Nga menukar empat tandas awam kepada tandas pintar dan kebimbangan awam terhadap Rang Undang-undang Pembaharuan Bandar. Coraknya jelas: membuat keputusan tanpa peka, asas dasar yang lemah, dan bantahan awam selepas fakta.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Inilah bahagian terbaik. Pada Julai 2025, apabila projek MyKiosk pertama kali dibangkitkan sebagai gajah putih, Nga mengundang SPRM menyiasat dan kemudian menuntut permohonan maaf daripada pengkritik apabila SPRM tidak menemui rasuah. Beliau berkata: “Kepada mereka yang membaling batu, jangan sorokkan tangan.”

Tetapi tiada rasuah tidak bermakna tiada kegagalan. Laporan SPRM Februari 2026 mengesahkan apa yang semua sudah tahu: projek itu dirancang dengan buruk, dipantau dengan buruk, dan dilaksanakan dengan buruk. Nga meraikan ketiadaan niat jenayah sambil mengabaikan kehadiran ketidakcekapan yang menakjubkan. Itu bukan tadbir urus — itu trofi penyertaan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Kementerian Nga mempunyai corak. Inisiatif tandas pintar dicemuh seluruh negara. Rang Undang-undang Pembaharuan Bandar mencetuskan bantahan awam. Setiap episod mengikut lengkung yang sama: lancar dulu, kaji kemudian, minta maaf bila ditangkap, kemudian tuntut kredit kerana tidak korup. Penyelidik PAS Dr Nurul Islam menyebutnya budaya kerja “laksana dulu, kaji kemudian” Kerajaan Madani.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Anda menjanjikan tadbir urus yang bersih dan cekap. SPRM baru memberitahu kementerian anda ia tidak pun memeriksa adakah sesiapa memerlukan MyKiosk sebelum membelanjakan wang awam untuknya. Itu bukan reformasi — itu pembaziran era BN yang sama yang anda dulu kutuk dari bangku pembangkang. Jika menteri BN melakukan ini, DAP akan mengadakan sidang akhbar berminggu-minggu. Apabila menteri anda sendiri? Senyap.

Sumber: FMT | FMT - Ti Lian Ker | The Star | Focus Malaysia

item.title: Nga Kor Ming's MyKiosk Project: No Needs Assessment, No KPIs, MACC Calls It a Governance Failure
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Housing

Date: 2026-02-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 10 February 2026, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) revealed that the Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) under Nga Kor Ming failed to carry out a proper needs assessment before launching the MyKiosk initiative — a nationwide programme to provide traders with modern kiosks.

MACC issued five governance improvement recommendations and stated: “The absence of a needs assessment and a comprehensive outcome-monitoring mechanism, including KPIs, hindered the evaluation of the project’s impact at the local authority level.”

Former MCA vice-president Ti Lian Ker immediately called for Nga’s resignation, saying: “When billions in public funds are at stake, failing to ask fundamental questions — who needs this, where it should be placed, and how success will be measured — is not just careless but irresponsible.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is basic governance 101. You don’t launch a nationwide programme with public money without first asking: Does anyone need this? The MyKiosk project was described as a white elephant after failing to meet government objectives, with poorly placed kiosks causing public inconvenience rather than helping traders.

Ti Lian Ker noted this added to “a growing record of questionable judgments and misplaced priorities” — including Nga’s directive to turn four public toilets into smart toilets and public unease over the Urban Renewal Bill. The pattern is clear: tone-deaf decision-making, weak policy grounding, and public backlash after the fact.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Here’s the best part. In July 2025, when the MyKiosk project was first flagged as a white elephant, Nga invited MACC to investigate and then demanded an apology from critics when MACC found no corruption. He said: “To those who threw stones, don’t hide your hands.”

But no corruption doesn’t mean no failure. The MACC’s February 2026 report confirmed what everyone already knew: the project was poorly planned, poorly monitored, and poorly executed. Nga celebrated the absence of criminal intent while ignoring the presence of spectacular incompetence. That’s not governance — that’s a participation trophy.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Nga’s ministry has a pattern. The smart toilet initiative was mocked nationally. The Urban Renewal Bill drew public protest. Each episode follows the same arc: launch first, study later, apologise when caught, then demand credit for not being corrupt. PAS researcher Dr Nurul Islam called it the “execute first, study later” work culture of the Madani Government.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You promised clean, competent governance. The MACC just told your ministry it didn’t even bother to check if anyone needed MyKiosk before spending public money on it. That’s not reform — that’s the same BN-era waste you used to condemn from the opposition bench. If a BN minister had done this, DAP would have held press conferences for weeks. When it’s your own minister? Silence.

Sources: FMT | FMT - Ti Lian Ker | The Star | Focus Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Pelajar Tunjuk Kad Merah Kepada Adam Adli Atas Janji AUKU Yang Dimungkiri — Anak Emas PH Sendiri Jadi Pemungkir
item.description:
Said by: Adam Adli Abdul Halim, Deputy Higher Education Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2026-02-09

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 9 Februari 2026, pelajar universiti berarak ke Parlimen menuntut pemansuhan Akta Universiti dan Kolej Universiti 1971 (AUKU). Sasaran mereka? Timbalan Menteri Pengajian Tinggi Adam Adli Abdul Halim — pernah menjadi aktivis pelajar paling terkenal Malaysia, kini penjelmaan hidup janji PH yang dimungkiri. Memansuhkan AUKU merupakan janji manifesto utama Pakatan Harapan dalam PRU14 (2018) dan PRU15 (2022). Pada lewat Januari 2026, Menteri Pengajian Tinggi Zambry Abd Kadir memberitahu Parlimen kerajaan “tidak bercadang” memansuhkan AUKU — pembalikan dasar yang disampaikan tanpa gembar-gembur. Pelajar menunjukkan kad merah kepada Adam Adli dan memanggilnya “pemungkir.” Zambry enggan menerima memorandum mereka. Adam Adli, 33, ditahan pada 2013 kerana menurunkan bendera di perhimpunan pelajar — tindakan yang menjadikannya simbol gerakan pelajar. PH memperjuangkan kesnya sebagai bukti AUKU tidak adil. Kini beliau duduk dalam kementerian yang menguatkuasakannya.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: PH berjanji memansuhkan AUKU dalam dua pilihan raya berturut-turut. Empat tahun berkuasa, Menteri Pengajian Tinggi kata “tidak bercadang” memansuhkannya. Ini bukan penangguhan — ini pembohongan. Janji itu tidak pernah dimaksudkan untuk ditunaikan; ia direka untuk menuai undi pelajar. Perjalanan peribadi Adam Adli ialah metafora paling memusnahkan untuk kerajaan PH: pelajar yang ditahan kerana menurunkan bendera sebagai bantahan kini duduk dalam kementerian yang mengekalkan undang-undang yang menjenayahkan bantahan pelajar. Pelajar yang mengundi PH berdasarkan janji ini kini membantah timbalan menteri PH sendiri. Keengganan Zambry menerima memorandum adalah tamparan simbolik — apabila kerajaan tidak sanggup menerima sehelai kertas daripada penunjuk perasaan pelajar, mesejnya jelas: undi anda berguna, suara anda tidak. AUKU menentukan sama ada 1.3 juta pelajar universiti Malaysia boleh mengambil bahagian dalam kehidupan politik negara. Mengekalkannya bermakna PH percaya pelajar patut mengundi untuk reformasi tetapi tidak menyokongnya.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Di bawah AUKU, pelajar boleh berdepan penggantungan atau penyingkiran kerana menyertai aktiviti politik. Undang-undang ini digubal pada 1971 selepas bantahan pelajar terhadap kemiskinan luar bandar — ia direka dari awal untuk menyekat belia yang sedar politik. PH tahu ini. PH berkempen menentangnya. Timbalan menteri PH sendiri menjadi mangsa. Dan kini PH mengekalkannya. Straits Times mencatat bantahan itu “menambah cabaran reformasi prapilihan raya Anwar” — merangka AUKU sebagai sebahagian daripada corak janji tergendala yang lebih luas yang menghakis sokongan belia. Jika anda tidak boleh menunaikan janji kepada pelajar yang percaya pada anda, jenama reformasi sudah mati.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Reformasi AUKU dijanjikan oleh setiap gabungan pembangkang sejak tsunami politik 2008. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) menjanjikan reformasi tetapi runtuh sebelum bertindak. PH 2.0 kini mempunyai lebih tiga tahun dan secara jelas membalikan janji. Corak sama seperti penghapusan tol, pemisahan AG-PP, dan pemansuhan Akta Hasutan: janji berani dalam pembangkang, tangguh dalam kerajaan, diam-diam tinggalkan. AUKU menyertai senarai ikrar manifesto yang merupakan hiasan kempen, bukan komitmen tadbir urus.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Timbalan menteri anda ditahan di bawah undang-undang ini. Beliau membina kerjaya melawannya. Kini beliau mempertahankan kementerian yang menguatkuasakannya. Jika anda tulis ini sebagai fiksyen, ia akan dikritik sebagai terlalu tepat. Mansuhkan AUKU atau pinda untuk membuang sekatan terhadap aktiviti politik pelajar — seperti yang anda janji dua kali. 1.3 juta pelajar di universiti Malaysia sedang memerhati. Dan mereka mengundi.

Sumber: Straits Times | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: Students Flash Red Cards at Adam Adli Over Broken UUCA Promise — PH's Own Poster Boy Turns Reneger
item.description:
Said by: Adam Adli Abdul Halim, Deputy Higher Education Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2026-02-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 9 February 2026, university students marched to Parliament demanding the abolition of the Universities and University Colleges Act 1971 (UUCA). Their target? Deputy Higher Education Minister Adam Adli Abdul Halim — once Malaysia’s most famous student activist, now the living embodiment of PH’s broken promise. Abolishing UUCA was a key manifesto promise by Pakatan Harapan in both GE14 (2018) and GE15 (2022). In late January 2026, Higher Education Minister Zambry Abd Kadir told Parliament the government had “no plans” to abolish UUCA — a policy reversal delivered without fanfare. Students flashed red cards at Adam Adli and called him a “reneger.” Zambry refused to receive their memorandum. Adam Adli, 33, was arrested in 2013 for lowering a flag at a student rally — an act that made him a symbol of the student movement. PH championed his case as proof that UUCA was unjust. Now he sits in the ministry that enforces it.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: PH promised to repeal UUCA in two consecutive elections. Four years into government, the Higher Education Minister says there are “no plans” to abolish it. This is not a delay — it is a lie. The promise was never intended to be kept; it was designed to harvest student votes. Adam Adli’s personal arc is the most devastating metaphor for the PH government: the student who was arrested for lowering a flag in protest now sits in the ministry that maintains the law that criminalises student protest. The students who voted for PH based on this promise are now protesting PH’s own deputy minister. Zambry’s refusal to receive the memorandum is a symbolic gut punch — when the government won’t even accept a piece of paper from student protesters, the message is clear: your vote was useful, your voice is not. UUCA determines whether 1.3 million Malaysian university students can participate in the political life of their country. Keeping it in place means PH believes students should vote for reform but not advocate for it.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Under UUCA, students can face suspension or expulsion for participating in political activities. The law was enacted in 1971 after student protests against rural poverty — it was designed from the start to suppress politically conscious youth. PH knew this. PH campaigned against it. PH’s own deputy minister was a victim of it. And now PH is keeping it. The Straits Times noted the protest “adds to Anwar’s pre-election reform challenges” — framing UUCA as part of a broader pattern of stalled promises eroding youth support. If you cannot keep promises to students who believed in you, the reform brand is dead.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: UUCA reform has been promised by every opposition coalition since the 2008 political tsunami. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) promised reform but collapsed before acting. PH 2.0 has now had over three years and explicitly reversed the promise. The pattern is identical to toll abolition, AG-PP separation, and the Sedition Act repeal: promise boldly in opposition, delay in government, quietly abandon. UUCA joins a growing list of manifesto pledges that were campaign decorations, not governance commitments.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your deputy minister was arrested under this law. He built his career fighting it. Now he defends the ministry that enforces it. If you wrote this as fiction, it would be criticised as too on-the-nose. Abolish UUCA or amend it to remove restrictions on student political activity — as you promised twice. The 1.3 million students in Malaysian universities are watching. And they vote.

Sources: Straits Times | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: [BM] Fahmi Fadzil: Kebebasan Bersuara Bukan Bermakna Bebas Menghina Dalam Talian
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2026-02-07

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 7 Februari 2026, Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil memberitahu orang ramai bahawa kebebasan bersuara tidak bermakna bebas menghina atau memfitnah dalam talian. Ini selepas Mahkamah Persekutuan mengembalikan Seksyen 233 Akta Komunikasi dan Multimedia (AKM), yang menjenayahkan kandungan yang dianggap “amat menyinggung” — peruntukan yang PH pernah tentang. Fahmi berkata pindaan 2024 menaikkan ambang daripada “menyinggung” kepada “amat menyinggung,” mendakwa ini menjadikannya lebih sukar disalahguna. Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian 2025 (ONSA) berkuat kuasa pada 1 Januari 2026, memberikan SKMM kuasa mengenakan penalti sehingga RM10 juta kepada platform yang gagal membuang kandungan berbahaya. SKMM — badan yang memutuskan apa yang “amat menyinggung” — melapor terus kepada kementerian Fahmi.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: “Amat menyinggung” ialah piawaian subjektif dan kabur yang boleh bermakna apa sahaja yang kerajaan mahu. Gabungan peruntukan AKM yang dikembalikan dan ONSA baharu memberikan kerajaan Malaysia salah satu kerangka pengawalseliaan ucapan paling berkuasa di Asia Tenggara. Platform berdepan denda RM10J; individu berdepan pendakwaan. Kesan menakutkan itu sengaja direka. PH dalam pembangkang berulang kali mengutuk AKM sebagai alat penindasan autoritarian. Pada 2018, manifesto PH menyeru “menyemak” AKM. Pada 2022, Pemuda PH memanggil Seksyen 233 “drakonian.” Dalam kerajaan, mereka memindanya sedikit, menamakan semula “menyinggung” kepada “amat menyinggung,” dan memanggilnya kemajuan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Siapa memutuskan apa yang “amat menyinggung”? SKMM, yang melapor kepada Fahmi. Fahmi, yang melapor kepada Anwar. Tiada semakan kehakiman bebas sebelum kandungan ditandai atau dibuang. Menteri yang memperjuangkan “kebebasan bersuara dengan had” juga menteri yang mengawal had. Musang menjaga reban ayam dan memberitahu ayam ia untuk perlindungan mereka sendiri. Rekod PH sendiri memusnahkan hujah Fahmi: apabila kerajaan menahan wartawan kerana bertanya soalan (Rex Tan), menyiasat aktivis atas pos media sosial, dan menggunakan Akta Hasutan lebih 90 kali dalam tiga tahun, pembelaan “kami hanya sasarkan kemudaratan sebenar” runtuh. Menamakan semula “menyinggung” kepada “amat menyinggung” bukan reformasi — ia penjenamaan semula.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sejak berkuasa, kerajaan Anwar telah menggunakan AKM, Akta Hasutan, dan undang-undang ucapan lain untuk menahan wartawan, aktivis, dan pengguna media sosial — termasuk wartawan FMT Rex Tan, aktivis Heidy Quah, dan pelbagai pengulas dalam talian yang mengkritik kerajaan. Akta Hasutan — yang PH berjanji memansuhkan — telah digunakan lebih 90 kali sejak 2024. AKM digunakan terhadap pengkritik di bawah Najib, di bawah Muhyiddin, dan kini di bawah Anwar. Alat bertukar tangan; penyalahgunaan berterusan. Setiap kerajaan Malaysia menggunakan undang-undang ucapan secara selektif: menyasarkan pengkritik sambil mengabaikan sekutu yang berkata perkara sama provokatifnya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda berjanji mereformasi AKM. Sebaliknya, anda menguatkannya. Anda berjanji memansuhkan Akta Hasutan. Sebaliknya, anda menggunakannya lebih daripada Najib. Anda berjanji kebebasan akhbar. Sebaliknya, SKMM anda menyekat saluran berita dan polis anda menahan wartawan di waktu subuh. Kenyataan Fahmi — “kebebasan bersuara bukan bermakna bebas menghina” — adalah hujah yang sama dibuat oleh setiap kerajaan yang pernah menyekat ucapan, dari POFMA Singapura hingga peraturan internet China. Formulanya universal: sahkan hak itu, takrifkan begitu sempit sehingga ia menjadi tidak bermakna. Pengundi PH mengharapkan seni bina penindasan ucapan diruntuhkan. Sebaliknya, anda menambah tingkat baharu ke bangunan.

Sumber: NST | Malaysiakini

item.title: Fahmi Fadzil: Free Speech Doesn't Mean Freedom to Offend Online
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2026-02-07

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 7 February 2026, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil told the public that free speech does not mean freedom to offend or defame online. This came after the Federal Court reinstated Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act (CMA), which criminalises content deemed “grossly offensive” — a provision PH once opposed. Fahmi said the 2024 amendments raised the threshold from “offensive” to “grossly offensive,” claiming this made it harder to misuse. The Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) took effect on 1 January 2026, giving MCMC powers to penalise platforms up to RM10 million for failing to remove harmful content. MCMC — the body that decides what is “grossly offensive” — reports directly to Fahmi’s ministry.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: “Grossly offensive” is a subjective and vague standard that can mean anything the government wants it to mean. The combination of the reinstated CMA provision and the new ONSA gives the Malaysian government one of the most powerful speech regulation frameworks in Southeast Asia. Platforms face RM10M fines; individuals face prosecution. The chilling effect is by design. PH in opposition repeatedly condemned the CMA as a tool of authoritarian suppression. In 2018, PH’s manifesto called for “reviewing” the CMA. In 2022, PH Youth called Section 233 “draconian.” In government, they amended it slightly, renamed “offensive” to “grossly offensive,” and called it progress.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Who decides what is “grossly offensive”? MCMC, which answers to Fahmi. Fahmi, who answers to Anwar. There is no independent judicial review before content is flagged or removed. The minister who champions “free speech with limits” is also the minister who controls the limits. The fox is guarding the henhouse and telling the chickens it is for their own protection. PH’s own track record demolishes Fahmi’s argument: when the government arrests a journalist for asking a question (Rex Tan), investigates activists for social media posts, and uses the Sedition Act 90+ times in three years, the “we only target real harm” defence collapses. Renaming “offensive” to “grossly offensive” is not reform — it is rebranding.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Since taking power, the Anwar government has used the CMA, Sedition Act, and other speech laws to arrest journalists, activists, and social media users — including FMT journalist Rex Tan, activist Heidy Quah, and multiple online commentators critical of the government. The Sedition Act — which PH promised to repeal — has been used over 90 times since 2024. The CMA was wielded against critics under Najib, under Muhyiddin, and now under Anwar. The tool changes hands; the abuse continues. Every Malaysian government has used speech laws selectively: targeting critics while ignoring allies who say equally provocative things.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You promised to reform the CMA. Instead, you strengthened it. You promised to repeal the Sedition Act. Instead, you used it more than Najib did. You promised press freedom. Instead, your MCMC blocks news outlets and your police arrest journalists at dawn. Fahmi’s statement — “free speech doesn’t mean freedom to offend” — is the same argument made by every government that has ever restricted speech, from Singapore’s POFMA to China’s internet regulations. The formula is universal: affirm the right, define it so narrowly it becomes meaningless. PH voters expected the architecture of speech suppression to be dismantled. Instead, you added new floors to the building.

Sources: NST | Malaysiakini

item.title: [BM] SPRM Siasat Pegawai Kanan Negeri Pulau Pinang Soal Urus Niaga Tanah — Chow Kata 'Kami Bukan Penyiasat'
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-31

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 31 Januari 2026, KM Pulau Pinang Chow Kon Yeow mengesahkan SPRM sedang menyiasat dakwaan salah guna kuasa melibatkan pegawai kanan negeri mengenai urus niaga tanah. Chow berkata kerajaan negeri akan ‘memberikan ruang penuh’ kepada SPRM dan tidak akan menjalankan siasatan dalaman sendiri. ‘Kami bukan penyiasat, tetapi kami akan memberikan kerjasama penuh,’ beliau memberitahu wartawan. Pegawai tersebut dilaporkan terus menjalankan tugas seperti biasa semasa siasatan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Seorang pegawai kanan negeri sedang disiasat SPRM atas salah guna kuasa mengenai urus niaga tanah — dan tindak balas kerajaan Pulau Pinang ialah membiarkan pegawai itu terus bekerja sambil enggan menjalankan sebarang semakan dalaman. ‘Kami bukan penyiasat’ secara teknikalnya benar tetapi secara moral bankrap. Kerajaan negeri ialah majikan. Ia mempunyai tanggungjawab memastikan pegawainya tidak menyalahgunakan kedudukan. Menyerahkan sepenuhnya akauntabiliti kepada SPRM sementara pegawai terus membuat keputusan bukan kerjasama — ia kebutaan yang sengaja.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Setiap syarikat swasta di Malaysia akan menggantung pekerja yang disiasat SPRM sambil menunggu keputusan — terutama jika siasatan melibatkan fungsi kerja pekerja. Kerajaan DAP Pulau Pinang membiarkan pegawai terus bekerja. Justifikasinya — ‘kami bukan penyiasat’ — adalah helah. Anda tidak perlu menjadi penyiasat untuk melaksanakan protokol tadbir urus asas: gantung sambil menunggu siasatan, selamatkan dokumen, jalankan semakan dalaman. Ini amalan tadbir urus korporat standard yang parti ‘pentadbiran cekap’ nampaknya tidak pernah dengar.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Ini bukan siasatan SPRM pertama Pulau Pinang di bawah DAP. Perbicaraan terowong Guan Eng masih berjalan. Dakwaan dana zakat disiasat. Urus niaga tanah dipersoal berulang kali. Corak konsisten: SPRM menyiasat, Pulau Pinang kata ‘kami bekerjasama,’ pegawai terus bekerja, dan tiada apa berubah secara dalaman sehingga (dan melainkan) pertuduhan difailkan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Pegawai kanan anda disiasat SPRM atas salah guna kuasa mengenai urus niaga tanah dan anda tidak sanggup menggantungnya? ‘Kami bukan penyiasat’ bukan falsafah tadbir urus — ia pengabaian tanggungjawab. Laksanakan semakan dalaman. Gantung pegawai sambil menunggu siasatan. Selamatkan dokumen urus niaga tanah yang berkaitan. Atau akui bahawa DAP Pulau Pinang tidak lebih baik dalam memantau diri sendiri berbanding mana-mana negeri BN.

Sumber: FMT | Bernama

item.title: MACC Probes Senior Penang State Official Over Land Deal — Chow Says 'We Are Not Investigators'
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-31

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 31 January 2026, Penang CM Chow Kon Yeow confirmed that MACC was investigating allegations of abuse of power involving a senior state official over a land deal. Chow said the state government would ‘give full space’ to MACC and would not conduct its own internal probe. ‘We are not investigators, but we will provide full cooperation,’ he told reporters. The official was reportedly continuing to perform duties as usual during the investigation.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A senior state official is under MACC investigation for abuse of power over a land deal — and the Penang government’s response is to let the official keep working while refusing to conduct any internal review. ‘We are not investigators’ is technically true but morally bankrupt. The state government is the employer. It has a duty to ensure its officials aren’t abusing their positions. Outsourcing accountability entirely to MACC while the official keeps making decisions is not cooperation — it’s wilful blindness.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Every private company in Malaysia would suspend an employee under MACC investigation pending the outcome — especially if the investigation involves the employee’s job functions. DAP’s Penang government lets the official keep working. The justification — ‘we are not investigators’ — is a cop-out. You don’t need to be investigators to implement basic governance protocols: suspend pending investigation, secure documents, conduct an internal review. These are standard corporate governance practices that the ‘competent administration’ party apparently hasn’t heard of.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This is not Penang’s first MACC investigation under DAP. The Guan Eng tunnel trial is ongoing. Zakat fund allegations were investigated. Land deals have been questioned repeatedly. The pattern is consistent: MACC investigates, Penang says ‘we’re cooperating,’ officials keep working, and nothing changes internally until (and unless) charges are filed.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your senior official is under MACC investigation for abuse of power over a land deal and you won’t even suspend them? ‘We are not investigators’ is not a governance philosophy — it’s an abdication of responsibility. Implement an internal review. Suspend the official pending investigation. Secure the relevant land deal documents. Or admit that DAP’s Penang is no better at self-policing than any BN state.

Sources: FMT | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Kualiti Air Sungai Merosot — Lebih Banyak Sungai Tercemar Pada 2025 Berbanding 2024
item.description:
Said by: Natural Resources Ministry

Category: Other

Date: 2026-01-28

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Menurut data yang dikeluarkan Kementerian Sumber Asli pada lewat Januari 2026, kualiti air sungai Malaysia merosot tahun ke tahun. Sungai bersih menurun daripada 486 kepada 468. Sungai berkualiti “sederhana” meningkat daripada 161 kepada 171. Sungai “sedikit tercemar” melonjak daripada 25 kepada 33. Angka bergerak ke arah yang salah merentasi setiap kategori. Kemerosotan ini berlaku ketika kerajaan secara agresif mendorong pengembangan perlombongan nadir bumi — satu tapak di Perak sudah menukarkan sungai kepada warna biru akibat pelepasan perlombongan — dan terus mempromosikan Malaysia sebagai peneraju “ekonomi hijau.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Sungai ialah penunjuk awal bagi tadbir urus alam sekitar. Jika kualiti air merosot sementara kerajaan mendakwa kemajuan alam sekitar, dakwaan itu kosong. Ini berlaku di bawah kerajaan yang mengumumkan Pelan Ketahanan Iklim, menetapkan sasaran ekonomi hijau, dan meletakkan Malaysia sebagai peka alam sekitar di pentas dunia. Data berkata sebaliknya. Lebih banyak sungai tercemar bermakna sumber air minuman lebih buruk, ekosistem terdegradasi, kos rawatan air lebih tinggi, dan risiko kesihatan bagi komuniti yang tinggal berhampiran laluan air terjejas — terutamanya rakyat Malaysia luar bandar dan berpendapatan rendah.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Kerajaan bermegah tentang sasaran ekonomi hijau sambil mengetuai kemerosotan alam sekitar yang boleh diukur. Anda tidak boleh serentak mendorong pengembangan perlombongan nadir bumi secara agresif, meluluskan pembangunan pusat data yang membebankan sumber air, dan membiarkan piawaian pelepasan industri tidak dikuatkuasakan — kemudian mendakwa kepimpinan alam sekitar dengan muka serius. Data kualiti sungai bukan subjektif. Ia diukur. Ia diterbitkan. Dan ia menunjukkan kemerosotan. Jurang antara retorik alam sekitar kerajaan dan hasil alam sekitarnya bukan masalah komunikasi — ia kegagalan tadbir urus. Apabila sungai anda semakin kotor di bawah pengawasan anda, sasaran hijau anda adalah rekaan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Penguatkuasaan alam sekitar Malaysia lemah secara kronik merentasi pentadbiran. Pembuangan toksik Sg Kim Kim di Johor (2019) memasukkan ratusan ke hospital. Sungai Perak bertukar biru akibat perlombongan nadir bumi (2025). Banjir monsun tahunan — diburukkan oleh penyahutanan dan perancangan saliran yang lemah — memindahkan puluhan ribu. Jabatan Alam Sekitar secara konsisten kurang dibiayai dan kurang kakitangan. Di bawah BN, perlindungan alam sekitar dikorbankan untuk pembangunan. Di bawah PH, pengorbanan sama berterusan di bawah jenama yang lebih hijau. Coraknya bukan partisan: korek sekarang, bersih tak pernah.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Terbitkan pecahan sungai demi sungai. Namakan pencemar. Kuatkuasakan piawaian pelepasan dengan penalti sebenar — bukan surat amaran yang difailkan dan dilupakan. Jika perlombongan nadir bumi menukarkan sungai biru dan kualiti air merosot secara nasional, naratif “ekonomi hijau” anda adalah pembohongan yang disokong data kementerian anda sendiri. Kerajaan yang mengukur kemerosotan alam sekitar dan tidak berbuat apa-apa mengenainya bukan mentadbir — ia mendokumentasikan kegagalannya sendiri. Biayai Jabatan Alam Sekitar dengan sewajarnya. Kuatkuasakan piawaian pelepasan industri. Atau berhenti berpura-pura anda mengambil berat tentang alam sekitar.

Sumber: Asian News Network

item.title: River Water Quality Declining — More Polluted Rivers in 2025 Than 2024
item.description:
Said by: Natural Resources Ministry

Category: Other

Date: 2026-01-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: According to data released by the Natural Resources Ministry in late January 2026, Malaysia’s river water quality deteriorated year-over-year. Clean rivers dropped from 486 to 468. “Moderate” quality rivers rose from 161 to 171. “Slightly polluted” rivers jumped from 25 to 33. The numbers are moving in the wrong direction across every category. This decline occurred as the government aggressively pushed rare earth mining expansion — one site in Perak already turned a river visibly blue from mining discharges — and continued to promote Malaysia as a “green economy” leader.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Rivers are the canary in the coal mine for environmental governance. If water quality declines while the government claims environmental progress, the claims are hollow. This is happening under a government that announced a Climate Resilience Plan, set green economy targets, and positioned Malaysia as environmentally conscious on the world stage. The data says otherwise. More polluted rivers mean worse drinking water sources, degraded ecosystems, higher water treatment costs, and health risks for communities living near affected waterways — disproportionately rural and lower-income Malaysians.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government boasts about green economy targets while presiding over measurable environmental degradation. You cannot simultaneously push aggressive rare earth mining expansion, approve data centre developments that strain water resources, and allow industrial discharge standards to go unenforced — then claim environmental leadership with a straight face. The river quality data is not subjective. It is measured. It is published. And it shows decline. The gap between the government’s environmental rhetoric and its environmental outcomes is not a communication problem — it is a governance failure. When your rivers are getting dirtier under your watch, your green targets are fiction.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s environmental enforcement has been chronically weak across administrations. The Sg Kim Kim toxic dumping in Johor (2019) hospitalised hundreds. The Perak River turned blue from rare earth mining (2025). Annual monsoon floods — worsened by deforestation and poor drainage planning — displace tens of thousands. The Department of Environment has been consistently underfunded and understaffed. Under BN, environmental protection was sacrificed for development. Under PH, the same sacrifice continues under a greener brand. The pattern is bipartisan: extract now, clean up never.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Publish the river-by-river breakdown. Name the polluters. Enforce discharge standards with real penalties — not warning letters that get filed and forgotten. If rare earth mining is turning rivers blue and water quality is declining nationally, your “green economy” narrative is a lie backed by your own ministry’s data. A government that measures environmental decline and does nothing about it is not governing — it is documenting its own failure. Fund the Department of Environment properly. Enforce industrial discharge standards. Or stop pretending you care about the environment.

Sources: Asian News Network

item.title: [BM] Kajian Malaysia: Analisis Kos-Manfaat Perjanjian Perdagangan AS Tunjuk 'Kebimbangan' Tetapi Kerajaan Kata Jangan Keluar
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Government

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-01-27

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Januari 2026, Bloomberg melaporkan kerajaan Malaysia mengumumkan analisis kos-manfaat rasmi terhadap perjanjian Agreed Reciprocal Trade (ART) AS-Malaysia yang kontroversi — berikutan kritikan bertambah daripada bekas AG Tommy Thomas (yang menyebutnya “perjanjian paling teruk sejak merdeka”), bekas PM Mahathir, kumpulan pemikir, dan ahli politik pembangkang. Tetapi dalam nafas yang sama, kerajaan memberi amaran kepada penggubal undang-undang agar tidak meninggalkan pakatan. East Asia Forum menerbitkan analisis terperinci bertajuk “The Art of the Deal Lost in US-Malaysia Pact,” berhujah Malaysia menyerahkan kawalan eksport mineral kritikal, hak percukaian perkhidmatan digital, dan dasar perdagangan bebas sebagai ganti pengurangan tarif daripada 25% kepada 19% — masih menghukum menurut sebarang piawaian. Butiran perjanjian pertama kali diterbitkan di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum dikongsi dengan Parlimen Malaysia.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Mengumumkan analisis kos-manfaat sambil mendahului kesimpulannya bukan tadbir urus — ia teater politik. “Kaji tetapi jangan keluar” memberitahu anda segalanya tentang pendekatan pentadbiran ini: akui masalah, ubah apa-apa pun tidak. Jika analisis menunjukkan perjanjian itu merugikan Malaysia, adakah kerajaan akan bertindak? Jawapannya sudah ada dalam rangkaian. Kajian itu wujud untuk menyerap tekanan, bukan untuk memaklumkan keputusan. Fakta bahawa Parlimen mengetahui perjanjian itu dari laman web Rumah Putih ialah kegagalan tadbir urus tertib pertama — perjanjian perdagangan yang mengekang kedaulatan negara sepatutnya memerlukan kelulusan parlimen sebelum ditandatangani, bukan selepas.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: “Kaji tetapi jangan keluar” ialah buku panduan Madani dalam bentuk ringkas: akui masalah, janjikan proses, ubah apa-apa pun tidak. Ia pendekatan sama yang digunakan untuk pemisahan AG-PP, pemansuhan AUKU, penghapusan tol, dan setiap reformasi lain yang tergendala. Analisis kos-manfaat sama ada akan dikuburkan, dipetik secara selektif, atau digunakan untuk mewajarkan penyesuaian kecil sementara perjanjian teras kekal tidak disentuh. Anwar melangkau badan perundangan mengenai perjanjian ekonomi paling penting dalam dekad. Apabila bekas AG anda sendiri menyebut perjanjian perdagangan anda yang paling teruk sejak merdeka dan respon anda “kami akan kaji tetapi kami kekalkan,” kajian itu bersifat pertunjukan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah perjanjian perdagangan di mana kerajaan berkomitmen dahulu dan menyemak kemudian. TPPA di bawah Najib berdepan kritikan serupa — Malaysia bersetuju dengan peruntukan yang pengkritik kata menjejaskan kedaulatan, harga farmaseutikal, dan hak buruh. PH 1.0 menarik diri daripada TPPA pada 2018, memanggilnya tidak adil. PH 2.0 menandatangani ART — yang pengkritik kata mengandungi peruntukan lebih ketat — dan kini mahu “mengkaji” sama ada ia idea yang baik. Corak berkomitmen pada perjanjian di bawah tekanan, kemudian menjalankan semakan selepas fakta yang tidak mengubah apa-apa, adalah tradisi Malaysia yang dwipartisan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Jika anda serius tentang analisis kos-manfaat, terbitkan terma penuh ART — bukan ringkasan Rumah Putih, tetapi perjanjian sebenar. Biarkan ahli ekonomi bebas, bukan pasukan petugas yang dilantik kerajaan, menilai perjanjian. Jika analisis menunjukkan Malaysia mendapat perjanjian buruk, beranilah berunding semula. “Kaji tetapi jangan keluar” bukan dasar — ia pengakuan bahawa anda sudah tahu jawapannya dan tidak mahu bertindak atasnya. Parlimen layak mendapat undian mengenai perjanjian perdagangan yang mengekang kedaulatan negara. Berikan mereka satu.

Sumber: Bloomberg | East Asia Forum

item.title: Malaysia Study: Cost-Benefit Analysis of US Trade Deal Shows 'Concerns' But Government Says Don't Quit
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Government

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-01-27

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Bloomberg reported that the Malaysian government announced a formal cost-benefit analysis of the controversial US-Malaysia Agreed Reciprocal Trade (ART) agreement — following months of mounting criticism from former AG Tommy Thomas (who called it “the worst agreement since independence”), former PM Mahathir, think tanks, and opposition politicians. But in the same breath, the government warned lawmakers not to quit the pact. East Asia Forum published a detailed analysis titled “The Art of the Deal Lost in US-Malaysia Pact,” arguing Malaysia surrendered critical mineral export controls, digital services taxation rights, and independent trade policy in exchange for a tariff reduction from 25% to 19% — still punitive by any standard. The deal’s details were first published on the White House website before being shared with Malaysian Parliament.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Announcing a cost-benefit analysis while pre-empting its conclusion is not governance — it is political theatre. “Study but don’t quit” tells you everything about this administration’s approach: acknowledge problems, change nothing. If the analysis shows the deal hurts Malaysia, will the government act? The answer is already in the framing. The study exists to absorb pressure, not to inform decisions. The fact that Parliament learned about the deal from the White House website is a governance failure of the first order — trade agreements that constrain national sovereignty should require parliamentary approval before signing, not after.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: “Study but don’t quit” is the Madani playbook in miniature: acknowledge the problem, promise a process, change nothing. It is the same approach applied to the AG-PP separation, UUCA repeal, toll abolition, and every other stalled reform. The cost-benefit analysis will either be buried, selectively quoted, or used to justify minor adjustments while the core agreement remains untouched. Anwar bypassed the legislature on the most consequential economic agreement in decades. When your own former AG calls your trade deal the worst since independence and your response is “we’ll study it but we’re keeping it,” the study is performative.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a history of trade agreements where the government commits first and reviews later. The TPPA under Najib faced similar criticism — Malaysia signed on to provisions critics said undermined sovereignty, pharmaceutical pricing, and labour rights. PH 1.0 withdrew from the TPPA in 2018, calling it unfair. PH 2.0 signed the ART — which critics say contains even more restrictive provisions — and now wants to “study” whether it was a good idea. The pattern of committing to deals under pressure, then conducting post-hoc reviews that change nothing, is a bipartisan Malaysian tradition.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: If you’re serious about the cost-benefit analysis, publish the full terms of the ART — not the White House summary, but the actual agreement. Let independent economists, not government-appointed task forces, evaluate the deal. If the analysis shows Malaysia got a bad deal, have the courage to renegotiate. “Study but don’t quit” is not a policy — it is a confession that you already know the answer and don’t want to act on it. Parliament deserves a vote on trade agreements that constrain national sovereignty. Give them one.

Sources: Bloomberg | East Asia Forum

item.title: [BM] Krisis Pembuangan Sisa Haram Selangor — Bertahun Janji, Sifar Hasil Di Bawah Amirudin
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 26 Januari 2026, Amirudin Shari mengisytiharkan ’tiada lagi tunding jari’ dan mengarahkan ’tindakan keras menyeluruh’ terhadap pembuangan sisa haram di seluruh Selangor. Beliau berkata akan melawat tapak secara peribadi bersama pihak berkuasa tempatan dan ‘mengambil tindakan tegas.’ Beliau mengarahkan Pejabat Tanah dan pihak berkuasa tempatan memberi tumpuan kepada kebersihan. Ini berikutan pengisytiharan serupa pada awal 2026 di mana kerajaan Selangor mengumumkan usaha diperhebat untuk ‘membasmi’ pembuangan sisa haram.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Amirudin menjadi Menteri Besar sejak Julai 2018. Beliau membuat janji serupa tentang pembuangan haram setiap tahun. Setiap tahun, pengumuman semakin berani — ’tindakan keras menyeluruh,’ ’tindakan tegas,’ ’tiada lagi alasan.’ Setiap tahun, tapak pembuangan haram berleluasa. Penduduk di kawasan seperti Kuala Langat, Jenjarom, dan Ijok terus menderita akibat sisa toksik, air tercemar, dan masalah pernafasan. Jurang antara retorik Amirudin dan realiti Selangor kini menjadi gaung.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: ‘Saya akan turun sendiri ke padang’ ialah apa yang ahli politik kata apabila mereka kehabisan idea dasar. Selepas 7+ tahun sebagai MB, jika Amirudin belum memperbaiki pembuangan haram, ia bukan kerana beliau tidak melawat tapak yang cukup — ia kerana infrastruktur penguatkuasaan, penalti, dan kehendak politik tidak wujud. Atau lebih teruk, kerana rangkaian pembuangan haram mempunyai hubungan politik yang melindungi mereka. ‘Tiada lagi tunding jari’ sendiri adalah tunding jari — kepada penunding jari terdahulu.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Pada 2020, Amirudin berjanji menangani pembuangan haram. Pada 2022, beliau berjanji lagi. Pada 2024, beliau mengisytiharkannya keutamaan. Pada 2026, beliau masih berjanji. Sisa masih di situ. Pada satu ketika, janji-janji itu sendiri menjadi pencemaran — memenuhi wacana awam dengan kebisingan sementara sisa sebenar bertimbun.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Tujuh tahun. Masalah sama. Janji sama. Kata sifat baru. Jika agensi penguatkuasaan anda tidak boleh menghentikan pembuangan haram selepas tujuh tahun kepimpinan anda, masalahnya ialah pemimpin, bukan agensi. Arahkan audit bebas setiap tapak pembuangan haram, terbitkan butiran pemilik tanah, dan jelaskan kenapa perintah penguatkuasaan tidak dilaksanakan. Atau buat ucapan yang sama pada Januari depan.

Sumber: Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Zulkifli Hasan Kata Tekanan Kerja Boleh Jadikan Orang Gay — Masyarakat Antarabangsa Ketawa, Malaysia Meringis
item.description:
Said by: Zulkifli Hasan, Minister in PM’s Department (Religious Affairs)

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 26 Januari 2026, Menteri Hal Ehwal Agama Zulkifli Hasan mengemukakan jawapan bertulis parlimen kepada AP PAS Siti Zailah Mohd Yusoff mengenai trend LGBT di Malaysia. Beliau memetik kajian 2017 dan menyenaraikan “pengaruh masyarakat, pengalaman seksual, tekanan kerja, dan faktor peribadi lain” sebagai kemungkinan punca “tingkah laku LGBT.” Dakwaan “tekanan kerja” segera menjadi viral — di peringkat global. CNA, SCMP, NDTV, Reuters, Hindustan Times, LGBTQ Nation, dan them.us semuanya melaporkannya. Rakyat Malaysia membanjiri media sosial dengan sindiran, secara sarkastik bertanya sama ada ahli parlimen sudah “menjadi gay” kerana mereka nampaknya tidak pernah bekerja cukup keras untuk mengalami tekanan. Zulkifli juga mendedahkan bahawa dari 2022 hingga 2025, 135 kes berkaitan “aktiviti LGBT” direkodkan sebagai tangkapan atau pendakwaan — di negara di mana hubungan sejenis masih dijenayahkan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Ini bukan sekadar memalukan — ia merosakkan. Seorang menteri kabinet, berucap dalam kapasiti parlimen rasmi, memetik sains palsu yang telah disangkal untuk menjelaskan orientasi seksual. Ini pendirian rasmi kerajaan dalam rekod di Hansard. Liputan antarabangsa menukarkan Malaysia menjadi bahan jenaka global sementara negara cuba menarik pelaburan asing, menjadi tuan rumah sidang kemuncak antarabangsa, dan meletakkan diri sebagai demokrasi majoriti Muslim yang progresif. Lebih penting, kenyataan itu menormalkan rangkaian identiti LGBT sebagai patologi — sesuatu yang disebabkan faktor luaran yang mungkin boleh “dibaiki.” Rangkaian ini menyokong penjenayahan berterusan hubungan sesama jantina dan penggunaan program “pemulihan” yang dibiayai negara.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Kajian 2017 yang dipetik Zulkifli — Sulaiman et al. — ialah kajian sosial Malaysia berskala kecil, bukan penemuan yang disemak rakan sebaya daripada mana-mana badan perubatan atau psikologi antarabangsa yang diiktiraf. Setiap organisasi perubatan dan psikologi utama dunia — WHO, APA, AMA, Royal College of Psychiatrists — telah menyatakan dengan jelas bahawa orientasi seksual tidak disebabkan tekanan, gaya hidup, atau faktor persekitaran. Menteri kemudian mengeluarkan kenyataan defensif berkata orang harus “merujuk jawapan bertulis penuh” — seolah masalahnya konteks. Bukan. Konteks menjadikannya lebih teruk: ia jawapan rasmi, dipertimbangkan, ditulis terlebih dahulu. Ini bukan tergelincir lidah. Ia sengaja.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang dan memalukan menteri membuat kenyataan yang jahil secara saintifik tentang orang LGBT. Pada 2018, TPM ketika itu Wan Azizah berkata kerajaan tidak akan mengiktiraf hak LGBT kerana ia “bertentangan dengan undang-undang alam.” Pada 2019, pegawai Hal Ehwal Agama mempromosikan kem “terapi penukaran.” Jabatan Agama Islam Wilayah Persekutuan Malaysia (JAWI) kerap menyerbu premis persendirian yang disyaki menganjurkan individu LGBT. PH dalam pembangkang mengkritik penganiayaan BN terhadap rakyat Malaysia LGBT; dalam kerajaan, PH meneruskan dasar dan retorik yang sama. Pelantikan Zulkifli Hasan sendiri ialah konsesi politik — beliau mewakili pertubuhan Melayu-Muslim dalam gabungan PH.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda berkempen atas “Malaysia Madani” — Malaysia yang bertamadun dan progresif. Menteri Hal Ehwal Agama anda baru memberitahu parlimen bahawa tekanan kerja menjadikan orang gay. Dunia ketawa. Reputasi Malaysia menerima pukulan lagi. Mana Anwar? Mana pembetulan PMO? Mana penolakan kabinet? Senyap. Kerana membetulkan Zulkifli akan mengecewakan asas konservatif yang PH amat perlukan. Jadi kerajaan membiarkan dakwaan sains palsu berdiri sebagai pendirian parlimen rasmi Malaysia mengenai orientasi seksual. Madani bermakna bertamadun. Tiada apa yang bertamadun tentang memetik sains sampah di parlimen untuk mewajarkan penjenayahan siapa yang orang cintai.

Sumber: CNA | NDTV | SCMP | LGBTQ Nation | Hindustan Times

item.title: Selangor's Illegal Waste Dumping Crisis — Years of Promises, Zero Results Under Amirudin
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 26 January 2026, Amirudin Shari declared ’no more finger pointing’ and ordered a ’total crackdown’ on illegal waste dumping across Selangor. He said he would personally visit sites with local authorities and ’take firm action.’ He instructed the Land Office and local authorities to focus on cleanliness. This follows a similar declaration in early 2026 where the Selangor government announced intensified efforts to ’eradicate’ illegal waste dumping.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Amirudin has been Menteri Besar since July 2018. He has made similar promises about illegal dumping every single year. Each year, the announcements get bolder — ’total crackdown,’ ‘firm action,’ ’no more excuses.’ Each year, illegal dump sites proliferate. Residents in areas like Kuala Langat, Jenjarom, and Ijok continue to suffer from toxic waste, contaminated water, and respiratory problems. The gap between Amirudin’s rhetoric and Selangor’s reality is now a canyon.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: ‘I will personally go to the ground’ is what politicians say when they’ve run out of policy ideas. After 7+ years as MB, if Amirudin hasn’t fixed illegal dumping, it’s not because he hasn’t visited enough sites — it’s because the enforcement infrastructure, penalties, and political will don’t exist. Or worse, because the illegal dumping networks have political connections that protect them. ‘No more finger pointing’ is itself finger pointing — at previous finger pointers.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: In 2020, Amirudin promised to tackle illegal dumping. In 2022, he promised again. In 2024, he declared it a priority. In 2026, he’s still promising. The waste is still there. At some point, the promises themselves become pollution — filling the public discourse with noise while the actual waste piles up.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Seven years. Same problem. Same promises. New adjectives. If your enforcement agencies can’t stop illegal dumping after seven years of your leadership, the problem is the leader, not the agencies. Commission an independent audit of every illegal dump site, publish the landowner details, and explain why enforcement orders aren’t being executed. Or just make the same speech next January.

Sources: Malay Mail

item.title: Zulkifli Hasan Says Work Stress Can Make People Gay — International Community Laughs, Malaysia Cringes
item.description:
Said by: Zulkifli Hasan, Minister in PM’s Department (Religious Affairs)

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On January 26, 2026, Religious Affairs Minister Zulkifli Hasan submitted a written parliamentary reply to PAS MP Siti Zailah Mohd Yusoff about LGBT trends in Malaysia. He cited a 2017 study and listed “societal influence, sexual experiences, work stress, and other personal factors” as possible causes of “LGBT behaviour.” The “work stress” claim went immediately viral — globally. CNA, SCMP, NDTV, Reuters, Hindustan Times, LGBTQ Nation, and them.us all covered it. Malaysians flooded social media with mockery, sarcastically asking whether parliament MPs had “turned gay” since they apparently never work hard enough to experience stress. Zulkifli also disclosed that from 2022 to 2025, 135 cases related to “LGBT activities” were recorded as arrests or prosecutions — in a country where same-sex relations remain criminalised.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This isn’t just embarrassing — it’s damaging. A cabinet minister, speaking in an official parliamentary capacity, cited debunked pseudoscience to explain sexual orientation. This is the government’s official position on record in Hansard. The international coverage turned Malaysia into a global punchline while the country tries to attract foreign investment, host international summits, and position itself as a progressive Muslim-majority democracy. More importantly, the remark normalises the framing of LGBT identity as a pathology — something caused by external factors that can presumably be “fixed.” This framing underpins the continued criminalisation of same-sex relations and the use of state-funded “rehabilitation” programmes.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The 2017 study Zulkifli cited — Sulaiman et al. — is a small-scale Malaysian social study, not a peer-reviewed finding from any recognised international medical or psychological body. Every major medical and psychological organisation in the world — the WHO, APA, AMA, Royal College of Psychiatrists — has stated clearly that sexual orientation is not caused by stress, lifestyle, or environmental factors. The minister later issued a defensive statement saying people should “refer to the full written answer” — as if the problem was context. It wasn’t. The context makes it worse: it was an official, considered, pre-written reply. This wasn’t a slip of the tongue. It was deliberate.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a long, inglorious history of ministers making scientifically illiterate statements about LGBT people. In 2018, then-Deputy PM Wan Azizah said the government would not recognise LGBT rights because they were “against the law of nature.” In 2019, Religious Affairs officials promoted “conversion therapy” camps. Malaysia’s Federal Territories Islamic Department (JAWI) regularly raids private premises suspected of hosting LGBT individuals. PH in opposition criticised BN’s persecution of LGBT Malaysians; in government, PH has continued the same policies and rhetoric. Zulkifli Hasan’s appointment itself was a political concession — he represents the Malay-Muslim establishment within PH’s coalition.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You campaigned on “Malaysia Madani” — a civilised, progressive Malaysia. Your Religious Affairs Minister just told parliament that work stress turns people gay. The world laughed. Malaysia’s reputation took yet another hit. Where was Anwar? Where was the PMO correction? Where was the cabinet disavowal? Silence. Because correcting Zulkifli would upset the conservative base PH desperately needs. So the government let a pseudoscientific claim stand as Malaysia’s official parliamentary position on sexual orientation. Madani means civilised. There is nothing civilised about citing junk science in parliament to justify the criminalisation of who people love.

Sources: CNA | NDTV | SCMP | LGBTQ Nation | Hindustan Times

item.title: [BM] Bahasa Melayu dan Sejarah Wajib Merentas SEMUA Sistem Pendidikan — Pengkritik Kata Ia Menyekat Kepelbagaian
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2026-01-21

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Januari 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengumumkan bahawa Bahasa Melayu dan Sejarah akan diwajibkan merentas semua sistem pendidikan di Malaysia — termasuk sekolah vernakular (SJKC dan SJKT), sekolah antarabangsa, dan institusi swasta. Anwar berkata keperluan ini bertujuan “mengukuhkan kedudukan Bahasa Melayu sebagai bahasa kebangsaan” dan memberikan “pemahaman bersama tentang sejarah negara.” Pengkritik berhujah bahawa ini menjejaskan perlindungan perlembagaan untuk pendidikan vernakular di bawah Perkara 152(1)(a) dan menyekat kepelbagaian yang menjadikan sistem pendidikan Malaysia unik. Analisis mendalam CNA menyatakan “politik dan kesukaran” pembaharuan pendidikan Malaysia, menggambarkan bagaimana “bahasa dan agama” kekal sebagai bidang dasar paling mudah terbakar.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Kebanyakan pelajar sekolah vernakular sudah pun belajar Bahasa Melayu — ia merupakan mata pelajaran wajib di semua sekolah bantuan kerajaan. Perubahan dasar ini adalah mengenai memperluaskan mata pelajaran wajib ke aliran yang secara tradisinya menetapkan keutamaan akademik sendiri. Sementara itu, krisis pendidikan sebenar langsung tidak ditangani: skor PISA terus merosot, enrolmen pengajian tinggi hanya 42.57% berbanding purata dunia 51.98%, guru melaporkan pelajar “lebih kurang ajar, lebih gelisah, kurang ingin tahu,” kes buli melonjak 17%, dan bahagian belanjawan pendidikan telah jatuh daripada 6.8% kepada 4.3% KDNK. Menambah mata pelajaran wajib kepada kurikulum yang sudah sedia sesak tidak meningkatkan kualiti pendidikan — ia menambah beban kerja tanpa membaiki pedagogi, prasarana, atau latihan guru.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Perpaduan nasional melalui kurikulum wajib adalah idea era BN yang memakai sut era PH. Mahathir mendesak inisiatif serupa. Begitu juga Muhyiddin. Bingkaian berubah; dasar tidak. Kiraan politik jelas: Bahasa Melayu dan Sejarah wajib bermain baik dengan pangkalan pengundi Melayu-Islam menjelang pilihan raya. Merit pendidikan adalah sekunder kepada isyarat politik. Ini adalah politik identiti yang menyamar sebagai pembaharuan kurikulum. Isu sebenar bukan sama ada pelajar belajar Bahasa Melayu — ia adalah sama ada kerajaan menggunakan retorik “perpaduan” untuk menyeragamkan pendidikan sambil mengabaikan kegagalan sistemik yang benar-benar memusnahkan sistem: kadar kegagalan matematik 25%, 10,160 calon tidak hadir SPM, dan guru yang terlalu terbeban dengan 30+ pelajar setiap kelas tanpa sokongan struktur langsung.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap PM Malaysia telah menggunakan dasar bahasa dan kurikulum sebagai alat politik. Inisiatif Sekolah Wawasan Mahathir pada awal 2000-an cuba menempatkan sekolah vernakular dan kebangsaan bersama. Ia ditentang dan sebahagian besarnya gagal. Dasar PPSMI (mengajar matematik dan sains dalam Bahasa Inggeris) diperkenalkan, ditarik balik, diperkenalkan semula sebagai DLP, dan kekal sebagai bola politik. Coraknya konsisten: gunakan dasar pendidikan untuk isyarat politik, hadapi tentangan, berundur atau lemahkan, kemudian cuba lagi dengan nama berbeza. PH hanyalah kerajaan terkini yang menggunakan tuas “perpaduan nasional melalui kurikulum wajib.”

🎯 TEGURAN PH: Sistem pendidikan anda sedang runtuh — skor PISA merosot, buli melonjak, guru kewalahan, pelajar tidak terlibat dan menatal di bawah meja — dan jawapan anda adalah menambah lebih banyak mata pelajaran wajib? Baiki pengajaran dahulu. Biayai sekolah dengan betul. Tangani 33% pelajar yang gagal sekurang-kurangnya satu mata pelajaran SPM. Kemudian baru bercakap tentang penambahan kurikulum. Menambah Bahasa Melayu dan Sejarah wajib kepada sekolah yang sudah mengajar Bahasa Melayu sambil mengabaikan keruntuhan kualiti pengajaran bukan pembaharuan — ia adalah pengalihan.

Sumber: Vietnam News | CNA

item.title: Mandatory Malay and History Across ALL Education Systems — Critics Say It Stifles Diversity
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2026-01-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced that Bahasa Melayu and History would be mandatory across all education systems in Malaysia — including vernacular schools (SJKC and SJKT), international schools, and private institutions. Anwar said the requirement aims to “bolster the status of Bahasa Melayu as the national language” and provide “a common understanding of national history.” Critics argued this undermines constitutional protections for vernacular education under Article 152(1)(a) and stifles the diversity that makes Malaysia’s education system unique. CNA’s in-depth analysis noted the “politics and difficulties” of Malaysian education reform, describing how “language and religion” remain the most combustible policy areas.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Most vernacular school students already learn Bahasa Melayu — it is a compulsory subject in all government-aided schools. The policy change is about expanding mandatory subjects into streams that have traditionally set their own academic priorities. Meanwhile, the actual education crisis goes completely unaddressed: PISA scores keep declining, tertiary enrollment is only 42.57% versus the global average of 51.98%, teachers report students are “ruder, more restless, less curious,” bullying cases surged 17%, and the education budget share has dropped from 6.8% to 4.3% of GDP. Adding mandatory subjects to already overcrowded curricula does not improve education quality — it increases workload without fixing pedagogy, infrastructure, or teacher training.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: National unity through compulsory curriculum is a BN-era idea wearing a PH-era suit. Mahathir pushed similar initiatives. So did Muhyiddin. The framing changes; the policy doesn’t. The political calculus is transparent: mandatory Malay and History plays well with the Malay-Muslim base ahead of elections. The educational merit is secondary to the political signalling. This is identity politics disguised as curriculum reform. The real issue isn’t whether students learn Malay — it’s whether the government is using “unity” rhetoric to homogenise education while ignoring the systemic failures that are actually destroying the system: 25% math failure rates, 10,160 SPM no-shows, and teachers overwhelmed by 30+ students per class with zero structural support.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian PM has used language and curriculum policy as a political tool. Mahathir’s Vision Schools initiative in the early 2000s attempted to co-locate vernacular and national schools. It was resisted and largely failed. The PPSMI policy (teaching math and science in English) was introduced, reversed, reintroduced as DLP, and remains a political football. The pattern is consistent: use education policy for political signalling, face backlash, retreat or water down, then try again under a different name. PH is simply the latest government to reach for the “national unity through mandatory curriculum” lever.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your education system is in freefall — declining PISA scores, surging bullying, overwhelmed teachers, students disengaged and scrolling under desks — and your response is to add more mandatory subjects? Fix the teaching first. Fund the schools properly. Address the 33% of students failing at least one SPM subject. Then talk about curriculum additions. Adding mandatory Malay and History to schools that already teach Malay while ignoring the collapse in teaching quality is not reform — it is deflection. Students don’t need more subjects. They need better education.

Sources: Vietnam News | CNA

item.title: [BM] Wartawan FMT Rex Tan Ditangkap Di Bawah Akta Hasutan Kerana Bertanya 'Soalan Sensitif'
item.description:
Said by: Royal Malaysia Police

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-17

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Januari 2026, wartawan Free Malaysia Today Rex Tan ditangkap pada waktu subuh di bawah Akta Hasutan 1948 era penjajah kerana bertanya soalan di perdebatan awam yang mengaitkan krisis Gaza dengan layanan terhadap etnik Cina di Malaysia. Polis tiba di rumah Tan awal pagi Sabtu — lima hari selepas perdebatan — dan menangkapnya di bawah Seksyen 4(1) Akta Hasutan. Soalan itu provokatif tetapi jelas berada dalam sempadan wacana awam yang sah. SCMP melaporkan tangkapan itu “menimbulkan keraguan terhadap rekod pembaharuan Anwar.” Pertubuhan kebebasan akhbar antarabangsa, termasuk RSF dan CPJ, mengecam tangkapan tersebut. Lebih 90 tangkapan berkaitan hasutan telah berlaku sejak Anwar mengambil alih kuasa pada akhir 2022.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Seorang wartawan ditangkap kerana bertanya soalan — bukan kerana hasutan, bukan kerana keganasan, bukan kerana fitnah. Kerana soalan di perdebatan awam. Jika ini adalah ambang untuk hasutan, maka setiap rakyat Malaysia yang pernah bertanya soalan yang tidak selesa tentang kaum, agama, atau dasar adalah penjenayah yang berpotensi. Tangkapan waktu subuh adalah taktik ugutan yang disengajakan — polis boleh memanggil Tan ke balai pada waktu pejabat. Sebaliknya, mereka memilih pendekatan yang paling memaksa secara psikologi. Ini bukan penguatkuasaan undang-undang. Ini adalah mesej kepada setiap wartawan lain: tapis sendiri atau hadapi nasib yang sama. Kedudukan kebebasan akhbar Malaysia telah terbantut di bawah Anwar walaupun janji pembaharuan.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Anwar berjanji untuk memansuhkan Akta Hasutan. Dalam manifesto PRU14, PH berjanji pemansuhan. Dalam PRU15, PH mengesahkan semula komitmen tersebut. Sebaliknya, kerajaan Anwar telah memperluaskan penggunaannya — lebih 90 tangkapan hasutan sejak berkuasa. Di bawah Najib — PM yang PH gulingkan sebahagiannya kerana autoritarianismenya — terdapat lebih sedikit pendakwaan hasutan setahun. Anwar menggunakan Akta Hasutan lebih agresif daripada orang yang digantikannya. Akta Hasutan adalah undang-undang era penjajah dari 1948, digubal oleh British untuk menyekat gerakan kemerdekaan. Anwar sendiri didakwa di bawahnya pada 2014. Beliau memanggilnya “undang-undang drakonian yang digunakan untuk membungkam perbezaan pendapat.” Beliau menghabiskan bertahun-tahun di penjara sebagai mangsa pendakwaan bermotif politik. Beliau tahu betul bagaimana rasanya tangkapan waktu subuh — dan beliau tetap melakukannya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: PH bukan sahaja mengekalkan setiap undang-undang penindas yang dijanjikan untuk dimansuhkan — ia menggunakannya lebih kerap dan terhadap sasaran yang lebih luas. Pendakwaan AKM, penguatkuasaan AKTA 829, tangkapan Akta Hasutan, penahanan SOSMA — kerajaan pembaharuan telah menjadi kerajaan penguatkuasaan. Di bawah BN, hasutan digunakan terhadap ahli politik pembangkang dan aktivis. Di bawah PH, ia digunakan terhadap wartawan yang bertanya soalan. Sasaran telah berkembang manakala alat kekal sama. Aktivis Heidy Quah disiasat. Pengulas media sosial telah ditangkap. Coraknya tidak dapat disangkal.

🎯 TEGURAN PH: Anda menangkap seorang wartawan pada waktu subuh kerana bertanya soalan. Anda berjanji untuk memansuhkan undang-undang yang digunakan untuk menangkapnya. Sebaliknya, anda telah menggunakannya lebih 90 kali — lebih daripada Najib. Anwar, anda adalah mangsa Akta Hasutan. Anda memanggilnya drakonian. Anda berkata ia patut dimansuhkan. Anda membina kerjaya politik atas ketidakadilan pendakwaan bermotif politik. Kini polis anda menangkap wartawan sebelum matahari terbit kerana bertanya soalan di perdebatan. Mansuhkan Akta Hasutan atau akui bahawa Reformasi hanyalah slogan yang anda gunakan untuk berkuasa — bukan prinsip yang anda berhasrat untuk mentadbir.

Sumber: SCMP

item.title: FMT Journalist Rex Tan Arrested Under Sedition Act for Asking a 'Sensitive Question'
item.description:
Said by: Royal Malaysia Police

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Free Malaysia Today journalist Rex Tan was arrested at dawn under the colonial-era Sedition Act 1948 for asking a question at a public debate that linked the Gaza crisis to the treatment of ethnic Chinese in Malaysia. Police arrived at Tan’s home early on a Saturday morning — five days after the debate — and arrested him under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act. The question was provocative but fell squarely within the bounds of legitimate public discourse. SCMP reported the arrest “casts doubts on Anwar’s reform record.” International press freedom organisations, including RSF and CPJ, condemned the arrest. Over 90 sedition-related arrests have occurred since Anwar took power in late 2022.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A journalist was arrested for asking a question — not for incitement, not for violence, not for defamation. For a question at a public debate. If this is the threshold for sedition, then every Malaysian who has ever asked an uncomfortable question about race, religion, or policy is a potential criminal. The dawn arrest is a deliberate intimidation tactic — police could have summoned Tan to a station during business hours. Instead, they chose the most psychologically coercive approach. This is not law enforcement. It is a message to every other journalist: self-censor or face the same fate. Malaysia’s press freedom ranking has stagnated under Anwar despite reform promises.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar promised to repeal the Sedition Act. In the GE14 manifesto, PH pledged abolition. In GE15, PH reaffirmed the commitment. Instead, the Anwar government has expanded its use — over 90 sedition arrests since taking power. Under Najib — the PM PH overthrew partly because of his authoritarianism — there were fewer sedition prosecutions per year. Anwar is using the Sedition Act more aggressively than the man he replaced. The Sedition Act is a colonial-era law from 1948, enacted by the British to suppress the independence movement. Anwar himself was charged under it in 2014. He called it “a draconian law used to silence dissent.” He spent years in prison as a victim of politically motivated prosecution. He knows exactly what dawn arrests feel like — and he is doing it anyway.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: PH has not only kept every repressive law it promised to repeal — it has used them more frequently and against a wider range of targets. CMA prosecutions, ONSA enforcement, Sedition Act arrests, SOSMA detentions — the reform government has become the enforcement government. Under BN, sedition was used against opposition politicians and activists. Under PH, it is used against journalists asking questions. The target has expanded while the tool remains the same. Activist Heidy Quah was investigated. Social media commentators have been arrested. The pattern is unmistakable.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You arrested a journalist at dawn for asking a question. You promised to repeal the law used to arrest him. Instead, you have used it over 90 times — more than Najib. Anwar, you were a victim of the Sedition Act. You called it draconian. You said it should be abolished. You built a political career on the injustice of politically motivated prosecution. Now your police arrest journalists before sunrise for asking questions at debates. Repeal the Sedition Act or admit that Reformasi was just a slogan you used to get into power — not a principle you intend to govern by.

Sources: SCMP

item.title: [BM] Anwar Menyeru 'Kesabaran dan Kebijaksanaan' Selepas Setiap Krisis — Tetapi Tidak Pernah Akauntabiliti
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Merentas setiap krisis besar dari 2024 hingga 2026, respons Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengikut templat yang sama: akui isu, seru “kesabaran dan kebijaksanaan,” ketepikan pengkritik, dan teruskan tanpa perubahan struktur. Apabila 47 pertuduhan Zahid digugurkan: “Saya tidak campur tangan dalam keputusan PG.” Apabila Shamsul Iskandar ditangkap mengeluarkan surat sokongan: “Surat itu kabur” — teguran sahaja. Apabila tawaran tahanan rumah Najib dibangkitkan: “Bertindak balas dengan kesabaran dan kebijaksanaan.” Apabila Bersih mengkritik SPRM: “Tidak adil” — suruh mereka tunggu. Apabila harga diesel naik: “Kita akan dikritik dengan fitnah dan pembohongan.” Apabila kedai KK Mart dibom api: “Jangan jadi pendakwa atau hakim.” Apabila PH dihapuskan di Sabah: “Kekalahan tidak akan hentikan pelaksanaan.” Apabila Rafizi menuduhnya serangan media sosial: memanggilnya “gangguan.”

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Menyeru ketenangan bukan pentadbiran. Ia adalah pengelakan konflik yang menyamar sebagai kenegarawanan. Dalam setiap kes, isu struktur yang mendasari — kebebasan pendakwaan, naungan, percanggahan gabungan, akauntabiliti institusi — kekal tidak ditangani. Skrip tidak pernah berubah kerana strategi tidak pernah berubah: serap pukulan, keluarkan kata-kata indah, tunggu kitaran berita bergerak, dan elak membuat sebarang keputusan yang mungkin mengganggu mana-mana puak dalam gabungan. PM yang bertindak balas terhadap setiap krisis dengan rayuan emosi yang sama bukan mengurus krisis — beliau menangguhkan hari perhitungan.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: “Kesabaran dan kebijaksanaan” bukan falsafah pentadbiran — ia adalah teknik pengalihan. Apabila 47 pertuduhan rasuah TPM anda digugurkan dan respons anda adalah “Saya tidak campur tangan,” anda bukan menghormati kebebasan institusi — anda bersembunyi di sebaliknya. Apabila kedai dibom api dan anda beritahu orang ramai jangan jadi “pendakwa atau hakim,” anda bukan meredakan ketegangan — anda gagal melindungi rakyat. Apabila Bersih — gerakan yang membantu meletakkan anda berkuasa — memanggil SPRM alat politik dan anda memanggilnya “tidak adil,” anda bukan mempertahankan institusi — anda mempertahankan diri sendiri. Strategi “kesabaran dan kebijaksanaan” berkesan kerana ia kedengaran munasabah. Tetapi kemunasabahan bukan sama dengan akauntabiliti.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Strategi “seru ketenangan” bukan unik kepada Anwar — ia adalah respons lalai setiap PM Malaysia menghadapi penelitian yang tidak selesa. Najib menyeru “kesabaran” semasa 1MDB. Muhyiddin menyeru “perpaduan” semasa Langkah Sheraton. Ismail Sabri menyeru “kestabilan” semasa kerajaan pintu belakangnya. Bahasa boleh ditukar ganti kerana tujuannya sama: beli masa, tidak ubah apa-apa. Apa yang menjadikan versi Anwar lebih menyinggung ialah beliau membina seluruh kerjayanya berjanji untuk menjadi berbeza. Beliau yang berkata akauntabiliti harus segera, telus, dan berstruktur. Berkuasa, beliau meraih skrip yang sama seperti setiap PM sebelumnya.

🎯 TEGURAN PH: Berhenti mengurus perasaan dan mula membaiki sistem. Setiap krisis yang anda tanggapi dengan “kesabaran dan kebijaksanaan” mempunyai punca yang sama: institusi yang menjawab kepada politik dan bukan kepada rakyat. Pisahkan PG dan PP. Tubuhkan IPCMC. Beri SPRM kemerdekaan sebenar. Baharui Lembaga Pengampunan. Ini adalah perubahan struktur yang anda janjikan. Sebaliknya, anda tawarkan kata-kata indah — kerana perubahan struktur akan mengekang kuasa anda sendiri. PM yang mengurus perasaan dan bukan membaiki sistem bukan mentadbir. Beliau menangguhkan hari perhitungan — dan bil tiba pada PRU16.

Sumber: FMT | Reuters

item.title: Anwar Calls for 'Patience and Wisdom' After Every Crisis — But Never Accountability
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Across every major crisis from 2024 to 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s response has followed the same template: acknowledge the issue, call for “patience and wisdom,” dismiss critics, and move on without structural change. When Zahid’s 47 charges were dropped: “I don’t interfere in AG decisions.” When Shamsul Iskandar was caught issuing support letters: “The letters were vague” — reprimand only. When Najib’s house arrest bid was raised: “Respond with patience and wisdom.” When Bersih criticised MACC: “Unfair” — told them to wait. When diesel prices hiked: “We will be criticised with slander and lies.” When KK Mart stores were firebombed: “Don’t be prosecutor or judge.” When PH was wiped out in Sabah: “Defeat won’t stop delivery.” When Rafizi accused him of social media attacks: called it a “distraction.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Calling for calm is not governance. It is conflict avoidance dressed as statesmanship. In every single case, the underlying structural issue — prosecutorial independence, patronage, coalition contradictions, institutional accountability — remains unaddressed. The script never changes because the strategy never changes: absorb the blow, issue platitudes, wait for the news cycle to move on, and avoid making any decision that might upset any faction of the coalition. A PM who responds to every crisis with the same emotional appeal is not managing crises — he is postponing reckonings.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: “Patience and wisdom” is not a governance philosophy — it is a deflection technique. When your DPM’s 47 corruption charges get dropped and your response is “I don’t interfere,” you are not respecting institutional independence — you are hiding behind it. When stores are firebombed and you tell the public not to be “prosecutor or judge,” you are not calming tensions — you are failing to protect citizens. When Bersih — the movement that helped put you in power — calls MACC a political tool and you call it “unfair,” you are not defending institutions — you are defending yourself. The “patience and wisdom” playbook works because it sounds reasonable. But reasonableness is not the same as accountability.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The “call for calm” strategy is not unique to Anwar — it is the default response of every Malaysian PM facing uncomfortable scrutiny. Najib called for “patience” during 1MDB. Muhyiddin called for “unity” during the Sheraton Move. Ismail Sabri called for “stability” during his backdoor government.
