Kemudian ia mengumumkan projek rel RM50 bilion lagi dan mendakwa ia “menyelesaikan trafik.” Ini bukan dasar pengangkutan — ia adalah program perbelanjaan prasarana yang menguntungkan syarikat pembinaan dan ahli politik yang memotong reben, bukan penumpang yang perlu pergi kerja. LRT3 telah ditangguhkan berulang kali. Kos MRT3 terus meningkat. Unjuran penumpang ECRL adalah fantasi. KRI memberitahu kerajaan pendekatan itu tidak berkesan, dan kerajaan membalas dengan mengumumkan lebih banyak perkara yang sama.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia telah membina prasarana rel selama berdekad — KTM Komuter, LRT, Monorel, MRT1, MRT2 — namun kadar pemilikan kereta terus meningkat dan kesesakan trafik terus memburuk. Setiap projek baru diumumkan dengan janji yang sama: yang ini akhirnya akan menyelesaikan trafik. Tidak ada yang berjaya. Sebabnya bersifat struktur: perancangan bandar Malaysia berpusatkan kereta mengikut reka bentuk. Tanpa mengubah reka bentuk — menghadkan tempat letak kereta, menaikkan kos bahan api, memperbaiki kebolehjalanan, membina rangkaian bas — menambah laluan rel adalah seperti membina baldi lebih besar manakala paip terus mengalir.

🎯 TEGURAN PH: Badan pemikir yang dibiayai kerajaan anda sendiri berkata rel sahaja tidak menyelesaikan trafik. Namun dasar pengangkutan anda adalah: bina lebih banyak rel. Baharui dasar tempat letak kereta. Baharui penetapan harga bahan api. Biayai rangkaian bas. Baiki prasarana pejalan kaki. Ini adalah perubahan yang membosankan dan sukar dari segi politik yang sebenarnya akan menjadikan pengangkutan awam berfungsi. Tetapi ia tidak menghasilkan peluang bergambar memotong reben, jadi ia tidak berlaku. Berhenti membina monumen mahal untuk keangkuhan politik dan mula membina SISTEM pengangkutan.

Sumber: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: Public Transport 'Functional But Fragile' — One Disruption and Everyone Drives
item.description:
Said by: Khazanah Research Institute (KRI)

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-31

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: A Khazanah Research Institute (KRI) study published in late December 2025 found that Greater KL’s public transport system is “functional but fragile” — minor disruptions cause commuters to abandon it entirely for private vehicles. KRI concluded that rail was “never meant to fix Malaysia’s traffic problem.” The study found that fuel remains artificially cheap, parking is abundant and affordable, walking environments are fragmented and hostile, and last-mile connectivity is inadequate. FMT’s analysis argued it is “an unfinished system, not infrastructure, that fails Malaysian commuters.” Yet the government continues announcing mega rail projects — MRT3, LRT3, ECRL — as if building more tracks alone will solve mobility.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Successive governments, including PH, keep announcing multi-billion ringgit rail projects as if infrastructure alone solves mobility. KRI’s own study — funded by the government’s own sovereign wealth fund — says it doesn’t. Without simultaneously reforming urban planning, parking policy, fuel pricing, and last-mile connectivity, every new rail line is just an expensive band-aid on a systemic failure. Billions are spent on tracks while the system remains one disruption away from collapse. Commuters who try public transport once and get stranded never come back.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government subsidises fuel (keeping driving artificially cheap), allows abundant free parking in urban areas (removing the cost incentive to use public transport), neglects pedestrian infrastructure (making it dangerous to walk to stations), and fails to invest in last-mile solutions (leaving commuters stranded between stations and destinations). Then it announces another RM50 billion rail project and claims it is “solving traffic.” This is not a transport policy — it is an infrastructure spending programme that benefits construction companies and politicians who cut ribbons, not commuters who need to get to work. LRT3 has been delayed repeatedly. MRT3 costs keep escalating. The ECRL’s ridership projections are fantasy. KRI told the government the approach doesn’t work, and the government responded by announcing more of the same.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has been building rail infrastructure for decades — KTM Komuter, LRT, Monorail, MRT1, MRT2 — yet car ownership rates keep climbing and traffic congestion keeps worsening. Each new project is announced with the same promise: this one will finally solve traffic. None has. The reason is structural: Malaysia’s urban planning is car-centric by design. Without changing the design — restricting parking, raising fuel costs, improving walkability, building bus networks — adding rail lines is like building a bigger bucket while the tap keeps running.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own government-funded think tank says rail alone doesn’t solve traffic. Yet your transport policy is: build more rail. Reform parking policy. Reform fuel pricing. Fund bus networks. Fix pedestrian infrastructure. These are the boring, politically difficult changes that would actually make public transport work. But they don’t generate ribbon-cutting photo ops, so they don’t happen. Stop building expensive monuments to political vanity and start building a transport SYSTEM.

Sources: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Tawaran Tahanan Rumah Najib Ditolak — Mahkamah Putuskan Adendum Diraja Tidak Sah
item.description:
Said by: High Court Judge Alice Loke / Najib Razak

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-22

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 22 Disember 2025, Mahkamah Tinggi KL memutuskan bahawa adendum diraja yang membenarkan bekas PM Najib Razak yang disabitkan kesalahan menjalani baki hukuman 1MDB di bawah tahanan rumah adalah tidak sah. Hakim Alice Loke berkata budi bicara Yang di-Pertuan Agong mesti dilaksanakan “demi kepentingan awam” dan mendapati adendum itu tidak mempunyai asas undang-undang yang kukuh. Al Jazeera dan Reuters kedua-duanya melaporkan keputusan tersebut. Sultan Selangor sebelum ini memberi amaran bahawa pengampunan tidak boleh “sewenang-wenangnya untuk pihak tertentu.” Kerajaan Anwar sudah pun memotong separuh hukuman Najib melalui Lembaga Pengampunan pada Februari 2024 — tawaran tahanan rumah adalah langkah seterusnya dalam corak kelonggaran yang semakin meningkat bagi seorang kleptokrat yang disabitkan kesalahan yang kini partinya menjadi sandaran Anwar.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Adendum tahanan rumah secara meluas dilihat sebagai perjanjian belakang tabir untuk mengekalkan UMNO dalam gabungan. Hukuman Najib mula-mula dipotong separuh (dari 12 ke 6 tahun), kemudian adendum tahanan rumah muncul sebagai cara untuk mengeluarkannya daripada penjara sepenuhnya sambil secara teknikal masih “menjalani” hukumannya. Penolakan mahkamah mendedahkan jentera politik di sebaliknya — ini bukan pengampunan berdasarkan merit, tetapi kelonggaran yang direka untuk pengurusan gabungan. Badan kehakiman mempertahankan garis yang eksekutif tidak mahu pertahankan. Keputusan Hakim Alice Loke adalah saat jarang kemerdekaan institusi dalam sistem di mana setiap semakan lain telah gagal.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Anwar membina kerjaya politiknya menentang Najib. Beliau menghabiskan bertahun-tahun menyebut 1MDB sebagai skandal kewangan terbesar dalam sejarah Malaysia. Beliau menuntut akauntabiliti. Kemudian beliau mengambil kuasa, memerlukan undi UMNO, dan mula secara sistematik mengurangkan akibat bagi orang yang mencuri berbilion. Pertama, Lembaga Pengampunan memotong separuh hukuman. Kemudian, adendum tahanan rumah muncul. Jika mahkamah tidak campur tangan, Najib — yang disabitkan mencuri daripada rakyat Malaysia — akan tinggal di sebuah rumah agam sementara negara masih berhutang RM50 bilion daripada skandalnya. Orang yang menyebabkan hutang dapat rumah agam. Rakyat yang membayar hutang dapat kenaikan tol dan kenaikan harga diesel. Inilah Malaysia Madani Anwar.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah bermasalah dengan pengampunan dan pengurangan hukuman untuk banduan yang mempunyai hubungan politik. Anwar sendiri mendapat manfaat daripada pengampunan diraja selepas PRU14 — yang penyokongnya berhujah adalah wajar memandangkan sifat bermotif politik dakwaan terhadapnya. Perbezaan dengan Najib ialah sabitan kesalahannya adalah untuk jenayah kewangan sebenar dengan kerugian berbilion yang didokumenkan. Memotong separuh hukumannya dan mencuba tahanan rumah menetapkan preseden: jika anda curi cukup banyak dan parti anda cukup berguna, akibatnya boleh dirunding. Amaran Sultan Selangor — bahawa pengampunan tidak boleh “sewenang-wenangnya untuk pihak tertentu” — adalah teguran diraja yang jarang berlaku yang tidak diendahkan oleh kerajaan.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Badan kehakiman menyelamatkan kamu daripada diri sendiri. Jika mahkamah tidak memutuskan adendum itu tidak sah, kamu akan menempatkan seorang kleptokrat yang disabitkan kesalahan di rumah agam sementara rakyat Malaysia membayar RM50 bilion untuk jenayahnya. Kamu sudah potong separuh hukumannya. Kamu sudah gugurkan 47 pertuduhan TPMnya. Berapa banyak lagi kelonggaran yang UMNO perlukan sebelum kamu akui Reformasi sudah mati dan kelangsungan gabungan sahaja yang penting? Mahkamah mempertahankan garis. Adakah kamu?

Sumber: Al Jazeera | Reuters

item.title: Najib House Arrest Bid Rejected — Court Rules Royal Addendum Invalid
item.description:
Said by: High Court Judge Alice Loke / Najib Razak

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-22

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 22 December 2025, the KL High Court ruled that the royal addendum allowing convicted former PM Najib Razak to serve his remaining 1MDB sentence under house arrest was invalid. Judge Alice Loke said the King’s discretion must be exercised “for the public good” and found the addendum lacked proper legal basis. Al Jazeera and Reuters both reported the ruling. The Selangor Sultan had earlier warned that pardons should not be “arbitrary for certain parties.” Anwar’s government had already halved Najib’s sentence via the Pardons Board in February 2024 — the house arrest bid was the next step in an escalating pattern of leniency for a convicted kleptocrat whose party Anwar now depends on.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The house arrest addendum was widely seen as a backroom deal to keep UMNO in the coalition. Najib’s sentence was first halved (from 12 to 6 years), then the house arrest addendum emerged as a way to get him out of prison entirely while technically still “serving” his sentence. The court’s rejection exposed the political machinery behind it — this was not clemency based on merit, but leniency engineered for coalition management. The judiciary held the line that the executive would not. Judge Alice Loke’s ruling was a rare moment of institutional independence in a system where every other check has failed.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar built his political career opposing Najib. He spent years calling 1MDB the greatest financial scandal in Malaysian history. He demanded accountability. Then he took power, needed UMNO’s votes, and began systematically reducing the consequences for the man who stole billions. First, the Pardons Board halved the sentence. Then, the house arrest addendum appeared. If the court hadn’t intervened, Najib — convicted of stealing from the Malaysian people — would be living in a mansion while the country still owes RM50 billion from his scandal. The man who caused the debt gets a mansion. The rakyat who pays the debt gets toll hikes and diesel price increases. This is Anwar’s Malaysia Madani.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a troubled history with pardons and sentence reductions for politically connected convicts. Anwar himself benefited from a royal pardon after GE14 — which his supporters argued was justified given the politically motivated nature of his conviction. The difference with Najib is that his conviction was for an actual financial crime with billions in documented losses. Halving his sentence and attempting house arrest sets a precedent: if you steal enough and your party is useful enough, the consequences are negotiable. The Selangor Sultan’s warning — that pardons should not be “arbitrary for certain parties” — was a rare royal rebuke that went unheeded by the government.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The judiciary saved you from yourself. If the court hadn’t ruled the addendum invalid, you would have put a convicted kleptocrat in a mansion while Malaysians pay RM50 billion for his crimes. You already halved his sentence. You already dropped his DPM’s 47 charges. How much more leniency does UMNO need before you admit that Reformasi is dead and coalition survival is all that matters? The court held the line. Will you?

Sources: Al Jazeera | Reuters

item.title: [BM] Janji Mansuh Tol Dimungkiri — Kerajaan Bayar RM591J Untuk BEKU Kenaikan Sahaja
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: PH berjanji untuk memansuhkan tol lebuhraya dalam kedua-dua PRU14 dan PRU15. Sebaliknya, pada Disember 2025, kerajaan mengumumkan ia akan membelanjakan RM591 juta semata-mata untuk menghalang kenaikan tol di 10 lebuhraya pada 2026 — membayar syarikat konsesi dengan wang awam supaya tidak menaikkan harga. Menteri Kerja Raya mengakui memansuhkan tol sepenuhnya akan menelan belanja “berbilion.” Gerakan mengejek janji yang dimungkiri. FMT bertanya sama ada Anwar akan “mengulangi ikrar PH untuk memansuhkan tol” menjelang PRU16. Pemandu Malaysia terus membayar tol DAN cukai mereka kini mensubsidi pembekuan itu. Tempoh bebas tol perayaan juga dibatalkan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Pemansuhan tol adalah salah satu janji manifesto PH yang paling konkrit dan boleh diukur — cukup spesifik sehingga pengundi boleh mengesahkan penyampaian dengan mudah. Realitinya: bukan sahaja tol tidak dimansuhkan, kerajaan membayar syarikat konsesi lebuhraya ratusan juta setiap tahun semata-mata untuk membekukan kenaikan kadar. RM591J yang dibelanjakan untuk pembekuan boleh membiayai penambahbaikan infrastruktur sebenar. Sebaliknya, ia mengalir kepada pemegang saham konsesi — syarikat yang mempunyai hubungan politik yang mengaut keuntungan daripada orang awam yang terperangkap.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Pengakuan Menteri Kerja Raya bahawa pemansuhan akan menelan belanja “berbilion” adalah pengakuan bahawa PH membuat janji itu dengan mengetahui ia tidak boleh ditunaikan. Kamu tidak boleh berkempen atas pemansuhan tol dua kali, memenangi kerajaan dua kali, dan kemudian kata ia terlalu mahal. Sama ada kamu tahu kosnya sebelum berjanji (dalam kes itu kamu berbohong kepada pengundi) atau kamu tidak ambil peduli untuk mengiranya (dalam kes itu kamu tidak cekap). Sistem tol mewakili cukai regresif terhadap mobiliti — pekerja berpendapatan rendah yang berulang-alik jarak jauh ke kilang membayar bahagian pendapatan yang tidak seimbang untuk tol. Jawapan PH terhadap ketidaksamaan ini: bayar konsesi dengan wang cukai untuk mengekalkan status quo.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Janji pemansuhan tol telah dibuat oleh setiap gabungan pembangkang sejak awal 2000-an. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) juga tidak memansuhkan tol — walaupun 22 bulan berkuasa. Perjanjian konsesi, yang banyak dianugerahkan di bawah pentadbiran terdahulu melalui proses yang dipersoalkan, distrukturkan untuk menjamin keuntungan swasta dengan belanja awam. Tiada kerajaan yang mempunyai kehendak politik untuk berunding semula atau membeli balik konsesi.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kamu berjanji untuk memansuhkan tol. Dua kali. Dalam dua pilihan raya umum. Kamu kini sudah berkuasa lebih tiga tahun. Tol masih ada. Kamu membelanjakan RM591J wang awam untuk MEMBEKUKAN mereka — bukan memansuhkan, membekukan. RM591J itu pergi kepada pemegang saham konsesi, bukan kepada rakyat. Berhenti membuat janji yang kamu tahu tidak boleh ditepati. Jika pemansuhan tol tidak mampu ditanggung, katakan secara jujur — dan terangkan pelan alternatif kamu. Atau lebih baik lagi, rundingan semula perjanjian konsesi yang mengunci Malaysia untuk membayar syarikat swasta selama-lamanya.

Sumber: The Edge | FMT

item.title: Toll Abolition Promise Broken — Govt Pays RM591M to FREEZE Hikes Instead
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: PH promised to abolish highway tolls in both GE14 and GE15. Instead, in December 2025, the government announced it would spend RM591 million just to prevent toll increases on 10 highways in 2026 — paying concessionaires with public money to not raise prices. The Works Minister admitted that fully abolishing tolls would cost “billions.” Gerakan mocked the broken promise. FMT asked whether Anwar would “repeat PH’s pledge to abolish tolls” ahead of GE16. Malaysian drivers continue paying tolls AND their taxes now subsidise the freeze. Festive toll-free periods were also cancelled.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Toll abolition was one of PH’s most concrete, measurable manifesto promises — specific enough that voters could easily verify delivery. The reality: not only were tolls not abolished, the government is paying highway concessionaires hundreds of millions annually just to freeze rate hikes. The RM591M spent on freezing could have funded actual infrastructure improvements. Instead, it flows to concessionaire shareholders — politically connected companies that profit from a captive public. This is the worst possible outcome: broken promise, continued tolls, AND public money transferred to private companies.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The Works Minister’s admission that abolition would cost “billions” is an admission that PH made the promise knowing it was undeliverable. You can’t campaign on toll abolition twice, win government twice, and then say it costs too much. Either you knew the cost before promising (in which case you lied to voters) or you didn’t bother calculating it (in which case you’re incompetent). The toll system represents a regressive tax on mobility — lower-income workers who commute long distances to factory jobs pay a disproportionate share of their income on tolls. PH’s response to this inequality: pay concessionaires with tax money to maintain the status quo.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The toll abolition promise has been made by every opposition coalition since the early 2000s. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) didn’t abolish tolls either — despite 22 months in government. The concession agreements, many awarded under previous administrations through questionable processes, are structured to guarantee private profits at public expense. No government has had the political will to renegotiate or buy back concessions. The concessionaires know this, which is why they keep raising toll rates and governments keep paying them not to.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You promised to abolish tolls. Twice. In two general elections. You’ve now been in power for over three years. Tolls are still there. You’re spending RM591M of public money to FREEZE them — not abolish them, freeze them. That RM591M goes to concessionaire shareholders, not to the rakyat. Stop making promises you know you can’t keep. If toll abolition is unaffordable, say so honestly — and explain your alternative plan. Or better yet, renegotiate the concession agreements that lock Malaysia into paying private companies forever. You won’t, because the concessionaires are politically connected. So the rakyat pays twice: once at the toll booth, once through taxes.

Sources: The Edge | FMT

item.title: [BM] Anwar Kata 'Masih Jauh' Soal Pilihan Raya Pada Disember 2025 — Kini Sedia PRU16 Kilat Untuk 2026
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 17 Disember 2025, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu editor dan wartawan kanan di taklimat di Seri Perdana bahawa beliau menolak sebarang kemungkinan pilihan raya umum kilat pada 2026. “Pilihan raya masih jauh lagi. Saya tidak terfikir tentangnya,” katanya. “Saya menumpukan pada bagaimana kita terus melaksanakan pembaharuan dan meringankan masalah rakyat mengenai kos sara hidup yang lebih tinggi.”

Menjelang Februari 2026, kurang daripada dua bulan kemudian, spekulasi semakin meningkat bahawa PRU16 boleh dipanggil seawal Julai 2026. CNA melaporkan orang dalam parti dan penjawat awam mengesahkan persediaan sedang berjalan. Reuters melaporkan pada Mac 2026 bahawa rakan gabungan sudah mengatur kedudukan untuk pilihan raya kilat.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Ini adalah PM yang berbohong kepada media tentang garis masa pilihan rayanya. Pada Disember, beliau berkata pilihan raya “masih jauh lagi” dan beliau langsung tidak terfikir tentangnya. Menjelang Februari, jentera partinya sendiri sedang bersiap sedia untuk pilihan raya kilat. Mandat semasa tidak tamat sehingga Februari 2028 — memanggil pilihan raya hampir dua tahun lebih awal adalah keputusan strategik besar yang tidak berlaku dalam sekelip mata.

Anwar sama ada berbohong kepada media pada Disember, atau beliau membuat perubahan strategik radikal dalam kurang dua bulan. Kedua-dua penjelasan tidak memberi keyakinan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

“Saya tidak terfikir tentangnya.” Itulah yang PM kata pada Disember. PM yang sama yang merupakan pengerusi partinya, ketua gabungannya, dan ahli politik kerjaya yang memikirkan tentang pilihan raya setiap hari selama 30 tahun.

Sudah tentu beliau sedang memikirkannya. Pilihan raya Sabah pada November 2025 — di mana PH dihancurkan — sudah memaksa pertimbangan semula strategik. Kekacauan pilihan raya parti PKR, pemergian Rafizi, dan rombakan kabinet semuanya adalah langkah yang hanya masuk akal jika kamu sedang mengatur kedudukan untuk pilihan raya umum awal.

Anwar memberitahu wartawan apa yang mereka mahu dengar — “tumpuan pada pembaharuan, tumpuan pada kos sara hidup” — sementara operasi politiknya sudah mengira unjuran kerusi dan aritmetik gabungan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Setiap PM Malaysia yang memanggil pilihan raya kilat telah menafikan merancangnya sehingga ke saat pengumuman. Najib menafikan merancang PRU14 sehingga beliau membubarkan Parlimen. Muhyiddin menafikan merancang pilihan raya negeri Melaka dan Johor sehingga ia dipanggil. Ia tradisi politik Malaysia: berbohong tentang masa pilihan raya, kemudian panggil pilihan raya apabila ia menguntungkan secara strategik.

Anwar melakukan tepat apa yang dikritiknya tentang pendahulunya.

🎯 SERUAN PH

Kamu beritahu media pada Disember bahawa pilihan raya “masih jauh lagi.” Dua bulan kemudian, gabungan kamu bersiap sedia untuk pilihan raya kilat. Sama ada kamu berbohong pada Disember atau kamu membuat keputusan demokratik besar secara tergesa-gesa. Kedua-duanya tidak boleh diterima untuk PM yang mendakwa berbeza daripada pendahulunya.

Sumber: The Star | Straits Times | CNA | Reuters

item.title: [BM] Pelantikan Hannah Yeoh Cetuskan Tentangan Berasaskan Kaum — PAS Dan UMNO Sama-Sama Bangkang
item.description:
Said by: Takiyuddin Hassan, PAS Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Disember 2025, Anwar melantik Hannah Yeoh sebagai Menteri Wilayah Persekutuan dalam rombakan kabinetnya. Setiausaha Agung PAS Takiyuddin Hassan segera membantah — bukan terhadap kelayakan Yeoh, tetapi terhadap bangsanya. Beliau berhujah pelantikan itu akan “mengukuhkan pengaruh DAP” di kawasan bandar dan merugikan Melayu. Kumpulan Melayu sayap kanan memberi amaran bahawa DAP kini “menguasai tanah pegangan Melayu” melalui portfolio Wilayah Persekutuan, yang menyelia KL, Putrajaya, dan Labuan. Malah pemimpin UMNO secara senyap menyuarakan kebimbangan. Anwar membalas: “Menolak seseorang kerana warna kulit dan bangsanya, ini memalukan pada zaman ini.” Reaksi media sosial terpolarisasi.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Tentangan ini mendedahkan betapa normalnya pengawalan etno-agama kekal dalam politik Malaysia — walaupun di bawah kerajaan yang kononnya reformis. Seorang menteri dinilai bukan berdasarkan kecekapan, rekod prestasi, atau visi dasar, tetapi berdasarkan etnik. Inilah perkauman struktur yang PH dipilih untuk dibongkar. Sebaliknya, parti yang melantik Yeoh pada masa sama menurunkan taraf portfolio DAP lain dalam rombakan yang sama untuk mengisyaratkan “kekuatan Melayu” kepada pengundi.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Percanggahan ini bersifat struktur: Anwar mahu kelihatan pelbagai kaum sambil menguruskan gabungan yang memerlukan optik Melayu-dahulu. Beliau melantik seorang wanita Malaysia Cina ke portfolio terkemuka, kemudian mengimbanginya dengan menurunkan taraf DAP di tempat lain dan menaikkan menteri UMNO. Penganalisis Azmi Hassan berkata rombakan itu “hanya boleh dibaca sebagai menghantar isyarat kepada Melayu bahawa Anwar mendesak kehadiran Melayu yang lebih kuat dalam kerajaan.” Pelantikan Yeoh adalah daun progresif di atas pokok etno-nasionalis.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap pelantikan DAP ke portfolio kanan mencetuskan tentangan berasaskan kaum yang sama. Apabila Lim Guan Eng menjadi Menteri Kewangan dalam PH 1.0, kumpulan Melayu membantah. Kitaran ini amat boleh diramal: lantik bukan Melayu, hadapi tentangan kaum, sama ada bertahan (sebentar) atau berundur.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Membela pelantikan Hannah Yeoh adalah perkara yang betul. Kini bela prinsip di sebaliknya — bahawa kecekapan lebih penting daripada etnik — merentas SELURUH kerajaan kamu. Berhenti membuat satu pelantikan pelbagai kaum sambil menjalankan kira-kira kaum di tempat lain. Dan jika PAS membantah kerana seorang menteri berbangsa Cina, panggil ia apa yang sebenar: perkauman.

Sumber: Malay Mail | Focus Malaysia

item.title: Anwar Says 'Long Way to Go' on Elections in December 2025 — Now Preparing Snap GE16 for 2026
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On December 17, 2025, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told editors and senior journalists at a briefing in Seri Perdana that he had ruled out any possibility of snap general elections in 2026. “The election still has a long way to go. I have not thought about it,” he said. “I am focusing on how we continue to implement reforms and alleviate the problems people face regarding the higher cost of living.”

By February 2026, less than two months later, speculation was mounting that GE16 could be called as early as July 2026. CNA reported that party insiders and civil servants confirmed preparations were underway. Reuters reported in March 2026 that coalition partners were already positioning themselves for snap polls.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is a PM lying to the press about his election timeline. In December, he said elections were “a long way to go” and he hadn’t even thought about it. By February, his own party machinery was gearing up for a snap election. The current mandate doesn’t expire until February 2028 — calling elections nearly two years early is a major strategic decision that doesn’t happen overnight.

Anwar either lied to the press in December, or he made a radical strategic shift in under two months. Neither explanation inspires confidence. If he lied, it’s calculated deception of the public. If he genuinely hadn’t considered it, then the snap election push was driven by panic over the Azam Baki scandal, the PKR internal fractures, and coalition instability — not strategic planning.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

“I have not thought about it.” That’s what the PM said in December. The same PM who is the chairman of his party, the head of his coalition, and a career politician who has been thinking about elections every day for 30 years.

Of course he was thinking about it. The Sabah election in November 2025 — where PH was routed — had already forced a strategic reckoning. The PKR party election mess, Rafizi’s departure, and the cabinet reshuffle were all moves that only make sense if you’re positioning for an early general election.

Anwar told journalists what they wanted to hear — “focus on reforms, focus on cost of living” — while his political operation was already calculating seat projections and coalition arithmetic. This is not a man who hasn’t thought about elections. This is a man who doesn’t want you to know he’s thinking about elections.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Every Malaysian PM who has called snap elections has denied planning them right up until the announcement. Najib denied planning GE14 until he dissolved Parliament. Muhyiddin denied planning the Melaka and Johor state elections until they were called. It’s a Malaysian political tradition: lie about election timing, then call elections when it’s strategically advantageous.

Anwar is doing exactly what he criticised his predecessors for doing. The reformist is following the BN playbook.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You told the press in December that elections were “a long way to go.” Two months later, your coalition is gearing up for snap polls. Either you lied in December or you’re making major democratic decisions on the fly. Neither is acceptable for a PM who claims to be different from his predecessors. If you’re going to call an early election, at least be honest about why — don’t pretend it’s about reform when it’s about survival.

Sources: The Star | Straits Times | CNA | Reuters | NST

item.title: Hannah Yeoh Appointment Sparks Race-Based Backlash — PAS and UMNO Both Object
item.description:
Said by: Takiyuddin Hassan, PAS Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2025, Anwar appointed Hannah Yeoh as Federal Territories Minister in his cabinet reshuffle. PAS Secretary-General Takiyuddin Hassan immediately objected — not to Yeoh’s qualifications, but to her race. He argued the appointment would “strengthen DAP’s influence” in urban areas and disadvantage Malays. Rightist Malay groups warned that DAP was now “controlling Malay landholding” through the Federal Territories portfolio, which oversees KL, Putrajaya, and Labuan. Even UMNO leaders quietly raised concerns. Anwar hit back: “To reject a person because of her colour and race, this is atrocious in this day and age.” Social media reactions were polarised.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The backlash exposes how normalised ethno-religious gatekeeping remains in Malaysian politics — even under a supposedly reformist government. A minister was judged not by competence, track record, or policy vision, but by ethnicity. This is the structural racism PH was elected to dismantle. Instead, the party that appointed Yeoh simultaneously downgraded other DAP portfolios in the same reshuffle to signal “Malay strength” to voters. Yeoh’s appointment was brave — but it was also the exception that proved the rule. The rest of the reshuffle was racial calculus.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The contradiction is structural: Anwar wants to appear multiracial while managing a coalition that requires Malay-first optics. He appoints one Chinese-Malaysian woman to a prominent portfolio, then compensates by downgrading DAP elsewhere and elevating UMNO ministers. The multiracial gesture is real but it is hedged — designed to be just bold enough for DAP voters, but not so bold that Malay voters feel threatened. Analyst Azmi Hassan said the reshuffle “can only be read as sending a signal to Malays that Anwar is pushing for stronger Malay presence in government.” Yeoh’s appointment is the progressive leaf on an ethno-nationalist tree.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every DAP appointment to a senior portfolio triggers the same race-based backlash. When Lim Guan Eng became Finance Minister in PH 1.0, Malay groups protested. When DAP won state seats, opponents framed it as Chinese encroachment. The cycle is depressingly predictable: appoint a non-Malay, face racial backlash, either stand firm (briefly) or retreat. Anwar stood firm on Yeoh — which is commendable — but his overall cabinet strategy still operates within the racial framework rather than dismantling it. A government that makes one multiracial appointment while conducting the rest of its governance on racial lines is not reforming race relations — it is managing optics.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Defending Hannah Yeoh’s appointment was the right thing to do. Now defend the principle behind it — that competence matters more than ethnicity — across your ENTIRE government. Stop making one multiracial appointment while conducting racial calculus everywhere else. If you believe race shouldn’t determine cabinet positions, prove it with every appointment — not just the one that gets you international praise. And if PAS objects because a minister is Chinese, call it what it is: racism. Not “cultural concern,” not “community sensitivity” — racism. You said it was “atrocious.” Now act like you mean it.

Sources: Malay Mail | Focus Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Rombakan Kabinet Anwar Cetuskan Kontroversi Kaum — Hannah Yeoh Menteri WP, Adam Adli Timbalan Hal Ehwal Agama
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 16 Disember 2025, Anwar Ibrahim mengumumkan rombakan kabinet keduanya, melibatkan 28 perubahan menteri dan timbalan menteri. Antara pelantikan paling kontroversi:

Hannah Yeoh dinamakan Menteri di Jabatan Perdana Menteri (Wilayah Persekutuan) — bukan Melayu pertama memegang portfolio tersebut. PAS dan kumpulan Melayu sayap kanan segera menyerang pelantikan itu, mendakwa ia akan mengukuhkan “dominasi DAP” di KL.

Adam Adli — bekas aktivis pelajar — dilantik Timbalan Menteri di Jabatan Perdana Menteri (Hal Ehwal Agama), mencetuskan persoalan tentang kelayakan dan latar belakangnya untuk menyelia hal ehwal tadbir urus Islam.

Rombakan itu datang selepas berbulan-bulan kekosongan berikutan peletakan jawatan Rafizi dan Nik Nazmi, dan secara meluas dilihat sebagai persediaan untuk kemungkinan pilihan raya kilat.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Pelantikan Hannah Yeoh mendedahkan percanggahan asas politik Malaysia: kerajaan yang mendakwa pelbagai kaum tetapi beroperasi dalam sistem di mana kaum menentukan siapa boleh memegang portfolio mana. Pelantikan Adam Adli menimbulkan persoalan sah tentang kecekapan. Tadbir urus hal ehwal agama memerlukan pengetahuan khusus dalam perundangan Islam, dinamik agama negeri-persekutuan, dan politik pihak berkuasa agama.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Ada karut di pelbagai pihak. Serangan berasaskan kaum terhadap Hannah Yeoh amat tercela — beliau mempunyai rekod prestasi kukuh merentas pelbagai portfolio. Bangsanya sepatutnya tidak relevan dengan kecekapannya.

Tetapi Anwar memasuki kontroversi ini dengan mata terbuka. Beliau tahu melantik menteri WP bukan Melayu akan mencetuskan tentangan kaum. Dan pelantikan Adam Adli? Itu semata-mata naungan. Ganjaran aktivis setia dengan portfolio yang tidak layak. Ia tepat apa yang PH kritik BN kerana melakukan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Setiap rombakan kabinet Malaysia menjana kontroversi kaum — ia sudah terbenam dalam sistem. Corak memberi ganjaran kesetiaan berbanding kecekapan juga bukan baru untuk PH.

🎯 SERUAN PH

Kamu kata kamu akan melantik menteri berdasarkan merit, bukan kaum. Kemudian kamu membuat pelantikan yang mencetuskan ribut kaum, dan bukannya berdiri teguh, rakan gabungan kamu UMNO bergegas meyakinkan Melayu. Pilih laluan. Dan sementara itu, terangkan kenapa bekas aktivis pelajar menyelia tadbir urus hal ehwal agama. Kerana “kesetiaan” bukan kelayakan.

Sumber: SCMP | Straits Times | Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Zafrul Dilantik Pengerusi MIDA Selepas Tinggalkan MITI — Pintu Pusingan Tak Pernah Berhenti Berputar
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MIDA Chairman

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Apabila rombakan kabinet Disember 2025 mengeluarkan Tengku Zafrul daripada portfolio MITI, beliau tidak dihantar ke bangku belakang. Sebaliknya, Anwar melantiknya sebagai Pengerusi MIDA (Lembaga Pembangunan Pelaburan Malaysia) — agensi yang berada di bawah bidang kuasa MITI — dan sebagai Penasihat Politik Kanan kepada PM. Orang yang menyelia MIDA sebagai menteri kini mengetuainya sebagai pelantikan politik.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Pintu pusingan antara penyeliaan kementerian dan jawatan pengerusi agensi adalah kebimbangan tadbir urus yang klasik. Zafrul meluluskan dasar, belanjawan, dan strategi MIDA sebagai Menteri MITI. Kini beliau mengetuai agensi yang sama yang dikawal selianya. Keputusan pelaburan yang dipengaruhinya sebagai menteri — termasuk pelaksanaan perjanjian perdagangan AS — kini akan dilaksanakan di bawah jawatan pengerusinya. Tiada tempoh penyejukan, tiada pemisahan, tiada tembok api.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: PH berkempen untuk menamatkan pintu pusingan antara politik dan GLC. IDEAS mendokumenkan 238 pelantikan politik ke GLC dan badan berkanun di bawah Anwar — melebihi kadar Mahathir 2.0. Pelantikan MIDA Zafrul adalah contoh paling terang-terangan: tiada pun berpura-pura tentang jarak antara peranan menteri dan pelantikan agensi. Orang yang sama, portfolio yang sama, gelaran berbeza. Ini bukan pembaharuan tadbir urus; ia permainan kerusi muzik dengan kad nama.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah BN, menteri yang kehilangan portfolio diberi sinekur GLC sebagai pampasan. PH mengkritik amalan ini tanpa henti. Di bawah Anwar, amalan itu diteruskan dengan PR yang lebih baik.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Kamu mendokumenkan 238 pelantikan politik. Kamu berkempen menentang pintu pusingan GLC. Kemudian kamu melantik bekas menteri MITI kamu sebagai pengerusi MIDA — agensi yang diselia MITI. Jika ini bukan pelantikan politik, apa namanya? Laksanakan tempoh penyejukan. Wujudkan jawatankuasa pelantikan bebas. Atau akui sahaja sistem naungan kamu berfungsi tepat seperti BN, dengan PR yang sedikit lebih baik.

Sumber: SCMP | FMT

item.title: Anwar's Cabinet Reshuffle Sparks Race Controversy — Hannah Yeoh as FT Minister, Adam Adli as Religious Affairs Deputy
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On December 16, 2025, Anwar Ibrahim announced his second cabinet reshuffle, involving 28 ministerial and deputy ministerial changes. Among the most controversial appointments:

Hannah Yeoh was named Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) — the first non-Malay to hold the portfolio. PAS and right-wing Malay groups immediately attacked the appointment, claiming it would strengthen “DAP dominance” in KL and threaten Malay interests. Anwar called the criticism “atrocious” and said race should not determine ministerial capability.

Adam Adli — a former student activist — was appointed Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), sparking questions about whether he had the credentials or background to oversee Islamic governance matters.

The reshuffle came after months of vacancies following Rafizi and Nik Nazmi’s resignations, and was widely seen as positioning for a potential snap election.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

The Hannah Yeoh appointment exposed the fundamental contradiction of Malaysian politics: a government that claims to be multiracial but operates in a system where race determines who can hold which portfolio. The FT Minister portfolio had always been held by a senior UMNO Malay. Anwar’s decision to give it to a Chinese DAP leader was either a bold reform move or a political miscalculation that handed ammunition to the opposition.

The Adam Adli appointment raised legitimate questions about competence. Religious affairs governance requires specific knowledge of Islamic jurisprudence, state-federal religious dynamics, and the politics of religious authorities. Appointing a former student activist with no demonstrated expertise in these areas looked like a political reward, not a merit-based decision.

The Straits Times noted that “controversies cloud Malaysian PM Anwar’s new Cabinet line-up” — not the kind of headline you want when you’re positioning for a snap election.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

There’s bullshit on multiple sides here. The race-based attacks on Hannah Yeoh are deplorable — she has a strong track record across multiple portfolios. Her race should be irrelevant to her competence.

But Anwar walked into this controversy with his eyes open. He knew appointing a non-Malay FT minister would trigger a racial backlash. If he wanted to make that stand on principle, fine — but then commit to it. Don’t make the appointment and then have UMNO’s Zahid Hamidi rush out to reassure Malays that it won’t undermine the “Malay agenda.” That sends the message that the appointment needs defending on racial grounds, which undermines the very principle Anwar claimed to be championing.

And the Adam Adli appointment? That’s just patronage. Reward a loyal activist with a portfolio he’s not qualified for. It’s exactly what PH criticised BN for doing.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Every Malaysian cabinet reshuffle generates racial controversy — it’s baked into the system. But PH was supposed to transcend this. In 2018, the appointment of Lim Guan Eng as Finance Minister was attacked on racial grounds. PH defended it then on merit. Seven years later, the same party is still fighting the same battles — suggesting that PH’s “multiracial” vision has not actually shifted the political landscape.

The pattern of rewarding loyalty over competence is also not new for PH. The Nurul Izzah adviser appointment, various political secretary scandals, and now Adam Adli all follow the same logic: political loyalty first, governance competence second.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You said you’d appoint ministers based on merit, not race. Then you made an appointment that triggered a racial firestorm, and instead of standing firm, your coalition partner UMNO rushed to reassure Malays. Pick a lane. Either you believe in multiracial meritocracy — and defend it without qualifiers — or admit that race still drives your Cabinet math. And while you’re at it, explain why a former student activist is overseeing religious affairs governance. Because “loyalty” is not a qualification.

Sources: SCMP | Straits Times | Malay Mail | FMT | US-ASEAN

item.title: Zafrul Appointed MIDA Chairman After Leaving MITI — The Revolving Door Never Stops Spinning
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MIDA Chairman

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: When the December 2025 cabinet reshuffle removed Tengku Zafrul from the MITI portfolio, he wasn’t sent to the backbench. Instead, Anwar appointed him as Chairman of MIDA (Malaysian Investment Development Authority) — the very agency that falls under MITI’s purview — and as Senior Political Advisor to the PM. The man who oversaw MIDA as minister now chairs it as a political appointee.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The revolving door between ministerial oversight and agency chairmanship is a textbook governance concern. Zafrul approved MIDA’s policies, budgets, and strategies as MITI Minister. Now he chairs the same agency he regulated. Investment decisions he influenced as minister — including the US trade deal’s implementation — will now be executed under his chairmanship. There is no cooling-off period, no separation, no firewall.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH campaigned on ending the revolving door between politics and GLCs. IDEAS documented 238 political appointments to GLCs and statutory bodies under Anwar — exceeding Mahathir 2.0’s rate. Zafrul’s MIDA appointment is the most brazen example: not even a pretence of distance between the ministerial role and the agency appointment. The same person, the same portfolio, different title. This is not governance reform; it’s musical chairs with name cards.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, ministers who lost portfolios were given GLC sinecures as compensation. PH criticised this practice relentlessly. Under Anwar, the practice continues with better PR — Zafrul’s appointment is framed as ‘continuity and expertise’ rather than what it is: a political reward and soft landing for a reshuffled ally.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You documented 238 political appointments. You campaigned against the GLC revolving door. Then you appointed your former MITI minister as chairman of MIDA — the agency MITI oversees. If this isn’t a political appointment, what is? Implement cooling-off periods. Create independent appointment committees. Or just admit that your patronage system works exactly like BN’s, with slightly better PR.

Sources: SCMP | FMT

item.title: [BM] Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum DEB Masih Tidak Boleh Disentuh — Pengkritik 'Sebulat Suara' Diabaikan
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Think Tanks

Category: Social

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Eurasia Review menerbitkan analisis komprehensif pada Disember 2025 mendapati “pendapat hampir sebulat suara” dalam kalangan penganalisis bahawa dasar ekonomi berasaskan kaum Malaysia — berakar umbi dalam Dasar Ekonomi Baru (DEB) 1971 — telah gagal dalam “matlamat menyeluruh perpaduan nasional.” Namun analisis itu menyimpulkan kerajaan “sangat tidak mungkin meninggalkan” dasar-dasar tersebut. DEB dan penggantinya mengekalkan keutamaan Bumiputera dalam kontrak kerajaan, pendidikan, perumahan, dan ekuiti korporat. Walaupun 50+ tahun dasar ini, Malaysia kekal berpecah mendalam mengikut garis etnik.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: DEB dan penggantinya telah menjadi rel ketiga politik Malaysia selama lebih 50 tahun. Setiap PM — termasuk yang “reformis” — enggan menyentuhnya. Apabila hampir setiap penganalisis bebas bersetuju sesuatu dasar telah gagal dalam objektif yang dinyatakan, dan kerajaan masih tidak mahu memperbaharuinya, dasar itu wujud untuk melayani kepentingan politik, bukan kepentingan nasional.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: DEB direka sebagai dasar 20 tahun (1971-1990). Kini sudah tahun ke-55. Sasaran ekuiti korporat Bumiputera 30% telah menjadi tiang gol yang sentiasa bergerak selama berdekad. Sementara itu, penerima utama keutamaan Bumiputera adalah golongan elit yang mempunyai hubungan politik, bukan Melayu biasa. Jurang kekayaan dalam komuniti Bumiputera sendiri sangat besar. Dasar berasaskan kaum yang direka untuk membantu Melayu miskin telah dirampas oleh Melayu kaya yang mempunyai hubungan — dan PH, seperti setiap kerajaan sebelumnya, enggan mengakui ini.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Mahathir memperkenalkan rangka kerja Wawasan 2020 yang sepatutnya membawa Malaysia melepasi ekonomi berasaskan kaum. Ia tidak berlaku. Abdullah Badawi menjanjikan DPN akan lebih “berasaskan merit.” Ia tidak. Rangka kerja Anwar menggunakan akronim berbeza untuk struktur yang sama. DEB tidak boleh dibunuh bukan kerana ia berfungsi, tetapi kerana membongkarnya memerlukan memberitahu berjuta pengundi Melayu bahawa sistem keutamaan yang identiti politik mereka dibina atasnya perlu berubah.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Penganalisis — termasuk badan pemikir kamu sendiri — kata sebulat suara bahawa dasar ekonomi berasaskan kaum telah gagal dalam matlamat perpaduan nasional. Penghijrahan otak semakin laju. Herotan pelaburan berterusan. “Malaysia Madani” tidak boleh wujud bersama dengan keutamaan ekonomi berasaskan kaum. Pilih satu. Atau akui bahawa “Madani” sentiasa hanya slogan — bukan hala tuju dasar.

Sumber: Eurasia Review

item.title: [BM] LRT3 Ditangguh LAGI — Masalah Perisian, Ujian Gagal, Penalti RM475J Menanti
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Laluan LRT3 dari Bandar Utama ke Johan Setia — projek berbilion ringgit yang bertujuan mengubah ketersambungan di Lembah Klang — telah ditangguhkan berulang kali kerana kegagalan perisian isyarat dan ketidakupayaan menyelesaikan ujian tanpa kerosakan. Menteri Pengangkutan Anthony Loke memberi amaran kontraktor mungkin menghadapi penalti melebihi RM475 juta untuk kelewatan tersebut. Tarikh pembukaan kekal tidak disahkan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: LRT3 sepatutnya beroperasi bertahun-tahun lalu. Kelewatan berulang bermakna penumpang terus bergantung kepada kereta, memburukkan kesesakan dan pencemaran di Lembah Klang. Wang awam terikat dalam sistem yang tiada siapa boleh guna — berbilion dilaburkan dengan pulangan sifar kepada penumpang. Kegagalan perisian isyarat menunjukkan isu lebih mendalam dengan pengurusan projek, keupayaan kontraktor, dan pengawasan kerajaan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Ancaman penalti Loke kedengaran kosong. Kerajaan mempunyai rekod berunding semula dan bukannya menguatkuasakan akibat terhadap kontraktor projek mega. Penalti diketepikan, tarikh akhir dilanjutkan, lebihan kos diserap oleh orang awam, dan tiada siapa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kelewatan infrastruktur di bawah PH tidak berbeza daripada kelewatan infrastruktur di bawah BN — kerajaan “reformasi” menyampaikan keputusan yang sama dengan kontraktor dan alasan yang sama.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sejarah projek mega infrastruktur Malaysia adalah katalog kelewatan, lebihan kos, dan kontrak yang dirunding semula. MRT2 ditangguh. ECRL dirunding semula. Pelan Induk Pengangkutan Pulau Pinang terbantut. LRT3 kini menyertai senarai. Corak merentas pentadbiran: umum projek bercita-cita tinggi, anugerah kontrak kepada firma yang mempunyai hubungan politik, terlepas tarikh akhir, ugut penalti, runding semula secara senyap, dan ulang.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Jika penalti RM475J terhutang, kutiplah. Jika kontraktor tidak boleh menyampaikan, gantikan. Jika perisian isyarat tidak berfungsi selepas bertahun-tahun ujian, cari sebabnya dan terbitkan penemuan. Berhenti mengugut akibat yang tidak pernah kamu kuatkuasakan. Kuatkuasakan RM475J itu. Kami cabar kamu.

Sumber: The Edge | Lowyat

item.title: [BM] Perkhidmatan Awam 90% Bumiputera — Melayu Pegang 80%+ Jawatan Tertinggi
item.description:
Said by: Eurasia Review Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Menurut analisis Eurasia Review yang diterbitkan pada Disember 2025, perkhidmatan awam Malaysia yang berjumlah 1.3 hingga 1.6 juta pekerja adalah kira-kira 90% Bumiputera. Melayu memegang 77.5% daripada semua jawatan dan lebih 80% jawatan pengurusan tertinggi. Malaysia Cina hanya memegang 5.7% dan Malaysia India 3.8% — di sebuah negara di mana Cina membentuk 23% dan India 7% daripada populasi. Straits Times melaporkan “perdebatan dasar Bumiputera-dahulu” telah mengambil “campuran kuat kesihatan, pendidikan dan politik.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Perkhidmatan awam yang tidak mencerminkan demografi negara tidak boleh melayani semua rakyat secara saksama. Pengambilan berasaskan kaum dalam perkhidmatan awam mengekalkan pengecualian etnik dan menjejaskan meritokrasi — prinsip yang Anwar berkempen atasnya. Apabila 90% kakitangan kerajaan datang daripada satu kumpulan etnik, seluruh jentera negara — daripada reka bentuk dasar hingga pelaksanaan hingga penyampaian perkhidmatan — ditapis melalui satu kanta budaya.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Jenama “Malaysia Madani” Anwar menjanjikan Malaysia yang bertamadun, meritokratik, dan pelbagai kaum. Namun lengan negara Malaysia yang paling ketara — perkhidmatan awam — kekal 90% satu kaum. Di bawah PH, ini tidak boleh disentuh. Bukan kerana pembaharuan mustahil, tetapi kerana 1.6 juta penjawat awam juga 1.6 juta undi — undi Melayu yang Anwar tidak mampu kehilangan menjelang PRU16. Kerajaan “reformis” tidak melakukan apa-apa — bukan program perintis, bukan sasaran, malah pengakuan awam pun tiada — untuk menangani ketidakseimbangan struktur ini.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Kuota perkhidmatan awam DEB diperkenalkan pada tahun 1970-an sebagai tindakan afirmatif. Lima puluh tahun kemudian, langkah “sementara” itu kekal, kuota berakar umbi, dan komposisi kaum hanya menjadi lebih homogen dari semasa ke semasa. Setiap PM — Mahathir, Abdullah, Najib, Muhyiddin, Anwar — meninggalkannya tanpa sentuhan.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Perkhidmatan awam kamu 90% satu kaum di negara pelbagai kaum. Jenama “Madani” kamu menjanjikan meritokrasi. Dua fakta ini tidak boleh wujud bersama. Tetapkan sasaran kepelbagaian. Buka pengambilan. Wujudkan laluan untuk rakyat Malaysia bukan Bumiputera berkhidmat untuk kerajaan mereka sendiri. Atau akui bahawa “Malaysia Madani” adalah slogan untuk eksport — jenama yang kamu jual kepada pelabur asing sambil menjalankan negara berasaskan kaum di tanah air.

Sumber: Eurasia Review | Straits Times

item.title: Civil Service is 90% Bumiputera — Malays Hold 80%+ of Top Posts
item.description:
Said by: Eurasia Review Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: According to Eurasia Review analysis published in December 2025, Malaysia’s civil service of 1.3 to 1.6 million employees is approximately 90% Bumiputera. Malays hold 77.5% of all posts and over 80% of top management positions. Chinese Malaysians hold just 5.7% and Indian Malaysians 3.8% — in a country where Chinese make up 23% and Indians 7% of the population. The Straits Times reported the “Bumiputera-first policy debate” has taken on a “heady mix of health, education and politics.” When a UiTM professor suggested opening Bumiputera-only programmes to other races to address specialist shortages, the backlash was immediate and the idea was shut down.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A civil service that doesn’t reflect the country’s demographics cannot serve all citizens equally. Race-based hiring in public service perpetuates ethnic exclusion and undermines meritocracy — the very principle Anwar campaigned on. The civil service is simultaneously Malaysia’s largest employer and its most racially homogeneous institution. When 90% of government servants come from one ethnic group, the entire apparatus of state — from policy design to implementation to service delivery — is filtered through one cultural lens. Non-Bumiputera citizens interact with a government that barely represents them.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar’s “Malaysia Madani” brand promises a civilised, meritocratic, multiracial Malaysia. Yet the most visible arm of the Malaysian state — the civil service — remains 90% one race. Under PH, this is untouchable. Not because reform is impossible, but because 1.6 million civil servants are also 1.6 million votes — overwhelmingly Malay votes that Anwar cannot afford to lose ahead of GE16. The “reformist” government has done nothing — not a pilot programme, not a target, not even a public acknowledgment — to address the structural imbalance. Meritocracy applies to everyone except the government’s own workforce.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The NEP’s civil service quotas were introduced in the 1970s as affirmative action. Fifty years later, the “temporary” measures are permanent, the quotas are entrenched, and the racial composition has only become more homogeneous over time. Every PM — Mahathir, Abdullah, Najib, Muhyiddin, Anwar — has left this untouched. The UiTM incident is instructive: even suggesting partial reform triggered enough backlash to kill the idea instantly. The third rail of Malaysian politics isn’t just electrified — it’s surrounded by a moat.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your civil service is 90% one race in a multiracial country. Your “Madani” brand promises meritocracy. These two facts cannot coexist. Set diversity targets. Open recruitment. Create pathways for non-Bumiputera Malaysians to serve their own government. Or admit that “Malaysia Madani” is a slogan for export — a brand you sell to foreign investors while running a race-based state at home. 1.6 million civil servants, 90% Bumiputera. That’s not meritocracy. That’s a monoculture wearing a multiracial mask.

Sources: Eurasia Review | Straits Times

item.title: LRT3 Delayed AGAIN — Software Glitches, Failed Test Runs, RM475M Penalties Looming
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The LRT3 line from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia — a multi-billion ringgit project intended to transform connectivity in the Klang Valley — has been postponed repeatedly due to signalling software failures and the inability to complete fault-free test runs. Transport Minister Anthony Loke warned contractors may face penalties exceeding RM475 million for the delays. The opening date remains unconfirmed. The Edge and Lowyat both reported the latest round of testing setbacks as the system failed to achieve the required reliability benchmarks.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: LRT3 was supposed to be operational years ago. Repeated delays mean commuters continue relying on cars, worsening congestion and pollution in the Klang Valley. Public money is tied up in a system nobody can use — billions invested with zero return to commuters. The signalling software failures suggest deeper issues with project management, contractor capability, and government oversight. Meanwhile, commuters who were told LRT3 would change their lives continue sitting in traffic.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Loke’s penalty threats ring hollow. The government has a track record of renegotiating rather than enforcing consequences on mega-project contractors. Penalties get waived, deadlines get extended, cost overruns get absorbed by the public, and nobody is held accountable. Infrastructure delays under PH are no different from infrastructure delays under BN — the “reform” government delivers the same results with the same contractors and the same excuses. If the RM475M in penalties are actually enforced, it would be the first time in Malaysian infrastructure history. Don’t hold your breath.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s mega-infrastructure project history is a catalogue of delays, cost overruns, and renegotiated contracts. MRT2 was delayed. The ECRL was renegotiated (costs ballooned from RM55B to reportedly higher). The Penang Transport Master Plan is stalled. LRT3 now joins the list. The pattern across administrations: announce ambitious projects, award contracts to politically connected firms, miss deadlines, threaten penalties, renegotiate quietly, and repeat. The contractors get paid regardless. The public gets delays regardless. Only the minister cutting the ribbon changes.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: If RM475M in penalties are owed, collect them. If the contractor can’t deliver, replace them. If the signalling software doesn’t work after years of testing, find out why and publish the findings. Stop threatening consequences you never enforce. Infrastructure accountability starts with one simple principle: contracts that aren’t met should have real consequences. Under BN, they didn’t. Under PH, they still don’t. Prove us wrong. Enforce the RM475M. We dare you.

Sources: The Edge | Lowyat

item.title: Race-Based Economic Policies NEP Still Untouchable — 'Unanimous' Critics Ignored
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Think Tanks

Category: Social

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Eurasia Review published a comprehensive analysis in December 2025 finding “almost unanimous opinion” among analysts that Malaysia’s race-based economic policies — rooted in the New Economic Policy (NEP) of 1971 — have failed their “overarching goal of national unity.” Yet the analysis concluded the government is “highly unlikely to abandon” them. The NEP and its successors (NDP, NVP, and now Anwar’s various economic frameworks) maintain Bumiputera preferences in government contracts, education, housing, and corporate equity. Despite 50+ years of these policies, Malaysia remains deeply divided along ethnic lines, brain drain continues at alarming rates, and economic inequality persists within the Bumiputera community itself.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The NEP and its successors have been the third rail of Malaysian politics for over 50 years. Every PM — including the “reformist” one — refuses to touch them. When virtually every independent analyst agrees a policy has failed its stated objective, and the government still won’t reform it, the policy exists to serve political interests, not national ones. Anwar’s multiracial “Malaysia Madani” brand is fundamentally incompatible with race-based economic preferences — but admitting that would cost Malay votes. So the contradiction persists and the country pays the price in brain drain, investment distortions, and communal resentment.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The NEP was designed as a 20-year policy (1971-1990). It’s now in its 55th year. The Bumiputera corporate equity target of 30% has been a moving goalpost for decades — with the government periodically changing the methodology to show either progress or continued need, depending on what serves the political moment. Meanwhile, the primary beneficiaries of Bumiputera preferences have been politically connected elites, not ordinary Malays. The wealth gap within the Bumiputera community is enormous. Race-based policies designed to help poor Malays have been captured by rich, connected Malays — and PH, like every government before it, refuses to acknowledge this because the fiction of universal Bumiputera benefit is essential to electoral politics.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Mahathir introduced the Vision 2020 framework that was supposed to move Malaysia beyond race-based economics. It didn’t. Abdullah Badawi promised the NDP would be more “merit-based.” It wasn’t. Najib’s NTP made similar promises. Anwar’s frameworks use different acronyms for the same structure. The NEP is unkillable not because it works, but because dismantling it would require telling millions of Malay voters that the preference system their political identity is built on needs to change. No politician has the courage to do this — least of all one who needs Malay votes to survive.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Analysts — your own think tanks included — say unanimously that race-based economic policies have failed their stated goal of national unity. Brain drain accelerates as non-Bumiputera professionals leave for countries where they’re judged on merit. Investment distortions persist. Communal resentment deepens. And your response is to maintain the policies unchanged because dismantling them would cost votes. “Malaysia Madani” cannot coexist with race-based economic preferences. Choose one. Or admit that “Madani” was always just a slogan — not a policy direction.

Sources: Eurasia Review

item.title: [BM] Anwar Janji Hapus Kemiskinan Tegar Menjelang Q1 2026 — Sekarang Mac 2026 Dan Tiada Siapa Bercakap Tentangnya
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-07

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 7 Disember 2025, di majlis penutup program Rancakkan Madani di Putrajaya, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim mengisytiharkan bahawa kadar kemiskinan tegar Malaysia telah jatuh kepada 0.09 peratus — melibatkan kira-kira 7,000 isi rumah — dan memberitahu penjawat awam bahawa memandangkan angka itu “terlalu kecil,” kerajaan boleh “menghapuskannya menjelang suku pertama 2026.” Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, Business Today — semuanya menjadikannya berita utama. Kini pertengahan Mac 2026, jauh masuk ke dalam Q1. Tiada pengisytiharan kejayaan, tiada kemas kini tentang 7,000 isi rumah itu, dan tiada sesiapa dalam kerajaan yang kelihatan membincangkannya.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini bukan kenyataan sambil lewa. Anwar menjadikan penghapusan kemiskinan tegar tunjang utama Bajet 2026, memperuntukkan RM15 bilion bantuan langsung, dan menggunakan angka 0.09% dalam pelbagai ucapan sebagai bukti kejayaan MADANI. Ahli ekonomi sudah pun mempersoalkan sama ada garis kemiskinan kerajaan — RM1,169 sebulan untuk kemiskinan tegar — benar-benar menggambarkan kesusahan hidup di negara yang sewa rumah sahaja boleh melebihi jumlah itu. Tarikh akhir Q1 2026 sepatutnya menjadi pencapaian kemuncak. Sebaliknya, ia senyap-senyap berlalu sementara kerajaan sibuk dengan krisis harga minyak, skandal Azam Baki, dan undang-undang sekatan kebebasan bersuara. Kesunyian itu memberitahu segalanya tentang betapa seriusnya janji itu dibuat.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Ini buku panduan PH yang sama: buat janji besar bergegar dengan tarikh akhir yang jelas, harap tiada siapa akan follow up, dan biarkan kitaran berita menguburkannya. Anwar bukan sekadar berkata “kita harap boleh kurangkan kemiskinan” — dia bagi suku tahun, peratusan, dan arahan khusus kepada penjawat awam. Kalau sasaran dah tercapai, mesti ada sidang akhbar, berita Bernama, dan hashtag. Ketiadaan sambutan itu sendiri adalah bukti. Sementara itu, rakyat yang sama yang Anwar janji nak angkat dari kemiskinan kini menyaksikan RON97 cecah RM3.85, diesel naik 26 peratus, dan harga makanan meningkat. 7,000 isi rumah yang berpendapatan bawah RM1,169 sebulan tak jadi lebih baik semata-mata kerana Perdana Menteri sudah beralih ke ucapan seterusnya.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Anwar ada corak menetapkan tarikh akhir yang tamat dalam kesunyian. Pada 2024, dia isytiharkan kemiskinan tegar “dihapuskan sepenuhnya” di KL, Melaka, dan Negeri Sembilan — dakwaan yang dipertikaikan ahli ekonomi yang menyatakan garis kemiskinan itu sendiri terlalu rendah untuk bermakna. Kerajaan kemudian senyap-senyap menyemak semula Garis Kemiskinan Makanan kepada RM1,236 pada 2024, yang sepatutnya menaikkan jumlah itu, bukan menurunkannya. Sebelum itu, reformasi subsidi dijanjikan “dalam masa beberapa bulan” selepas memerintah pada 2022 — kini 2026 dan subsidi bersasar hanya meliputi RON95, dengan reformasi yang lebih luas ditangguhkan selama-lamanya. Coraknya konsisten: umum, tuntut kredit, teruskan, jangan sekali-kali bertanggungjawab atas hasilnya.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Anda beritahu 35 juta rakyat Malaysia bahawa 7,000 isi rumah sahaja yang memisahkan kerajaan anda daripada penghapusan kemiskinan tegar. Anda beri diri sendiri masa sehingga Mac 2026. Sekarang Mac 2026. Mana 7,000 keluarga itu? Sudah keluar dari kemiskinan, atau anda hanya berhenti mengira? Anda jadikan ini tajuk utama Bajet, tunjang Rancakkan Madani, dan bukti MADANI berjaya. Kalau berjaya, tunjukkan resit. Kalau gagal, mengaku. Satu perkara yang anda tidak boleh buat ialah berpura-pura janji itu tidak pernah dibuat — kerana Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, dan Business Today semuanya ada rekodnya.

Sumber: Malay Mail | Bernama | The Star | Business Today | The Vibes

item.title: Anwar Promised to End Hardcore Poverty by Q1 2026 — It's March 2026 and Nobody's Talking About It
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-07

📌 What Happened

On December 7, 2025, at the closing ceremony of the Rancakkan Madani programme in Putrajaya, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim publicly declared that Malaysia’s hardcore poverty rate had fallen to 0.09 per cent — affecting roughly 7,000 households — and told civil servants that since the number was “so small,” the government could “end it by the first quarter of 2026.” Multiple outlets — Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, Business Today — ran the claim as headline news. It is now mid-March 2026, deep into Q1. There has been no victory declaration, no update on the 7,000 households, and no one in government appears to be talking about it.

🔥 Why It Matters

This was not an off-the-cuff remark. Anwar made hardcore poverty eradication a centrepiece of Budget 2026, allocated RM15 billion in direct assistance, and used the 0.09% figure in multiple speeches as proof of MADANI’s success. Economists had already questioned whether the government’s poverty line — RM1,169 per month for hardcore poverty — meaningfully captures deprivation in a country where urban rents alone can exceed that threshold. The Q1 2026 deadline was supposed to be the crowning achievement. Instead, it has quietly lapsed while the government is consumed by oil price crises, Azam Baki scandals, and speech-restriction laws. The silence tells you everything about how seriously the promise was made.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

This is the classic PH playbook: make a bold, headline-friendly promise with a specific deadline, count on nobody following up, and hope the news cycle buries it. Anwar didn’t just say “we hope to reduce poverty” — he gave a quarter, a percentage, and a specific instruction to civil servants. If the target had been hit, there would be a press conference, a Bernama wire, and a hashtag. The absence of celebration is the evidence. Meanwhile, the same rakyat Anwar promised to lift out of poverty are now watching RON97 hit RM3.85, diesel surge 26 per cent, and food prices climb. The 7,000 households earning under RM1,169 a month are not better off because the Prime Minister moved on to his next speech.

📜 Previous Cases

Anwar has a pattern of setting deadlines that expire in silence. In 2024, he declared hardcore poverty “totally eliminated” in KL, Melaka, and Negeri Sembilan — a claim economists disputed, noting the poverty line itself was too low to be meaningful. The government later quietly revised the Food Poverty Line to RM1,236 in 2024, which should have increased the count, not decreased it. Before that, subsidy reform was promised “within months” of taking office in 2022 — it is now 2026 and targeted subsidies cover only RON95, with the broader reform indefinitely shelved. The pattern is consistent: announce, claim credit, move on, never account for the outcome.

🎯 PH Callout

You told 35 million Malaysians that 7,000 households stood between your government and the eradication of hardcore poverty. You gave yourself until March 2026. It is March 2026. Where are the 7,000 families? Are they out of poverty, or did you just stop counting? You made this the headline of your Budget, the centrepiece of Rancakkan Madani, and the proof that MADANI works. If it worked, show the receipts. If it didn’t, say so. The one thing you cannot do is pretend the promise was never made — because Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, and Business Today all have it on record.

Sources: Malay Mail | Bernama | The Star | Business Today | The Vibes

item.title: [BM] Pembantu PM Sendiri Shamsul Iskandar Didakwa Rasuah — Perang Antirasuah Makan Diri Sendiri
item.description:
Said by: Reuters / Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 4 Disember 2025, setiausaha politik kanan Anwar Ibrahim, Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, didakwa menerima rasuah berjumlah RM176,829 daripada seorang ahli perniagaan tempatan. Pertuduhan mendakwa Shamsul menggunakan kedudukannya yang istimewa di pejabat Perdana Menteri untuk membantu ahli perniagaan tersebut mendapatkan lesen perlombongan mineral di Sabah, antara lain. Shamsul meletak jawatan beberapa hari sebelum didakwa, mengaku tidak bersalah, dan diberikan jaminan. Beliau menyifatkan pertuduhan itu sebagai “serangan” terhadapnya. Reuters melaporkan pertuduhan itu “meningkatkan penelitian terhadap ikrar perdana menteri untuk membanteras rasuah dengan ketara.” CNA melaporkan Anwar kini menghadapi “krisis rasuah” di samping “perpecahan parti PKR” dan “kegelisahan gabungan PH.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Ini bukan pegawai kecil di agensi jauh. Ini setiausaha politik kanan PM — seseorang yang mempunyai akses terus dan harian kepada pejabat paling berkuasa di negara ini. Pertuduhan mendakwa beliau menjalankan operasi bayar-untuk-main bagi lesen perlombongan dari dalam pejabat PM. Jika dakwaan itu benar, ibu pejabat perang antirasuah itu sendiri adalah tempat rasuah.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Ini Anwar yang sama yang membina seluruh kerjaya politiknya atas retorik antirasuah. Beliau menghabiskan berdekad sebagai kompas moral Malaysia terhadap rasuah. Beliau masuk penjara (atas pertuduhan yang dikatakannya bermotif politik). Beliau membina Reformasi sebagai gerakan menentang penyalahgunaan kuasa. Dan pembantunya yang paling rapat didakwa menjual lesen perlombongan dari pejabat PM. Ironinya mencekik: orang yang berikrar untuk membersihkan Malaysia kini mempunyai pejabatnya sendiri sebagai tempat jenayah. Shamsul menyifatkan pertuduhan itu sebagai “serangan” menggemakan pembelaan setiap ahli politik rasuah Malaysia — dan PH dahulu mengejek pembelaan itu apabila ahli politik BN menggunakannya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Sejarah rasuah pembantu dan setiausaha Malaysia amat luas. Di bawah Najib, pembantu dan rakan kongsi terlibat dalam skim berkaitan 1MDB. Coraknya: pembantu berkuasa menggunakan kedekatan dengan PM untuk mengkomersialkan akses. Pejabat Anwar tidak berbeza — ia adalah lelaran terkini.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Pembantu terdekat kamu didakwa rasuah kerana menjual akses kepada lesen perlombongan dari PEJABAT KAMU. Bukan pegawai negeri yang jauh. Bukan pegawai kecil. Setiausaha politik kamu. Orang yang duduk di luar pintu kamu. Apa yang kamu tahu? Bila kamu tahu? Perang antirasuah tidak terselamat apabila ibu pejabatnya sendiri menjadi tempat jenayah.

Sumber: Reuters | CNA

item.title: [BM] Rasuah Balak Sabah — Berbilion Pembalakan Haram Sementara Shamsul Iskandar Dapat Lesen Perlombongan Dari Pejabat PMX
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Groups / MACC Investigations

Category: Other

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Sabah, rumah kepada antara hutan hujan primer terakhir Malaysia, terus menghadapi pembalakan haram dan tidak mampan secara besar-besaran yang didorong oleh rasuah dalam peruntukan lesen. Krisis ini dibawa ke perhatian tajam apabila setiausaha politik kanan Anwar sendiri, Shamsul Iskandar, didakwa pada 4 Disember 2025 menerima rasuah berjumlah RM176,829 daripada seorang ahli perniagaan tempatan untuk memudahkan lesen perlombongan mineral di Sabah. Reuters melaporkan pertuduhan itu mendedahkan rasuah “yang berpunca terus daripada pejabat PM.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Hutan Sabah adalah antara yang paling kaya biodiversiti di Bumi, rumah kepada orang utan, gajah pigmi, dan beribu spesies endemik. Pembalakan haram dan korup bukan sahaja memusnahkan ekosistem yang tidak boleh diganti — ia menjejaskan mata pencarian komuniti orang asal, menyumbang kepada banjir dan hakisan tanah, merampas warisan semula jadi generasi akan datang, dan menyebabkan kerajaan negeri kerugian berbilion dalam hasil yang hilang.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Hakikat bahawa lesen perlombongan dan pembalakan didakwa dimudahkan melalui pejabat PM sendiri — melalui setiausaha politik kanannya — bermakna rasuah dalam peruntukan sumber asli menjangkau ke puncak tertinggi kerajaan Madani. Ini bukan pegawai nakal di daerah terpencil; ini rasuah sistematik dengan laluan terus ke Putrajaya. PH berjanji untuk melindungi alam sekitar Malaysia dan melawan rasuah. Sebaliknya, orang PH sendiri didakwa mengkomersialkan akses kepada sumber Sabah dari pejabat PM.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Rasuah balak Sabah sudah berdekad lamanya. Di bawah BN, konsesi pembalakan diagihkan sebagai naungan politik — paling terkenal di bawah bekas KM Musa Aman. Coraknya dwipartisan: sesiapa yang mengawal akses kepada sumber asli Sabah menggunakan akses itu untuk keuntungan politik dan peribadi. Kerajaan berbeza, rasuah sama, hutan yang sama terbakar.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Pembantu kamu sendiri didakwa menerima rasuah untuk lesen perlombongan Sabah. DARI PEJABAT KAMU. Hutan Sabah tidak perlukan satu lagi “pasukan petugas” atau “jawatankuasa khas.” Mereka perlukan kerajaan yang orangnya sendiri tidak memudahkan kemusnahan mereka. Terbitkan setiap lesen perlombongan dan pembalakan yang dikeluarkan semasa tempoh PH. Dedahkan siapa memohon, siapa meluluskan, dan siapa mendapat manfaat.

Sumber: Reuters

item.title: PM's Own Aide Shamsul Iskandar Charged with Bribery — Anti-Corruption Crusade Eats Itself
item.description:
Said by: Reuters / Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 4 December 2025, Anwar Ibrahim’s senior political secretary Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin was charged with receiving bribes totalling RM176,829 from a local businessman. The charges allege Shamsul used his privileged position in the Prime Minister’s office to assist the businessman in obtaining mineral mining licences in Sabah, among other favours. Shamsul resigned just days before being charged, pleaded not guilty, and was granted bail. He called the charges an “attack” against him. Reuters reported the charges “significantly increased scrutiny on the premier’s pledge to crack down on corruption.” CNA reported Anwar now faces a “graft crisis” alongside “PKR party fractures” and “PH coalition jitters.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is not a minor official in a distant agency. This is the PM’s senior political secretary — someone with direct, daily access to the most powerful office in the country. The charges allege he was running a pay-for-play operation for mining licences from inside the PM’s office. If the allegations are true, the anti-corruption crusade’s own headquarters was a corruption venue. Reuters framing it as increasing “scrutiny on the premier’s pledge to crack down on corruption” is diplomatic understatement — this demolishes the pledge entirely.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: This is the same Anwar who built his entire political career on anti-corruption rhetoric. He spent decades as Malaysia’s moral compass on corruption. He went to prison (on charges he said were politically motivated). He built Reformasi as a movement against the abuse of power. And his closest aide was allegedly selling mining licences from the PM’s office. The irony is suffocating: the man who vowed to clean up Malaysia now has his own office as a crime scene. Shamsul calling the charges an “attack” echoes every corrupt Malaysian politician’s defence — and PH used to mock that defence when BN politicians used it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s history of aide and secretary corruption is extensive. Under Najib, aides and associates were implicated in 1MDB-related schemes. Under Muhyiddin, political secretaries were linked to procurement irregularities. The pattern: powerful aides use proximity to the PM to monetise access, knowing that their position provides both opportunity and protection. Anwar’s office is not different — it is the latest iteration. The Shamsul case is particularly damaging because it involves Sabah mining licences, connecting to the broader Sabah resource corruption ecosystem that PH promised to dismantle.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your closest aide was charged with bribery for selling access to mining licences from YOUR office. Not a distant state official. Not a minor functionary. Your political secretary. The person who sat outside your door. What did you know? When did you know it? Did Shamsul’s activities never come to your attention despite years of proximity? Either you knew and tolerated it, or you didn’t know — in which case your office management is dangerously negligent. Answer the question. The anti-corruption crusade doesn’t survive its own headquarters being a crime scene.

Sources: Reuters | CNA

item.title: Sabah Timber Corruption — Billions in Illegal Logging While Shamsul Iskandar Got Mining Licences from PMX's Office
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Groups / MACC Investigations

Category: Other

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Sabah, home to some of Malaysia’s last remaining primary rainforests, continues to face massive illegal and unsustainable logging driven by corruption in licence allocation. The crisis was brought into sharp focus when Anwar’s own senior political secretary, Shamsul Iskandar, was charged on 4 December 2025 with receiving bribes totalling RM176,829 from a local businessman for facilitating mineral mining licences in Sabah. Reuters reported the charges exposed corruption “emanating directly from the PM’s office.” The Shamsul case revealed how natural resource extraction in East Malaysia is systematically corrupted by political patronage — with the corruption pipeline running all the way to the Prime Minister’s doorstep.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Sabah’s forests are among the most biodiverse on Earth, home to orangutans, pygmy elephants, and thousands of endemic species. Illegal and corrupt logging doesn’t just destroy irreplaceable ecosystems — it undermines the livelihoods of indigenous communities, contributes to flooding and soil erosion, robs future generations of natural heritage, and costs the state government billions in lost revenue. The corruption-logging nexus turns public natural resources into private profit for politically connected actors.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The fact that mining and logging licences were allegedly being facilitated through the PM’s own office — through his senior political secretary — means corruption in natural resource allocation goes to the very top of the Madani government.
