SCMP menyiarkannya. Ini adalah infrastruktur komunikasi anda. Ini adalah jenama Madani anda. Betulkan.

Sumber: SCMP | Bernama | FMT | CNA

item.title: [BM] Tengku Zafrul Rundingkan Perjanjian Perdagangan AS Yang Pengkritik Panggil 'Perjanjian Terburuk Sejak Merdeka' — Kemudian Dinaikkan Pangkat
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, former MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 26 Oktober 2025, Menteri MITI ketika itu Tengku Zafrul menandatangani Perjanjian Perdagangan Timbal Balik (ART) dengan Amerika Syarikat di Sidang Kemuncak ASEAN ke-47 di Kuala Lumpur. Perjanjian itu mengkomitkan Malaysia untuk menyediakan akses pasaran keutamaan bagi produk AS merentas bahan kimia, jentera, kenderaan, tenusu, ayam, daging babi, beras, dan etanol bahan api. Malaysia juga komited untuk tidak mengenakan larangan atau kuota ke atas eksport mineral kritikal atau nadir bumi ke AS. Sebagai balasan, AS mengekalkan tarif timbal balik 19% ke atas barangan Malaysia — dengan hanya 60.4% baris tarif pada kadar sifar. Pengkritik dari seluruh spektrum politik, termasuk Mahathir dan Rafizi Ramli, memanggilnya penyerahan berat sebelah.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Helaian fakta USTR diterbitkan di laman web Rumah Putih sebelum dikongsi dengan Parlimen Malaysia — bermakna Washington memberitahu dunia apa yang Malaysia persetujui sebelum penggubal undang-undang Malaysia sendiri tahu. Perjanjian itu memerlukan Malaysia menyelaraskan dengan sekatan AS, bertindak terhadap ‘amalan tidak adil’ oleh firma negara ketiga (baca: China), dan membuka pasaran dalam sektor di mana pengeluar Malaysia terdedah. Eurasia Review menyatakan perjanjian itu ‘disusun tergesa-gesa untuk menyenangkan presiden AS semasa lawatannya’ dan ’tidak bertujuan untuk menarik banyak perhatian.’ Ia menarik banyak perhatian.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Zafrul mempertahankan perjanjian itu sebagai ‘senario terbaik’ — seolah-olah berunding dari posisi lemah adalah satu pencapaian. Beliau memberitahu Parlimen: ‘Kita tiada pilihan selain berunding.’ Itu bukan pembelaan; itu pengakuan. Dasar luar neutral Malaysia — yang dikekalkan selama berdekad-dekad — terjejas dalam satu majlis tandatangan. Ganjaran Zafrul untuk ini? Kenaikan pangkat kepada Penasihat Politik Kanan PM dan pelantikan sebagai Pengerusi MIDA. Dalam Malaysia PH, anda dinaikkan pangkat kerana menandatangani penyerahan kedaulatan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia pernah menandatangani perjanjian perdagangan sebelum ini tanpa tahap kontroversi ini. CPTPP dirundingkan selama bertahun-tahun dengan penelitian parlimen yang meluas. ART digegas dalam beberapa minggu, ditandatangani di sidang kemuncak, dan dipertahankan selepas fakta. Corak keputusan ekonomi PH — dari rangkaian dwi 5G ke ECRL ke ini — adalah konsisten: buat pilihan yang selesa secara politik, kemudian habiskan berbulan-bulan mempertahankannya.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH: Menteri anda menandatangani perjanjian yang mengkomitkan Malaysia kepada pematuhan sekatan AS dan jaminan eksport mineral, dan pembelaan anda adalah ‘kita tiada pilihan.’ Anda sentiasa ada pilihan — anda memilih untuk tunduk. Kemudian anda naikkan pangkat orang yang melakukannya. Jika ini benar-benar ‘senario terbaik,’ posisi rundingan Malaysia di bawah PH lebih teruk daripada yang sesiapa bayangkan.

Sumber: CNA | USTR | Eurasia Review

item.title: Malaysia Committed to Never Banning Rare Earth Exports to the US — Under Zafrul's Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / USTR

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Under the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade signed by Tengku Zafrul on 26 October 2025, Malaysia committed to not imposing ‘quantitative restrictions’ (bans or quotas) on exports of critical or rare-earth minerals to the United States. The USTR fact sheet explicitly highlights this as a US victory. Meanwhile, Zafrul dismissed Mahathir’s claim that Malaysia agreed to supply unprocessed rare earth at low returns, saying existing policy mandates domestic processing before export.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Rare earth minerals are the 21st century’s most strategic resource — essential for semiconductors, defence systems, EVs, and renewable energy. China controls roughly 60% of global rare earth mining and 90% of processing. The US desperately needs alternative supplies, and Malaysia — with Lynas’s Kuantan plant — is a key node. By committing in writing to never restrict exports to the US, Malaysia surrendered one of its most powerful geopolitical leverage points for nothing more than maintaining the status quo tariff.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Indonesia, Australia, and other mineral-rich nations are using export controls as negotiating leverage to attract downstream processing and technology transfer. Malaysia gave that leverage away. The commitment means that even if a future government wants to use rare earth as a bargaining chip — as China has done effectively — Malaysia is contractually bound not to. Zafrul traded a strategic asset for a tariff rate that didn’t even improve.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: China’s rare earth export restrictions have been its most effective trade weapon for decades. Indonesia’s nickel export ban forced companies to build smelters domestically, creating jobs and value-add. Malaysia had the same opportunity — and signed it away in a trade deal.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You just gave the United States a written guarantee that Malaysia will never use its rare earth resources as leverage. Not for technology transfer. Not for downstream jobs. Not for better trade terms. For nothing. Your minister says Malaysia’s processing-first policy is intact — but the agreement says you can’t restrict exports. When Washington comes knocking for raw minerals, your ‘policy’ will fold against a treaty obligation.

Sources: USTR Fact Sheet | FMT

item.title: RTM Misidentifies THREE ASEAN Leaders on Live TV — Calls Singapore's PM 'Lee Hsien Loong' at Summit Malaysia Hosted
item.description:
Said by: RTM / Communications Ministry under Fahmi Fadzil

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 26 October 2025, during the live broadcast of the opening ceremony of the 47th ASEAN Summit — which Malaysia was hosting — the national broadcaster Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) misidentified three Southeast Asian leaders:

  • Singapore PM Lawrence Wong was called Lee Hsien Loong (his predecessor)
  • Thailand PM Anutin Charnvirakul was called Srettha Thavisin (his predecessor)
  • Indonesia President Prabowo Subianto was called Jokowi (his predecessor) RTM issued two separate apology statements. The broadcasting department said it “took the matter seriously” and that “appropriate action had been taken.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This was Malaysia’s moment on the world stage. The ASEAN Summit is the region’s premier diplomatic event, and Malaysia was the host. The entire region was watching. And Malaysia’s national broadcaster couldn’t get the names of three neighbouring heads of state right.

This isn’t a minor slip. These weren’t obscure officials — they were the leaders of Singapore, Thailand, and Indonesia, Malaysia’s closest neighbours. The fact that RTM’s commentators were working from outdated information suggests nobody at the broadcaster bothered to update their briefing materials. In the age of Google, this is inexcusable.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

RTM falls under the Communications Ministry, led by Fahmi Fadzil. This is the same ministry that talks about digital transformation, AI regulation, and building Malaysia’s media capabilities. They can’t even brief their commentators on who the current leaders of neighbouring countries are.

The apology — issued in two separate statements because they initially only acknowledged two of the three errors — was the cherry on top. You don’t get to embarrass Malaysia on live television at the summit Malaysia is hosting and then say “appropriate action has been taken” without telling anyone what that action was.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

RTM has been a running joke for decades. Underfunded, poorly managed, and treated as a government mouthpiece rather than a professional broadcaster. Under PH, there was talk of reforming state media. Instead, they got a Communications Minister who focuses on regulating TikTok for teenagers while RTM can’t name three world leaders correctly.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You hosted the ASEAN Summit. You wanted the world to see Malaysia as a serious, modern, competent nation. And your national broadcaster called three neighbouring leaders by their predecessors’ names on live TV. The entire region laughed at us. Mothership in Singapore ran it. CNA ran it. SCMP ran it. This is your communications infrastructure. This is your Madani brand. Fix it.

Sources: SCMP | Bernama | FMT | CNA

item.title: Tengku Zafrul Negotiated the US Trade Deal Critics Call 'Worst Agreement Since Independence' — Then Got Promoted
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, former MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 26 October 2025, then-MITI Minister Tengku Zafrul signed the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) with the United States at the 47th ASEAN Summit in Kuala Lumpur. The deal committed Malaysia to providing preferential market access for US products across chemicals, machinery, vehicles, dairy, poultry, pork, rice, and fuel ethanol. Malaysia also committed to not imposing bans or quotas on the export of critical or rare-earth minerals to the US. In return, the US maintained a 19% reciprocal tariff on Malaysian goods — with only 60.4% of tariff lines zero-rated. Critics from across the political spectrum, including Mahathir and Rafizi Ramli, called it a one-sided surrender.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The USTR fact sheet was published on the White House website before it was shared with Malaysia’s Parliament — meaning Washington told the world what Malaysia agreed to before Malaysia’s own legislators knew. The deal requires Malaysia to mirror US sanctions, act against ‘unfair practices’ by third-country firms (read: China), and open markets in sectors where Malaysian producers are vulnerable. Eurasia Review noted the deal was ‘hastily put together to please the US president during his visit’ and was ’not intended to catch much attention.’ It caught plenty.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Zafrul defended the deal as the ‘best case scenario’ — as if negotiating from weakness is an achievement. He told Parliament: ‘We had no choice but to negotiate.’ That is not a defence; it’s a confession. Malaysia’s neutral foreign policy — maintained for decades — was compromised in one signing ceremony. Zafrul’s reward for this? A promotion to Senior Political Advisor to the PM and appointment as MIDA chairman. In PH’s Malaysia, you get promoted for signing away sovereignty.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has signed trade agreements before without this level of controversy. The CPTPP was negotiated over years with extensive parliamentary scrutiny. The ART was rushed through in weeks, signed at a summit, and defended after the fact. The pattern of PH economic decisions — from 5G dual network to ECRL to this — is consistent: make the politically convenient choice, then spend months defending it.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your minister signed a deal that commits Malaysia to US sanctions compliance and mineral export guarantees, and your defence is ‘we had no choice.’ You always have a choice — you chose to capitulate. Then you promoted the man who did it. If this was really the ‘best case scenario,’ Malaysia’s negotiating position under PH is worse than anyone imagined.

Sources: CNA | USTR | Eurasia Review

item.title: US Trade Deal Details Published on White House Website Before Malaysian Parliament Was Told
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / White House

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: When the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade was signed on 26 October 2025, the USTR published a detailed fact sheet on the White House website outlining Malaysia’s commitments — including preferential market access for US goods, commitments on rare earth minerals, and tariff schedules. Malaysian MPs and the public learned the details of their own country’s trade agreement from an American government website. The deal’s terms were published in Washington before they were presented in Putrajaya.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A sovereign nation’s trade commitments should be communicated to its own parliament and citizens before they are announced by the other party. The fact that Malaysians had to read the White House website to understand what their government had agreed to is a humiliation of parliamentary sovereignty. It also meant the US controlled the narrative — the terms were framed as an American victory before Malaysia could spin its own story.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH promised transparent governance and parliamentary supremacy. Then Zafrul signed a trade agreement and let Washington publish the terms first. Fahmi Fadzil is the Communications Minister — where was the proactive Malaysian disclosure? The sequence tells you everything about the power dynamics: the US dictated the terms, dictated the timeline, and dictated the narrative. Malaysia’s role was to sign and defend.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s TPPA negotiations under Najib were also criticised for opacity — but at least the government attempted to control its own narrative. Under PH, the narrative was literally ceded to the White House. Eurasia Review noted the deal was ‘hastily put together’ and ’not intended to catch much attention.’ The haste is the point — rushed deals favour the stronger party.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You let the White House tell Malaysians what their own government agreed to. That is not a partnership of equals. That is a relationship where one party writes the press release and the other party nods. If you can’t even control the communications around your own trade deals, how do you expect Malaysians to believe you controlled the negotiations?

Sources: USTR Fact Sheet | Eurasia Review

item.title: US-Malaysia Trade Deal Called 'Worst Agreement Since Independence' and 'Act of Surrender'
item.description:
Said by: Tommy Thomas, Former Attorney General (and critics)

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

The Deal That Sold Malaysia’s Sovereignty

On 26 October 2025, Anwar Ibrahim and Donald Trump signed the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) at the ASEAN Summit. Former Attorney-General Tommy Thomas called it the worst agreement Malaysia has entered since independence in 1957. He was not exaggerating.

The Facts

Fact: The deal maintains a 19% US tariff on Malaysian goods — down from 25%, but still punitive by any standard. Malaysia’s effective tariff on US goods before this deal was roughly 6%. The US got the better end by a factor of three.

Fact: Malaysia committed to: enforce US tariff evasion rules against third countries, align with US export controls and sanctions regimes, not ban or restrict exports of critical minerals or rare earths to the US, refrain from imposing digital services taxes on US tech companies, and purchase additional American goods and services.

Fact: A termination clause allows the US to reimpose the original 25% tariff if Malaysia enters a trade deal that “jeopardizes essential US interests.” This effectively gives Washington veto power over Malaysia’s independent trade policy — including with China, Malaysia’s largest trading partner.

Fact: The deal’s full text was first published on the White House website before being shared with Malaysian Parliament. Malaysian lawmakers learned the terms of their own trade agreement from a foreign government’s press release.

Fact: Former PM Mahathir said the provisions amounted to “handing over” the country’s independence. Even for a man who oversaw his own share of questionable deals, this one was a bridge too far.

Fact: East Asia Forum’s analysis, titled “The Art of the Deal Lost in US-Malaysia Pact,” argued Malaysia surrendered critical mineral export controls, digital services taxation rights, and independent trade policy in exchange for a tariff cut that still left Malaysia paying three times what the US pays.

Fact: The Guardian reported that Anwar “defended” the deal by arguing it was “the best Malaysia could get under the circumstances” — an argument that concedes the deal is bad while claiming it could have been worse.

Fact: SCMP reported Anwar “slammed” critics for accusing him of surrendering sovereignty — but never addressed the substance of their arguments, only their tone.

Why It Matters

  • This is not a reciprocal deal by any definition of the word. Malaysia makes extensive, binding concessions across minerals, digital taxation, sanctions alignment, and procurement. The US reduces a punitive tariff by 6 percentage points and retains the right to reimpose it at will. In any negotiation textbook, this is called capitulation.
  • The sovereignty clause is the most dangerous provision. If Malaysia wants to deepen trade ties with China — its largest trading partner — the US can terminate the ART and reimpose 25% tariffs. Malaysia has effectively given Washington a veto over its foreign economic policy.
  • Publishing the details on the White House website before informing Parliament is not just embarrassing — it is a constitutional affront. Trade agreements that constrain national sovereignty should require parliamentary debate and approval before signing. Anwar bypassed the legislature on the most consequential economic agreement in decades.
  • Every ASEAN neighbour is watching. Indonesia walked away from a similar US proposal. Vietnam negotiated harder. Thailand delayed. Malaysia signed first and asked questions later. This weakens Malaysia’s negotiating position in every future bilateral and multilateral trade discussion.
  • The “best we could get” defence is an admission of weakness, not a justification. If this was truly the best outcome, it means Malaysia’s diplomatic leverage is weaker than the government has claimed — which raises questions about the entire Madani foreign policy brand. *Sources: The Guardian · East Asia Forum · SCMP · *Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Kes Buli Sekolah Melonjak 17% — 7,681 Kes Pada 2024 Meskipun 'Reformasi'
item.description:
Said by: Fadhlina Sidek, Education Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-18

Schools recorded 7,681 bullying cases in 2024, up from 6,528 in 2023 — a 17% increase. Teachers report students are ‘ruder, more restless, less curious.’ Education Ministry’s disciplinary reforms are clearly not working.

item.title: [BM] Kes Buli Sekolah Melonjak 17% — 7,681 Kes Pada 2024 Walaupun 'Reformasi'
item.description:
Said by: Fadhlina Sidek, Education Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-18

Sekolah mencatatkan 7,681 kes buli pada 2024, meningkat daripada 6,528 pada 2023 — kenaikan 17%. Guru melaporkan pelajar ’lebih kurang ajar, lebih gelisah, kurang ingin tahu.’ Reformasi disiplin Kementerian Pendidikan jelas tidak berkesan.

item.title: [BM] Sistem Persekolahan Malaysia Dalam 'Kemerosotan' — Pelajar Lebih Biadap, Kurang Ingin Tahu, Guru Terbeban
item.description:
Said by: FMT Opinion / Teachers

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 18 Oktober 2025, Free Malaysia Today menerbitkan rencana pendapat oleh guru-guru barisan hadapan yang menggambarkan sistem persekolahan dalam kemerosotan yang jelas. Penceritaan mereka terus terang: pelajar menatal TikTok di bawah meja semasa pengajaran, membentak guru apabila ditegur, enggan bekerjasama dengan aktiviti kelas, dan menunjukkan sedikit atau tiada rasa ingin tahu tentang apa yang diajar.

Guru-guru menggambarkan perjuangan yang kalah — bukan terhadap pelajar, tetapi terhadap algoritma. Gelung dopamin TikTok telah menjadi pihak berkuasa sebenar bilik darjah. Dan secara struktur, guru tiada apa-apa untuk melawan: tiada pedagogi yang dikemas kini, tiada dasar pengurusan peranti, tiada sokongan kesihatan mental, dan tiada pengakuan institusi bahawa masalah itu wujud.

Rencana itu melukiskan gambaran profesion dalam krisis senyap — bergaji rendah, kurang dihormati, dan kini dikalahkan oleh skrin telefon.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING

Sistem pendidikan Malaysia sudah bermasalah sebelum TikTok. Skor PISA telah mendatar atau menurun selama lebih sedekad. Keputusan PISA 2022 meletakkan pelajar Malaysia di bawah purata OECD dalam bacaan, matematik, dan sains — corak yang tidak bertambah baik secara bermakna sejak Malaysia mula menyertainya.

Apa yang diterangkan dalam rencana FMT bukan masalah baru. Ia adalah gejala yang jelas daripada pengabaian sistematik selama berdekad-dekad:

  • Bilik darjah sesak dengan 35–40 pelajar seorang guru di banyak sekolah bandar dan separuh bandar
  • Kurikulum lapuk yang mengutamakan hafalan berbanding pemikiran kritis
  • Keletihan guru didorong oleh beban pentadbiran, gaji rendah, dan sifar autonomi
  • Tiada rangka kerja literasi digital — pelajar ada peranti tetapi tiada panduan berstruktur untuk menggunakannya secara produktif
  • Penyerahan ibu bapa — telefon telah menjadi pengasuh, dan sekolah dijangka membaiki apa yang rumah telah tinggalkan Kemerosotan bukan sedang datang. Ia sudah tiba. Dan tiada siapa di Putrajaya yang melayannya sebagai krisis.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Menteri Pendidikan Fadhlina Sidek telah menghabiskan tempoh tugasnya mengumumkan inisiatif yang sedap didengar — program pengekodan, hab STEM, kempen “kegembiraan belajar” — sementara asas struktur sistem reput di bawahnya.

Mengenai kebajikan guru:

  • Guru-guru ditimbus dengan kerja perkeranian, kemasukan data, dan pentadbiran kokurikulum yang tiada kaitan dengan pengajaran. Respons kementerian adalah menjanjikan “mengurangkan beban kerja guru” — janji yang dibuat oleh setiap menteri pendidikan sekurang-kurangnya sejak 2015. Tiada apa yang berubah. Mengenai disiplin bilik darjah:

  • Tiada rangka kerja nasional yang koheren untuk menguruskan penggunaan peranti di sekolah. Sesetengah sekolah mengharamkan telefon sepenuhnya; yang lain mengabaikan isu itu sepenuhnya. Kementerian tidak mengeluarkan arahan mengikat, menyerahkan setiap guru untuk berjuang sendiri terhadap ekonomi perhatian berbilion dolar. Mengenai reformasi kurikulum:

  • Kurikulum kebangsaan (KSSM) terakhir dikemas kini pada 2017. Ia masih menekankan prestasi peperiksaan berbanding pembelajaran tulen. Sistem ini menghasilkan pelajar yang boleh lulus SPM tetapi tidak boleh menulis perenggan yang koheren, menilai sumber, atau mengadakan perbualan berterusan tentang apa-apa yang tiada di skrin. Mengenai pembiayaan:

  • Malaysia membelanjakan kira-kira 3.2% daripada KDNK untuk pendidikan — di bawah purata global 4.3% dan jauh di bawah rakan serantau seperti Vietnam (4.1%) dan Thailand (3.8%). PH berjanji untuk mengutamakan perbelanjaan pendidikan. Bajet 2025 dan 2026 bercerita lain. Pendekatan kementerian adalah kosmetik. Umumkan program rintis. Ambil gambar. Teruskan. Sementara itu, satu generasi pelajar sedang dipinggirkan secara langsung.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

  • Keputusan PISA 2022: Malaysia mendapat 398 dalam matematik, 388 dalam bacaan, dan 398 dalam sains — semuanya di bawah purata OECD. Prestasi mendatar sejak 2012 meskipun berbilion dibelanjakan untuk pendidikan.
  • 2019: Krisis beban kerja guru. Kesatuan Kebangsaan Profesion Perguruan (NUTP) secara terbuka memberi amaran bahawa guru menghabiskan lebih banyak masa dengan kertas kerja daripada mengajar. Kementerian menjanjikan reformasi. Guru melaporkan tiada perubahan bermakna.
  • 2023: Perdebatan larangan telefon. Selepas pelbagai insiden viral pelajar menggunakan telefon di kelas, seruan untuk larangan telefon nasional mendapat momentum. Kementerian memilih “garis panduan” berbanding arahan, menyerahkan penguatkuasaan kepada sekolah individu.
  • 2024: Pengangguran graduan. Data Bank Negara dan DOSM menunjukkan kira-kira 1 daripada 4 graduan Malaysia bawah 30 tahun sama ada menganggur atau kurang guna tenaga. Sistem pendidikan menghasilkan graduan yang ekonomi tidak perlukan dengan kemahiran yang pasaran tidak mahu.
  • Pelan Pembangunan Pendidikan Malaysia 2013–2025. Pelan tindakan itu berjanji untuk membawa Malaysia ke “satu pertiga teratas” penanda aras pendidikan antarabangsa menjelang 2025. Tarikh akhir telah berlalu. Malaysia tidak pun hampir dengan sasaran.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH

Fadhlina, guru-guru anda memberitahu anda — secara terbuka, dalam media kebangsaan — bahawa mereka kehilangan bilik darjah mereka. Pelajar lebih biadap. Kurang ingin tahu. Kurang terlibat. Dan sistem tidak menawarkan guru apa-apa untuk melawan.

Respons anda adalah program rintis, sesi bergambar, dan janji-janji kabur tentang “pembelajaran abad ke-21.” Tiada satupun yang sampai kepada guru yang berdiri di hadapan 38 remaja yang lebih suka menonton TikTok 15 saat daripada belajar apa-apa.

Anda mewarisi sistem yang rosak. Itu benar. Tetapi anda berjanji untuk membaikinya. Dua tahun berlalu, skor PISA tidak bergerak, guru-guru lebih keletihan daripada sebelumnya, dan kurikulum masih melayan hafalan sebagai penguasaan.

Jika PH mahu mendakwa ia mentadbir secara berbeza, mulakan dengan satu institusi yang menyentuh setiap keluarga Malaysia. Danainya dengan sewajarnya. Bebaskan guru daripada kerja birokrasi. Laksanakan rangka kerja literasi digital dan pengurusan peranti yang sebenar. Reformasikan kurikulum supaya ia memberi ganjaran kepada pemikiran, bukan penghafalan.

Atau teruskan apa yang anda buat — dan saksikan seluruh generasi menatal menuju ketidakrelevanan.

Sumber: FMT | PISA 2022 | World Bank — Malaysia Education | Malaysiakini

item.title: [BM] Sistem Persekolahan Malaysia Dalam 'Kemerosotan' — Pelajar Lebih Kurang Ajar, Kurang Ingin Tahu, Guru Terbeban
item.description:
Said by: FMT Opinion / Teachers

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 18 Oktober 2025, Free Malaysia Today menerbitkan rencana pendapat oleh guru barisan hadapan yang menggambarkan sistem persekolahan dalam kemerosotan yang ketara. Perincian mereka terus terang: pelajar menatal TikTok di bawah meja semasa pengajaran, membalas kasar kepada guru apabila ditegur, enggan bekerjasama dengan aktiviti bilik darjah, dan menunjukkan sedikit atau tiada rasa ingin tahu tentang apa yang diajar.

Guru menggambarkan perjuangan yang semakin kalah — bukan melawan pelajar, tetapi melawan algoritma. Gelung dopamin TikTok telah menjadi pihak berkuasa sebenar bilik darjah. Dan secara struktur, guru tidak mempunyai apa-apa untuk membalas: tiada pedagogi yang dikemas kini, tiada dasar pengurusan peranti, tiada sokongan kesihatan mental, dan tiada pengiktirafan institusi bahawa masalah itu wujud.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING

Sistem pendidikan Malaysia sudah bermasalah sebelum TikTok. Skor PISA telah statik atau menurun selama lebih sedekad. Keputusan PISA 2022 meletakkan pelajar Malaysia di bawah purata OECD dalam bacaan, matematik, dan sains — corak yang tidak bertambah baik secara bermakna sejak Malaysia mula menyertai.

Apa yang diterangkan rencana FMT bukan masalah baru. Ia adalah gejala ketara berdekad pengabaian sistemik:

  • Bilik darjah yang sesak dengan 35–40 pelajar setiap guru di banyak sekolah bandar dan separuh bandar
  • Kurikulum lapuk yang mengutamakan hafalan berbanding pemikiran kritis
  • Keletihan guru didorong oleh beban pentadbiran, gaji rendah, dan tiada autonomi
  • Tiada rangka kerja celik digital — pelajar ada peranti tetapi tiada panduan berstruktur
  • Penyerahan ibu bapa — telefon telah menjadi pengasuh, dan sekolah dijangka membaiki apa yang rumah telah tinggalkan Kemerosotan bukan akan datang. Ia sudah ada di sini. Dan tiada siapa di Putrajaya yang melayannya sebagai krisis.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT

Menteri Pendidikan Fadhlina Sidek telah menghabiskan tempoh beliau mengumumkan inisiatif yang sedap didengar — program pengekodan, hab STEM, kempen “kegembiraan belajar” — sementara asas struktur sistem reput di bawah.

Mengenai kebajikan guru: Guru ditimbus dengan kerja perkeranian, kemasukan data, dan pentadbiran kokurikulum yang tiada kaitan dengan pengajaran. Janji “mengurangkan beban kerja guru” telah dibuat sejak sekurang-kurangnya 2015. Tiada yang berubah.

Mengenai disiplin bilik darjah: Tiada rangka kerja nasional yang koheren untuk menguruskan penggunaan peranti di sekolah.

Mengenai reformasi kurikulum: Kurikulum nasional (KSSM) terakhir dikemas kini pada 2017. Ia masih menekankan prestasi peperiksaan berbanding pembelajaran sebenar.

Mengenai pembiayaan: Malaysia membelanjakan kira-kira 3.2% KDNK untuk pendidikan — di bawah purata global 4.3%.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

  • Keputusan PISA 2022: Malaysia memperoleh 398 dalam matematik, 388 dalam bacaan, dan 398 dalam sains — semuanya di bawah purata OECD.
  • 2019: Krisis beban kerja guru. NUTP secara terbuka memberi amaran bahawa guru menghabiskan lebih banyak masa untuk kertas kerja berbanding mengajar.
  • 2024: Pengangguran graduan. Kira-kira 1 daripada 4 graduan Malaysia bawah 30 tahun sama ada menganggur atau kurang guna tenaga.
  • Pelan Pendidikan Malaysia 2013–2025. Berjanji membawa Malaysia ke “satu pertiga teratas” penanda aras pendidikan antarabangsa menjelang 2025. Tarikh akhir telah berlalu. Malaysia tidak pun hampir ke sasaran.

🎯 TEGURAN PH

Fadhlina, guru anda memberitahu anda — secara terbuka, di media nasional — bahawa mereka kehilangan bilik darjah mereka. Pelajar lebih kurang ajar. Kurang ingin tahu. Kurang terlibat. Dan sistem tidak menawarkan guru apa-apa untuk membalas.

Jika PH mahu mendakwa ia mentadbir secara berbeza, mulakan dengan satu institusi yang menyentuh setiap keluarga Malaysia. Biayai dengan wajar. Bebaskan guru daripada kerja birokrasi. Reformasi kurikulum supaya ia menghargai pemikiran, bukan regurgitasi.

Sumber: FMT | PISA 2022 | World Bank

item.title: Malaysia's Schooling System in 'Decline' — Students Ruder, Less Curious, Teachers Overwhelmed
item.description:
Said by: FMT Opinion / Teachers

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 18 October 2025, Free Malaysia Today published an opinion piece by frontline teachers describing a schooling system in visible decline. The accounts were blunt: students scrolling TikTok under desks during lessons, snapping back at teachers when corrected, refusing to cooperate with classroom activities, and showing little to no curiosity about what’s being taught.

Teachers described fighting a losing battle — not against students, but against the algorithm. TikTok’s dopamine loop has become the real classroom authority. And structurally, teachers have nothing to fight back with: no updated pedagogy, no device management policy, no mental health support, and no institutional acknowledgment that the problem even exists.

The piece painted a picture of a profession in quiet crisis — underpaid, under-respected, and now outgunned by a phone screen.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Malaysia’s education system was already in trouble before TikTok. PISA scores have been stagnant or declining for over a decade. The 2022 PISA results placed Malaysian students below the OECD average in reading, mathematics, and science — a pattern that has not meaningfully improved since Malaysia first participated.

What the FMT piece describes is not a new problem. It is the visible symptom of decades of systemic neglect:

  • Overcrowded classrooms with 35–40 students per teacher in many urban and semi-urban schools
  • Outdated curricula that prioritise rote memorisation over critical thinking
  • Teacher burnout driven by administrative overload, low pay, and zero autonomy
  • No digital literacy framework — students have devices but no structured guidance on using them productively
  • Parental abdication — the phone has become the babysitter, and schools are expected to fix what homes have abandoned The decline isn’t coming. It’s here. And no one in Putrajaya is treating it as a crisis.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek has spent her tenure announcing feel-good initiatives — coding programmes, STEM hubs, “joy of learning” campaigns — while the structural foundations of the system rot underneath.

On teacher welfare:

  • Teachers are buried in clerical work, data entry, and co-curricular admin that has nothing to do with teaching. The ministry’s response has been to promise “reducing teacher workload” — a promise made by every education minister since at least 2015. Nothing has changed. On classroom discipline:

  • There is no coherent national framework for managing device use in schools. Some schools ban phones outright; others ignore the issue entirely. The ministry has issued no binding directive, leaving every teacher to fend for themselves against a billion-dollar attention economy. On curriculum reform:

  • The national curriculum (KSSM) was last updated in 2017. It still emphasises exam performance over genuine learning. The system produces students who can pass SPM but cannot write a coherent paragraph, evaluate a source, or hold a sustained conversation about anything that isn’t on a screen. On funding:

  • Malaysia spends roughly 3.2% of GDP on education — below the global average of 4.3% and well below regional peers like Vietnam (4.1%) and Thailand (3.8%). PH promised to prioritise education spending. The 2025 and 2026 budgets tell a different story. The ministry’s approach is cosmetic. Announce a pilot programme. Take photos. Move on. Meanwhile, a generation of students is being failed in real time.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

  • 2022 PISA Results: Malaysia scored 398 in mathematics, 388 in reading, and 398 in science — all below the OECD average. Performance has flatlined since 2012 despite billions in education spending.
  • 2019: Teacher workload crisis. The National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) publicly warned that teachers were spending more time on paperwork than teaching. The ministry promised reform. Teachers report no meaningful change.
  • 2023: Phone ban debate. After multiple viral incidents of students using phones in class, calls for a national phone ban gained traction. The ministry opted for “guidelines” rather than a directive, leaving enforcement to individual schools.
  • 2024: Graduate unemployment. Bank Negara and DOSM data showed that roughly 1 in 4 Malaysian graduates under 30 were either unemployed or underemployed. The education system is producing graduates the economy doesn’t need with skills the market doesn’t want.
  • Malaysia Education Blueprint 2013–2025. The blueprint promised to bring Malaysia into the “top third” of international education benchmarks by 2025. The deadline has passed. Malaysia is nowhere near the target.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

Fadhlina, your teachers are telling you — publicly, in national media — that they are losing their classrooms. Students are ruder. Less curious. Less engaged. And the system offers teachers nothing to fight back with.

Your response has been pilot programmes, photo ops, and vague promises about “21st-century learning.” None of it reaches the teacher standing in front of 38 teenagers who would rather watch a 15-second TikTok than learn anything.

You inherited a broken system. That’s true. But you promised to fix it. Two years in, the PISA scores haven’t moved, the teachers are more burned out than ever, and the curriculum still treats memorisation as mastery.

If PH wants to claim it governs differently, start with the one institution that touches every Malaysian family. Fund it properly. Free teachers from bureaucratic busywork. Implement a real digital literacy and device management framework. Reform the curriculum so it rewards thinking, not regurgitation.

Or keep doing what you’re doing — and watch an entire generation scroll its way to irrelevance.

Sources: FMT | PISA 2022 | World Bank — Malaysia Education | Malaysiakini

item.title: School Bullying Cases Surge 17% — 7,681 Cases in 2024 Despite 'Reforms'
item.description:

item.title: [BM] Menteri Pendidikan Dikecam — 'Sistem Gagalkan Guru Dan Pelajar' Kata Menteri PH Sendiri
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad / Fadhlina Sidek (Education Minister)

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-17

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Menteri Pendidikan Fadhlina Sidek menghadapi kritikan terbuka dari pelbagai pihak — termasuk rakan gabungannya sendiri, Menteri Sumber Asli Nik Nazmi, yang secara terbuka menyatakan bahawa ‘krisis pendidikan berpunca daripada sistem yang gagalkan kedua-dua guru dan pelajar.’ Dengan 7,681 kes buli pada 2024 (naik 17%), 10,160 pelajar tidak hadir SPM, kadar kegagalan matematik 25%, dan 30% calon SPM gagal sekurang-kurangnya satu subjek, sistem ini dalam keruntuhan.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Apabila rakan kabinet anda sendiri secara terbuka mengkritik portfolio anda, bermakna masalah itu sudah tidak boleh dinafikan. Angka-angkanya membinasakan: subjek berprestasi terbaik dalam SPM ialah ‘Quran dan Sunnah’ pada kadar lulus 84%, sementara matematik hanya 75% dan sains 74%. Sistem yang menghasilkan pengetahuan agama lebih berkesan daripada kecekapan asas matematik bukan menyediakan pelajar untuk abad ke-21.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Respons kerajaan bersifat kosmetik: mansuhkan peperiksaan (UPSR dan PMR dimansuhkan), turunkan ambang lulus (20% dilaporkan boleh lulus matematik), dan jalankan kempen komunikasi. Mereka pada dasarnya menyembunyikan masalah dengan membuang alat pengukuran. Seperti yang ditulis seorang penganalisis: ‘perjalanannya nampaknya hanya untuk mempromosikan ketidakcekapan dari satu peringkat ke peringkat lain sehingga pelajar sampai ke SPM.’ Apabila anda mansuhkan ujian yang gagal dan bukannya membaiki sekolah yang gagal, anda memilih kejahilan berbanding reformasi.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Pelan Pembangunan Pendidikan Malaysia 2013-2025 sepatutnya menjadi penyelesaian menyeluruh. Ia adalah dokumen yang direka cantik yang hampir tidak mengubah apa-apa. Sebelum itu, ada Pelan Induk Pembangunan Pendidikan 2006-2010. Sebelum itu, inisiatif Sekolah Bestari. Berdekad-dekad ‘pelan transformasi’ telah menghasilkan keputusan PISA terburuk Malaysia dalam sejarah. Coraknya jelas: umumkan pelan → danai kempen PR → abaikan pelaksanaan → umumkan pelan baru.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Menteri anda sudah memegang jawatan sejak 2022. Keputusan telah menjadi LEBIH TERUK, bukan lebih baik. Daripada akauntabiliti, anda beri peluang bergambar. Daripada membiayai sekolah, anda membiayai JAKIM (RM2.6B) dan propaganda J-Kom (RM85.5J). Kanak-kanak Malaysia layak mendapat pendidikan bertaraf dunia, bukan pelan komunikasi bertaraf dunia tentang pendidikan. Pecat menteri pendidikan anda atau baiki sistem — lebih baik kedua-duanya.

Sumber

  • The Star — Krisis Pendidikan
  • TheIns — Bagaimana Pendidikan Malaysia Gagal
  • Fulcrum — Fikirkan Pelaksanaan

item.title: Education Minister Under Fire — 'System Failed Both Teachers and Students' Says Own PH Minister
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad / Fadhlina Sidek (Education Minister)

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-17

📌 What Happened

Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek faced open criticism from multiple quarters — including her own coalition colleague, Natural Resources Minister Nik Nazmi, who publicly stated that the ’education crisis stems from a system that failed both teachers and students.’ With 7,681 bullying cases in 2024 (up 17%), 10,160 SPM no-shows, 25% math failure rates, and 30% of SPM candidates failing at least one subject, the system is in freefall.

🔥 Why It Matters

When your own cabinet colleague publicly criticises your portfolio, it means the problem has become undeniable. The numbers are catastrophic: the best-performing subject in SPM is ‘Quran and Sunnah’ at 84% pass rate, while mathematics languishes at 75% and science at 74%. A system that produces religious knowledge more effectively than basic numeracy is not preparing students for the 21st century.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government’s response has been cosmetic: remove exams (UPSR and PMR abolished), lower pass thresholds (20% reportedly passes math), and run communication campaigns. They’ve essentially hidden the problem by removing the measurement tools. As one analyst wrote: ’the journey seems to be just to promote incompetence from one stage to the other until students reach SPM.’ When you abolish failing tests instead of fixing failing schools, you’re choosing ignorance over reform.

📜 Previous Cases

The Malaysia Education Blueprint 2013-2025 was supposed to be the comprehensive solution. It was a beautifully designed document that changed almost nothing. Before that, there was the Education Development Master Plan 2006-2010. Before that, the Smart Schools initiative. Decades of ’transformation plans’ have produced Malaysia’s worst PISA results in history. The pattern is clear: announce plan → fund PR campaign → ignore implementation → announce new plan.

🎯 PH Callout

Your minister has been in the job since 2022. Results have gotten WORSE, not better. Instead of accountability, you give her photo opportunities. Instead of funding schools, you fund JAKIM (RM2.6B) and J-Kom propaganda (RM85.5M). Malaysian children deserve a world-class education, not a world-class communications plan about education. Fire your education minister or fix the system — preferably both.

Sources

  • The Star — Education Crisis
  • TheIns — How Malaysia’s Education Is Failing
  • Fulcrum — Think of Implementation

item.title: [BM] Dana SMUE RM200 Juta Didakwa Diseleweng — SPRM Siasat Skim Warga Emas Selangor
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-16

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Suruhanjaya Pencegahan Rasuah Malaysia melancarkan siasatan terhadap dakwaan penyelewengan RM200 juta daripada Skim Mesra Usia Emas (SMUE) Selangor — skim kebajikan warga emas negeri itu. Beberapa pegawai yang dikaitkan dengan skim itu disoal siasat. Dana SMUE direka untuk menyediakan bantuan kewangan kepada warga emas Selangor, menjadikan dakwaan penyelewengan itu amat keji — wang yang dimaksudkan untuk warga emas didakwa dialihkan ke tempat lain di bawah pemantauan PH.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: RM200 juta bukan kesilapan pembundaran — ia adalah jumlah besar yang didakwa dicuri daripada program kebajikan untuk warga emas. Selangor adalah permata mahkota PH, negeri terkaya, ditadbir secara berterusan oleh PKR/PH sejak 2008. Jika RM200 juta boleh hilang daripada dana kebajikan warga emas di negeri yang dikawal PH, apa yang ia katakan tentang dakwaan PH sebagai gabungan ’tadbir urus bersih’? Dana itu sepatutnya membantu golongan paling lemah. Sebaliknya, ia mungkin telah membantu orang dalam.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: PH telah mentadbir Selangor selama hampir 18 tahun. Ini bukan masalah warisan BN — ini berlaku di bawah pemantauan PH, di bawah lantikan PH, di negeri PH. Parti yang membina jenamanya atas antirasuah menghadapi siasatan rasuah di negeri paling selamatnya. Pejabat Amirudin sangat senyap mengenai siasatan itu. Tiada kenyataan umum menuntut akauntabiliti. Tiada penggantungan pegawai. Tiada audit bebas diumumkan. Senyap.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Ini menyertai siasatan perlombongan pasir, pembekuan aset MBI, dan dakwaan kekayaan yang tidak dapat dijelaskan sebagai sebahagian daripada corak. Selangor di bawah PH bukan model tadbir urus bersih — ia adalah negeri di mana SPRM terus menemui sesuatu. Pada satu ketika, satu siasatan adalah pengecualian; empat siasatan adalah corak.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH: RM200 juta daripada dana warga emas. Di negeri anda. Di bawah pemantauan anda. Dengan lantikan anda. Jika BN melakukan ini, anda akan mengadakan perhimpunan kebangsaan. Oleh kerana ia negeri anda, anda mengadakan sidang akhbar tentang pengurusan sisa. Tuntut audit awam penuh SMUE. Gantung pegawai-pegawai tersebut. Dan beritahu rakyat ke mana wang itu pergi.

Sumber: FMT | Facebook/FMT

Tiada penggantungan pegawai. Tiada audit bebas diumumkan. Senyap.

item.title: RM200 Million SMUE Fund Allegedly Misappropriated — MACC Probes Selangor Senior Citizens' Scheme
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission launched an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of RM200 million from Selangor’s Skim Mesra Usia Emas (SMUE) — the state’s elderly welfare scheme. Multiple officials linked to the scheme were questioned. The SMUE fund was designed to provide financial assistance to Selangor’s senior citizens, making the alleged misappropriation particularly egregious — money meant for the elderly allegedly diverted elsewhere under PH’s watch.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: RM200 million is not a rounding error — it’s a massive sum allegedly stolen from a welfare programme for senior citizens. Selangor is PH’s crown jewel, the richest state, run continuously by PKR/PH since 2008. If RM200 million can go missing from an elderly welfare fund in a PH-controlled state, what does that say about PH’s claim to be the ‘clean governance’ coalition? The fund was supposed to help the most vulnerable. Instead, it may have helped insiders.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH has governed Selangor for nearly 18 years. This isn’t an inherited BN problem — this happened on PH’s watch, under PH’s appointees, in PH’s state. The party that built its brand on anti-corruption is facing a corruption probe in its safest state. Amirudin’s office has been conspicuously quiet about the investigation. No public statements demanding accountability. No suspension of officials. No independent audit announced. Silence.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This joins the sand mining probe, the MBI asset freeze, and the unexplained wealth allegations as part of a pattern. Selangor under PH isn’t a model of clean governance — it’s a state where MACC keeps finding things. At some point, one investigation is an outlier; four investigations is a pattern.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: RM200 million from a senior citizens’ fund. In your state. Under your watch. With your appointees. If BN did this, you’d hold a national rally. Since it’s your state, you hold a press conference about waste management. Demand a full public audit of SMUE. Suspend the officials. And tell the rakyat where the money went.

Sources: FMT | Facebook/FMT

item.title: [BM] 77% Rakyat Malaysia Bergelut Dengan Kos Sara Hidup Meningkat — Tapi Anwar Kata Ekonomi 'Kukuh'
item.description:
Said by: Consumer Frontiers International survey

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-15

77% Berbanding KDNK

Pada Oktober 2025, tinjauan mendapati bahawa 77% rakyat Malaysia berkata mereka bergelut dengan kos sara hidup yang meningkat. Pada bulan yang sama, kerajaan memetik pertumbuhan KDNK dan angka pelaburan sebagai bukti bahawa ekonomi kukuh.

Fakta

Fakta: Consumer Frontiers International mendapati 77% rakyat Malaysia bergelut dengan kos meningkat walaupun gaji meningkat.

Fakta: DOSM melaporkan purata pendapatan isi rumah boleh guna bulanan meningkat 3.2% kepada RM7,584 pada 2024.

Fakta: Tetapi ahli ekonomi Lee Heng Guie memberi amaran: “Kita juga mesti mengiktiraf bahawa kos sara hidup terus meningkat.” Keuntungan sebenar sedang dihakis oleh inflasi.

Fakta: The Star meletakkan tajuk: “Kos sara hidup yang meningkat meredamkan keuntungan pendapatan.”

Kenapa Ia Penting

  • Pertumbuhan KDNK dan pelaburan yang diluluskan adalah angka makro. Ia tidak mencerminkan apa yang 77% penduduk sedang alami.
  • Apabila tiga perempat rakyat anda berkata mereka bergelut, memetik angka pelaburan adalah pekak nada.
  • Jurang antara statistik kerajaan dan pengalaman sebenar adalah jurang yang mewujudkan gerakan protes. *Sumber: Business Today · *The Star

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Amaran: Dakwaan Farhash Jejaskan Kelayakan Antirasuah PM
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-15

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Rafizi secara terbuka menggesa Anwar untuk berhadapan dengan dakwaan terhadap Farhash Wafa, memberi amaran bahawa CPI Malaysia mendatar pada 50 mata — skor yang sama seperti 2015 semasa kemuncak skandal 1MDB. Kata ia ‘amat berbahaya jika pentadbiran dilihat tidak konsisten dalam memerangi rasuah.’

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Apabila Menteri Ekonomi anda sendiri secara terbuka memberi amaran bahawa kelayakan antirasuah anda sedang runtuh, masalah itu tidak boleh dinafikan. Rafizi secara efektif memberitahu Anwar: keengganan anda bertindak terhadap Farhash secara terukur merosakkan skor rasuah negara. CPI 50 di bawah Anwar adalah sama seperti di bawah Najib semasa 1MDB — perbandingan itu sahaja sudah membinasakan.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Amaran Rafizi diabaikan; beliau kemudian dipinggirkan daripada kabinet. Menteri yang berkata benar dihukum, sementara pembantu yang dituduh rasuah dilindungi. Ini bukan reformasi — ia adalah buku panduan lama dalam bungkusan baru.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Menteri paling cekap anda memberi amaran secara terbuka bahawa melindungi Farhash sedang memusnahkan kelayakan antirasuah anda. Respons anda adalah meminggirkan menteri dan melindungi pembantu. Dalam mana-mana kerajaan reformasi, itu terbalik. Dalam kerajaan anda, ia adalah prosedur operasi standard.

Sumber

  • The Vibes — Rafizi Mengenai Farhash

item.title: 77% of Malaysians Struggle With Rising Living Costs — But Anwar Says Economy Is 'Strong'
item.description:
Said by: Consumer Frontiers International survey

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-15

The 77% vs The GDP

In October 2025, a survey found that 77% of Malaysians said they struggled with rising living costs. In the same month, the government was citing GDP growth and investment figures as evidence that the economy was strong.

The Facts

Fact: Consumer Frontiers International found 77% of Malaysians struggled with rising costs despite rising wages.

Fact: DOSM reported average monthly disposable household income increased 3.2% to RM7,584 in 2024.

Fact: But economist Lee Heng Guie warned: “We must also recognise that the cost of living continues to rise.” Real gains were being eroded by inflation.

Fact: The Star headlined it: “Rising cost of living dampens income gains.”

Why It Matters

  • GDP growth and approved investments are macro numbers. They do not reflect what 77% of the population is experiencing.
  • When three-quarters of your citizens say they’re struggling, citing investment figures is tone-deaf.
  • The gap between government statistics and lived experience is the gap that creates protest movements. *Sources: Business Today · *The Star

item.title: Rafizi Warns: Farhash Allegations Undermine PM's Anti-Corruption Credentials
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-15

📌 What Happened

Rafizi publicly urged Anwar to confront allegations against Farhash Wafa, warning that Malaysia’s CPI flatlined at 50 points — the same score as 2015 during the peak 1MDB scandal. Said it’s ’extremely dangerous if the administration is seen as inconsistent in combating corruption.'

🔥 Why It Matters

When your own Economy Minister publicly warns that your anti-corruption credentials are collapsing, the problem is undeniable. Rafizi effectively told Anwar: your refusal to act against Farhash is measurably damaging the country’s corruption score. A CPI of 50 under Anwar is the same as under Najib during 1MDB — that comparison alone is devastating.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Rafizi’s warning was ignored; he was later sidelined from cabinet. The minister who told the truth was punished, while the aide accused of corruption was protected. This is not reform — it is the old playbook in new packaging.

🎯 PH Callout

Your most competent minister warned you publicly that protecting Farhash was destroying your anti-corruption credentials. Your response was to sideline the minister and protect the aide. In any reform government, that’s backwards. In yours, it’s standard operating procedure.

Sources

  • The Vibes — Rafizi on Farhash

item.title: [BM] Anwar Periksa Kerja Sendiri — PM Yang Juga Menteri Kewangan Bentangkan Bajet Dan Dakwa Ia Hebat
item.description:
Said by: Opposition / Budget Analysts

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Bajet 2026 dibentangkan oleh Anwar Ibrahim — sebagai Menteri Kewangan — kepada parlimen, di mana beliau duduk sebagai Perdana Menteri. Beliau kemudian mempertahankan bajetnya sendiri di parlimen, memuji pencapaiannya, dan menolak kritikan. Orang yang menulis peperiksaan juga orang yang memberikan markah.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Dalam mana-mana demokrasi yang berfungsi, fungsi kewangan mempunyai pengawasan bebas daripada kepimpinan politik. Apabila satu orang mengawal kedua-dua agenda politik dan dompet negara, tiada semakan bermakna terhadap keputusan fiskal. Peruntukan bajet menjadi alat politik tanpa penelitian bebas. Peranan Parlimen sebagai badan pengawasan bajet terjejas apabila PM/MK mengawal kedua-dua eksekutif dan agenda fiskal.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Anwar mengkritik Najib SECARA KHUSUS kerana memegang kedua-dua portfolio PM dan MK, berhujah ia membolehkan 1MDB dengan menghapuskan pengawasan. Kemudian Anwar mengambil KEDUA-DUA portfolio sendiri. Apabila ditanya mengenai ini, respons beliau pada dasarnya ialah ‘percayalah, saya berbeza.’ Itu bukan reformasi institusi — ia adalah pengecualian peribadi. Kelemahan struktur yang sama yang membolehkan 1MDB wujud di bawah Anwar.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Najib sebagai PM+MK: 1MDB berlaku. Mahathir sebagai PM: peranan Menteri Kewangan menyediakan sedikit pemisahan. Di bawah prinsip tadbir urus baik, pengawasan fiskal memerlukan kebebasan institusi, bukan kepercayaan peribadi. Singapura memisahkan peranan tersebut. UK memisahkan peranan tersebut. Kebanyakan demokrasi yang berfungsi memisahkan peranan tersebut.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Anda dipilih kerana PM terakhir yang memegang kedua-dua portfolio mencuri berbilion. Penyelesaian anda untuk mencegah 1MDB seterusnya ialah… meniru kelemahan struktur yang sama. Anda memeriksa kerja sendiri, dan tidak mengejutkan, anda terus memberi diri sendiri A+. Lantik Menteri Kewangan berasingan atau berhenti berpura-pura reformasi institusi penting bagi anda.

Sumber

  • Reuters — Bajet Malaysia & Kos Sara Hidup

Apabila ditanya tentang ini, responsnya pada dasarnya ialah ‘percayalah, saya berbeza.’ Itu bukan reformasi institusi — ia adalah pengecualian peribadi. 📜 Kes Terdahulu

Di bawah prinsip tadbir urus baik, pengawasan fiskal memerlukan kebebasan institusi, bukan kepercayaan peribadi. Singapura memisahkan peranan tersebut. UK memisahkan peranan tersebut. Kebanyakan demokrasi yang berfungsi memisahkan peranan tersebut.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda menandakan kerja rumah sendiri, dan tidak menghairankan, anda terus memberi diri sendiri A+. Lantik Menteri Kewangan berasingan atau berhenti berpura-pura reformasi institusi penting bagi anda.

Sumber

item.title: Anwar Marks His Own Homework — PM Who Is Also Finance Minister Presents Budget and Claims It's Great
item.description:
Said by: Opposition / Budget Analysts

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-10

📌 What Happened

Budget 2026 was presented by Anwar Ibrahim — as Finance Minister — to parliament, where he sits as Prime Minister. He then defended his own budget in parliament, praised its achievements, and dismissed criticism. The person who wrote the exam is also the person who grades it.

🔥 Why It Matters

In any functional democracy, the finance function has oversight independent from the political leadership. When one person controls both the political agenda and the national wallet, there is no meaningful check on fiscal decisions. Budget allocations become political tools without independent scrutiny. Parliament’s role as a budget oversight body is undermined when the PM/FM controls both the executive and the fiscal agenda.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar criticised Najib SPECIFICALLY for holding both PM and FM portfolios, arguing it enabled 1MDB by removing oversight. Then Anwar took BOTH portfolios himself. When asked about this, his response was essentially ’trust me, I’m different.’ That’s not institutional reform — it’s personal exceptionalism. The same structural vulnerability that enabled 1MDB exists under Anwar.

📜 Previous Cases

Najib as PM+FM: 1MDB happened. Mahathir as PM: the Finance Minister role provided some separation. Under good governance principles, fiscal oversight requires institutional independence, not personal trust. Singapore separates the roles. The UK separates the roles. Most functional democracies separate the roles.

🎯 PH Callout

You were elected because the last PM who held both portfolios stole billions. Your solution to preventing the next 1MDB was… to replicate the exact same structural vulnerability. You’re marking your own homework, and unsurprisingly, you keep giving yourself A+. Appoint a separate Finance Minister or stop pretending institutional reform matters to you.

Sources

  • Reuters — Malaysia Budget & Living Costs

item.title: [BM] Krisis Kemampuan Perumahan: B40 Dan M40 Terkunci Kerana Harga Kekal Di Luar Jangkauan
item.description:
Said by: Multiple economists and NAPIC

Category: Housing

Date: 2025-10-07

Janji Bumbung Yang Tidak Pernah Tiba

Meskipun kerajaan Anwar mengehadkan rumah mampu milik pada RM300,000, realiti bagi rakyat Malaysia B40 dan M40 kekal suram. Krisis kemampuan perumahan — salah satu masalah sosioekonomi paling serius abad ke-21 — hanya bertambah dalam di bawah PH.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Menggunakan pendapatan median B40 sebagai penanda aras, rumah perlu berharga di bawah RM300,000 untuk dianggap mampu milik. Kerajaan menetapkan ini sebagai harga siling.

Fakta: Harga rumah nasional meningkat secara kumulatif 15.2% dari 2019 hingga S3 2025, tetapi dalam terma sebenar (diselaraskan inflasi), kenaikan hanya 4.2%.

Fakta: 54.3% transaksi kediaman KL adalah di bawah RM500,000, manakala 22.8% melebihi RM1 juta — menunjukkan pasaran yang sangat terbelah.

Fakta: Penyelidikan menggambarkan satu “dinamik tidak mampan, mengunci sebahagian besar rakyat — terutamanya M40 dan B40 atas — daripada pemilikan rumah.”

Kenapa Ia Penting

  • Menetapkan had harga bukan sama dengan membina rumah pada harga itu. Di mana unit-unitnya?
  • Rakyat Malaysia muda sedang diketepikan daripada bandar mereka sendiri. Ini adalah krisis generasi.
  • Janji perumahan PH — seperti banyak yang lain — wujud di atas kertas tetapi bukan di atas tanah. *Sumber: The Edge · *Penyelidikan

item.title: [BM] Krisis Mampu Milik Perumahan: B40 Dan M40 Tersingkir Kerana Harga Di Luar Jangkauan
item.description:
Said by: Multiple economists and NAPIC

Category: Housing

Date: 2025-10-07

Janji Bumbung Yang Tidak Pernah Tiba

Walaupun kerajaan Anwar menetapkan siling rumah mampu milik pada RM300,000, realiti bagi rakyat Malaysia B40 dan M40 kekal suram. Krisis mampu milik perumahan — salah satu masalah sosioekonomi paling serius abad ke-21 — hanya semakin mendalam di bawah PH.

Fakta

Fakta: Menggunakan pendapatan median B40 sebagai penanda aras, rumah perlu dihargai di bawah RM300,000 untuk dianggap mampu milik. Kerajaan menetapkan ini sebagai harga siling.

Fakta: Harga rumah nasional meningkat secara kumulatif 15.2% dari 2019 ke S3 2025, tetapi secara sebenar (diselaraskan inflasi), kenaikan hanya 4.2%.

Fakta: 54.3% transaksi kediaman KL adalah di bawah RM500,000, sementara 22.8% melebihi RM1 juta — menunjukkan pasaran yang sangat berpecah.

Fakta: Penyelidikan menggambarkan “dinamik yang tidak mampan, mengunci sebahagian besar rakyat — terutamanya M40 dan B40 lebih tinggi — di luar pemilikan rumah.”

Kenapa Ia Penting

  • Menetapkan siling harga tidak sama dengan membina rumah pada harga itu. Di mana unit-unitnya?
  • Rakyat Malaysia muda disingkirkan dari bandar sendiri. Ini adalah krisis generasi.
  • Janji perumahan PH — seperti banyak yang lain — wujud di atas kertas tetapi bukan di lapangan. *Sumber: *The Edge

item.title: Housing Affordability Crisis: B40 and M40 Locked Out as Prices Remain Beyond Reach
item.description:
Said by: Multiple economists and NAPIC

Category: Housing

Date: 2025-10-07

The Promise of a Roof That Never Comes

Despite Anwar’s government capping affordable housing at RM300,000, the reality for B40 and M40 Malaysians remains bleak. The housing affordability crisis — one of the most serious socio-economic problems of the 21st century — has only deepened under PH.

The Facts

Fact: Using B40 median income as a benchmark, homes need to be priced below RM300,000 to be considered affordable. The government set this as the ceiling price.

Fact: National house prices increased by a cumulative 15.2% from 2019 to Q3 2025, but in real (inflation-adjusted) terms, the increase was just 4.2%.

Fact: 54.3% of KL residential transactions were below RM500,000, while 22.8% were above RM1 million — showing a deeply split market.

Fact: Research describes an “unsustainable dynamic, locking up a significant portion of the people — particularly the M40 and higher B40 — out of homeownership.”

Why It Matters

  • Setting a price cap is not the same as building homes at that price. Where are the units?
  • Young Malaysians are being priced out of their own cities. This is a generational crisis.
  • PH’s housing promises — like so many others — exist on paper but not on the ground. *Sources: The Edge · *Research

item.title: [BM] Tiong King Sing Hidang Arak Di Majlis Makan Malam Rasmi Pelancongan, Kemudian Berbohong Tentang Siapa Penganjurnya
item.description:
Said by: Tiong King Sing, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-05

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Menteri Pelancongan, Seni dan Budaya Tiong King Sing dikecam pada Oktober 2025 selepas minuman beralkohol dihidangkan di majlis makan malam gala semasa Global Travel Meet — acara yang dianjurkan di bawah bendera Tourism Malaysia. Klip video menunjukkan bir dan wain dihidangkan kepada tetamu Muslim, menyebabkan beberapa orang meninggalkan lebih awal.

Tiong pada mulanya menafikan tanggungjawab, mendakwa sektor swasta menganjurkan jamuan dan ia “bukan sebahagian daripada program rasmi Tourism Malaysia.” Tetapi bekas Menteri Kesihatan Khairy Jamaluddin menyiarkan tangkapan skrin di Instagram menunjukkan Tourism Malaysia disenaraikan sebagai penganjur majlis makan malam gala, mengejek Tiong dengan “Liar, liar pants on fire.”

Anwar akhirnya mengeluarkan “amaran tegas” kepada Tiong tetapi tidak memecatnya.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING

Ini adalah seorang menteri yang berbohong secara rasmi mengenai acara kementeriannya sendiri, ditangkap dengan bukti dokumentari, dan menghadapi sifar akibat selain “amaran tegas.” Dalam kerajaan yang menjanjikan integriti dan akauntabiliti, seorang menteri yang berbohong secara terbuka dan ditangkap sepatutnya menghadapi akibat sebenar — bukan tepukan di pergelangan tangan.

Insiden itu juga mendedahkan percanggahan mendalam dalam pengurusan gabungan Anwar. Tiong dari GPS (Gabungan Parti Sarawak), rakan gabungan kritikal. Memecatnya berisiko kehilangan sokongan Sarawak. Jadi akauntabiliti mengambil tempat belakang kepada aritmetik gabungan.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Pembelaan Tiong adalah kelas induk dalam memanipulasi. Logo Tourism Malaysia ada di latar belakang acara. Kakitangan Tourism Malaysia hadir. Tetapi entah bagaimana ia adalah kesalahan “sektor swasta.” Apabila Khairy mengeluarkan bukti, Tiong beralih kepada menyatakan “penyesalan mendalam” tanpa benar-benar memohon maaf.

Dan respons Anwar? “Amaran tegas.” Anwar yang sama yang pernah berjanji menteri-menterinya akan dipegang kepada standard tertinggi. Seorang menteri yang berbohong secara terbuka mengenai acaranya sendiri mendapat tamparan ringan. Jika ini menteri BN di bawah Najib, PH akan menuntut peletakan jawatan dalam 24 jam.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Tiong King Sing adalah menteri yang sama yang menjerit “Tahu tak siapa saya?” di lapangan terbang pada Februari 2025 — corak hak istimewa dan keangkuhan. Beliau juga sebelum ini terlibat dalam kontroversi berkaitan pengambilan alkohol sebelum insiden gala ini. Orang ini mempunyai rekod memalukan kerajaan dan menghadapi sifar akibat sebenar.

Di bawah BN, menteri yang berbohong mengenai acara awam dikoyak-koyak oleh pembangkang ketika itu. Amnesia terpilih PH mengenai standard akauntabiliti adalah tema berulang.

🎯 TEGURAN KEPADA PH

PH menjanjikan Kerajaan Madani — kerajaan bertamadun yang dibina atas integriti. Seorang menteri yang berbohong tentang siapa menganjurkan acara rasmi, ditangkap basah, dan hanya menghadapi “amaran tegas” bukan integriti. Ia adalah kekebalan. Sama ada standard anda terpakai kepada semua menteri anda — termasuk rakan gabungan dari Sarawak — atau ia tidak bermakna apa-apa. Tiong sepatutnya digugurkan daripada Kabinet. Hakikat bahawa dia tidak memberitahu rakyat Malaysia segala-galanya yang mereka perlu tahu tentang di mana kelangsungan gabungan berada berbanding akauntabiliti.

Sumber: Malay Mail | CNA | SCMP | MalaysiaNow | The Star

item.title: [BM] Tiong King Sing Hidang Arak Di Majlis Rasmi Pelancongan, Kemudian Berbohong Tentang Siapa Penganjurnya
item.description:

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Menteri Pelancongan, Seni dan Budaya Tiong King Sing dikecam pada Oktober 2025 selepas minuman beralkohol dihidangkan di majlis makan malam rasmi semasa Global Travel Meet — acara yang dianjurkan di bawah bendera Tourism Malaysia. Anwar akhirnya mengeluarkan “amaran keras” kepada Tiong tetapi tidak memecatnya.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING

Ini adalah menteri yang berbohong secara rasmi tentang acara kementeriannya sendiri, ditangkap dengan bukti dokumentari, dan menghadapi sifar akibat selain “amaran keras.” Dalam kerajaan yang menjanjikan integriti dan akauntabiliti, menteri yang berbohong secara terbuka dan ditangkap sepatutnya menghadapi akibat sebenar — bukan tepukan di pergelangan tangan.

Insiden itu juga mendedahkan percanggahan mendalam dalam pengurusan gabungan Anwar. Tiong daripada GPS (Gabungan Parti Sarawak), rakan gabungan kritikal. Memecatnya berisiko kehilangan sokongan Sarawak. Jadi akauntabiliti mengambil tempat duduk belakang kepada aritmetik gabungan.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT

Pembelaan Tiong adalah kelas induk dalam pencahayaan gas. Logo Tourism Malaysia ada di latar belakang acara. Kakitangan Tourism Malaysia hadir. Tetapi entah bagaimana ia adalah kesalahan “sektor swasta.” Apabila Khairy mengemukakan bukti, Tiong beralih kepada menyatakan “penyesalan mendalam” tanpa benar-benar memohon maaf.

Dan respons Anwar? “Amaran keras.” Anwar yang sama yang pernah berjanji menterinya akan dipegang pada standard tertinggi. Menteri yang berbohong secara terbuka tentang acaranya sendiri mendapat tepukan pergelangan tangan. Jika ini menteri BN di bawah Najib, PH akan menuntut peletakan jawatan dalam 24 jam.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Tiong King Sing adalah menteri yang sama yang menjerit “Tahukah anda siapa saya?” di lapangan terbang pada Februari 2025 — corak hak istimewa dan keangkuhan. Beliau juga sebelum ini terlibat dalam kontroversi berkaitan alkohol sebelum insiden gala ini.

🎯 TEGURAN PH

PH menjanjikan Kerajaan Madani — kerajaan bertamadun dibina atas integriti. Menteri yang berbohong tentang siapa yang menganjurkan acara rasmi, ditangkap merah tangan, dan hanya menghadapi “amaran keras” bukan integriti. Ia adalah impuniti. Sama ada standard anda terpakai kepada semua menteri anda — termasuk rakan gabungan dari Sarawak — atau ia tidak bermakna apa-apa. Tiong sepatutnya digugurkan dari Kabinet.

Sumber: Malay Mail | CNA | SCMP | MalaysiaNow | The Star

item.title: Tiong King Sing Serves Alcohol at Official Tourism Gala, Then Lies About Who Organised It
item.description:
Said by: Tiong King Sing, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Tiong King Sing came under fire in October 2025 after alcoholic beverages were served at a gala dinner during the Global Travel Meet — an event organised under the Tourism Malaysia banner. Video clips showed beer and wine being served to Muslim guests, prompting several to leave early.

Tiong initially denied responsibility, claiming the private sector organised the catering and that it was “not part of Tourism Malaysia’s official programme.” But former Health Minister Khairy Jamaluddin posted screenshots on Instagram showing Tourism Malaysia was listed as the organiser of the gala dinner, mocking Tiong with “Liar, liar pants on fire.”

Anwar eventually issued a “stern warning” to Tiong but did not sack him.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is a minister lying on record about his own ministry’s event, getting caught with documentary evidence, and facing zero consequences beyond a “stern warning.” In a government that promised integrity and accountability, a minister who lies publicly and gets caught should face real consequences — not a pat on the wrist.

The incident also exposed the deep contradictions in Anwar’s coalition management. Tiong is from GPS (Gabungan Parti Sarawak), a critical coalition partner. Sacking him would risk losing Sarawak’s support. So accountability takes a back seat to coalition arithmetic.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Tiong’s defence was a masterclass in gaslighting. The Tourism Malaysia logo was on the event backdrop. Tourism Malaysia staff were present. But somehow it was the “private sector’s” fault. When Khairy produced the receipts, Tiong pivoted to expressing “deep regret” without actually apologising.

And Anwar’s response? A “stern warning.” The same Anwar who once promised that his ministers would be held to the highest standards. A minister who lies publicly about his own event gets a slap on the wrist. If this were a BN minister under Najib, PH would have demanded a resignation within 24 hours.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Tiong King Sing is the same minister who screamed “Do you know who I am?” at an airport in February 2025 — a pattern of entitlement and arrogance. He was also previously embroiled in controversies linked to alcohol consumption before this gala incident. The man has a track record of embarrassing the government and facing no real consequences.

Under BN, ministers who lied about public events were torn apart by the then-opposition. PH’s selective amnesia about accountability standards is a recurring theme.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

PH promised Kerajaan Madani — a civilised government built on integrity. A minister who lies about who organised an official event, gets caught red-handed, and faces only a “stern warning” is not integrity. It’s impunity. Either your standards apply to all your ministers — including coalition partners from Sarawak — or they mean nothing. Tiong should have been dropped from Cabinet. The fact that he wasn’t tells Malaysians everything they need to know about where coalition survival ranks versus accountability.

Sources: Malay Mail | CNA | SCMP | MalaysiaNow | The Star

item.title: [BM] Bajet Sukan Dipotong — Atlet Bergelut Sementara Kerajaan Belanja Berbilion Untuk GLC
item.description:
Said by: National Sports Council / Athletes

Category: Other

Date: 2025-10-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Atlet Malaysia secara konsisten mengadu tentang pembiayaan yang tidak mencukupi, kemudahan latihan yang lemah, dan sistem sokongan yang tidak memadai, walaupun kerajaan menemui berbilion untuk penyelamatan GLC, pelantikan politik, dan faedah perkhidmatan awam. Bajet pembangunan sukan telah dihimpit apabila tekanan fiskal meningkat, menyerahkan atlet dalam sukan Olimpik dan Sukan Asia untuk bergantung kepada penajaan swasta atau pembiayaan sendiri.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

Kemudahan latihan dalam banyak sukan sudah lapuk berbanding pesaing serantau seperti Thailand, Indonesia, dan Vietnam. Prestasi pasukan bola sepak kebangsaan telah menjadi sumber malu negara, dengan kemudahan dan pembangunan belia ketinggalan meskipun sukan ini mempunyai pengikut popular yang besar. Apabila atlet Malaysia berjaya — seperti yang kadangkala berlaku dalam badminton, terjun, atau berbasikal — kerajaan cepat menuntut kredit. Apabila mereka gagal, kekurangan pembiayaan sistematik yang menjadikan kegagalan tidak dapat dielakkan tidak diperiksa.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Perbezaan antara bajet JAKIM RM2.6 bilion untuk hal ehwal agama dan peruntukan yang jauh lebih kecil untuk pembangunan sukan memberitahu anda segala-galanya tentang keutamaan kerajaan. Malaysia bercita-cita menjadi negara sukan tetapi mendanainya seperti perkara sampingan.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Anda mahu pingat emas tetapi mendanai atlet seolah-olah mereka adalah baris perbelanjaan yang perlu diminimumkan. Anda mahu kebanggaan negara dalam sukan tetapi membelanjakan lebih banyak untuk birokrasi agama daripada pembangunan atletik. Pilih satu: sama ada danai sukan dengan sewajarnya atau berhenti berpura-pura peduli apabila pingat tidak datang.

Sumber

  • New Mandala — Pendidikan Tinggi
