item.title: [BM] Sekolah Cina Dan Tamil Dijadikan Bola Politik Setiap Bajet — Dijanjikan Jutaan, Diberikan Sedikit
item.description:
Said by: Dong Zong / Tamil Foundation / MCA / MIC

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Setiap kitaran bajet Malaysia menghasilkan teater yang sama: komuniti sekolah Cina dan Tamil melobi terdesak untuk dana penyelenggaraan, pembinaan dan pembangunan. Ahli politik mengumumkan peruntukan dengan penuh kemeriahan. Kemudian pencairan sebenar tidak mencukupi, tertangguh, atau disertakan syarat yang mengehadkan penggunaan. Kitaran ini berulang setiap tahun, dengan komuniti bukan Melayu menunjukkan rasa syukur terhadap peruntukan yang tidak memadai.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

1.3 juta pelajar menghadiri sekolah aliran Cina (SJKC) dan ratusan ribu menghadiri sekolah aliran Tamil (SJKT). Sekolah-sekolah ini mendidik sebahagian besar penduduk bukan Melayu dan secara konsisten mengatasi sekolah kebangsaan dalam metrik tertentu. Namun mereka menerima pembiayaan kerajaan yang jauh lebih rendah bagi setiap pelajar secara sistematik, memaksa komuniti membiayai sendiri infrastruktur yang sepatutnya menjadi tanggungjawab kerajaan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH, yang sangat bergantung kepada undi Cina dan India, sepatutnya telah menyelesaikan ketidaksamarataan yang telah berdekad lamanya ini. Sebaliknya, PH bermain permainan yang sama seperti BN: gunakan pengumuman bajet sebagai peluang bergambar dengan pemimpin komuniti Cina dan India, hasilkan tajuk berita penuh kesyukuran, kemudian gagal menyampaikan. Jurang struktur dalam pembiayaan setiap pelajar antara sekolah kebangsaan (SK) dan sekolah vernakular kekal kerana menanganinya memerlukan sama ada lebih banyak wang (yang kerajaan dakwa tiada) atau mengakui bahawa sistem semasa adalah diskriminasi (yang ia enggan lakukan).

📜 Kes Terdahulu

BN menggunakan peruntukan sekolah vernakular sebagai naungan politik — menawarkan dana sebagai pertukaran sokongan komuniti. MCA dan MIC wujud terutamanya untuk mendapatkan peruntukan ini, mengekalkan kebergantungan. PH sepatutnya memecahkan kitaran ini dengan membiayai sekolah secara saksama tanpa mengira bahasa pengantar. Sebaliknya, PH mengekalkan model BN: sekolah Cina dan India mendapat apa yang PH putuskan untuk diberikan, bila PH putuskan untuk memberikan.

🎯 Teguran PH

Rakyat Malaysia bukan Melayu tidak mengundi PH untuk meneruskan model naungan BN dengan tampilan lebih baik. Mereka mengundi untuk LAYANAN SAMA RATA. Biayai sekolah vernakular pada kadar setiap pelajar yang sama seperti sekolah kebangsaan. Bukan sebagai ihsan. Bukan sebagai peluang bergambar pengumuman bajet. Sebagai kewajipan perlembagaan di bawah Perkara 152 dan kesaksamaan pendidikan asas. Atau akui bahawa ‘Madani untuk semua’ ada tanda bintangnya: *terma dan syarat tertakluk berdasarkan etnik.

Sumber

  • New Mandala — Menyelamatkan Pendidikan Tinggi Malaysia
  • FMT — Kemerosotan Sistem Persekolahan Malaysia

item.title: Chinese and Tamil Schools Used as Political Football Every Budget — Promised Millions, Delivered Scraps
item.description:
Said by: Dong Zong / Tamil Foundation / MCA / MIC

Category: Education

Date: 2025-10-01

📌 What Happened

Every Malaysian budget cycle produces the same theatre: Chinese and Tamil school communities lobby desperately for maintenance, construction, and development funds. Politicians announce allocations with fanfare. Then the actual disbursement falls short, is delayed, or comes with conditions that limit use. The cycle repeats annually, with non-Malay communities performing gratitude for inadequate allocations.

🔥 Why It Matters

1.3 million students attend Chinese-medium schools (SJKC) and hundreds of thousands attend Tamil-medium schools (SJKT). These schools educate a significant portion of the non-Malay population and consistently outperform national schools in certain metrics. Yet they receive systematically less government funding per student, forcing communities to self-fund infrastructure that should be the government’s responsibility.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH, which depends heavily on Chinese and Indian votes, should have resolved this decades-old inequity. Instead, PH plays the same game as BN: use budget announcements as photo opportunities with Chinese and Indian community leaders, generate grateful headlines, then under-deliver. The structural disparity in per-student funding between national schools (SK) and vernacular schools remains because addressing it would require either more money (which the government claims it doesn’t have) or admitting that the current system is discriminatory (which it won’t).

📜 Previous Cases

BN used vernacular school allocations as political patronage — offering funds in exchange for community support. MCA and MIC existed primarily to secure these allocations, perpetuating dependency. PH was supposed to break this cycle by funding schools equitably regardless of medium of instruction. Instead, PH has maintained the BN model: Chinese and Indian schools get what PH decides to give them, when PH decides to give it.

🎯 PH Callout

Non-Malay Malaysians didn’t vote PH to continue BN’s patronage model with better optics. They voted for EQUAL treatment. Fund vernacular schools at the same per-student rate as national schools. Not as a favour. Not as a budget announcement photo op. As a constitutional obligation under Article 152 and basic educational equity. Or admit that ‘Madani for all’ has an asterisk: *terms and conditions apply based on ethnicity.

Sources

  • New Mandala — Rescuing Malaysian Higher Education
  • FMT — Decline of Malaysia’s Schooling System

item.title: Sports Budget Slashed — Athletes Struggle While Government Spends Billions on GLCs
item.description:
Said by: National Sports Council / Athletes

Category: Other

Date: 2025-10-01

📌 What Happened

Malaysian athletes consistently complain about inadequate funding, poor training facilities, and insufficient support systems, even as the government finds billions for GLC bailouts, political appointments, and civil service perks. The sports development budget has been squeezed as fiscal pressures mount, leaving athletes in Olympic and Asian Games sports to rely heavily on private sponsorship or self-funding.

🔥 Why It Matters

Training facilities in many sports are outdated compared to regional competitors like Thailand, Indonesia, and Vietnam. The national football team’s performance has been a particular source of national embarrassment, with facilities and youth development lagging despite the sport’s massive popular following. When Malaysian athletes succeed — as they occasionally do in badminton, diving, or cycling — the government is quick to claim credit. When they fail, the systemic underfunding that made failure inevitable goes unexamined.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The contrast between the RM2.6 billion JAKIM budget for religious affairs and the significantly smaller allocation for sports development tells you everything about government priorities. Malaysia aspires to be a sporting nation but funds it like an afterthought.

🎯 PH Callout

You want gold medals but fund athletes like they’re an expense line to be minimised. You want national pride in sports but spend more on religious bureaucracy than athletic development. Pick one: either fund sports properly or stop pretending to care when the medals don’t come.

Sources

  • New Mandala — Higher Education

item.title: [BM] Anthony Loke Akui Masyarakat Malaysia 'Terpolarisasi' — Tapi Salahkan Pembangkang Mainkan Isu Kaum
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2025-09-11

Terpolarisasi, Tapi Jangan Salahkan Kami

Pada September 2025, Menteri Pengangkutan Anthony Loke memberitahu rakyat Malaysia di Hong Kong bahawa masyarakat telah menjadi “lebih terpolarisasi” dengan retorik kaum yang semakin sengit. Diagnosisnya tepat. Preskripsinya — salahkan pembangkang — adalah ketidakjujuran intelektual.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Di sebuah acara di Hong Kong, Loke mengakui bahawa Malaysia semakin berpecah mengikut garisan kaum, kelas, dan pendidikan, dengan media sosial menguatkan ucapan kebencian dan maklumat palsu.

Fakta: Beliau mengaitkan polarisasi terutamanya kepada “pembangkang yang memainkan isu-isu sedemikian untuk modal politik” — seolah-olah kerajaan tidak menanggung sebarang tanggungjawab terhadap keretakan tersebut.

Fakta: Ini datang daripada kerajaan yang merombak kabinetnya mengikut garisan kaum pada Disember 2025, memberikan UMNO lebih banyak jawatan untuk mengukuhkan sokongan Melayu sambil mengurangkan kehadiran DAP.

Fakta: Garis panduan antara agama yang dicadangkan JAKIM pada 2024 — yang menyekat cara bukan Islam boleh terlibat dalam dialog agama — memecahbelahkan komuniti dan dikritik meluas oleh kumpulan masyarakat sivil sebagai diskriminasi.

Fakta: Kerajaan tidak membuat sebarang langkah untuk mereformasi dasar tindakan afirmatif berasaskan kaum (kuota era DEB dalam pendidikan, perolehan, dan perumahan), walaupun manifesto PH menjanjikan pendekatan “berasaskan keperluan”.

Fakta: Kerajaan Anwar sangat bergantung kepada retorik kaum dan agama apabila sesuai — termasuk ucapan Melayu-dahulu Anwar sendiri di perhimpunan agung UMNO, pembelaannya terhadap “hak istimewa” Melayu-Islam, dan penggunaan Akta Hasutan oleh gabungannya terhadap mereka yang mempersoalkan dasar kaum.

Fakta: Laporan SCMP menyatakan bahawa walaupun optimisme Loke tentang “mengatasi cabaran,” metrik polarisasi kaum Malaysia — diukur melalui data tinjauan tentang kepercayaan antara etnik — telah bertambah buruk sejak 2023.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Mengakui negara terpolarisasi sambil menyalahkan hanya pembangkang adalah setara politik dengan mendiagnosis penyakit dan menetapkan ubat orang lain. Kerajaan mengawal dasar, pelantikan, pengawalseliaan media, dan jentera negara. Jika polarisasi semakin buruk semasa pemerintahan anda, anda yang bertanggungjawab.
  • Tindakan kerajaan sendiri telah menyumbang secara aktif kepada perpecahan: rombakan kabinet yang mengutamakan pengurusan gabungan kaum berbanding kecekapan, garis panduan antara agama yang mengasingkan minoriti, dasar berasaskan kaum berterusan yang PH janji untuk direformasi, dan PM yang bertukar kod antara retorik reformasi untuk penonton antarabangsa dan retorik etno-agama untuk khalayak domestik.
  • Loke membuat kenyataan ini di Hong Kong — bukan di Malaysia. Ahli politik Malaysia sangat terus terang tentang kegagalan domestik apabila berucap kepada penonton asing. Di tanah air, masalah yang sama dinafikan atau dialihkan. Jurang antara apa yang mereka katakan di luar negara dan apa yang mereka lakukan di dalam negara adalah ukuran sebenar tadbir urus.
  • Pembangkang memang memainkan kad kaum. Populisme etno-agama PN memang nyata dan berbahaya. Tetapi menyalahkan PN atas polarisasi sementara kerajaan anda sendiri mengekalkan dasar berasaskan kaum, mengambil hati konservatif agama, dan menggunakan undang-undang hasutan terhadap mereka yang mencabar ortodoksi kaum bukanlah analisis — ia adalah pengalihan.
  • Jika seorang menteri DAP — dari parti yang sepatutnya mewakili reformasi pelbagai kaum — tidak dapat mendiagnosis dengan jujur mengapa Malaysia terpolarisasi, maka PH telah sepenuhnya menyerap buku panduan BN: akui gejalanya, salahkan musuh, ubah tiada apa-apa. Sumber: SCMP

item.title: Anthony Loke Admits Malaysian Society Is 'Polarised' — But Blames Opposition for Playing Up Race
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2025-09-11

Polarised, But Don’t Blame Us

In September 2025, Transport Minister Anthony Loke told Malaysians in Hong Kong that society had become “more polarised” with intensifying race rhetoric. His diagnosis was accurate. His prescription — blame the opposition — was intellectually dishonest.

The Facts

Fact: At an event in Hong Kong, Loke acknowledged that Malaysia was increasingly divided along racial, class, and education lines, with social media amplifying hate speech and misinformation.

Fact: He attributed the polarisation primarily to “the opposition playing up such issues for political capital” — as though the government bore no responsibility for the fractures.

Fact: This came from a government that reshuffled its cabinet along racial lines in December 2025, giving UMNO more positions to shore up Malay support while reducing DAP’s presence.

Fact: JAKIM’s proposed interfaith guidelines in 2024 — which restricted how non-Muslims could engage in religious dialogue — divided communities and were widely criticised by civil society groups as discriminatory.

Fact: The government has made no move to reform race-based affirmative action policies (NEP-era quotas in education, procurement, and housing), despite PH’s manifesto promising a “needs-based” approach.

Fact: Anwar’s government has relied heavily on racial and religious rhetoric when convenient — including Anwar’s own Malay-first speeches at UMNO general assemblies, his defence of Malay-Muslim “special rights,” and his coalition’s use of the Sedition Act against those who question racial policies.

Fact: The SCMP report noted that despite Loke’s optimism about “overcoming challenges,” Malaysia’s racial polarisation metrics — measured by survey data on inter-ethnic trust — have worsened since 2023.

Why It Matters

  • Admitting the country is polarised while blaming only the opposition is the political equivalent of diagnosing the disease and prescribing someone else’s medicine. The government controls policy, appointments, media regulation, and the machinery of state. If polarisation is worsening on your watch, you own it.
  • The government’s own actions have actively contributed to division: a cabinet reshuffle that prioritised racial coalition management over competence, interfaith guidelines that alienated minorities, continued race-based policies that PH promised to reform, and a PM who code-switches between reform rhetoric for international audiences and ethno-religious rhetoric for domestic ones.
  • Loke made these remarks in Hong Kong — not in Malaysia. Malaysian politicians are remarkably candid about domestic failures when speaking to foreign audiences. At home, the same problems are denied or deflected. The gap between what they say abroad and what they do domestically is the real measure of governance.
  • The opposition absolutely plays the race card. PN’s ethno-religious populism is real and dangerous. But blaming PN for polarisation while your own government maintains race-based policies, panders to religious conservatives, and uses sedition laws against those who challenge racial orthodoxy is not analysis — it is deflection.
  • If even a DAP minister — from the party that was supposed to represent multiracial reform — cannot honestly diagnose why Malaysia is polarised, then PH has fully absorbed the BN playbook: acknowledge the symptom, blame the enemy, change nothing. *Source: *SCMP

item.title: [BM] Farhash Wafa Dibebaskan SPRM Atas Perlombongan Sabah — Muda Kecam 'Cuba Gembirakan PM'
item.description:
Said by: Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-09

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

SPRM membebaskan bekas pembantu Anwar, Farhash Wafa, atas skandal perlombongan Sabah, walaupun Bloomberg mendakwa Anwar mengarahkan SPRM supaya tidak menyiasat. Muda menuduh ketua SPRM Azam Baki ‘cuba bertahan dengan menggembirakan PM.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Apabila pembantu politik PM sendiri dituduh rasuah dalam urus niaga perlombongan dan agensi antirasuah membebaskan mereka tanpa penjelasan awam yang boleh dipercayai, kebebasan agensi tersebut dipersoalkan. Dakwaan Bloomberg — bahawa Anwar campur tangan secara langsung untuk menghentikan siasatan — adalah letupan besar. Jika benar, ia bermakna pejuang antirasuah menghalang siasatan antirasuah. Jika palsu, kerajaan tidak memberikan bukti untuk menyangkalnya.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Walau bagaimana pun, kredibiliti SPRM hancur. Agensi antirasuah yang tidak boleh menyiasat lingkungan dalaman PM bukan agensi antirasuah — ia adalah alat politik. Polanya jelas: sekutu dibebaskan, pengkritik disiasat.

🎯 Teguran PH

Agensi antirasuah anda membebaskan pembantu anda sementara Bloomberg melaporkan anda mengarahkan mereka supaya tidak menyiasat. Anda tidak menafikan laporan Bloomberg dengan bukti. Anda tidak menjelaskan mengapa pembebasan SPRM boleh dipercayai. Anda hanya bergerak dan berharap tiada siapa yang perasan. Kami perasan.

Sumber

  • MalaysiaNow — Farhash Dibebaskan
  • Malaysiakini — SPRM & Farhash

item.title: Farhash Wafa Cleared by MACC Over Sabah Mining — Muda Slams 'Pleasing the PM'
item.description:
Said by: Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-09

📌 What Happened

MACC cleared Anwar’s former aide Farhash Wafa over the Sabah mining scandal, despite Bloomberg alleging Anwar ordered MACC not to investigate. Muda accused MACC chief Azam Baki of ’trying to survive by pleasing the PM.'

🔥 Why It Matters

When a PM’s own political aide is accused of corruption in a mining deal and the anti-corruption agency clears them without a credible public explanation, the agency’s independence is in question. Bloomberg’s allegation — that Anwar directly intervened to stop the investigation — is explosive. If true, it means the anti-corruption crusader is obstructing anti-corruption investigations. If false, the government has not provided evidence to refute it.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Either way, MACC’s credibility is shattered. An anti-graft agency that cannot investigate the PM’s inner circle is not an anti-graft agency — it is a political tool. The pattern is clear: allies get cleared, critics get investigated.

🎯 PH Callout

Your anti-corruption agency cleared your aide while Bloomberg reports you told them not to investigate. You haven’t denied the Bloomberg report with evidence. You haven’t explained why MACC’s clearing is credible. You’ve just moved on and hoped nobody would notice. We noticed.

Sources

  • MalaysiaNow — Farhash Cleared
  • Malaysiakini — MACC & Farhash

item.title: [BM] Keluarga T20 Kini Guna Hospital Kerajaan — Golongan Kaya Pun Tak Mampu Penjagaan Kesihatan Swasta
item.description:
Said by: Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations / The Star

Category: Health

Date: 2025-09-08

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Gabungan Persatuan-Persatuan Pengguna Malaysia (FOMCA) membunyikan penggera bahawa malah keluarga T20 (20% berpendapatan tertinggi) kini beralih ke hospital kerajaan kerana penjagaan kesihatan swasta menjadi terlalu mahal. Pengurus kanan FOMCA Saral James Maniam memanggilnya ‘bendera merah besar.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Apabila rakyat Malaysia terkaya mendapati penjagaan kesihatan swasta tidak mampu dibayar, keseluruhan model pembiayaan penjagaan kesihatan telah runtuh. Keluarga-keluarga ini membanjiri hospital kerajaan yang sudah terbeban, mewujudkan barisan lebih panjang dan hasil lebih buruk untuk pesakit B40 dan M40 yang tiada alternatif. Sistem ini mengalami gelung kemusnahan: kos swasta meningkat → T20 beralih ke awam → sistem awam terbeban → kualiti merosot → tiada siapa mendapat penjagaan memadai.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Respons Menteri Kesihatan Dzulkefly Ahmad ialah ‘subsidi bersasar’ — pada dasarnya lebih banyak birokrasi ujian kelayakan berbanding menangani punca. Malaysia membelanjakan 2.83% KDNK untuk kesihatan awam manakala Thailand membelanjakan 3.8%, Vietnam 3.0%, dan negara maju 6-10%. Kerajaan TAHU sistem ini kurang dibiayai tetapi enggan memperuntukkan lebih kerana wang dihabiskan untuk pencen perkhidmatan awam, JAKIM, pelantikan politik, dan bayaran hutang.

🎯 Teguran PH

Apabila malah golongan KAYA tidak mampu sakit di Malaysia, sistem penjagaan kesihatan anda bukan sahaja gagal golongan miskin — ia gagal semua orang. Berhenti mengumumkan ‘pelan transformasi’ dan mula mentransformasi hospital. Setiap hari anda berlengah diukur dalam penderitaan pesakit.

Sumber

  • The Star — Krisis Penjagaan Kesihatan Melanda Semua Kumpulan Pendapatan
  • P4H — Bom Jangka Penjagaan Kesihatan

item.title: [BM] Sistem Penjagaan Kesihatan Malaysia: 'Bom Jangka' — 2.83% KDNK Sementara Negara Lain Belanja Dua Kali Ganda
item.description:
Said by: P4H Network / University of Malaya Working Paper / The Star

Category: Health

Date: 2025-09-08

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pelbagai analisis bebas telah mengisytiharkan sistem penjagaan kesihatan Malaysia sebagai ‘bom jangka.’ Perbelanjaan kesihatan awam hanya pada 2.83% KDNK pada 2023, jauh di bawah paras yang disyorkan WHO dan rakan serantau. Nisbah doktor-pesakit 1:417 menyembunyikan krisis sebenar di sektor awam, yang mengendalikan 80% katil hospital dengan 54% doktor. Negara ini kekurangan 11,000 pakar. Doktor muda bekerja 84 jam seminggu.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Malah keluarga T20 (berpendapatan tertinggi) kini beralih ke hospital kerajaan kerana penjagaan kesihatan swasta menjadi tidak mampu dibayar. Apabila golongan kaya mula menggunakan hospital kerajaan, keseluruhan model pembiayaan penjagaan kesihatan telah runtuh. Hospital kerajaan menghadapi kesesakan, kekurangan peralatan, dan keletihan kakitangan secara serentak.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Respons kerajaan? ‘Subsidi bersasar’ dan ‘pelan pemulihan’ penjagaan kesihatan. Tetapi pakar memberi amaran ini berisiko ‘meninggalkan golongan miskin.’ Kertas kerja Universiti Malaya menyeru ‘anjakan paradigma’ daripada menganggap pembiayaan kesihatan sebagai penggunaan kepada menganggapnya sebagai ‘pelaburan strategik negara.’ Sebaliknya, kerajaan meningkatkan bajet JAKIM dan pencen perkhidmatan awam sementara hospital runtuh.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Penjagaan kesihatan Malaysia pernah menjadi kebanggaan negara. Kerosakan bermula dengan dekad pembiayaan kurang merentas pentadbiran BN dan kini PH. Doktor kontrak dijanjikan jawatan tetap — masih menunggu. Graduan perubatan menghadapi masa depan tidak menentu sementara kerajaan mempercepatkan lantikan politik ke jawatan GLC yang selesa.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda menjanjikan reformasi penjagaan kesihatan. Sebaliknya, anda telah mempengerusikan sistem di mana rakyat Malaysia benar-benar meninggal dunia dalam senarai tunggu untuk penjagaan pakar — 14 pakar bedah kardiotoraks untuk 33 juta orang. Respons anda ialah satu lagi jawatankuasa, satu lagi ‘pelan,’ satu lagi sidang akhbar. Baiki hospital anda dahulu.

Sumber

  • P4H — Bom Jangka Penjagaan Kesihatan
  • Kertas Kerja UM
  • The Star — Krisis Penjagaan Kesihatan

item.title: Malaysia's Healthcare System: A 'Ticking Bomb' — 2.83% of GDP While Countries Spend Double
item.description:
Said by: P4H Network / University of Malaya Working Paper / The Star

Category: Health

Date: 2025-09-08

📌 What Happened

Multiple independent analyses have declared Malaysia’s healthcare system a ’ticking bomb.’ Public health expenditure sits at just 2.83% of GDP in 2023, far below WHO-recommended levels and regional peers. The doctor-to-patient ratio of 1:417 masks the true crisis in the public sector, which handles 80% of hospital beds with 54% of doctors. The country is 11,000 specialists short. Junior doctors work 84-hour weeks.

🔥 Why It Matters

Even T20 (top income) families are now switching to public hospitals because private healthcare has become unaffordable. When the rich start using government hospitals, the entire healthcare financing model has broken down. Public hospitals face overcrowding, equipment shortages, and staff burnout simultaneously.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government’s response? ‘Targeted subsidies’ and a healthcare ‘reset plan.’ But experts warn this risks ’leaving the poor behind.’ The University of Malaya working paper called for a ‘paradigm shift’ from treating health funding as consumption to treating it as ‘strategic national investment.’ Instead, the government increases JAKIM’s budget and civil service pensions while hospitals crumble.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia’s healthcare was once the pride of the nation. The rot began with decades of underfunding across BN and now PH administrations. Contract doctors were promised permanent posts — still waiting. Medical graduates face uncertain futures while the government fast-tracks political appointees to cushy GLC positions.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised healthcare reform. Instead, you’ve presided over a system where Malaysians are literally dying on waiting lists for specialist care — 14 cardiothoracic surgeons for 33 million people. Your response is another committee, another ‘plan,’ another press conference. Fix your hospitals first.

Sources

  • P4H — Healthcare Ticking Bomb
  • UM Working Paper
  • The Star — Healthcare Crisis

item.title: T20 Families Now Using Public Hospitals — The Rich Can't Afford Private Healthcare Anymore
item.description:
Said by: Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations / The Star

Category: Health

Date: 2025-09-08

📌 What Happened

The Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations (FOMCA) sounded alarms that even T20 families (top 20% income earners) are switching to public hospitals because private healthcare has become prohibitively expensive. FOMCA senior manager Saral James Maniam called it a ‘big red flag.’

🔥 Why It Matters

When Malaysia’s wealthiest citizens find private healthcare unaffordable, the entire healthcare financing model has collapsed. These families flood already-overwhelmed public hospitals, creating longer queues and worse outcomes for B40 and M40 patients who have no alternative. The system is experiencing a doom loop: rising private costs → T20 shift to public → public system overwhelmed → quality drops → nobody gets adequate care.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad’s response was ’targeted subsidies’ — essentially more means-testing bureaucracy rather than addressing root causes. Malaysia spends 2.83% of GDP on public health while Thailand spends 3.8%, Vietnam 3.0%, and developed countries 6-10%. The government KNOWS the system is underfunded but refuses to allocate more because the money goes to civil service pensions, JAKIM, political appointments, and debt servicing.

🎯 PH Callout

When even the RICH can’t afford to be sick in Malaysia, your healthcare system hasn’t just failed the poor — it’s failed everyone. Stop announcing ’transformation plans’ and start transforming hospitals. Every day you delay is measured in patient suffering.

Sources

  • The Star — Healthcare Crisis Hitting All Income Groups
  • P4H — Healthcare Ticking Bomb

item.title: [BM] Jerebu Merentas Sempadan Terus Kembali — Tindakan Kerajaan: 'Pantau Dan Harap Indonesia Bekerjasama'
item.description:
Said by: Environment Ministry / ASEAN Coordination

Category: Other

Date: 2025-09-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Walaupun Perjanjian ASEAN mengenai Pencemaran Jerebu Merentas Sempadan ditandatangani pada 2002 dan diratifikasi oleh semua negara anggota, Malaysia terus mengalami episod jerebu berkala yang disebabkan terutamanya oleh pembakaran pertanian di Indonesia. Respons kerajaan kekal sebahagian besarnya diplomatik — meminta kerjasama Indonesia dan mengeluarkan nasihat kesihatan — berbanding menangani sumbangan Malaysia sendiri melalui kebakaran tanah gambut domestik dan syarikat ladang milik Malaysia yang beroperasi di Indonesia.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Pemantauan kualiti udara semasa episod jerebu secara rutin menunjukkan bacaan IPU tidak sihat, menjejaskan kesihatan berjuta-juta rakyat Malaysia, menutup sekolah, dan mengurangkan produktiviti. Kos ekonomi jerebu berjumlah berbilion ringgit apabila mengambil kira penjagaan kesihatan, kehilangan produktiviti, kesan pelancongan, dan kesan kesihatan pernafasan jangka panjang. Perubahan iklim menjadikan keadaan lebih kondusif untuk kebakaran, namun penebangan hutan Malaysia sendiri berterusan pada kadar yang membimbangkan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Isu asas ialah keuntungan kelapa sawit diutamakan berbanding kualiti udara, dan budi bahasa diplomatik menghalang Malaysia daripada mempertanggungjawabkan syarikat Indonesia (dan milik Malaysia). Selepas dua dekad, masalah jerebu kekal pada dasarnya tidak ditangani. Kerajaan bersuara keras semasa musim jerebu dan senyap apabila langit cerah.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda menghadiri persidangan iklim antarabangsa dan membuat ikrar tentang kelestarian. Tetapi anda tidak akan berdepan dengan syarikat ladang — termasuk yang dimiliki Malaysia — yang menyebabkan jerebu yang rakyat anda sedut. Pemantauan bukan tadbir urus. Harapan bukan dasar. Selepas 20 tahun buku panduan yang sama, sama ada ubah pendekatan atau akui anda telah memilih keuntungan berbanding paru-paru.

Sumber

  • Malay Mail / Amnesty International — Kegagalan Reformasi

item.title: [BM] Percanggahan Gabungan Anwar: PM Reformis, TPM Korup, Dan Parti Yang Membolehkan 1MDB
item.description:
Said by: Multiple analysts and commentators

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-01

Gabungan Yang Mustahil

Anwar Ibrahim mentadbir Malaysia melalui gabungan yang menjadikan reformasi mustahil secara struktur. Lelaki yang berkempen selama berdekad menentang rasuah kini bergantung kepada parti yang membolehkan kleptokrasi terbesar dunia.

Watak-Watak Utama

  • Anwar Ibrahim (PKR): Membina kerjayanya atas reformasi, antirasuah, dan tadbir urus bersih.
  • Ahmad Zahid Hamidi (UMNO): Timbalan PM. Menghadapi 47 pertuduhan rasuah. Semuanya digugurkan.
  • DAP: Suara reformasi paling konsisten dalam gabungan. Dipinggirkan secara sistematik dalam rombakan Disember 2025.
  • UMNO: Parti Najib. Mengancam untuk menyemak semula hubungan gabungan apabila AP DAP meraikan kekalahan mahkamah Najib.

Masalah Struktur

Seperti yang dinyatakan Fair Observer, gabungan Anwar pada asasnya tidak stabil kerana ahli-ahlinya mempunyai kepentingan yang bertentangan:

  • Pengundi PH mahu reformasi, akauntabiliti, dan perubahan institusi.
  • UMNO mahu perlindungan untuk pemimpinnya, naungan untuk asasnya, dan dasar Melayu-dahulu.
  • Semakin kuat Anwar mendesak reformasi, semakin besar risikonya kehilangan UMNO. Semakin beliau memenuhi UMNO, semakin beliau kehilangan asas reformasi.

Hasilnya

Apa yang anda dapat bukan reformasi. Apa yang anda dapat ialah pengurusan. Penyelenggaraan gabungan yang berselindung di sebalik tadbir urus. Setiap keputusan besar — NFA Zahid, rombakan kabinet, undur AUKU, pusingan U garis panduan JAKIM — masuk akal hanya melalui lensa mengekalkan gabungan bersama, bukan menjadikan Malaysia lebih baik.

Sumber: Fair Observer

item.title: Anwar's Coalition Contradictions: Reformist PM, Corrupt DPM, and a Party That Enabled 1MDB
item.description:
Said by: Multiple analysts and commentators

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-09-01

The Impossible Coalition

Anwar Ibrahim governs Malaysia through a coalition that makes reform structurally impossible. The man who campaigned for decades against corruption now depends on the party that enabled the world’s largest kleptocracy.

The Cast of Characters

  • Anwar Ibrahim (PKR): Built his career on reformasi, anti-corruption, and clean governance.
  • Ahmad Zahid Hamidi (UMNO): Deputy PM. Faced 47 corruption charges. All dropped.
  • DAP: The coalition’s most consistent reform voice. Systematically sidelined in the December 2025 reshuffle.
  • UMNO: Najib’s party. Threatened to review coalition ties when a DAP MP celebrated Najib’s court loss.

The Structural Problem

As Fair Observer noted, Anwar’s coalition is fundamentally unstable because its members have opposing interests:

  • PH voters want reform, accountability, and institutional change.
  • UMNO wants protection for its leaders, patronage for its base, and Malay-first policies.
  • The harder Anwar pushes reform, the more he risks losing UMNO. The more he accommodates UMNO, the more he loses the reform base.

The Result

What you get is not reform. What you get is management. Coalition maintenance disguised as governance. Every major decision — the Zahid NFA, the cabinet reshuffle, the UUCA backtrack, the JAKIM guidelines U-turn — makes sense only through the lens of keeping the coalition together, not making Malaysia better.

*Source: *Fair Observer

item.title: Transboundary Haze Keeps Returning — Government's Response: 'Monitor and Hope Indonesia Cooperates'
item.description:
Said by: Environment Ministry / ASEAN Coordination

Category: Other

Date: 2025-09-01

📌 What Happened

Despite the ASEAN Agreement on Transboundary Haze Pollution signed in 2002 and ratified by all member states, Malaysia continues to suffer periodic haze episodes caused primarily by agricultural burning in Indonesia. The government’s response remains largely diplomatic — requesting Indonesian cooperation and issuing health advisories — rather than addressing Malaysia’s own contribution through domestic peatland fires and Malaysian-owned plantation companies operating in Indonesia.

🔥 Why It Matters

Air quality monitoring during haze episodes routinely shows unhealthy API readings, affecting millions of Malaysians’ health, shutting schools, and reducing productivity. The economic cost of haze runs into billions of ringgit when accounting for healthcare, lost productivity, tourism impacts, and long-term respiratory health effects. Climate change is making conditions more conducive to fires, yet Malaysia’s own deforestation continues at alarming rates.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The fundamental issue is that palm oil profits are prioritised over air quality, and diplomatic niceties prevent Malaysia from holding Indonesian (and Malaysian-owned) companies accountable. After two decades, the haze problem remains essentially unaddressed. The government talks tough during haze season and goes silent when the skies clear.

🎯 PH Callout

You attend international climate conferences and make pledges about sustainability. But you won’t confront the plantation companies — including Malaysian-owned ones — that cause the haze your rakyat breathes. Monitoring is not governance. Hope is not policy. After 20 years of the same playbook, either change the approach or admit you’ve chosen profits over lungs.

Sources

  • Malay Mail / Amnesty International — Reform Failures

item.title: [BM] Rang Undang-Undang Pembaharuan Bandar Nga Kor Ming Beri Seorang Menteri Kuasa Mengatasi Hak Milik — AP Sendiri Pun Memberontak
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Housing

Date: 2025-08-25

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada Ogos 2025, Menteri Perumahan dan Kerajaan Tempatan Nga Kor Ming membentangkan Rang Undang-Undang Pembaharuan Bandar 2025, yang direka untuk mempercepatkan pembangunan semula perumahan bandar yang uzur. Rang undang-undang itu mencadangkan menurunkan ambang persetujuan untuk pembangunan semula — bermakna pemilik hartanah boleh dipaksa menjual atau berpindah walaupun tanpa persetujuan sebulat suara.

Bantahan datang serta-merta dan merentas parti. Pengkritik memanggil rang undang-undang itu “cacat, menyalahi undang-undang dan tanpa legitimasi,” menuduh Nga menumpukan terlalu banyak kuasa di tangan seorang menteri. Rang undang-undang itu membenarkan pembangunan semula diteruskan dengan hanya 80% persetujuan penduduk, mengatasi hak milik 20% yang selebihnya.

Paling luar biasa, pemberontakan datang dari dalam barisan PH sendiri. Tujuh ahli parlimen PKR dan beberapa AP BN enggan menyokong rang undang-undang itu, memaksa kerajaan menangguhkan bacaan kedua dan ketiga dari Ogos ke Oktober. Menjelang Januari 2026, Nga masih menjalankan “sesi perundingan” tanpa garis masa yang jelas untuk pembentangan semula.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Ini adalah rang undang-undang yang akan mengubah secara asas hak milik di Malaysia. Di bawah Akta Hak Milik Strata 1985 yang sedia ada, persetujuan sebulat suara diperlukan untuk pembangunan semula. Rang undang-undang Nga menurunkannya ke 80%, bermakna satu daripada lima pemilik hartanah boleh dipaksa keluar dari rumah mereka tanpa kerelaan.

Badan pemikir ISIS memberi amaran rang undang-undang itu “kehilangan komponen utama” dan boleh membawa kepada “penyingkiran penduduk tempatan dan gentrifikasi komuniti untuk memberi laluan kepada pembangunan swasta.” The Straits Times menggambarkan bantahan awam yang semakin meningkat atas kebimbangan undang-undang itu akan “memaksa pemilik minoriti menyerahkan hak milik mereka.”

Apabila AP gabungan anda sendiri enggan menyokong rang undang-undang anda, itu bukan khianat pembangkang — itu kegagalan tadbir urus.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Respons Nga terhadap kritikan adalah keangkuhan PH yang klasik: beliau menyerang pembangkang kerana “tidak menyokong” rang undang-undang itu, seolah-olah masalahnya politik dan bukan substansi. Tetapi bukan hanya pembangkang. Rakan gabungan sendiri enggan mengundinya.

Rang undang-undang ini menumpukan kuasa besar pada Menteri Perumahan untuk menentukan kawasan mana yang “usang” dan layak untuk pembangunan semula paksa. Siapa yang untung daripada kuasa itu? Pemaju. Siapa yang rugi? Penduduk bandar berpendapatan rendah — ramai daripada mereka warga emas Melayu di KL — yang akan dipaksa menerima apa-apa terma yang kerajaan rundingi bagi pihak mereka.

Nga berjanji penduduk akan mendapat “rumah lebih besar dan berkualiti tinggi” dan rumah sementara semasa pembinaan. Tetapi janji daripada menteri yang program MyKiosk-nya gagal semakan SPRM dan tandas BMW-nya menghasilkan sifar keputusan tidak membawa banyak kredibiliti.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang dan buruk penyingkiran bandar secara paksa. Penduduk Kampung Baru telah berjuang selama berdekad menentang tekanan pembangunan semula. Pengusiran Kampung Sungai Baru pada 2025 — di mana penduduk dipaksa keluar di tengah kontroversi Rang Undang-Undang Pembaharuan Bandar — menunjukkan tepat apa yang dikhuatiri pengkritik.

Di bawah BN, PH menjerit tentang dasar mesra pemaju yang menyingkirkan komuniti. Kini PH menulis undang-undang yang menjadikannya lebih mudah bagi pemaju untuk mengatasi hak milik. Bezanya? PH memanggilnya “pembaharuan bandar” bukannya “pembangunan.”

🎯 TEGURAN PH

Anda berjanji melindungi rakyat daripada pembangunan yang memangsa. Kemudian menteri anda membentangkan rang undang-undang yang membenarkan kerajaan memaksa orang keluar dari rumah mereka dengan 80% persetujuan, dan menyerang sesiapa yang membantah. Apabila AP anda sendiri enggan menyokong rang undang-undang anda, itu bukan masalah komunikasi — itu masalah substansi. Baiki rang undang-undang atau jatuhkan. Jangan tindas hak milik orang dan panggil ia reformasi.

Sumber: NST | CNA | Straits Times | ISIS Malaysia | Malay Mail

item.title: Nga Kor Ming's Urban Renewal Bill Gives One Minister Power to Override Property Rights — Even His Own MPs Revolt
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Housing

Date: 2025-08-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In August 2025, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming tabled the Urban Renewal Bill 2025, designed to fast-track redevelopment of ageing urban housing. The bill proposed lowering the consent threshold for redevelopment — meaning property owners could be forced to sell or relocate even without unanimous agreement.

The backlash was immediate and bipartisan. Critics called the bill “defective, unlawful and devoid of legitimacy,” accusing Nga of concentrating too much power in one minister’s hands. The bill would allow redevelopment to proceed with just 80% resident consent, overriding the property rights of the remaining 20%.

Most remarkably, the revolt came from within PH’s own ranks. Seven PKR lawmakers and several BN MPs refused to support the bill, forcing the government to delay the second and third readings from August to October. By January 2026, Nga was still conducting “consultation sessions” with no clear timeline for retabling.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is a bill that would fundamentally alter property rights in Malaysia. Under the existing Strata Titles Act 1985, unanimous consent is required for redevelopment. Nga’s bill drops that to 80%, meaning one in five property owners can be forced out of their homes against their will.

The ISIS think tank warned the bill was “missing key components” and could lead to “displacement of local populations and gentrification of communities to make way for private development.” The Straits Times described mounting public backlash over fears the law would “force minority owners to give up their property rights.”

When your own coalition MPs refuse to back your bill, that’s not opposition mischief — that’s a governance failure.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Nga’s response to criticism was classic PH arrogance: he slammed the opposition for “not supporting” the bill, as if the problem were politics rather than substance. But it wasn’t just the opposition. His own coalition partners refused to vote for it.

The bill concentrates enormous power in the Housing Minister to determine which areas are “dilapidated” and eligible for forced redevelopment. Who benefits from that power? Developers. Who loses? Low-income urban residents — many of them elderly Malays in KL — who would be forced to accept whatever terms the government negotiates on their behalf.

Nga promised that residents would get “larger and better-quality homes” and temporary housing during construction. But promises from a minister whose MyKiosk programme failed MACC review and whose BMW toilets have produced zero results don’t carry much credibility.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysia has a long and ugly history of forced urban displacement. Kampung Baru residents have fought for decades against redevelopment pressure. The Kampung Sungai Baru evictions in 2025 — where residents were forced out amid the Urban Renewal Bill controversy — showed exactly what critics feared.

Under BN, PH screamed about developer-friendly policies that displaced communities. Now PH is writing laws that make it even easier for developers to override property rights. The difference? PH calls it “urban renewal” instead of “development.”

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You promised to protect the rakyat from predatory development. Then your minister tabled a bill that lets the government force people out of their homes with 80% consent, and slammed anyone who objected. When your own MPs refuse to support your bill, that’s not a communication problem — it’s a substance problem. Fix the bill or drop it. Don’t bulldoze people’s property rights and call it reform.

Sources: NST | CNA | Straits Times | ISIS Malaysia | Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Tebus Guna Pulau Selatan Pulau Pinang — Musnahkan Mata Pencarian 4,000 Nelayan Demi Impian Pemaju
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister / DAP

Category: Social

Date: 2025-08-20

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Projek tebus guna Pulau Selatan Pulau Pinang (PSI) — kini dijenamakan semula sebagai ‘Silicon Island’ — adalah pelan pembangunan mega kerajaan negeri pimpinan DAP untuk mencipta 930 hektar tanah buatan di luar pantai selatan Pulau Pinang. Sehingga Ogos 2025, 80 hektar telah ditebus guna. Projek ini asalnya tiga pulau, kemudian dikecilkan kepada satu selepas bantahan awam. Lebih 4,000 nelayan pesisir terjejas. Hanya RM11.7 juta telah dibayar kepada 791 nelayan di bawah Pelan Pengurusan Kesan Sosial, dan 118 daripada 295 nelayan aktif menerima bot dan enjin baru bernilai RM10 juta. Nelayan kalah dalam cabaran mahkamah terhadap kelulusan Penilaian Kesan Alam Sekitar.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Ini adalah projek tebus guna tanah terbesar dalam sejarah Pulau Pinang, dan ia ditolak oleh DAP — parti yang meletakkan dirinya sebagai pro-rakyat dan pro-alam sekitar. Penyelidikan akademik mengesahkan projek ini menyebabkan ‘kehilangan kekal dan tidak boleh dipulihkan terhadap ekosistem.’ Nelayan yang telah bekerja di perairan ini selama bergenerasi sedang disingkirkan dengan pampasan minimum. Projek ini direka untuk memperluaskan Zon Perindustrian Bebas Bayan Lepas untuk pembuatan semikonduktor — objektif korporat yang dibiayai dengan memusnahkan mata pencarian komuniti.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: DAP dalam pembangkang pasti akan memimpin protes menentang ini. Kerajaan negeri BN yang menebus guna 930 hektar laut, menyingkirkan ribuan nelayan, dan kalah kes mahkamah atas kerosakan alam sekitar pasti akan menjadi isu kempen impian DAP. Tetapi apabila DAP melakukannya? Ia ‘pembangunan untuk rakyat.’ Nelayan yang kehilangan mata pencarian bukan ‘rakyat’ dalam pengiraan DAP — mereka adalah kerosakan sampingan untuk angka FDI.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Pulau Pinang mempunyai sejarah panjang tebus guna — Gurney Drive, Tanjung Tokong — tetapi tiada yang sebesar ini. Kumpulan masyarakat sivil termasuk SAM dan Penang Forum telah mendokumentasikan kemusnahan alam sekitar dan penyingkiran komuniti. Nelayan dan NGO ‘kecewa’ apabila cabaran mahkamah mereka gagal. Polanya jelas: DAP mentadbir Pulau Pinang seperti negeri pemaju, mengutamakan pelaburan berbanding komuniti.

🎯 TEGURAN PH: 4,000 nelayan disingkirkan. RM11.7 juta pampasan untuk 791 daripada mereka — itu RM14,800 setiap seorang untuk kehilangan mata pencarian. 930 hektar dasar laut dimusnahkan. Dan anda panggil ini pembangunan? Ini pengekstrakan — mengambil daripada laut, mengambil daripada nelayan, dan memberi kepada syarikat semikonduktor. Jika ini model tadbir urus PH, nelayan Pulau Pinang kini tahu tepat berapa nilai nyawa mereka bagi DAP.

Sumber: FMT | Al Jazeera | Aliran

item.title: Penang South Islands Reclamation — Destroying 4,000 Fishermen's Livelihoods for a Developer's Dream
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister / DAP

Category: Social

Date: 2025-08-20

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Penang South Islands (PSI) reclamation project — now rebranded as ‘Silicon Island’ — is the DAP-led state government’s mega-development plan to create 930 hectares of artificial land off Penang’s southern coast. As of August 2025, 80 hectares had been reclaimed. The project was originally three islands, later downsized to one after public outcry. More than 4,000 coastal fishermen are affected. Only RM11.7 million has been paid to 791 fishermen under the Social Impact Management Plan, and 118 of 295 active fishermen received new boats and engines worth RM10 million. Fishermen lost their court challenge to the Environmental Impact Assessment approval.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is the largest land reclamation project in Penang’s history, and it’s being pushed through by DAP — the party that positions itself as pro-people and pro-environment. Academic research confirms the project causes ‘permanent and irreversible loss to the ecosystem.’ Fishermen who have worked these waters for generations are being displaced with minimal compensation. The project is designed to extend Bayan Lepas Free Industrial Zone for semiconductor manufacturing — a corporate objective funded by destroying a community’s livelihood.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP in opposition would have led protests against this. A BN state government reclaiming 930 hectares of sea, displacing thousands of fishermen, and losing court cases over environmental damage would have been DAP’s dream campaign issue. But when DAP does it? It’s ‘development for the people.’ The fishermen who lost their livelihoods are not ’the people’ in DAP’s calculation — they’re collateral damage for FDI numbers.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Penang has a long history of reclamation — Gurney Drive, Tanjung Tokong — but nothing on this scale. Civil society groups including SAM and Penang Forum have documented environmental destruction and community displacement. Fishermen and NGOs were ‘dismayed’ when their court challenge failed. The pattern is clear: DAP governs Penang like a developer-state, prioritising investment over communities.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: 4,000 fishermen displaced. RM11.7 million in compensation for 791 of them — that’s RM14,800 each for losing a livelihood. 930 hectares of seabed destroyed. And you call this development? This is extraction — taking from the sea, taking from the fishermen, and giving to semiconductor companies. If this is PH’s model of governance, the fishermen of Penang now know exactly how much their lives are worth to DAP.

Sources: FMT | Al Jazeera | Aliran

item.title: [BM] Kontroversi Penjenamaan Semula Tabung Haji — Dakwaan Perbelanjaan RM20J, Ketegangan Kaum Meningkat

item.title: [BM] Krisis K-Pod Dan Vape Dalam Kalangan Belia — Wabak Dadah Baru Sementara Kerajaan Lambat Bertindak
item.description:
Said by: DPM Ahmad Zahid / AADK

Category: Health

Date: 2025-08-10

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Krisis dadah baru yang dahsyat sedang melanda sekolah dan komuniti Malaysia: K-pod dan vape berlapis dadah sintetik menjadi mekanisme penghantaran dadah pilihan belia Malaysia. Timbalan PM Ahmad Zahid mengakui keadaan berada pada ’tahap kritikal,’ dengan kes penyalahgunaan dadah melonjak 32.5% dari 2023 ke 2024 dan perangsang jenis amfetamina terlibat dalam lebih 70% kes.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Fenomena K-pod — kartrij vape berlapis dadah sintetik — amat berbahaya kerana ia menyamarkan penggunaan dadah sebagai vape biasa, menjadikan pengesanan oleh ibu bapa dan guru hampir mustahil. Platform media sosial telah menjadi saluran pengedaran, dengan pengedar mengiklan melalui Telegram dan TikTok.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Respons kerajaan ialah Pelan Komunikasi Anti-Dadah 2025-2027 — pada dasarnya kempen perhubungan awam sedangkan yang diperlukan ialah penguatkuasaan dan pintasan. Malaysia perlu ‘mengubah strateginya’ menurut Zahid, tetapi perubahan strategik tidak terwujud melebihi sidang akhbar. Sementara itu, satu generasi belia Malaysia sedang diubah secara kimia.

🎯 Teguran PH

TPM anda memanggilnya ’tahap kritikal’ dan respons anda ialah pelan komunikasi. Kanak-kanak menghisap vape dadah sintetik di tandas sekolah dan anda menulis risalah. Tutup saluran Telegram. Kuatkuasakan di sempadan. Biayai intervensi peringkat sekolah. Atau akui bahawa ’tahap kritikal’ hanyalah satu lagi frasa yang anda gunakan untuk sidang akhbar sebelum bergerak.

Sumber

  • CNA — Penyalahgunaan Dadah Pada Tahap Kritikal
  • CNA — Krisis K-Pod Dan Vape

item.title: K-Pods and Vape Crisis Among Youth — New Drug Epidemic While Government Plays Catch-Up
item.description:
Said by: DPM Ahmad Zahid / AADK

Category: Health

Date: 2025-08-10

📌 What Happened

A devastating new drug crisis is sweeping through Malaysian schools and communities: K-pods and synthetic drug-laced vapes are becoming the drug delivery mechanism of choice for young Malaysians. Deputy PM Ahmad Zahid acknowledged the situation was at a ‘critical level,’ with drug abuse cases surging 32.5% from 2023 to 2024 and amphetamine-type stimulants involved in over 70% of cases.

🔥 Why It Matters

The K-pod phenomenon — vape cartridges laced with synthetic drugs — is particularly insidious because it disguises drug use as ordinary vaping, making detection by parents and teachers nearly impossible. Social media platforms have become distribution channels, with dealers advertising through Telegram and TikTok.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government’s response has been the Anti-Drug Communication Plan 2025-2027 — essentially a PR campaign when what’s needed is enforcement and interdiction. Malaysia needs to ‘change its strategy’ according to Zahid, but the strategic change hasn’t materialised beyond press conferences. Meanwhile, a generation of young Malaysians is being chemically rewired.

🎯 PH Callout

Your DPM calls it ‘critical level’ and your response is a communication plan. Kids are vaping synthetic drugs in school bathrooms and you’re writing brochures. Shut down the Telegram channels. Enforce at the border. Fund school-level intervention. Or admit that ‘critical level’ is just another phrase you use for press conferences before moving on.

Sources

  • CNA — Drug Abuse at Critical Level
  • CNA — K-Pods and Vape Crisis

item.title: [BM] Institut Keamanan Toda: Anwar 'Jadikan Dasar Berasaskan Kaum LEBIH BURUK, Bukan Lebih Baik' — Cuba Mengatasi Islamis Dalam Pengislaman
item.description:
Said by: Toda Peace Institute Analysis

Category: Social

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Institut Keamanan Toda menerbitkan analisis menyeluruh bertajuk ‘Pelbagai Peperangan Anwar Ibrahim di Malaysia,’ menyimpulkan bahawa Anwar ‘bukan sekadar enggan mengganggu status quo; beliau sebenarnya menjadikannya lebih buruk dengan cuba mengatasi Islamis dalam pengislaman melalui isyarat, dasar dan kenyataan yang dianggap sangat membimbangkan oleh minoriti.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini adalah institut penyelidikan keamanan antarabangsa yang menyatakan bahawa PM Malaysia yang kononnya reformis sedang MEMBURUKKAN diskriminasi kaum, bukan mengurangkannya. Sistem dasar berasaskan kaum yang telah dilembagakan — yang digambarkan sebagai ‘membunuh merit, menyekat persaingan dan inovasi, membiakkan rasa tidak puas hati minoriti, dan melahirkan kronisme dengan mencipta elit Melayu kaya tanpa berbuat banyak untuk Melayu miskin’ — sedang diperkukuh bukannya direformasi di bawah Anwar.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Gerakan Reformasi Anwar secara jelas ‘menuntut pengakhiran sistem diskriminasi kaum yang dilembagakan ini.’ Itu adalah JANJI TERAS. Bukan komitmen sampingan — sebab asas mengapa minoriti menyokongnya. Kenaikannya ‘secara wajar mencetuskan harapan keadilan kaum.’ Sebaliknya, beliau bersaing dengan PAS tentang siapa boleh lebih Melayu-Islam, sementara minoriti yang mengundi PH secara beramai-ramai mendapat retorik kosong dan peluang bergambar dengan pengumuman peruntukan sekolah Cina sekali-sekala.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Pada April 2025, apabila seorang profesor UiTM mencadangkan membuka latihan pembedahan kardiotoraks kepada pelajar bukan Bumiputera (kerana rakyat mati akibat kekurangan pakar bedah), kerajaan terpaku. Keperluan wajib Bahasa Melayu untuk pekerja asing dikenakan. Dasar Bumiputera diisytiharkan ’tidak boleh dirunding’ dalam rundingan perdagangan AS. ICERD kekal tidak diratifikasi. Perkhidmatan awam kekal 90% Bumiputera.

🎯 Teguran PH

Anda meminta rakyat Malaysia bukan Melayu mempercayai anda. Mereka melakukannya — mereka mengundi PH dalam jumlah besar. Sebagai balasan, anda bukan sahaja mengekalkan setiap dasar diskriminasi tetapi, menurut analisis bebas, menjadikan keadaan LEBIH BURUK. Anda telah menukar hak minoriti untuk undi Melayu-Islam. Setiap rakyat Malaysia minoriti yang mengundi PH layak mendapat permohonan maaf — dan penjelasan mengapa kepercayaan mereka dijadikan senjata terhadap kepentingan mereka sendiri.

Sumber

  • Institut Keamanan Toda — Laporan 184
  • CSIS — Kematian Reformasi

item.title: [BM] MITI Dituduh Curi Konsep Pavilion Ekspo Dunia Osaka — Agensi Kreatif Hantar Surat Tuntutan
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / Current Media Group

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Ogos 2025, MITI di bawah Tengku Zafrul dihantar surat tuntutan oleh Current Media Group (CMG), sebuah konsultansi kreatif yang mendakwa kementerian mencuri konsepnya untuk pavilion Ekspo Dunia 2025 Osaka Malaysia tanpa memberikan pengiktirafan atau bayaran. Dakwaan itu menyatakan pitching asal CMG digunakan sebagai asas reka bentuk pavilion tanpa kontrak atau kredit. Zafrul menjawab perkara itu dalam jawapan parlimen kepada AP Kepong Lim Lip Eng.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Jika sebuah kementerian kerajaan menggunakan harta intelek syarikat swasta tanpa bayaran atau kredit, itu adalah kecurian — tanpa mengira sama ada ia dipanggil ‘inspirasi’ atau ‘pembangunan selari.’ Pavilion Ekspo Dunia adalah pameran nasional berprofil tinggi dengan bajet yang besar. Dakwaan bahawa MITI mengambil konsep tanpa membayarnya menimbulkan persoalan serius tentang integriti perolehan dan penghormatan harta intelek dalam kementerian.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Kerajaan ‘reformis’ yang menceramah dunia tentang tadbir urus baik dan urusan adil didakwa mencuri konsep kreatif daripada syarikat Malaysia. PH naik berkuasa menjanjikan perolehan telus dan penghormatan terhadap proses wajar. Jika MITI tidak mampu menugaskan reka bentuk pavilion tanpa menerima surat tuntutan daripada pencipta asal, apa yang dikatakan tentang bagaimana kontrak lain yang lebih besar diuruskan di sebalik tabir?

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Kecurian harta intelek kerajaan daripada kontraktor swasta adalah aduan berulang di Malaysia. Agensi kreatif, arkitek, dan perunding telah lama melaporkan bahawa pitching kerajaan dilayan sebagai kerja percuma — idea diambil, kontrak pergi ke tempat lain, dan pencipta asal ditinggalkan. Di bawah BN, ini adalah standard. Di bawah PH, ia sepatutnya berubah.

🎯 TEGURAN PH: Anda mewakili Malaysia di pentas dunia di Osaka dan anda tidak mampu membayar orang yang mereka bentuknya? Jika dakwaan itu benar, pampaskan CMG dan siasat bagaimana ini berlaku. Jika palsu, keluarkan dokumen perolehan. Ketelusan berfungsi dua hala — anda tidak boleh menceramah dunia tentang tadbir urus sementara kementerian anda sendiri dihantar surat tuntutan atas kecurian harta intelek.

Sumber: Marketing Interactive

item.title: MITI Accused of Stealing World Expo Osaka Pavilion Concept — Creative Agency Serves Letter of Demand
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / Current Media Group

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In August 2025, MITI under Tengku Zafrul was served a letter of demand by Current Media Group (CMG), a creative consultancy that claimed the ministry stole its concept for Malaysia’s World Expo 2025 Osaka pavilion without providing recognition or payment. The allegation was that CMG’s original pitch was used as the basis for the pavilion design without a contract or credit. Zafrul addressed the matter in a parliamentary reply to Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: If a government ministry used a private company’s intellectual property without payment or credit, that is theft — regardless of whether it’s called ‘inspiration’ or ‘parallel development.’ World Expo pavilions are high-profile national showcases with significant budgets. The allegation that MITI took a concept without paying for it raises serious questions about procurement integrity and intellectual property respect within the ministry.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The ‘reformist’ government that lectures the world about good governance and fair dealing allegedly stole a creative concept from a Malaysian company. PH came to power promising transparent procurement and respect for due process. If MITI can’t even commission a pavilion design without a letter of demand from the original creator, what does that say about how other, larger contracts are handled behind closed doors?

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Government IP theft from private contractors is a recurring complaint in Malaysia. Creative agencies, architects, and consultants have long reported that government pitches are treated as free work — ideas are taken, contracts go elsewhere, and the original creators are ghosted. Under BN, this was standard. Under PH, it was supposed to change.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You represent Malaysia on the world stage at Osaka and you can’t even pay the people who designed it? If the allegation is true, compensate CMG and investigate how this happened. If it’s false, release the procurement documents. Transparency works both ways — you don’t get to lecture the world about governance while your own ministry is served letters of demand for IP theft.

Sources: Marketing Interactive

item.title: Toda Peace Institute: Anwar 'Making Race-Based Policies WORSE, Not Better' — Trying to Out-Islamise the Islamists
item.description:
Said by: Toda Peace Institute Analysis

Category: Social

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 What Happened

The Toda Peace Institute published a comprehensive analysis titled ‘The Many Wars of Malaysia’s Anwar Ibrahim,’ concluding that Anwar ‘isn’t just reluctant to disturb the status quo; he is in fact making it worse by trying to out-Islamize the Islamists with gestures, policies and statements that the minorities find deeply worrying.’

🔥 Why It Matters

This is an international peace research institute saying that Malaysia’s supposedly reformist PM is WORSENING racial discrimination, not reducing it. The institutionalised system of race-based policies — described as ‘killing off merit, stifling competition and innovation, breeding minority resentments, and spawning cronyism by creating a rich Malay elite without doing much for poor Malays’ — is being strengthened rather than reformed under Anwar.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar’s Reformasi movement explicitly ‘sought an end to this system of institutionalized racial discrimination.’ That was the CORE PROMISE. Not a peripheral commitment — the fundamental reason minorities supported him. His ascent ‘understandably triggered hopes of racial justice.’ Instead, he’s competing with PAS on who can be more Malay-Muslim, while minorities who voted PH in droves get empty rhetoric and photo ops with the occasional Chinese school allocation announcement.

📜 Previous Cases

In April 2025, when a UiTM professor suggested opening cardiothoracic surgery training to non-Bumiputera students (because people were dying from surgeon shortages), the government was paralysed. The mandatory Malay language requirement for foreign workers was imposed. Bumiputera policies were declared ’non-negotiable’ in US trade talks. ICERD remains unratified. The civil service remains 90% Bumiputera.

🎯 PH Callout

You asked non-Malay Malaysians to trust you. They did — they voted for PH in overwhelming numbers. In return, you’ve not only maintained every discriminatory policy but, according to independent analysis, made things WORSE. You’ve traded minority rights for Malay-Muslim votes. Every minority Malaysian who voted PH deserves an apology — and an explanation for why their trust was weaponised against their own interests.

Sources

  • Toda Peace Institute — Report 184
  • CSIS — The Death of Reformasi

item.title: [BM] Krisis Doktor Kontrak: Ribuan Letak Jawatan Kerana Kerajaan Mungkiri Janji Jawatan Tetap
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Medical Association and contract doctors

Category: Health

Date: 2025-07-28

Doktor-Doktor Sedang Pergi

Menjelang pertengahan 2025, sistem penjagaan kesihatan Malaysia diisytiharkan “dalam keadaan kritikal” oleh FMT. Krisis doktor kontrak — bom jangka yang PH warisi dan janji untuk diselesaikan — semakin teruk sahaja.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Antara 2022 dan 2024, 705 doktor dan 1,394 jururawat dengan lebih lima tahun perkhidmatan meletak jawatan daripada hospital kerajaan.

Fakta: Persatuan Perubatan Malaysia menyeru pemansuhan sepenuhnya sistem kontrak, yang mengunci doktor dalam kontrak 3+2+2+4 tahun tanpa jaminan pekerjaan.

Fakta: Inflasi perubatan mencecah 12.6% pada 2025 — mengatasi purata global dan Asia Pasifik.

Fakta: KKM memotong tawaran pelantikan tetap sebanyak 31% untuk 2024-2025 walaupun peletakan jawatan semakin meningkat.

Fakta: Di bawah Rancangan Malaysia Ke-13, kerajaan menjanjikan RM40 bilion untuk penjagaan kesihatan. Sama ada ia akan terlaksana adalah persoalan lain.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Doktor pergi kerana kerajaan tidak mahu memberi mereka jawatan tetap. Ini bukan kekangan sumber — ini adalah pilihan dasar.
  • Anda tidak boleh membina sistem penjagaan kesihatan bertaraf dunia di atas belakang pekerja kontrak tanpa kepastian kerjaya.
  • RM40 bilion dalam perancangan tidak bermakna apa-apa jika orang yang menyampaikan penjagaan kesihatan sedang melangkah keluar pintu. Sumber: FMT · MMA/CodeBlue · The Edge

item.title: Contract Doctors Crisis: Thousands Resign as Government Breaks Promise on Permanent Posts
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Medical Association and contract doctors

Category: Health

Date: 2025-07-28

The Doctors Are Leaving

By mid-2025, Malaysia’s healthcare system was declared “in critical condition” by FMT. The contract doctor crisis — a ticking bomb PH inherited and promised to fix — had only gotten worse.

The Facts

Fact: Between 2022 and 2024, 705 doctors and 1,394 nurses with over five years of service resigned from public hospitals.

Fact: The Malaysian Medical Association called for the complete abolition of the contract system, which locks doctors into 3+2+2+4 year contracts with no job security.

Fact: Medical inflation hit 12.6% in 2025 — outpacing global and Asia-Pacific averages.

Fact: MOH cut permanent appointment offers by 31% for 2024-2025 even as resignations were rising.

Fact: Under the 13th Malaysia Plan, the government promised RM40 billion for healthcare. Whether it materialises is another question.

Why It Matters

  • Doctors are leaving because the government won’t give them permanent jobs. This is not a resource constraint — it is a policy choice.
  • You cannot build a world-class healthcare system on the backs of contract workers with no career certainty.
  • RM40 billion in plans means nothing if the people delivering healthcare are walking out the door. *Sources: FMT · MMA/CodeBlue · *The Edge

item.title: [BM] 18,000 Berarak Tuntut Anwar Turun — Himpunan 'Turun Anwar' Penuhi Dataran Merdeka Atas Janji Mungkir
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Public / Opposition Coalition

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 26 Julai 2025, dianggarkan 18,000 orang memenuhi Dataran Merdeka di Kuala Lumpur, memakai baju-T hitam dan bandana berslogan “Turun Anwar”. Ia merupakan bantahan besar pertama menentang kerajaan sejak Anwar berkuasa pada 2022.

Reuters melaporkan: “Ribuan orang berhimpun di ibu negara Malaysia menuntut peletakan jawatan Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim, ketika rasa tidak puas hati orang ramai meningkat terhadap kos sara hidup yang melonjak dan dakwaan gagal menunaikan janji reformasi.”

Bekas PM Mahathir Mohamad, berusia 100 tahun, menjadi penceramah utama. Beliau berkata kepada orang ramai: “Dia beri semua orang RM100 tetapi naikkan cukai pula. Dia tidak faham betapa teruknya golongan berpendapatan rendah menderita.”

Bekas PM Muhyiddin Yassin berkata rakyat telah mengadu tentang kenaikan harga daripada cukai jualan dan perkhidmatan yang diperluaskan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

18,000 orang tidak hadir dalam kepanasan KL untuk membantah suka-suka. Himpunan ini adalah referendum terhadap janji yang dimungkiri. Tuntutan bantahan itu seperti kad markah kegagalan PH:

  • Kos sara hidup tidak dikurangkan
  • Janji manifesto PRU15 dimungkiri
  • Harga naik, gaji statik
  • Kronisme dan nepotisme semakin teruk
  • Dasar Madani “dimainkan di atas kertas” sahaja
  • Pendidikan dan kesihatan diabaikan
  • Semangat reformasi dikhianati
  • Anak muda hilang harapan soal perumahan dan pekerjaan Tajuk Bloomberg berkata segalanya: “Janji Mungkir Anwar Cetus Bantahan Menuntut Penyingkirannya.”

💩 MENGAPA IA MENGARUT

Semasa Anwar dalam pembangkang, beliau meraikan bantahan jalanan sebagai suara rakyat. Beliau berarak bersama Bersih. Beliau menyeru hak berhimpun secara aman. Kini apabila jalanan menentang beliau, respons kerajaan adalah membingkainya sebagai kacau-bilau anjuran pembangkang.

Penganjur bantahan menyenaraikan 16 rungutan khusus. Tiada satu pun tentang kaum atau agama. Semuanya tentang tadbir urus, ekonomi, dan akauntabiliti. Ini adalah orang ramai pelbagai kaum yang bersatu oleh kekecewaan terhadap kerajaan yang menjanjikan segalanya dan menyampaikan alasan.

Al Jazeera menyatakan ia adalah “bantahan besar pertama sejak Anwar diangkat berkuasa.” Kepentingannya jelas: bulan madu sudah tamat.

📜 KES-KES TERDAHULU

Malaysia mempunyai tradisi membanggakan bantahan jalanan memacu perubahan politik. Himpunan Bersih dari 2007-2016 memainkan peranan penting membina momentum yang akhirnya menjatuhkan BN. Anwar adalah penerima manfaat gerakan itu. Kini gerakan itu telah berpaling kepadanya. Ironinya begitu puitis.

🎯 SERUAN PH

18,000 rakyat Malaysia berdiri dalam panas untuk memberitahu anda mereka kecewa. Bukan marah kepada pembangkang. Bukan dimanipulasi ejen asing. Kecewa dengan anda. Mereka berarak kerana anda menjanjikan reformasi dan menyampaikan status quo. Anda janji kos lebih rendah dan sampaikan perluasan SST. Anda janji tadbir urus bersih dan sampaikan DNAA Zahid. Jalanan yang membawa anda berkuasa kini meminta anda pergi. Adakah anda mendengar?

Sumber: Reuters | Bloomberg | Al Jazeera | Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Puluhan Ribu Berarak Tuntut Anwar Letak Jawatan Atas Kos Sara Hidup — Bantahan Besar Pertama Sejak PRU15
item.description:
Said by: Opposition parties and protesters

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-26

Jalanan Bersuara

Pada 26 Julai 2025, puluhan ribu rakyat Malaysia berarak ke Dataran Merdeka di Kuala Lumpur, menuntut Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim meletak jawatan. Himpunan itu — bantahan besar pertama sejak PRU15 — didorong oleh kos sara hidup yang meningkat dan janji reformasi yang dimungkiri.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Al Jazeera melaporkan ribuan berkumpul di Dataran Merdeka, menuntut Anwar meletak jawatan.

Fakta: Tajuk Bloomberg terus terang: “Janji Mungkir Anwar Cetus Bantahan Menuntut Penyingkirannya.”

Fakta: Himpunan dianjurkan parti pembangkang tetapi menarik penyertaan awam luas didorong kemarahan kos sara hidup.

Fakta: Menjelang Oktober 2025, tinjauan mendapati 77% rakyat Malaysia berkata mereka terbeban dengan kos sara hidup yang meningkat.

Fakta: Hanya beberapa hari sebelum bantahan, Anwar mengumumkan bayaran tunai RM100 kepada semua warga dewasa dan pemotongan harga RON95 — dilihat meluas sebagai langkah mendahului.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Apabila puluhan ribu berarak menentang kerajaan yang menjanjikan “keluarga Malaysia” dan reformasi, ia bukan bantahan pinggiran. Ia adalah keputusan.
  • Bantuan RM100 adalah rawatan politik — pelekat luka pada kesakitan ekonomi struktur.
  • 77% terbeban kos sara hidup. Itu bukan naratif pembangkang. Itu realiti negara. *Sumber: Al Jazeera · Bloomberg · *Business Today

item.title: 18,000 March to Oust Anwar — 'Turun Anwar' Rally Fills Dataran Merdeka Over Broken Promises
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Public / Opposition Coalition

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 26 July 2025, an estimated 18,000 people filled Dataran Merdeka (Independence Square) in Kuala Lumpur, wearing black t-shirts and bandannas with the slogan “Turun Anwar” (Step Down Anwar). It was the first major anti-government protest since Anwar came to power in 2022.

Reuters reported: “Thousands of people rallied in the Malaysian capital to demand the resignation of Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, as public discontent grows over rising living costs and allegations of failing to deliver promised reforms.”

Former PM Mahathir Mohamad, aged 100, was the keynote speaker. He told the crowd: “He gave everyone RM100 but raised taxes instead. He doesn’t understand how badly the lower-income groups are suffering.”

Former PM Muhyiddin Yassin said people had been complaining about price hikes from the expanded sales and service tax.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

18,000 people don’t show up in the KL heat to protest for fun. This rally was a referendum on broken promises. The protest’s demands read like a scorecard of PH failures:

  • Cost of living not reduced
  • GE15 manifesto promises broken
  • Prices rising, salaries stagnant
  • Cronyism and nepotism worsening
  • Madani policy is “played on paper” only
  • Education and health neglected
  • The spirit of reformasi betrayed
  • Young people losing hope on housing and jobs Bloomberg’s headline said it all: “Anwar’s Broken Promises Spark Protests Demanding His Ouster.”

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

When Anwar was in opposition, he celebrated street protests as the voice of the rakyat. He marched with Bersih. He called for the right to peaceful assembly. Now that the streets are against him, the government’s response was to frame it as opposition-organised troublemaking.

The protest organisers listed 16 specific grievances. Not one was about race or religion. They were all about governance, economics, and accountability. This was a multiracial crowd united by disappointment in a government that promised everything and delivered excuses.

Al Jazeera noted it was “the first major protest since Anwar was propelled to power.” The significance is clear: the honeymoon is over.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysia has a proud tradition of street protest driving political change. Bersih rallies from 2007-2016 were instrumental in building the momentum that eventually toppled BN. Anwar was a beneficiary of that movement. Now the movement has turned on him. The irony is poetic.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

18,000 Malaysians stood in the sun to tell you they’re disappointed. Not angry at the opposition. Not manipulated by foreign agents. Disappointed in you. They marched because you promised reform and delivered status quo. You promised lower costs and delivered SST expansion. You promised clean governance and delivered Zahid’s DNAA. The streets that carried you to power are now asking you to leave. Are you listening?

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | Al Jazeera | Straits Times

item.title: Tens of Thousands March Demanding Anwar Resign Over Cost of Living — First Major Protest Since GE15
item.description:
Said by: Opposition parties and protesters

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-26

The Streets Speak

On 26 July 2025, tens of thousands of Malaysians marched to Merdeka Square in Kuala Lumpur, demanding Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim step down. The rally — the first major protest since GE15 — was driven by rising costs and broken reform promises.

The Facts

Fact: Al Jazeera reported thousands gathered at Independence Square, calling for Anwar to resign.

Fact: Bloomberg’s headline was blunt: “Anwar’s Broken Promises Spark Protests Demanding His Ouster.”

Fact: The rally was organized by opposition parties but attracted broad public participation driven by cost of living anger.

Fact: By October 2025, a survey found 77% of Malaysians said they struggled with rising living costs.

Fact: Just days before the protest, Anwar announced a RM100 cash payment to all adult citizens and a further RON95 price cut — widely seen as a pre-emptive measure.

Why It Matters

  • When tens of thousands march against a government that promised “keluarga Malaysia” and reform, it is not a fringe protest. It is a verdict.
  • The RM100 handout was political triage — a band-aid on structural economic pain.
  • 77% struggling with cost of living. That is not an opposition narrative. That is a national reality. *Sources: Al Jazeera · Bloomberg · *Business Today
