item.title: [BM] SPRM Mahu Rampas RM3 Bilion Aset Keluarga Daim — Pengkritik Persoal Sama Ada Keadilan Atau Dendam
item.description:
Said by: MACC (Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-11

Merampas RM3 Bilion Daripada Keluarga Si Mati

Pada Julai 2025, SPRM mengumumkan ia sedang berusaha merampas dan melucuthakkan lebih daripada RM3 bilion aset merentasi 11 negara yang dikaitkan dengan mendiang Daim Zainuddin, keluarganya dan rakan-rakannya. Daim telah meninggal dunia lapan bulan sebelumnya, mengekalkan ketidasalahannya.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Daim berkhidmat dua kali sebagai Menteri Kewangan dan dikreditkan membantu Malaysia pulih daripada Krisis Kewangan Asia 1997.

Fakta: Beliau didakwa pada Januari 2024 kerana gagal mengisytihar aset, berikutan pendedahan Pandora Papers. Pertuduhan digugurkan selepas kematiannya pada November 2024.

Fakta: Walaupun beliau meninggal dunia, SPRM meneruskan siasatan dan mengeluarkan notis pelucuthakan baharu pada Disember 2025.

Fakta: Daim menafikan semua kesalahan, berkata siasatan itu “bermotif politik.”

Fakta: Bloomberg bertanya terus: “Bolehkah Malaysia merampas $700 juta daripada tokoh perniagaan yang sudah meninggal?”

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Jika kekayaan Daim diperolehi secara haram, pelucuthakan adalah wajar. Tetapi mengejar keluarga si mati sementara 47 pertuduhan Zahid digugurkan menimbulkan persoalan tidak selesa tentang keadilan terpilih.
  • Kredibiliti SPRM bergantung pada konsistensi. Mengejar berbilion Daim sambil mempertahankan skandal saham Azam Baki kelihatan seperti sasaran politik. *Sumber: Bloomberg · Bloomberg · *Malay Mail

item.title: MACC Seeks to Seize RM3 Billion in Daim Family Assets — Critics Ask If It's Justice or Vendetta
item.description:
Said by: MACC (Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-11

Seizing RM3 Billion From a Dead Man’s Family

In July 2025, MACC announced it was seeking to seize and forfeit more than RM3 billion in assets across 11 countries linked to the late Daim Zainuddin, his family, and associates. Daim had died eight months earlier, maintaining his innocence.

The Facts

Fact: Daim served twice as Finance Minister and was credited with helping Malaysia recover from the 1997 Asian Financial Crisis.

Fact: He was charged in January 2024 for failing to declare assets, following Pandora Papers revelations. The charge was dropped after his death in November 2024.

Fact: Despite his death, MACC continued investigations and issued fresh forfeiture notices in December 2025.

Fact: Daim had denied all wrongdoing, saying the investigations were “politically motivated.”

Fact: Bloomberg asked directly: “Could Malaysia seize $700 million from a dead tycoon?”

Why It Matters

  • If Daim’s wealth was illegally obtained, forfeiture is justified. But pursuing a dead man’s family while Zahid’s 47 charges were dropped raises uncomfortable questions about selective justice.
  • MACC’s credibility depends on consistency. Going after Daim’s billions while defending Azam Baki’s share scandal looks like political targeting. *Sources: Bloomberg · Bloomberg · *Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Perbicaraan Rasuah Lim Guan Eng — Pegawai SPRM Beri Keterangan Mengenai Wang Diberi Kepada Bekas KM Pulau Pinang
item.description:
Said by: Lim Guan Eng, former Penang CM / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-10

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 10 Julai 2025, seorang pegawai penyiasat SPRM memberitahu Mahkamah Sesyen bahawa semasa siasatan penipuan pada Disember 2019, Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli — ketika itu pengarah Consortium Zenith Construction (CZCSB) — mendedahkan beliau telah memberikan wang kepada kedua-dua G. Gnanaraja dan Lim Guan Eng berkaitan projek terowong dasar laut Pulau Pinang. Penguasa Kanan SPRM Ng Heng Jun mengesahkan beliau telah memaklumkan pegawai atasan mengenai kenyataan Zarul. Projek terowong itu, bernilai RM6.3 bilion, telah digambarkan oleh saksi SPRM sebagai ‘serupa penipuan tanah.’

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Lim Guan Eng adalah pemimpin paling terkenal DAP dan Ketua Menteri Pulau Pinang selama 10 tahun. Beliau adalah wajah model tadbir urus DAP. Jika dakwaan rasuah terowong dibuktikan, ia bukan sahaja membabitkan seorang ahli politik — ia membabitkan keseluruhan legasi DAP di Pulau Pinang. Projek RM6.3 bilion yang saksi SPRM gambarkan sebagai ‘penipuan tanah’ akan menjadi kes rasuah terbesar dikaitkan dengan pemimpin DAP dalam sejarah parti.

💩 MENGAPA IA MENGARUT: DAP telah memposisikan diri sebagai parti antirasuah selama berdekad. Keseluruhan jenamanya adalah tadbir urus bersih dan meritokrasi. Perbicaraan terowong Pulau Pinang mengancam meruntuhkan jenama itu. Dan respons DAP? Senyap daripada kepimpinan kebangsaan. Tiada tuntutan terbuka untuk akauntabiliti penuh. Tiada semakan bebas proses perolehan Pulau Pinang di bawah Guan Eng. Parti yang menuntut akauntabiliti daripada semua orang lain tidak mampu menuntutnya daripada diri sendiri.

📜 KES-KES TERDAHULU: Projek terowong diluluskan semasa pemerintahan Guan Eng, menggunakan mekanisme ‘permintaan cadangan’ dan bukannya tender terbuka — kaedah perolehan yang pengkritik kata memihak syarikat berkait. Kekaburan perolehan yang sama dikritik dalam Pelan Induk Pengangkutan Pulau Pinang (PTMP). Di bawah DAP, projek mega Pulau Pinang secara konsisten mengelak proses tender terbuka.

🎯 SERUAN PH: Bekas KM Pulau Pinang anda sedang dibicarakan atas rasuah berkaitan projek RM6.3 bilion yang SPRM panggil ‘penipuan tanah.’ KM Pulau Pinang semasa anda kata projek terowong itu ‘masih aktif.’ Parti anda tidak berkata apa-apa bermakna tentang akauntabiliti. Sama ada tuntut ketelusan penuh daripada pemimpin sendiri atau berhenti berpura-pura DAP adalah parti antirasuah. Anda tidak boleh dua-dua.

Sumber: Malay Mail

item.title: Lim Guan Eng Corruption Trial — MACC Officer Testifies About Money Given to Former Penang CM
item.description:
Said by: Lim Guan Eng, former Penang CM / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 10 July 2025, a MACC investigating officer told the Sessions Court that during a fraud investigation in December 2019, Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli — then director of Consortium Zenith Construction (CZCSB) — revealed he had given money to both G. Gnanaraja and Lim Guan Eng in connection with the Penang undersea tunnel project. MACC Senior Superintendent Ng Heng Jun confirmed he had informed his superiors about Zarul’s statement. The tunnel project, worth RM6.3 billion, has been described by MACC witnesses as ‘akin to a land scam.’

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Lim Guan Eng was DAP’s most prominent leader and Penang’s Chief Minister for 10 years. He’s the face of DAP’s governance model. If the tunnel corruption allegations are proven, it doesn’t just implicate one politician — it implicates DAP’s entire Penang legacy. A RM6.3 billion project that MACC witnesses describe as a ’land scam’ would be the biggest corruption case linked to a DAP leader in the party’s history.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP has positioned itself as the anti-corruption party for decades. Its entire brand is clean governance and meritocracy. The Penang tunnel trial threatens to demolish that brand. And DAP’s response? Silence from the national leadership. No public demand for full accountability. No independent review of Penang’s procurement processes under Guan Eng. The party that demands accountability from everyone else cannot bring itself to demand it from its own.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The tunnel project was approved during Guan Eng’s tenure, using a ‘request for proposal’ mechanism rather than open tender — a procurement method that critics say favours connected companies. The same procurement opacity was criticised in the Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP). Under DAP, Penang’s mega-projects consistently avoided open tender processes.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your former Penang CM is on trial for corruption related to a RM6.3 billion project that MACC calls a ’land scam.’ Your current Penang CM says the tunnel project is ‘still active.’ Your party has said nothing meaningful about accountability. Either demand full transparency from your own leaders or stop pretending DAP is the anti-corruption party. You can’t have it both ways.

Sources: Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] GLC Sebagai Tempat Buang Politik — 47.5 Lantikan Setahun, Lebih Banyak Daripada Mahathir 2.0
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS Think Tank Research

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Penyelidikan badan pemikir IDEAS mendapati kerajaan Anwar melantik purata 47.5 lantikan politik setahun ke Syarikat Berkaitan Kerajaan (GLC) dan badan berkanun — melebihi kadar Mahathir 2.0 sebanyak 43 setahun dan jauh melebihi matlamat lantikan berasaskan merit. Sebanyak 238 lantikan politik didokumenkan sepanjang tahun-tahun pertama Anwar.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

GLC mengawal bahagian besar ekonomi Malaysia — Petronas, Khazanah, TNB, Telekom, MAHB, dan berpuluh-puluh lagi. Apabila jawatan lembaga pengarah diisi berdasarkan kesetiaan politik dan bukannya kecekapan, akibatnya besar: keputusan strategik yang lemah, rasuah, dan salah urus. Setiap lantikan politik yang kurang kemahiran untuk peranan mereka merugikan ekonomi dari segi kecekapan, inovasi, dan nilai pemegang saham.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

PH bertanding atas platform JELAS menamatkan naungan politik dalam GLC. Anwar sendiri mengkritik amalan BN. Beliau menjanjikan lantikan berasaskan merit dan akauntabiliti prestasi. Sebaliknya, beliau melantik sekutu politik pada kadar LEBIH PANTAS daripada pendahulunya. Reformis yang berjanji menamatkan naungan telah mengindustrikannya.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Di bawah Najib, lantikan GLC adalah tunjang naungan UMNO. Skandal 1MDB sendiri sebahagiannya dimungkinkan oleh lembaga yang dilantik secara politik yang gagal dalam pengawasan. Di bawah Mahathir 2.0, 43/tahun. Di bawah Anwar, 47.5/tahun. Setiap kerajaan menjanjikan reformasi; setiap kerajaan mengukuhkan naungan lebih dalam.

🎯 Seruan PH

47.5 lantikan politik setahun. LEBIH daripada kerajaan Mahathir. Anda tidak mereformasi tadbir urus GLC — anda mempercepatkan mesin naungan. Setiap lantikan politik tidak layak di lembaga GLC mewakili seorang profesional berkelayakan yang dilepaskan. Ini adalah rasuah dengan langkah tambahan.

Sumber

  • Eurasia Review — Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum
  • Rosa Luxemburg — Malaysia 2024

item.title: [BM] Reformasi Perkhidmatan Awam: Tiga Tahun Bercakap, Sifar Perubahan Struktur — 1.6 Juta Birokrat Tidak Disentuh
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS / Public Policy Analysts

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Anwar Ibrahim memasuki pejabat menjanjikan reformasi perkhidmatan awam menyeluruh — pemodenan, meritokrasi, mengecilkan birokrasi yang gemuk yang memakan bahagian besar belanjawan negara. Tiga tahun kemudian, perkhidmatan awam kekal pada dasarnya tidak berubah: 1.3-1.6 juta pekerja (tidak termasuk polis dan tentera), 90% Bumiputera, memakan kira-kira 40% daripada jumlah perbelanjaan mengurus kerajaan dalam gaji dan pencen.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Badan pemikir IDEAS mendapati kerajaan Anwar sebenarnya meningkatkan lantikan politik ke GLC dan badan berkanun, purata 47.5 setahun — melebihi kadar Mahathir 2.0 sebanyak 43 setahun. Birokrasi yang gemuk bermakna Malaysia membelanjakan lebih banyak untuk gaji penjawat awam berbanding perbelanjaan pembangunan. Kewajipan pencen terus membengkak tanpa reformasi struktur.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Cadangan untuk penilaian berasaskan prestasi, pengecilan saiz, dan kenaikan pangkat berasaskan merit tidak ke mana kerana undi perkhidmatan awam adalah penting secara politik — tiada kerajaan mahu menggusarkan 1.6 juta pengundi dan keluarga mereka. Reformasi sentiasa pembohongan; perkhidmatan awam adalah tabung undi PH sama seperti BN dahulu.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda janji meritokrasi. Anda sampaikan lebih banyak lantikan politik daripada Mahathir. Anda janji pengecilan saiz. Birokrasi membesar. Anda janji pemodenan. Ketidakcekapan yang sama berterusan. Pada satu ketika, ‘reformasi’ berhenti menjadi janji dan menjadi bahan jenaka. Anda sudah melepasi titik itu dua tahun lalu.

Sumber

  • Eurasia Review — Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum

item.title: Civil Service Reform: Three Years of Talk, Zero Structural Change — 1.6 Million Bureaucrats Untouched
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS / Public Policy Analysts

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-04

📌 What Happened

Anwar Ibrahim entered office promising sweeping civil service reform — modernisation, meritocracy, right-sizing a bloated bureaucracy that consumes a massive share of the national budget. Three years later, the civil service remains essentially unchanged: 1.3-1.6 million employees (excluding police and military), 90% Bumiputera, consuming approximately 40% of total government operating expenditure in salaries and pensions.

🔥 Why It Matters

IDEAS think tank found that the Anwar government actually increased political appointments to GLCs and statutory bodies, averaging 47.5 per year — exceeding even Mahathir 2.0’s rate of 43 per year. The bloated bureaucracy means Malaysia spends more on civil servant wages than on development spending. Pension obligations continue to balloon with no structural reform.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Proposals for performance-based assessments, right-sizing, and merit-based promotions have gone nowhere because the civil service vote is politically crucial — no government wants to antagonise 1.6 million voters and their families. Reform was always a lie; the civil service is PH’s vote bank just as it was BN’s.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised meritocracy. You delivered more political appointments than Mahathir. You promised right-sizing. The bureaucracy grew. You promised modernisation. The same inefficiencies persist. At some point, ‘reform’ stops being a promise and becomes a punchline. You passed that point two years ago.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: GLCs as Political Dumping Ground — 47.5 Appointees Per Year, Even MORE Than Mahathir 2.0
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS Think Tank Research

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-07-04

📌 What Happened

Research by IDEAS think tank found that the Anwar government appointed an average of 47.5 political appointees per year to Government-Linked Companies (GLCs) and statutory bodies — exceeding even Mahathir 2.0’s rate of 43 per year and far surpassing the stated goal of merit-based appointments. A total of 238 political appointments were documented across Anwar’s first years.

🔥 Why It Matters

GLCs control a massive share of the Malaysian economy — Petronas, Khazanah, TNB, Telekom, MAHB, and dozens more. When board positions are filled based on political loyalty rather than competence, the consequences are enormous: poor strategic decisions, corruption, and mismanagement. Every political appointee who lacks the skills for their role costs the economy in efficiency, innovation, and shareholder value.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH ran on an EXPLICIT platform of ending political patronage in GLCs. Anwar himself criticised BN’s practice. He promised merit-based appointments and performance accountability. Instead, he’s appointing political allies at a FASTER rate than his predecessors. The reformist who promised to end patronage has industrialised it.

📜 Previous Cases

Under Najib, GLC appointments were a cornerstone of UMNO patronage. The 1MDB scandal itself was partly enabled by politically-appointed boards that failed in oversight. Under Mahathir 2.0, 43/year. Under Anwar, 47.5/year. Each government promises reform; each government entrenches patronage deeper.

🎯 PH Callout

47.5 political appointees per year. MORE than Mahathir’s government. You didn’t reform GLC governance — you accelerated the patronage machine. Every unqualified political appointee on a GLC board represents a qualified professional passed over. This is corruption with extra steps.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies
  • Rosa Luxemburg — Malaysia 2024

item.title: [BM] Kontroversi Pelantikan Kehakiman 2025 — Pilihan Anwar Untuk Ketua Hakim Negara Cetus Kemarahan

item.title: [BM] Tarif Elektrik Industri Dinaikkan Pada Hari Sama SST Diperluaskan — 'Periuk Berita Buruk'
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-01

Periuk Berita Buruk

Pada 1 Julai 2025, Malaysia dilanda tamparan berganda: SST yang diperluaskan berkuat kuasa, meliputi sewaan, penjagaan kesihatan, pendidikan dan banyak lagi, seiring kenaikan tarif elektrik untuk sektor perindustrian. Straits Times memanggilnya “periuk berita buruk ekonomi.”

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Perluasan SST berkuat kuasa 1 Julai 2025, mengenakan cukai ke atas enam kategori perkhidmatan baharu termasuk sewaan, pembinaan, perkhidmatan kewangan, penjagaan kesihatan swasta, dan pendidikan.

Fakta: Pada hari yang sama, tarif elektrik meningkat untuk sektor perindustrian.

Fakta: Kerajaan juga merancang mengurangkan subsidi gas memasak untuk kedai makan komersial dan melaksanakan reformasi subsidi RON95 menjelang akhir tahun.

Fakta: Straits Times menyatakan “perluasan SST hanyalah langkah kontroversi terbaru” oleh kerajaan.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Mana-mana satu langkah ini boleh ditangani. Kesemua pada garis masa yang sama adalah serangan kos sara hidup.
  • Kesan terkumpul kepada perniagaan kecil adalah dahsyat: cukai lebih tinggi, elektrik lebih tinggi, minyak lebih tinggi, dan subsidi dikurangkan.
  • Kerajaan kata setiap langkah adalah “perlu.” Tetapi keperluan tanpa penjujukan tetap tadbir urus yang lemah. *Sumber: *Straits Times

item.title: Electricity Tariffs Hiked for Industry on Same Day as SST Expansion — 'Hotpot of Bad News'
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-07-01

The Hotpot of Bad News

On 1 July 2025, Malaysia was hit by a double whammy: the expanded SST took effect, covering rental, healthcare, education, and more, alongside increased electricity tariffs for the industrial sector. The Straits Times called it a “hotpot of bad economic news.”

The Facts

Fact: SST expansion took effect 1 July 2025, taxing six new service categories including rental, construction, financial services, private healthcare, and education.

Fact: On the same day, electricity tariffs increased for the industrial sector.

Fact: The government was also planning to reduce cooking gas subsidies for commercial eateries and implement the RON95 subsidy reform by year end.

Fact: The Straits Times noted the “widening of the SST is just the latest controversial move” by the government.

Why It Matters

  • Any one of these measures is manageable. All of them on the same timeline is a cost of living assault.
  • The cumulative effect on small businesses is devastating: higher tax, higher electricity, higher fuel, and reduced subsidies.
  • The government says each measure is “necessary.” But necessity without sequencing is still bad governance. *Source: *Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Syed Saddiq Dibebaskan Selepas '5 Tahun Azab' — Adakah Ia Pendakwaan Politik?
item.description:
Said by: Court of Appeal / Prosecution

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-25

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Mahkamah Rayuan sebulat suara membebaskan Syed Saddiq daripada kesemua 4 pertuduhan, menegur Mahkamah Tinggi kerana tidak menolak kes itu lebih awal. Beliau telah dijatuhkan hukuman 7 tahun penjara dan sebatan oleh mahkamah bawahan. Pendakwaan kemudian merayu ke Mahkamah Persekutuan, yang memberikan ’tempoh bertenang’ yang jarang berlaku. SCMP memanggilnya ‘5 tahun azab.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Seorang ahli politik pembangkang muda telah melalui setengah dekad prosiding undang-undang yang Mahkamah Rayuan kata sepatutnya ditolak jauh lebih awal. Sama ada bermotif politik atau tidak, sistem telah mengecewakan beliau. Sabitan asal begitu lemah sehinggakan mahkamah rayuan jelas kecewa.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Jika badan kehakiman boleh melakukan ini kepada seorang AP yang sedang berkhidmat, ia boleh melakukannya kepada sesiapa. Kes ini menimbulkan persoalan tentang budi bicara pendakwaan di bawah beberapa Peguam Negara berturut-turut — dan mengapa kerajaan merayu walaupun selepas pembebasan. Pendakwaan terpilih kekal sebagai senjata politik paling berkuasa di Malaysia.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda mendakwa memperjuangkan kebebasan kehakiman dan proses sewajarnya. Tetapi apabila seorang AP pembangkang dibebaskan selepas lima tahun azab — dan perkhidmatan pendakwaan anda masih merayu — anda bukan mempertahankan keadilan, anda memanjangkan penganiayaan. Tarik balik rayuan atau jelaskan mengapa keputusan sebulat suara Mahkamah Rayuan tidak mencukupi.

Sumber

  • SCMP — Syed Saddiq Dibebaskan
  • FMT — Tempoh Bertenang Mahkamah Persekutuan

item.title: Syed Saddiq Acquitted After '5 Years of Hell' — Was It Political Prosecution?
item.description:
Said by: Court of Appeal / Prosecution

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-25

📌 What Happened

Court of Appeal unanimously acquitted Syed Saddiq of all 4 charges, admonishing the High Court for not throwing out the case earlier. He had been sentenced to 7 years jail and caning by the lower court. The prosecution then appealed to the Federal Court, which gave a rare ‘cooling-off period.’ SCMP called it ‘5 years of hell.’

🔥 Why It Matters

A young opposition politician was put through half a decade of legal proceedings that the Court of Appeal said should have been dismissed far earlier. Whether or not it was politically motivated, the system failed him. The original conviction was so weak that the appellate court was visibly frustrated.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

If the judiciary can do this to a sitting MP, it can do it to anyone. The case raises questions about prosecutorial discretion under successive AGs — and why the government appealed even after the acquittal. Selective prosecution remains Malaysia’s most potent political weapon.

🎯 PH Callout

You claim to champion judicial independence and due process. But when an opposition MP is acquitted after five years of hell — and your prosecution service still appeals — you’re not defending justice, you’re prolonging persecution. Drop the appeal or explain why the Court of Appeal’s unanimous verdict isn’t good enough.

Sources

  • SCMP — Syed Saddiq Acquitted
  • FMT — Federal Court Cooling-Off

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Ramli Dipaksa Keluar Dari Kementerian Ekonomi — Anwar Kata 'Tiada Perlu Rombak Apa-apa'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-16

Menteri Ekonomi Pergi Dan PM Mengangkat Bahu

Pada Jun 2025, Rafizi Ramli — boleh dikatakan menteri paling berasaskan data dalam kabinet Anwar — meletak jawatan sebagai Menteri Ekonomi selepas kalah dalam perlumbaan timbalan presiden PKR kepada Nurul Izzah. Respons Anwar amat santai.

Fakta-Fakta

Fakta: Rafizi meletak jawatan daripada kabinet pada 16 Jun 2025, berikutan kekalahannya dalam pilihan raya parti PKR.

Fakta: Anwar memberitahu wartawan: “Saya tidak nampak keperluan untuk merombak apa-apa setakat ini. Saya ucapkan selamat maju jaya kepada Rafizi.”

Fakta: Portfolio Kementerian Ekonomi kekal kosong selama enam bulan sehingga rombakan Disember 2025, apabila Akmal Nasrullah dilantik.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Kementerian Ekonomi bukan portfolio kecil. Membiarkannya kosong selama setengah tahun semasa menguruskan reformasi subsidi, tekanan kos sara hidup, dan penggabungan fiskal adalah kecuaian.
  • Sikap santai Anwar menunjukkan ekonomi sebenar tidak pernah sepenting ekonomi politik — iaitu pengurusan gabungan dan penempatan pilihan raya.
  • Rafizi sejak itu muncul sebagai pengkritik dalaman, memberi amaran bahawa Malaysia “tidak mampu lagi” menangguhkan reformasi. SCMP bertanya pada Februari 2026 sama ada Rafizi kini adalah “hati nurani gerakan reformasi” — gelaran yang pernah dipegang Anwar. *Sumber: Bloomberg · SCMP · *Reuters

item.title: Rafizi Ramli Forced Out of Economy Ministry — Anwar Says 'No Need to Reshuffle Anything'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-16

The Economy Minister Leaves and the PM Shrugs

In June 2025, Rafizi Ramli — arguably the most data-driven minister in Anwar’s cabinet — resigned as Economy Minister after losing the PKR deputy president race to Nurul Izzah. Anwar’s response was breathtakingly casual.

The Facts

Fact: Rafizi resigned from cabinet on 16 June 2025, following his defeat in the PKR party elections.

Fact: Anwar told reporters: “I do not see the need to reshuffle anything so far. I wish all the best to Rafizi.”

Fact: The Economy Ministry portfolio remained vacant for six months until the December 2025 reshuffle, when Akmal Nasrullah was appointed.

Why It Matters

  • The Economy Ministry is not a minor portfolio. Leaving it vacant for half a year while managing subsidy reform, cost of living pressures, and fiscal consolidation is negligence.
  • Anwar’s casual dismissal suggests the real economy was never as important as the political economy — meaning coalition management and election positioning.
  • Rafizi has since emerged as an internal critic, warning that Malaysia “can no longer afford” to delay reforms. SCMP asked in February 2026 whether Rafizi is now “the conscience of the reformasi movement” — a title Anwar once held. *Sources: Bloomberg · SCMP · *Reuters

item.title: [BM] Beban Pensijilan Halal Wajib Ke Atas PKS Bukan Islam — Bayar Atau Tutup Kedai
item.description:
Said by: SME Owners / JAKIM

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Keperluan pensijilan halal JAKIM yang semakin meluas mengenakan kos pematuhan yang ketara ke atas perniagaan makanan, termasuk yang dimiliki oleh bukan Islam. Perniagaan mesti membayar untuk pensijilan, mematuhi keperluan menyeluruh meliputi ramuan, penyediaan, penyimpanan dan kakitangan, serta menjalani audit berkala. Belanjawan JAKIM sebanyak RM2.6 bilion menjadikannya salah satu agensi paling banyak dibiayai dalam kerajaan — lebih banyak daripada banyak agensi yang berkhidmat terus kepada semua rakyat Malaysia.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Bagi perniagaan makanan kecil bukan Islam — kedai kopi Cina, restoran India, kedai masakan campuran — keperluan pensijilan halal mewujudkan beban kewangan dan operasi yang boleh menjadi terlalu membebankan. Pilihan menjadi: belanja beribu-ribu untuk pematuhan halal (dalam perniagaan yang mungkin tidak melayani majoriti pelanggan Islam) atau hilang akses kepada kontrak kerajaan, acara, dan ruang komersial tertentu yang memerlukan pensijilan halal.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

JAKIM menerima RM2.6 bilion setahun — lebih daripada belanjawan sukan, lebih daripada perlindungan alam sekitar, dan tidak seimbang dengan sumbangannya kepada KDNK atau kebajikan sosial. Rejim pensijilan yang semakin meluas berfungsi sebagai cukai agama ke atas perniagaan, dengan kos akhirnya diserahkan kepada pengguna. Bagi seorang PM yang memperjuangkan imej ‘sederhana’ di peringkat antarabangsa, pertumbuhan kuasa dan belanjawan JAKIM di bawah pengawasannya menceritakan kisah berbeza di dalam negara.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Kontroversi KK Mart pada 2024 — di mana rangkaian kedai serbaneka diboikot dan menghadapi pertuduhan jenayah kerana secara tidak sengaja menjual stoking dengan reka bentuk berkaitan Allah — menunjukkan bagaimana penguatkuasaan agama boleh memusnahkan perniagaan atas insiden kecil. Di bawah PH, agensi agama tidak diikat; mereka dikuatkan.

🎯 Seruan PH

Kerajaan ‘Madani’ anda membelanjakan RM2.6 bilion untuk JAKIM sementara penjagaan kesihatan runtuh dan sekolah menghasilkan pelajar yang tidak boleh mengira. Perniagaan bukan Islam mensubsidi birokrasi agama melalui kos pematuhan wajib. Jika ‘Malaysia Madani’ bermakna semua rakyat Malaysia, mengapa peruntukan belanjawan menunjukkan ia bermakna hanya infrastruktur agama satu komuniti?

Sumber

  • CNA — Anwar Hadapi Krisis Rasuah
  • Eurasia Review — Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum

item.title: [BM] CSIS Isytihar 'Kematian Reformasi' — Gerakan Anwar Sendiri Diisytihar Mati Oleh Badan Pemikir Washington
item.description:
Said by: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), salah satu badan pemikir dasar paling berpengaruh di Washington, menerbitkan analisis yang memusnahkan bertajuk ‘Kematian Reformasi: Anwar Ibrahim, UMNO, dan Pengkhianatan Sebuah Gerakan.’ Artikel itu berhujah bahawa identiti politik Anwar telah ‘semakin ditakrifkan oleh kompromi yang beliau buat untuk memastikan kedudukannya dalam kerajaan.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Reformasi bukan sekadar gerakan politik — ia adalah janji generasi. Berjuta-juta rakyat Malaysia, daripada pelajar universiti hingga keluarga kelas pekerja, melaburkan berdekad harapan, bantahan, dan pengorbanan dalam kepercayaan bahawa Anwar Ibrahim mewakili politik yang berbeza secara asasi. CSIS mengisytiharkannya mati bukan retorik pembangkang — ia adalah keputusan pertubuhan dasar antarabangsa.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Anwar menghabiskan 11 tahun dalam penjara, menahan dua perbicaraan liwat, terselamat daripada pengasingan politik, dan membina seluruh gerakan atas satu perkataan: reformasi. Beliau meminta rakyat Malaysia mempercayainya. Mereka percaya. Dan apa yang mereka dapat? Gabungan dengan UMNO, parti yang memenjarakannya. Zahid Hamidi, yang menjadi anjing serang UMNO, kini timbalan beliau. Orang yang melawan sistem ADALAH sistem itu.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pada 2018, PH menang atas mandat reformasi tetapi runtuh dalam 22 bulan akibat pengkhianatan dalaman (Langkah Sheraton). Anwar sepatutnya menjadi pembetulan — pemimpin yang akhirnya akan menyampaikan. Sebaliknya, beliau membentuk gabungan yang lebih berkompromi daripada PH 1.0.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda membina gerakan atas janji perubahan. Anda meminta rakyat berarak, berisiko ditangkap, untuk percaya. Dan kemudian anda berganding dengan orang yang tepat yang mereka bantah. Itu bukan pragmatisme — itu pengkhianatan muktamad. Reformasi tidak mati kerana ia gagal. Ia mati kerana anda membunuhnya.

Sumber

  • CSIS — Kematian Reformasi

item.title: [BM] Kelemahan Ringgit Berterusan Walaupun Pertumbuhan KDNK — Mata Wang Tak Percaya Kisah Ekonomi Anwar
item.description:
Said by: BNM / Currency Markets

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Walaupun naratif pertumbuhan KDNK yang optimistik daripada kerajaan, ringgit Malaysia kekal lemah secara berterusan berbanding dolar AS dan mata wang serantau, berlegar sekitar paras terendah dalam sejarah. Pasaran mata wang — yang menghimpunkan pertimbangan kolektif ribuan pelabur dan penganalisis — pada dasarnya mengundi tidak percaya terhadap asas ekonomi Malaysia.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Kelemahan ringgit membawa kesan langsung kepada rakyat Malaysia biasa: barangan import menjadi lebih mahal (menyumbang kepada inflasi makanan yang dirasai rakyat tetapi tidak dicerminkan sepenuhnya oleh statistik rasmi), kos pendidikan luar negara meningkat, dan penghijrahan otak semakin pesat kerana profesional Malaysia mendapati kemahiran mereka bernilai berlipat ganda di negara mata wang lebih kukuh.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kerajaan menunjuk kepada faktor global termasuk dasar monetari AS dan ketidaktentuan geopolitik, dan ini memang faktor sebenar. Tetapi perbandingan serantau mendedahkan ringgit telah mengatasi prestasi beberapa rakan ASEAN, menunjukkan faktor khusus Malaysia sedang bermain: ketidaktentuan politik, skandal SPRM, kebimbangan tentang kelestarian fiskal dengan nisbah hutang-kepada-KDNK 64.6%, dan persoalan tentang kebolehpercayaan reformasi ekonomi. Pasaran mata wang adalah barometer kejujuran yang brutal terhadap keyakinan negara. Kelemahan ringgit menceritakan kisah yang tidak diceritakan oleh angka KDNK kerajaan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda boleh memilih angka KDNK untuk sidang akhbar. Tetapi anda tidak boleh memutar pasaran forex. Ringgit tidak peduli tentang poin cakap anda — ia bertindak balas kepada asas. Dan sekarang, asas tersebut mengatakan pasaran tidak percaya kisah ekonomi anda. Betulkan substansi, dan mata wang akan mengikut. Terus berputar, dan ia tidak akan.

Sumber

  • Bloomberg — Anwar Mengenai Tekanan Hutang

item.description:

item.title: [BM] Krisis Persaraan KWSP — Purata Baki RM47,600 Pada Umur 55 Bermakna KEMISKINAN Di Hari Tua
item.description:
Said by: EPF Data / Retirement Adequacy Studies

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Data KWSP secara konsisten menunjukkan majoriti rakyat Malaysia mempunyai simpanan persaraan yang sangat tidak mencukupi. Baki median pada umur 55 (umur pengeluaran wajib) adalah kira-kira RM47,600 — cukup untuk bertahan kira-kira 5 tahun pada RM800/bulan, jauh di bawah mana-mana garis kemiskinan yang munasabah. Dasar kerajaan membenarkan pengeluaran KWSP berulang (Akaun 3 menyaksikan RM14.79 bilion dikeluarkan oleh 4.63 juta pencarum) telah mempercepatkan kehabisan dana persaraan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Malaysia tidak mempunyai jaring keselamatan sosial menyeluruh untuk pesara selain KWSP (untuk sektor swasta) dan pencen (untuk penjawat awam). Apabila KWSP habis — yang berlaku untuk kebanyakan pesara dalam 5-10 tahun — warga emas Malaysia menghadapi kesengsaraan. Tiada pencen usia tua sejagat, kemudahan jagaan warga emas awam yang tidak mencukupi, dan bantuan sosial yang terhad. Satu generasi rakyat Malaysia sedang menuju kemiskinan persaraan.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Daripada mengukuhkan simpanan persaraan, kerajaan telah berulang kali merompak KWSP untuk kemenangan politik jangka pendek. Akaun 3 — skim pengeluaran terbaru — dibentangkan sebagai ‘fleksibiliti’ tetapi hakikatnya kerajaan membenarkan rakyat membelanjakan masa depan mereka untuk bertahan pada masa kini. Apabila anda tidak mampu membetulkan gaji, kos sara hidup, atau pekerjaan, jawapan yang mudah secara politik ialah ‘biarkan mereka makan persaraan mereka.’ Ia bukan penyelesaian — ia menendang malapetaka 20 tahun ke hadapan.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pengeluaran KWSP semasa COVID-19 (di bawah PN/BN) menghabiskan RM145 bilion daripada dana. PH mengkritik pengeluaran itu sebagai tidak bertanggungjawab. Kemudian PH memperkenalkan Akaun 3 dengan syarat pengeluaran yang lebih mudah. Setiap kerajaan — BN, PN, PH — telah melayan KWSP sebagai tabung simpanan untuk kemudahan politik. Pesara tahun 2040 akan membayar harganya.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda mengkritik Muhyiddin kerana membenarkan pengeluaran KWSP COVID. Kemudian anda mencipta Akaun 3, menjadikannya LEBIH MUDAH untuk mengeluarkan simpanan persaraan. RM14.79 bilion lesap dalam beberapa bulan. Anda telah memperdagangkan jaminan kewangan persaraan 4.63 juta rakyat Malaysia untuk kelulusan politik jangka pendek. Apabila orang-orang ini berumur 70 dan melarat, mereka akan ingat siapa yang membenarkan mereka membelanjakan hari tua mereka hari ini.

Sumber

  • KWSP — Belanjawan 2026
  • CNA — Pelan Pembangunan Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Pengumuman FDI Lawan Pekerjaan Sebenar — Angka RM Nampak Hebat, Tapi Mana Pekerjaan Untuk Rakyat Malaysia?
item.description:
Said by: MIDA / Economic Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Kerajaan kerap mengumumkan angka Pelaburan Langsung Asing (FDI) yang besar — berbilion ringgit dalam komitmen daripada gergasi teknologi, pengeluar dan pengendali pusat data. Pengumuman ini menjana tajuk berita yang mengagumkan. Walau bagaimanapun, jurang antara FDI yang diumumkan dan pelaburan sebenar yang direalisasikan, dan antara pelaburan dan pekerjaan sebenar rakyat Malaysia, kekal ketara.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Pengumuman FDI adalah kemenangan PR yang mudah — anda berdiri di sebelah CEO asing, tandatangan MOU, dan umumkan berbilion. Tetapi FDI diluluskan ≠ FDI direalisasikan (banyak projek ditangguh atau dibatalkan). FDI direalisasikan ≠ pekerjaan rakyat Malaysia (banyak pelaburan berintensif modal dengan pekerjaan minimum, atau menggaji pakar asing). Dan pekerjaan yang diwujudkan sering bukan jawatan berkualiti tinggi, bergaji baik yang menangani cabaran pengangguran dan genangan gaji Malaysia.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Pusat data — permata mahkota pengumuman FDI — menggaji sangat sedikit orang berbanding saiz pelaburan mereka. Pusat data bernilai berbilion ringgit mungkin menggaji 50-200 kakitangan tetap, kebanyakan pakar teknikal. Bandingkan dengan kilang yang menggaji ribuan. Kerajaan mempromosikan angka FDI yang nampak mengagumkan di atas kertas sementara pengangguran belia kekal pada 12.28% dan pengangguran graduan berterusan. Angka tajuk berita tidak diterjemahkan kepada kehidupan rakyat Malaysia biasa.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

BN juga kerap mengumumkan angka FDI yang besar sementara kualiti pekerjaan tergenang. Program Transformasi Ekonomi Najib menghasilkan statistik pelaburan yang mengagumkan tetapi gagal menaikkan gaji median secara bermakna atau mengurangkan ketidaksamaan. PH berjanji strategi FDI mereka akan berbeza — lebih berkualiti, lebih banyak pekerjaan, lebih banyak pemindahan teknologi. Strateginya identik: umumkan angka besar, harap tiada siapa semak data pekerjaan.

🎯 Seruan PH

Berhenti mengumumkan komitmen FDI seolah-olah ia pencapaian. Umumkan PEKERJAAN. Umumkan GAJI. Umumkan berapa rakyat Malaysia mendapat pekerjaan, pada gaji berapa, dalam peranan apa. Satu bilion ringgit FDI yang mewujudkan 100 pekerjaan bukan kisah kejayaan ekonomi — ia pemberian insentif cukai yang menguntungkan pemegang saham asing sementara rakyat Malaysia menghantar makanan mereka dengan motosikal untuk RM6 sehantar.

Sumber

  • ISEAS — Transformasi Bumiputera 2035
  • Bloomberg — Tekanan Hutang Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Perangkap Kebergantungan Buruh Asing — Ekonomi Tak Boleh Berfungsi Tanpa Pekerja Yang Malaysia Layan Macam Barang Buangan
item.description:
Said by: Labour Economists / ILO

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Malaysia kekal sangat bergantung kepada pekerja asing — dianggarkan 2-3 juta berdokumen dan mungkin berjuta lagi tanpa dokumen — dalam sektor kritikal termasuk pembinaan, pembuatan, pertanian, dan perkhidmatan. Dasar pekerja asing kerajaan berayun antara liberalisasi dan sekatan berdasarkan angin politik dan bukannya perancangan ekonomi, mewujudkan ketidaktentuan kronik bagi majikan dan pekerja.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Kebergantungan buruh asing mendedahkan kegagalan Malaysia untuk bergerak naik rantaian nilai — ekonomi masih bergantung secara struktur kepada buruh murah yang boleh dieksploitasi dan bukannya automasi, teknologi, dan pekerja domestik berkemahiran. Kebergantungan ini menekan gaji tempatan, membolehkan eksploitasi, dan mewujudkan ketegangan sosial.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Setiap kerajaan berjanji ‘mengurangkan kebergantungan kepada buruh asing’ dan ‘bergerak ke ekonomi bernilai tinggi.’ Setiap kerajaan kemudian membenarkan berjuta pekerja asing kerana industri yang membiayai parti politik tidak boleh berfungsi tanpa mereka. Ladang kelapa sawit, syarikat pembinaan, dan kilang MEMERLUKAN buruh asing murah. Ahli politik MEMERLUKAN pembiayaan kempen daripada industri ini. Hasilnya ialah kitaran janji reformasi dan kebergantungan sebenar yang berkekalan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Dasar pekerja asing BN sama kacau dan membolehkan eksploitasi. Laporan Pemerdagangan Orang Jabatan Negara AS telah menandakan isu buruh paksa Malaysia selama bertahun-tahun — di bawah BN, PN, dan PH. Sistem levi pekerja asing pelbagai peringkat, yang berulang kali ditangguhkan, sepatutnya menjadi penyelesaian PH. Ia bukan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Ekonomi anda secara literal tidak boleh membina bangunan, menuai buah kelapa sawit, atau menjalankan restoran tanpa pekerja asing — pekerja yang tiada hak, tiada perlindungan, dan tiada suara. Kerangka ‘Madani’ anda nampaknya tidak merangkumi berjuta manusia yang sebenarnya menggerakkan ekonomi anda. Betulkan eksploitasi, betulkan gaji, betulkan kebergantungan — atau berhenti berpura-pura Malaysia adalah ekonomi yang bertamadun.

Sumber

  • ISEAS — Reformasi Subsidi Petrol
  • Eurasia Review — Dasar Ekonomi Berasaskan Kaum

item.title: CSIS Declares 'The Death of Reformasi' — Anwar's Own Movement Pronounced Dead by Washington Think Tank
item.description:
Said by: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), one of Washington’s most influential policy think tanks, published a devastating analysis titled ‘The Death of Reformasi: Anwar Ibrahim, UMNO, and the Betrayal of a Movement.’ The piece argues that Anwar’s political identity has been ‘increasingly defined by the compromises he has made to secure his position within the government.’

🔥 Why It Matters

Reformasi wasn’t just a political movement — it was a generational promise. Millions of Malaysians, from university students to working-class families, invested decades of hope, protest, and sacrifice in the belief that Anwar Ibrahim represented a fundamentally different kind of politics. CSIS declaring it dead isn’t opposition rhetoric — it’s the verdict of the international policy establishment.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar spent 11 years in prison, endured two sodomy trials, survived political exile, and built an entire movement around one word: reform. He asked Malaysians to trust him. They did. And what did they get? A coalition with UMNO, the very party that imprisoned him. Zahid Hamidi, who was UMNO’s attack dog, is now his deputy. The man who fought the system IS the system.

📜 Previous Cases

In 2018, PH won on a reform mandate but collapsed within 22 months due to internal betrayal (Sheraton Move). Anwar was supposed to be the corrective — the leader who would finally deliver. Instead, he formed an even more compromised coalition than PH 1.0.

🎯 PH Callout

You built a movement on the promise of change. You asked people to march, to risk arrest, to believe. And then you partnered with the exact people they marched against. That’s not pragmatism — that’s the ultimate betrayal. Reformasi isn’t dead because it failed. It’s dead because you killed it.

Sources

  • CSIS — The Death of Reformasi

item.title: EPF Retirement Crisis — Average Balance of RM47,600 at Age 55 Means POVERTY in Old Age
item.description:
Said by: EPF Data / Retirement Adequacy Studies

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

EPF data consistently shows that the majority of Malaysians have grossly inadequate retirement savings. The median balance at age 55 (mandatory withdrawal age) is approximately RM47,600 — enough to last roughly 5 years at RM800/month, well below any reasonable poverty line. The government’s policy of allowing repeated EPF withdrawals (Account 3 saw RM14.79 billion withdrawn by 4.63 million contributors) has accelerated retirement fund depletion.

🔥 Why It Matters

Malaysia has no comprehensive social safety net for retirees outside of EPF (for private sector) and pensions (for civil servants). When EPF runs dry — which it does for most retirees within 5-10 years — elderly Malaysians face destitution. There is no universal old-age pension, inadequate public aged care facilities, and limited social assistance. A generation of Malaysians is heading toward retirement poverty.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Instead of strengthening retirement savings, the government has repeatedly raided EPF for short-term political wins. Account 3 — the latest withdrawal scheme — was presented as ‘flexibility’ but in reality it’s the government allowing citizens to spend their future to survive the present. When you can’t fix wages, cost of living, or employment, the politically easy answer is ’let them eat their retirement.’ It’s not a solution — it’s kicking the catastrophe 20 years down the road.

📜 Previous Cases

EPF withdrawals during COVID-19 (under PN/BN) depleted RM145 billion from the fund. PH criticised those withdrawals as irresponsible. Then PH introduced Account 3 with even easier withdrawal terms. Every government — BN, PN, PH — has treated EPF as a piggy bank for political convenience. The retirees of 2040 will pay the price.

🎯 PH Callout

You criticised Muhyiddin for allowing COVID EPF withdrawals. Then you created Account 3, making it even EASIER to withdraw retirement savings. RM14.79 billion gone in months. You’ve traded the financial security of 4.63 million Malaysians’ retirement for short-term political approval. When these people are 70 and destitute, they’ll remember who let them spend their old age today.

Sources

  • EPF — Belanjawan 2026
  • CNA — Malaysia Development Plan

item.title: FDI Announcements vs Actual Jobs — RM Numbers Look Great, But Where Are the Jobs for Malaysians?
item.description:
Said by: MIDA / Economic Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

The government regularly announces massive Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) figures — billions of ringgit in commitments from tech giants, manufacturers, and data centre operators. These announcements generate impressive headlines. However, the gap between announced FDI and actual realised investment, and between investment and actual Malaysian employment, remains significant.

🔥 Why It Matters

FDI announcements are easy PR wins — you stand next to a foreign CEO, sign an MOU, and announce billions. But approved FDI ≠ realised FDI (many projects are delayed or cancelled). Realised FDI ≠ Malaysian jobs (many investments are capital-intensive with minimal employment, or employ foreign specialists). And the jobs that are created are often not the high-quality, well-paying positions that address Malaysia’s unemployment and wage stagnation challenges.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Data centres — the crown jewel of FDI announcements — employ remarkably few people relative to their investment size. A billion-ringgit data centre might employ 50-200 permanent staff, mostly technical specialists. Compare that to a factory employing thousands. The government promotes FDI numbers that look impressive on paper while youth unemployment stays at 12.28% and graduate unemployment remains persistent. The headline numbers don’t translate to the lives of ordinary Malaysians.

📜 Previous Cases

BN also regularly announced massive FDI figures while employment quality stagnated. Najib’s Economic Transformation Programme produced impressive investment statistics but failed to meaningfully raise median wages or reduce inequality. PH promised that their FDI strategy would be different — more quality, more jobs, more technology transfer. The strategy is identical: announce big numbers, hope nobody checks the employment data.

🎯 PH Callout

Stop announcing FDI commitments as if they’re achievements. Announce JOBS. Announce WAGES. Announce how many Malaysians got employed, at what salary, in what roles. A billion ringgit in FDI that creates 100 jobs is not an economic success story — it’s a tax incentive giveaway that benefits foreign shareholders while Malaysians deliver their food on motorcycles for RM6 per trip.

Sources

  • ISEAS — Bumiputera Transformation 2035
  • Bloomberg — Malaysia Debt Pressure

item.title: Foreign Labour Dependency Trap — Economy Can't Function Without Workers Malaysia Treats Like Disposable Parts
item.description:
Said by: Labour Economists / ILO

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

Malaysia remains heavily dependent on foreign workers — estimated at 2-3 million documented and possibly millions more undocumented — in critical sectors including construction, manufacturing, agriculture, and services. The government’s foreign worker policy oscillates between liberalisation and restriction based on political winds rather than economic planning, creating chronic uncertainty for both employers and workers.

🔥 Why It Matters

Foreign worker dependency reveals Malaysia’s failure to move up the value chain — the economy is still structurally reliant on cheap, exploitable labour rather than automation, technology, and skilled domestic workers. This dependency suppresses local wages, enables exploitation, and creates social tensions.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Every government promises to ‘reduce dependency on foreign labour’ and ‘move to a high-value economy.’ Every government then allows millions of foreign workers because the industries that fund political parties can’t function without them. Palm oil plantations, construction companies, and factories NEED cheap foreign labour. Politicians NEED campaign funding from these industries. The result is a permanent cycle of promised reform and actual dependency.

📜 Previous Cases

BN’s foreign worker policies were equally chaotic and exploitation-enabling. The US State Department’s Trafficking in Persons report has flagged Malaysia’s forced labour issues for years — under BN, PN, and PH. The multi-tier foreign worker levy system, repeatedly delayed, was supposed to be PH’s solution. It wasn’t.

🎯 PH Callout

Your economy literally can’t build a building, harvest a palm oil fruit, or run a restaurant without foreign workers — workers who have no rights, no protections, and no voice. Your ‘Madani’ framework apparently doesn’t extend to the millions of human beings who actually make your economy function. Fix the exploitation, fix the wages, fix the dependency — or stop pretending Malaysia is a civilised economy.

Sources

  • ISEAS — Petrol Subsidy Reform
  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: Mandatory Halal Certification Burden on Non-Muslim SMEs — Pay Up or Close Down
item.description:
Said by: SME Owners / JAKIM

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

JAKIM’s expanding halal certification requirements impose significant compliance costs on food businesses, including those owned by non-Muslims. Businesses must pay for certification, comply with extensive requirements covering ingredients, preparation, storage, and staffing, and undergo regular audits. JAKIM’s budget of RM2.6 billion makes it one of the best-funded agencies in government — better funded than many agencies that directly serve all Malaysians.

🔥 Why It Matters

For small non-Muslim food businesses — Chinese coffee shops, Indian restaurants, mixed-cuisine eateries — halal certification requirements create a financial and operational burden that can be prohibitive. The choice becomes: spend thousands on halal compliance (in a business that may not even serve a majority Muslim clientele) or lose access to government contracts, events, and certain commercial spaces that require halal certification.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

JAKIM receives RM2.6 billion annually — more than the sports budget, more than environmental protection, and disproportionate to its actual contribution to GDP or social welfare. The expanding certification regime functions as a religious tax on business, with the costs ultimately passed to consumers. For a PM who champions a ‘moderate’ image internationally, the growth of JAKIM’s power and budget under his watch tells a different story domestically.

📜 Previous Cases

The KK Mart controversy in 2024 — where a convenience store chain was boycotted and faced criminal charges for inadvertently selling socks with an Allah-related design — showed how religious enforcement can destroy businesses over minor incidents. Under PH, religious agencies have not been reined in; they’ve been emboldened.

🎯 PH Callout

Your ‘Madani’ government spends RM2.6 billion on JAKIM while healthcare crumbles and schools produce students who can’t do math. Non-Muslim businesses subsidise a religious bureaucracy through mandatory compliance costs. If ‘Malaysia Madani’ means all Malaysians, why does budget allocation suggest it means only one community’s religious infrastructure?

Sources

  • CNA — Anwar Faces Graft Crisis
  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: Ringgit Weakness Persists Despite GDP Growth — Currency Doesn't Believe Anwar's Economic Story
item.description:
Said by: BNM / Currency Markets

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

Despite the government’s optimistic GDP growth narrative, the Malaysian ringgit has remained persistently weak against the US dollar and regional currencies, hovering around historically low levels. The currency markets — which aggregate the collective judgment of thousands of investors and analysts — are essentially voting no-confidence on Malaysia’s economic fundamentals.

🔥 Why It Matters

The weak ringgit has direct consequences for ordinary Malaysians: imported goods become more expensive (contributing to the food inflation that rakyat experience but official statistics undercount), overseas education costs more, and the brain drain accelerates as Malaysian professionals find their skills worth multiples more in stronger-currency countries.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government has pointed to global factors including US monetary policy and geopolitical uncertainty, and these are real factors. But regional comparisons reveal that the ringgit has underperformed several ASEAN peers, suggesting Malaysia-specific factors are at play: political uncertainty, the MACC scandal, concern about fiscal sustainability with 64.6% debt-to-GDP, and questions about the credibility of economic reforms. Currency markets are brutally honest barometers of national confidence. The ringgit’s weakness tells a story the government’s GDP figures don’t.

🎯 PH Callout

You can cherry-pick GDP numbers for press conferences. But you can’t spin the forex market. The ringgit doesn’t care about your talking points — it responds to fundamentals. And right now, the fundamentals say the market doesn’t believe your economic story. Fix the substance, and the currency will follow. Keep spinning, and it won’t.

Sources

  • Bloomberg — Anwar on Debt Pressure

item.title: [BM] Nik Nazmi Letak Jawatan Menteri Alam Sekitar Selepas Kalah Pilihan Raya Parti PKR — Dedahkan Politik Feudal PH
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 28 Mei 2025, Menteri Sumber Asli dan Kelestarian Alam Sekitar Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad menyerahkan perletakan jawatan daripada Kabinet, berkuat kuasa 4 Julai. Perletakan jawatannya datang beberapa jam selepas Menteri Ekonomi Rafizi Ramli juga meletak jawatan — kedua-duanya berikutan kekalahan mereka dalam pilihan raya dalaman PKR lima hari sebelumnya.

Nik Nazmi kehilangan jawatan Naib Presiden PKR dan berkata beliau tidak boleh lagi berkhidmat sebagai menteri tanpa mandat parti sepenuhnya. “Menjadi menteri boleh memerangkap anda dalam ‘gelembung’ — dengan keistimewaan, pengiring keselamatan, dan protokol di setiap acara,” katanya. “Saya percaya terlalu melekat dengan gaya hidup sedemikian boleh berbahaya.”

Anwar secara terbuka meminta Nik Nazmi mempertimbangkan semula. Nik Nazmi enggan, berkata keputusannya muktamad dan beliau akan menumpukan kepada berkhidmat kepada pengundi Setiawangsanya.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Dua menteri Kabinet meletak jawatan dalam beberapa jam antara satu sama lain atas politik parti dalaman bukanlah tadbir urus biasa. Ia mendedahkan hakikat bahawa pelantikan menteri PH pada asasnya dipacu oleh dinamik puak parti, bukan kecekapan atau kepentingan nasional.

Pilihan raya parti PKR yang mencetuskan perletakan jawatan ini sendiri dicemari dakwaan penipuan undi dan nepotisme — anak perempuan Anwar, Nurul Izzah, menewaskan Rafizi untuk jawatan timbalan presiden. Apabila anak perempuan PM menang pertandingan dalaman dan yang kalah dipaksa keluar daripada Kabinet, itu bukan pembaharuan demokratik. Itu politik feudal berselindung di sebalik retorik reformasi.

Pemergian serentak Menteri Ekonomi dan Menteri Alam Sekitar mewujudkan ketidaktentuan dasar berbulan-bulan dalam dua portfolio kritikal, dengan kekosongan tidak diisi sehingga Disember 2025.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Nik Nazmi layak menerima sedikit pujian atas pendiriannya yang berprinsip — beliau antara sedikit ahli politik Malaysia yang rela melepaskan kuasa. Tetapi sistem yang menghasilkan keputusan ini busuk.

Pilihan raya parti PKR sepatutnya menjadi latihan demokratik dalaman. Sebaliknya, ia menjadi perang proksi untuk penyatuan kuasa keluarga Anwar. Kemenangan Nurul Izzah ke atas Rafizi bukan sekadar pertandingan parti — ia adalah anak perempuan PM menewaskan teknokrat ekonomi paling cekap beliau, membawa kepada kekosongan dasar berbulan-bulan di Kementerian Ekonomi.

Dan respons Anwar? Beliau memohon Nik Nazmi untuk kekal — bukan kerana kesinambungan dasar, tetapi kerana dua perletakan jawatan menteri nampak buruk. PM tidak mampu mengawal kesannya daripada pilihan raya partinya sendiri. Ini parti ‘reformis’ yang kononnya berbeza daripada politik feudal UMNO.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Sejarah UMNO dipenuhi pergolakan kuasa dalaman yang melimpah ke dalam tadbir urus — pergaduhan puak antara Mahathir dan Ku Li, antara Mahathir dan Anwar, antara Najib dan Muhyiddin. PKR sepatutnya berbeza. Ia diasaskan atas prinsip reformasi. Sebaliknya, ia mereplikakan politik dalaman berasaskan naungan yang sama, lengkap dengan pertempuran penggantian dinasti.

Pilihan raya PKR 2025 juga menggemakan pilihan raya parti 2018, yang turut dicemari dakwaan penyelewengan serupa. Parti ini tidak pernah berjaya mengadakan pilihan raya dalaman yang bersih dalam seluruh sejarahnya.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda membina PKR atas janji reformasi demokratik. Kemudian pilihan raya parti anda dicemari dakwaan penipuan undi, anak perempuan anda menewaskan reformis ekonomi paling terkemuka anda, dan dua menteri meletak jawatan sebagai protes. Hasilnya? Kekosongan dasar berbulan-bulan di kementerian Ekonomi dan Alam Sekitar. Ini bukan reformasi — ini UMNO dengan jenama yang lebih baik. Jika PKR tidak boleh menjalankan pilihan raya dalaman yang bersih, mengapa rakyat Malaysia harus mempercayai anda untuk mereformasi negara?

Sumber: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | Malay Mail | ISEAS

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Ramli Letak Jawatan Menteri Ekonomi Selepas Dimalukan PKR — Reformis Yang Disingkirkan
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 28 Mei 2025, Menteri Ekonomi Rafizi Ramli menyerahkan perletakan jawatannya daripada Kabinet, berkuat kuasa 17 Jun. Sekutunya, Menteri Sumber Asli Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, turut mengumumkan pemergiannya, berkuat kuasa 4 Julai.

Rafizi berkata: “Saya memasuki politik untuk memupuk budaya politik baru yang berasaskan akauntabiliti dan mandat rakyat. Kekalahan saya baru-baru ini dalam pilihan raya PKR menandakan saya tidak lagi memiliki mandat parti.”

Beliau menyatakan bahawa di negara yang berprinsipkan demokrasi, “pemimpin yang kalah dalam pilihan raya parti harus memberi laluan kepada pemenang.” Tindakan terakhir beliau sebagai menteri adalah menyiapkan Rancangan Malaysia Ke-13.

Anwar dilaporkan menggesa Rafizi mempertimbangkan semula. Beliau enggan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Rafizi adalah reformis paling berkredibiliti dalam Kabinet Anwar. Beliau mereka bentuk PADU, mendorong rasionalisasi subsidi, memperjuangkan tadbir urus berasaskan data, dan menjadi enjin intelektual di sebalik agenda reformasi ekonomi PH. Pemergiannya meninggalkan kekosongan pada saat Malaysia memerlukan pengawasan ekonomi yang mantap.

Bloomberg menyebutnya “tamparan kepada kerajaan Anwar.” Straits Times menyatakan pemergiannya “akan meninggalkan jurang pada masa reformasi ekonomi Malaysia terbantut.” CNA melaporkan pilihan raya parti “dicemari dakwaan penipuan undi dan nepotisme.”

Dua menteri meletak jawatan atas pilihan raya parti bukanlah biasa. Ia isyarat bahawa sesuatu amat tidak kena di dalam PKR.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Anwar kehilangan menteri terbaiknya kerana beliau membenarkan — atau membolehkan — anak perempuannya mencabar dan menghancurkan penyandang. Rafizi tidak kalah kerana tidak cekap. Beliau kalah kerana jentera parti digerakkan untuk anak perempuan bos.

Hakikat bahawa Anwar kemudian meminta Rafizi untuk kekal adalah bahagian paling sinis. Anda mendalangi penghinaan, kemudian meminta yang dihina untuk terus bekerja untuk anda. Itu bukan kepimpinan — itu manipulasi.

Sejak pemergian Rafizi, beliau menjadi pengkritik yang semakin lantang. Beliau memberi amaran kepada Anwar jangan “memandang rendah sentimen rakyat.” Beliau menggesa penggantungan ketua SPRM Azam Baki. Beliau kini sendiri di bawah siasatan SPRM — pemasaan yang pengkritik panggil sebagai tindakan balas.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Politik Malaysia mempunyai tradisi lama menyingkirkan saingan dalaman. Mahathir meminggirkan Anwar pada 1998. Kini Anwar secara efektif meminggirkan Rafizi pada 2025. Kaedahnya berbeza — pilihan raya parti dan bukannya tuduhan liwat — tetapi hasilnya sama: saingan paling berkebolehan pemimpin disingkirkan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda kehilangan minda ekonomi terbaik anda kerana politik keluarga lebih penting daripada kepentingan nasional. Rafizi membina kredibiliti dasar anda. Beliau sebab pelabur memandang serius PH. Dan anda biarkan beliau pergi — atau menolaknya keluar. Rancangan Malaysia Ke-13 adalah hadiah terakhirnya kepada anda. Apa yang anda lakukan dengannya sejak itu?

Sumber: Straits Times | Bloomberg | FMT | CNA

item.title: [BM] Rekod 236 Tan Syabu Dirampas Di Rantau — Malaysia Hab Transit Utama
item.description:
Said by: UNODC Report 2025

Category: Health

Date: 2025-05-28

UNODC reported a record 236 tons of methamphetamine seized in East and Southeast Asia in 2024, up 24% from 2023. Malaysia busted its largest-ever maritime drug trafficking ring — 18+ tonnes worth RM375M. Drug use is increasing among older demographics. Government approved a ‘communication plan’ — not enforcement reform. This matters because Malaysia abolished mandatory death for drug trafficking, then watched seizures hit record highs. The government’s response to a worsening drug crisis was a ‘communication plan’ — essentially a PR strategy instead of structural enforcement reform. Malaysia sits on a major trafficking corridor and its ports are clearly being exploited. Record seizures can mean better enforcement or a bigger problem — and when the UNODC says the regional supply is surging 24% year-on-year, it’s the latter. A government that responds to a drug tsunami with a communications strategy is not serious about the problem. Sources: UNODC | Vietnam Plus

item.title: Nik Nazmi Resigns as Environment Minister After Losing PKR Party Election — Exposes PH's Feudal Politics
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On May 28, 2025, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad tendered his resignation from Cabinet, effective July 4. His resignation came hours after Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli also resigned — both following their defeats in PKR’s internal party elections five days earlier.

Nik Nazmi lost his PKR vice-president position and said he could no longer serve as minister without a full party mandate. “Being a minister can trap you in a ‘bubble’ — with privileges, security escorts, and protocols at every event,” he said. “I believe that becoming too attached to such a lifestyle can be dangerous.”

Anwar publicly asked Nik Nazmi to reconsider. Nik Nazmi refused, saying his decision was firm and he would focus on serving his Setiawangsa constituents.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Two Cabinet ministers resigning within hours of each other over internal party politics is not normal governance. It exposes the fact that PH ministerial appointments are fundamentally driven by party faction dynamics, not competence or national interest.

The PKR party election that triggered these resignations was itself marred by allegations of vote-rigging and nepotism — Anwar’s daughter Nurul Izzah defeated Rafizi for deputy president. When the PM’s daughter wins an internal contest and the losers are forced out of Cabinet, that’s not democratic renewal. That’s feudal politics dressed up in reform rhetoric.

The simultaneous departure of the Economy Minister and Environment Minister created months of policy uncertainty in two critical portfolios, with vacancies unfilled until December 2025.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Nik Nazmi deserves some credit for his principled stance — he’s one of the few Malaysian politicians who voluntarily gave up power. But the system that produced this outcome is rotten.

PKR’s party elections are supposed to be internal democratic exercises. Instead, they became proxy wars for the Anwar family’s consolidation of power. Nurul Izzah’s victory over Rafizi wasn’t just a party contest — it was the PM’s daughter defeating his most capable economic technocrat, leading to months of policy vacuum in the Economy Ministry.

And Anwar’s response? He begged Nik Nazmi to stay — not because of policy continuity, but because two ministerial resignations looked bad. The PM couldn’t even control the fallout from his own party’s elections. This is the “reformist” party that’s supposed to be different from UMNO’s feudal politics.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

UMNO’s history is littered with internal power struggles that spilled into governance — factional fights between Mahathir and Ku Li, between Mahathir and Anwar, between Najib and Muhyiddin. PKR was supposed to be different. It was founded on reformasi principles. Instead, it’s replicated the exact same patronage-driven internal politics, complete with a dynastic succession battle.

The PKR 2025 election also echoed the 2018 party polls, which were marred by similar allegations of irregularities. The party has never managed a clean internal election in its entire history.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You built PKR on the promise of democratic reform. Then your party election was marred by vote-rigging allegations, your daughter defeated your most prominent economic reformer, and two ministers resigned in protest. The result? Months of policy vacuum in the Economy and Environment ministries. This isn’t reform — it’s UMNO with better branding. If PKR can’t even run a clean internal election, why should Malaysians trust you to reform the country?

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | Malay Mail | ISEAS

item.title: Rafizi Ramli Resigns as Economy Minister After PKR Humiliation — The Reformist Who Got Purged
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 28 May 2025, Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli submitted his resignation from Cabinet, effective 17 June. His ally, Natural Resources Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, also announced his exit, effective 4 July.

Rafizi said: “I entered politics to cultivate a new political culture grounded in accountability and the people’s mandate. My recent defeat in the PKR elections signifies that I no longer possess the party’s mandate.”

He noted that in countries with democratic principles, “leaders who lose in party elections should make way for the victors.” His final act as minister was completing the 13th Malaysia Plan.

Anwar reportedly urged Rafizi to reconsider. He refused.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Rafizi was the most credible reformist in Anwar’s Cabinet. He designed PADU, pushed subsidy rationalisation, championed data-driven governance, and was the intellectual engine behind PH’s economic reform agenda. His departure left a vacuum at exactly the moment Malaysia needed steady economic stewardship.

Bloomberg called it “a blow to Anwar’s government.” The Straits Times noted his exit “will leave a gap at a time when Malaysia’s economic reforms are stalling.” CNA reported that the party election was “marred by allegations of vote-rigging and nepotism.”

Two ministers quitting over a party election isn’t normal. It’s a signal that something is deeply wrong inside PKR.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Anwar lost his best minister because he allowed — or enabled — his daughter to challenge and destroy the incumbent. Rafizi didn’t lose because he was incompetent. He lost because the party machinery was mobilised for the boss’s daughter.

The fact that Anwar then asked Rafizi to stay is the most cynical part. You orchestrate a humiliation, then ask the humiliated to keep working for you. That’s not leadership — that’s gaslighting.

Since Rafizi’s departure, he has become an increasingly vocal critic. He warned Anwar not to “underestimate public sentiment.” He called for MACC chief Azam Baki’s suspension. He’s now under MACC investigation himself — a timing that critics call retaliatory.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysian politics has a long tradition of purging internal rivals. Mahathir sidelined Anwar in 1998. Now Anwar has effectively sidelined Rafizi in 2025. The method is different — party elections instead of sodomy charges — but the outcome is the same: the leader’s most capable rival is removed.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You lost your best economic mind because family politics mattered more than national interest. Rafizi built your policy credibility. He was the reason investors took PH seriously. And you let him walk — or pushed him out. The 13th Malaysia Plan was his last gift to you. What have you done with it since?

Sources: Straits Times | Bloomberg | FMT | CNA
