🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Hutang PTPTN mewujudkan perangkap kemiskinan antara generasi: graduan memasuki tenaga kerja sudah berhutang, menghadapi ekonomi dengan pengangguran belia 12.28%, memperoleh gaji yang hampir tidak menanggung kos sara hidup (gaji minimum RM1,500), dan perlu membayar balik pinjaman pelajar sambil membina kehidupan. Ramai tidak mampu — sebab itulah kadar bayaran balik begitu rendah. Kitaran ini memakan dirinya sendiri: pendidikan tidak mampu → hutang → gaji rendah → tidak mampu bayar balik → isu kelayakan kredit → tidak mampu dapatkan gadai janji → status penyewa kekal.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH menjanjikan pelbagai reformasi PTPTN termasuk pelepasan hutang untuk graduan berpendapatan rendah, fleksibiliti bayaran balik yang dilanjutkan, dan menjadikan pendidikan lebih mampu milik untuk mengurangkan pinjaman masa depan. Kemajuan adalah marginal. Kerajaan bercakap tentang pendidikan sebagai pelaburan tetapi melayan PTPTN sebagai agensi kutipan hutang. Pelajar yang meminjam untuk mendapatkan ijazah yang ekonomi tidak hargai (kerana sistem pendidikan tidak menghasilkan graduan yang boleh diambil bekerja) sedang dihukum atas kegagalan peringkat sistem yang bukan salah mereka.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pendekatan BN juga tidak mencukupi — pendekatan lobak merah dan rotan terhadap kutipan pinjaman tanpa menangani isu asas kualiti pendidikan dan ketidakpadanan pasaran buruh. PH 1.0 menjanjikan reformasi PTPTN tetapi runtuh sebelum pelaksanaan. PH 2.0 telah membuat penyesuaian berperingkat tetapi krisis struktur kekal. Wang yang dipinjamkan menghasilkan graduan yang tidak dapat mencari pekerjaan yang sepadan, yang kemudian tidak dapat membayar balik pinjaman, yang menjejaskan keupayaan dana untuk meminjamkan kepada pelajar masa depan. Ia gelung azab.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Sistem pendidikanmu menghasilkan graduan yang tidak dapat mencari kerja (pengangguran belia 12.28%). Ekonomimu tidak membayar graduan cukup untuk membayar balik pinjaman mereka (gaji minimum RM1,500). Dan PTPTNmu terus meminjamkan untuk ijazah yang tidak membawa kepada pekerjaan. Kamu telah mencipta mesin kemiskinan yang direka sempurna: pinjam untuk pendidikan → dapat ijazah → tiada kerja sepadan → tidak mampu bayar balik pinjaman → tidak mampu beli rumah → kekal dalam kemiskinan. Betulkan salurannya atau berhenti berlakon masalahnya ialah graduan yang malas.

Sumber: Taylor’s University | FMT

item.title: [BM] Malaysia Enggan Ratifikasi ICERD — Anwar Tunduk Kepada Politik Perkauman Soal Perjanjian Hak Asasi Antarabangsa
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Malay-Muslim Groups

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Malaysia kekal antara sedikit negara di dunia yang belum meratifikasi Konvensyen Antarabangsa Mengenai Penghapusan Segala Bentuk Diskriminasi Kaum (ICERD). Pada 2018, percubaan PH meratifikasi ICERD ditinggalkan selepas bantahan besar-besaran yang dipimpin kumpulan Melayu-Muslim yang berhujah ia akan menjejaskan keistimewaan Bumiputera. Anwar, pernah pemimpin progresif yang komited kepada kesamarataan, tidak menyentuh semula isu ini sejak menjadi PM.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Ratifikasi ICERD akan mengikat Malaysia untuk menghapuskan diskriminasi kaum dalam undang-undang dan amalan. Keengganan Malaysia meratifikasi — bersama negara seperti Korea Utara dan Myanmar — meletakkannya dalam kumpulan yang memalukan. Keengganan itu mengisyaratkan kepada masyarakat antarabangsa bahawa Malaysia tidak sanggup mendedahkan dasar berasaskan kaum kepada penelitian antarabangsa.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Anwar menghabiskan berdekad memperjuangkan “Malaysian Malaysia” — kesamarataan tanpa mengira kaum. Beliau menubuhkan gabungan pelbagai kaum PH atas prinsip semua rakyat Malaysia layak dilayan sama rata. Ratifikasi ICERD adalah ungkapan semula jadi prinsip itu. Sebaliknya, Anwar menerima kekalahan 2018 dan teruskan. Reformis telah menyerah kepada kuasa yang dibinanya untuk dilawan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia menandatangani ICERD pada 1966 tetapi tidak pernah meratifikasinya. Setiap kerajaan memutuskan bahawa kos politik mencabar diskriminasi kaum lebih tinggi daripada kos mengekalkannya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu membina gabungan atas kesamarataan pelbagai kaum. Manifesto kamu menjanjikan ‘Malaysia Baru’ di mana kaum tidak menentukan hak. Meratifikasi ICERD adalah ungkapan paling ringkas dan langsung janji itu. Sebaliknya, kamu menerima kekalahan 2018 dan teruskan — sama seperti setiap kerajaan sebelum kamu. Sama ada ICERD konsisten dengan Malaysia yang kamu janjikan, atau Malaysia yang kamu janjikan adalah pembohongan. Yang mana satu?

Sumber: UN OHCHR | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: [BM] Penghijrahan Otak Semakin Pantas — 1.86 Juta Rakyat Malaysia Telah Pergi, Dan Yang Terbaik Terus Meninggalkan
item.description:
Said by: World Bank / Talent Corporation Data

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

1.86 million Malaysians living abroad, disproportionately highly skilled. Public hospital doctors work 84-hour weeks for poverty wages. 11,000 specialist shortage directly linked to brain drain. Structural causes — race-based policies, low wages, poor governance — remain unaddressed.

item.title: [BM] Penyahutanan Kelapa Sawit Berterusan Tanpa Kawalan — Ikrar Alam Sekitar Lawan Keuntungan Ladang
item.description:
Said by: Environmental NGOs / SAM

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Walaupun komitmen Malaysia kepada perjanjian iklim antarabangsa dan matlamat pembangunan mampan, penyahutanan untuk ladang kelapa sawit terus merosakkan hutan hujan tropika antara yang tertua dan paling biodiversiti di Bumi. Skim pensijilan Minyak Sawit Mampan Malaysia (MSPO) dikritik NGO antarabangsa sebagai pencucian hijau yang tidak mencukupi. Kualiti air sungai Malaysia merosot — sungai bersih turun dari 486 ke 468 pada 2025. Sungai Perak bertukar biru akibat pelepasan perlombongan nadir bumi pada November 2025. Spesies terancam termasuk orang utan, gajah pygmy, dan harimau Malaya menghadapi habitat yang semakin mengecil.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Malaysia menyumbang kira-kira 25% pengeluaran minyak sawit global, dan kebergantungan ekonomi terhadap komoditi ini mencipta rintangan politik yang besar terhadap pembaharuan alam sekitar yang tulen. Kos alam sekitar — kehilangan biodiversiti, pencemaran sungai, pelepasan karbon daripada saliran tanah gambut — tidak dikira dalam angka KDNK tetapi ditanggung oleh komuniti yang bergantung pada air bersih, hutan sihat, dan ekosistem yang berfungsi.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Skim pensijilan MSPO adalah pelekat, bukan standard. Ia membenarkan kemerosotan alam sekitar berterusan sambil menyediakan penampilan kelestarian yang memuaskan pembeli antarabangsa. Kerajaan bercakap tentang kelestarian di sidang kemuncak COP dan persidangan antarabangsa sambil meluluskan pembangunan ladang baru di dalam negara.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah BN, kadar penyahutanan Malaysia antara yang tertinggi di dunia. Era Mahathir menyaksikan penukaran besar-besaran hutan primer kepada ladang. Di bawah PH 1.0, moratorium lesen kelapa sawit baru seketika dibincangkan. Di bawah PH 2.0, MSPO diperluaskan tetapi tidak diperkukuhkan. Setiap kerajaan membayar lip service kepada perlindungan alam sekitar sambil melindungi industri ladang.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu menandatangani perjanjian iklim dan bergambar di sidang kemuncak COP. Kemudian kamu pulang dan luluskan lebih banyak ladang. Skim MSPO kamu hiasan tingkap. Sungai kamu semakin kotor. Hutan kamu semakin kecil. Perkukuhkan MSPO mengikut standard antarabangsa. Kuatkuasakan undang-undang pembalakan haram. Atau terus berucap kelestarian sementara asas alam sekitar negara kamu runtuh.

Sumber: Asian News Network | FOE Malaysia

item.title: [BM] Perkahwinan Kanak-Kanak MASIH Sah — PH Janji Hapuskannya, Kemudian Tidak Buat Apa Kerana 'Sensitiviti Islam'
item.description:
Said by: Sisters in Islam / UNICEF / Women’s Rights Groups

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Walaupun bertahun-tahun kempen oleh pertubuhan hak wanita dan janji jelas daripada ahli politik PH, perkahwinan kanak-kanak kekal sah di Malaysia. Di bawah undang-undang keluarga Islam (bidang kuasa negeri), gadis semuda 16 tahun boleh berkahwin dengan kelulusan Mahkamah Syariah, dan dalam praktiknya, perkahwinan yang lebih muda telah didokumentasikan.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Perkahwinan kanak-kanak merampas pendidikan, kemerdekaan ekonomi, dan autonomi tubuh gadis. Ia dikaitkan dengan kadar keganasan rumah tangga yang lebih tinggi, kemiskinan, komplikasi kesihatan daripada kehamilan awal, dan kelemahan antara generasi. UNICEF telah berulang kali menggesa Malaysia menaikkan umur minimum perkahwinan kepada 18 tahun untuk semua.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH mempunyai majoriti besar di parlimen dengan sokongan BN. Mereka mempunyai jumlah untuk menggubal perubahan. Mereka memilih untuk tidak berbuat demikian. Alasannya sentiasa ‘sensitiviti Islam’ dan ‘bidang kuasa negeri.’ Tetapi apabila PH mahukan sesuatu dilakukan — seperti Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian — tiba-tiba penyelarasan negeri-persekutuan bukan halangan. Perkahwinan kanak-kanak berterusan kerana kos politik untuk menghapuskannya melebihi manfaat politik.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pada 2018, TPM PH 1.0 Wan Azizah berjanji untuk menghapuskan perkahwinan kanak-kanak. Janji itu tidak ke mana. Menjelang 2020, PH 1.0 runtuh tanpa meluluskan sebarang undang-undang. PH 2.0 Anwar tidak pun membangkitkan isu ini secara bermakna. Janji itu telah dikitar semula merentasi dua kerajaan PH tanpa sebarang tindakan perundangan.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu mempunyai jumlah parlimen. Kamu mempunyai hujah moral. Kamu mempunyai tekanan antarabangsa. Dan kamu MASIH tidak akan bertindak kerana kamu takut mengecewakan pengundi Melayu-Islam konservatif. Kamu telah membuat pengiraan politik bahawa masa depan gadis kecil bernilai kurang daripada margin undi. Itu bukan tadbir urus — itu pengecut.

Sumber: Malay Mail / Amnesty International

item.title: [BM] Proton Masih Bergantung Pada Perlindungan Kerajaan — 40 Tahun 'Kereta Nasional' Dan Masih Tak Boleh Bersaing
item.description:
Said by: Automotive Industry Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Selepas 40 tahun dan berbilion dalam subsidi awam, pengecualian cukai, dan dasar perdagangan perlindungan, Proton masih tidak boleh bersaing di peringkat antarabangsa tanpa perlindungan kerajaan. Duti import yang tinggi — duti eksais sehingga 100% ke atas kereta asing — melambungkan harga kenderaan untuk 33 juta rakyat Malaysia. Kereta yang sama berharga separuh di Thailand atau Indonesia. Kerajaan Anwar tidak menunjukkan kecenderungan untuk mereformasi dasar perdagangan automotif atau menghapuskan perlindungan yang merugikan pengguna Malaysia berbilion setiap tahun.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Pemilikan kereta bukan kemewahan di Malaysia — ia keperluan di negara dengan pengangkutan awam yang tidak mencukupi di luar KL. Duti import tinggi bermakna keluarga Malaysia membelanjakan bahagian pendapatan yang tidak seimbang untuk kenderaan. Honda City yang berharga RM65,000 di Thailand berharga lebih RM100,000 di Malaysia. Toyota Corolla di Indonesia berharga kira-kira RM80,000; di Malaysia, lebih RM130,000. Dasar ‘kereta nasional’ memaksa 33 juta rakyat mensubsidi ketidakupayaan satu syarikat untuk bersaing — melalui harga lebih tinggi pada setiap kenderaan yang mereka beli.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Proton sepatutnya menjadi industri bayi sementara — dilindungi beberapa tahun sehingga ia boleh berdiri sendiri. Empat puluh tahun kemudian, ia masih tak boleh berdiri sendiri. Syarikat ini dijual kepada Geely (China) pada 2017 kerana tidak boleh hidup tanpa rakan kongsi asing. Namun duti import kekal, melindungi syarikat yang bukan lagi sepenuhnya milik Malaysia. Perlindungan itu bukan lagi untuk juara nasional — ia melayani usaha sama dengan pembuat kereta China sementara pengguna Malaysia membayar harga melambung untuk setiap kereta di jalan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Projek kereta nasional telah menjadi lembu suci politik sejak Mahathir melancarkan Proton pada 1983. Setiap kerajaan mengekalkan perlindungan kerana lobi automotif — pengedar, pembekal, pekerja — mewakili undi. BN melindungi Proton selama 35 tahun. PH 1.0 tidak membuat perubahan. PH 2.0 tidak membuat perubahan. Dasar ini bertahan bukan kerana ia berkesan tetapi kerana membongkarnya akan menimbulkan kemarahan pihak berkepentingan. Sementara itu, Thailand dan Indonesia — tanpa perlindungan kereta nasional — telah menjadi hab pembuatan automotif utama. Malaysia tidak.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Keluarga Malaysia membayar dua kali ganda untuk kereta kerana dasar berusia 40 tahun yang melindungi syarikat yang kini separuh dimiliki China. Pengangkutan awam tidak mencukupi. Pemilikan kereta wajib. Dan kamu mengekalkan duti import yang melambungkan harga untuk semua orang. Hapuskan perlindungan automotif secara berperingkat dalam 5-10 tahun. Laburkan penjimatan dalam pengangkutan awam. Biarkan pengguna Malaysia membeli kereta pada harga serantau. Atau terus memaksa 33 juta rakyat mensubsidi dasar perindustrian yang gagal — dan tertanya-tanya kenapa kos sara hidup menjadi masalah politik terbesar kamu.

Sumber: Paultan.org | Nikkei Asia | The Edge

item.title: [BM] SOSMA Masih Tidak Dimansuhkan — PH Guna Undang-Undang Keselamatan Yang Mereka Panggil 'Drakonian' Untuk Tahan Tanpa Bicara
item.description:
Said by: Lawyers for Liberty / Bar Council / Amnesty International

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Akta Kesalahan Keselamatan (Langkah-Langkah Khas) 2012 (SOSMA), yang membenarkan penahanan tanpa bicara sehingga 28 hari dan menyekat pengawasan kehakiman kes keselamatan, kekal beroperasi sepenuhnya di bawah kerajaan Anwar. PH secara jelas berjanji untuk memansuhkan SOSMA dalam kedua-dua manifesto 2018 dan 2022, memanggilnya ‘drakonian’ dan tidak serasi dengan tadbir urus demokratik.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Penahanan tanpa bicara secara asasnya tidak serasi dengan kedaulatan undang-undang. SOSMA memberikan kerajaan kuasa untuk menahan individu selama hampir sebulan tanpa tuduhan dan tanpa penelitian kehakiman penuh. Di tangan yang salah, ia alat untuk penganiayaan politik. Setiap hari ia kekal dalam undang-undang ialah hari mana-mana rakyat Malaysia boleh ditahan tanpa perlindungan yang dijanjikan demokrasi.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH memanggil SOSMA ‘drakonian’ apabila BN menggunakannya. Ahli politik PH memberi ucapan mengecamnya. Lawyers for Liberty mendokumentasikan penyalahgunaannya. Majlis Peguam menyeru pemansuhannya. Amnesty International membenderakannya setiap tahun. Kini PH berkuasa dengan majoriti besar, dan SOSMA kekal. Tidak dipinda. Tidak direformasi. Tidak dimansuhkan. Tidak berubah dan tersedia untuk digunakan.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Di bawah BN, SOSMA digunakan terhadap aktivis politik. Di bawah PH 1.0, tiada pemansuhan dalam 22 bulan. Di bawah PH 2.0, lebih tiga tahun, tiada pemansuhan. Akta Hasutan: cerita sama. PPPA: cerita sama. SETIAP undang-undang represif yang PH janjikan untuk dimansuhkan: cerita sama. Corak ini bukan kebetulan.

🎯 Seruan Terhadap PH

Kamu mempunyai jumlah. Kamu mempunyai mandat. Kamu menghabiskan BERTAHUN-TAHUN memanggil SOSMA drakonian. Mansuhkannya. Kamu tidak akan — kerana mempunyai kuasa untuk menahan tanpa bicara adalah mudah, walaupun untuk ‘reformis.’ Keenggananmu membuktikan penentanganmu sentiasa tentang siapa yang memegang kuasa, bukan tentang prinsip.

Sumber: Freedom House | Malay Mail / Amnesty

item.title: [BM] Tuntutan Autonomi Sabah Dan Sarawak Masih Tidak Dipenuhi — Malaysia Timur Kekal Anak Tiri Yang Dilupakan
item.description:
Said by: Sabah & Sarawak Leaders / GPS

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Walaupun pindaan perlembagaan 2021 memulihkan status Sabah dan Sarawak sebagai rakan setara dalam Persekutuan, Malaysia Timur kekal dipinggirkan secara struktur. Sabah adalah negeri termiskin Malaysia. Syor Perjanjian Malaysia 1963 (MA63) berhabuk. Pertikaian royalti minyak dan gas kekal tidak diselesaikan. Perkongsian setara wujud di atas kertas tetapi bukan dalam peruntukan bajet, pelaburan infrastruktur, atau perwakilan politik.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Sabah dan Sarawak menyumbang sumber — minyak, gas, kayu, tanah — yang membina ekonomi moden Malaysia. Sebagai balasan, mereka menerima infrastruktur, penjagaan kesihatan, pendidikan, dan ketersambungan terburuk di negara ini. Rangka kerja MA63 menjamin layanan setara yang tidak pernah disampaikan. Apabila Sabah kekal negeri termiskin walaupun duduk di atas beberapa sumber asli terkaya Malaysia, sistem persekutuan telah gagal Malaysia Timur.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Anwar berjanji menghormati MA63 dan melayan Sabah dan Sarawak sebagai rakan setara. Tiga tahun kemudian, tuntutan khusus — royalti minyak yang ditingkatkan, kawalan imigresen, autonomi pendidikan, pelaburan infrastruktur — kekal tidak diselesaikan. Pindaan perlembagaan adalah kemenangan simbolik yang tidak mengubah sebarang butiran bajet. Sabah masih mempunyai kadar kemiskinan tertinggi. Sarawak masih mempunyai infrastruktur jalan terburuk.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah setiap kerajaan, coraknya sama: janjikan autonomi Malaysia Timur, kaji isu, tangguhkan pelaksanaan, dan bergantung pada aritmetik gabungan untuk mengelak akauntabiliti. Enam puluh tahun selepas Perjanjian Malaysia, terma perjanjian kekal tidak ditunaikan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu meminda Perlembagaan untuk memulihkan status rakan setara. Kemudian kamu gagal menyusuli dengan bajet setara, infrastruktur setara, dan perkhidmatan setara. Sabah masih negeri termiskin. Sarawak masih mempunyai jalan terburuk. Tingkatkan peruntukan persekutuan kepada Malaysia Timur berkadar dengan sumbangan sumber mereka. Selesaikan pertikaian royalti minyak. Sampaikan infrastruktur setaraf Semenanjung Malaysia. Atau terus panggil mereka ‘rakan setara’ sambil melayan mereka seperti anak syarikat.

Sumber: Malaysiakini | Borneo Post | East Asia Forum

item.title: Brain Drain Accelerating — 1.86 Million Malaysians Have Left, and the Best Keep Leaving
item.description:
Said by: World Bank / Talent Corporation Data

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

1.86 million Malaysians living abroad, disproportionately highly skilled. Public hospital doctors work 84-hour weeks for poverty wages. 11,000 specialist shortage directly linked to brain drain. Structural causes — race-based policies, low wages, poor governance — remain unaddressed.

item.title: Child Marriage STILL Legal — PH Promised to End It, Then Did Nothing Because 'Islamic Sensitivities'
item.description:
Said by: Sisters in Islam / UNICEF / Women’s Rights Groups

Category: Religion

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Despite years of campaigning by women’s rights organisations and explicit promises from PH politicians, child marriage remains legal in Malaysia. Under Islamic family law (state jurisdiction), girls as young as 16 can be married with Shariah court approval, and in practice, younger marriages have been documented.

🔥 Why It Matters

Child marriage robs girls of education, economic independence, and bodily autonomy. It is associated with higher rates of domestic violence, poverty, health complications from early pregnancy, and intergenerational disadvantage. UNICEF has repeatedly urged Malaysia to raise the minimum marriage age to 18 for all.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH has a supermajority in parliament with BN support. They have the numbers to legislate change. They choose not to. The excuse is always ‘Islamic sensitivities’ and ‘state jurisdiction.’ But when PH wants something done — like the Online Safety Act — suddenly state-federal coordination isn’t a barrier. Child marriage persists because the political cost of ending it outweighs the political benefit.

📜 Previous Cases

In 2018, PH 1.0 Deputy PM Wan Azizah pledged to end child marriage. The pledge went nowhere. By 2020, PH 1.0 collapsed without passing any legislation. Anwar’s PH 2.0 hasn’t even raised the issue meaningfully. The promise has been recycled across two PH governments without any legislative action.

🎯 PH Callout

You have the parliamentary numbers. You have the moral argument. You have international pressure. And you STILL won’t act because you’re terrified of upsetting conservative Malay-Muslim voters. You’ve made a political calculation that little girls’ futures are worth less than vote margins. That’s not governance — that’s cowardice.

Sources

  • Malay Mail / Amnesty International — Reform Failures

item.title: Civil Servants Get Pensions-for-Life, Private Workers Get Depleting EPF — The Two-Tier Retirement System
item.description:
Said by: Public Finance Analysts / EPF

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

1.3-1.6M civil servants get defined-benefit pensions for life funded by taxpayers. 16M private sector workers rely on EPF with median balance of RM47,600 that runs out in 5 years. No government has had the courage to reform it because the civil service is a critical vote bank.

item.title: Climate Action Failure — Malaysia's Paris Agreement Pledges Are Fantasy, Emissions Keep Rising
item.description:
Said by: Climate Action Tracker / Environmental Groups

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Despite being a signatory to the Paris Agreement and making ambitious pledges on carbon emission reductions, Malaysia’s actual emissions trajectory continues upward. Deforestation continues, coal power plants remain operational, data centre energy consumption grows, and the government has offered no credible pathway to meeting its stated climate commitments.

🔥 Why It Matters

Climate change is already affecting Malaysia through more severe floods (2024 devastation), prolonged droughts, rising sea levels threatening coastal communities, and biodiversity loss. The country is simultaneously a victim and contributor — its palm oil industry and deforestation are significant global emission sources. Failure to act on climate isn’t just bad policy — it’s an existential threat to Malaysia’s future.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH’s 2022 manifesto explicitly promised environmentally friendly governance benchmarked against ‘International Best Practices.’ Instead, the government has approved massive data centres without environmental impact assessments, continued approving logging quotas that exceed sustainability levels, maintained coal-fired power generation, and offered no timeline for renewable energy transition. Anwar lectures the world about moral failing on Gaza while failing the planet on climate. The manifesto promise of strict logging quota enforcement is particularly laughable given continued deforestation.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia’s climate pledges have been consistently unmatched by action across all administrations. BN treated climate change as an inconvenience to economic growth. PH 1.0 made promises but collapsed before implementation. PH 2.0 is maintaining the tradition of ambitious targets and non-existent action. The Penang South Islands reclamation — a massive environmental destruction project — is being approved under a PH state government.

🎯 PH Callout

Your manifesto promised environmental governance benchmarked against international best practices. Your reality: continued deforestation, rivers turning blue from pollution, floods devastating communities annually, and climate pledges that are pure fiction. You can’t lecture the world about moral hypocrisy on Palestine while being morally bankrupt on the planet. The environment can’t vote — which is why you don’t care about it.

Sources

  • PH GE15 Manifesto (PDF)
  • FOE Malaysia — Penang Reclamation
  • East Asia Forum — Reform Aspirations

item.title: Digital Divide Persists — Rural Communities Still Without Reliable Internet While Government Talks 5G
item.description:
Said by: MCMC / Rural Development Ministry

Category: Education

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of early 2025, millions of Malaysians in rural Sabah, Sarawak, and the east coast of Peninsular Malaysia still lack reliable internet access. While the government spent billions on the 5G DNB versus Telekom Malaysia dual-network fiasco, the JENDELA programme to improve basic digital infrastructure faced delays and coverage gaps. Students in rural schools were severely disadvantaged during COVID-19 distance learning, and the digital divide has not meaningfully narrowed since. MCMC’s own data shows connectivity gaps between urban and rural areas remain significant, with many rural schools relying on unstable satellite connections or no connectivity at all.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The digital divide compounds every other form of marginalisation. Rural students can’t access online learning resources. Small businesses can’t participate in e-commerce. Healthcare providers can’t use telemedicine. Government services that have moved online are inaccessible to communities without internet. Kids in longhouses and kampungs can’t load a basic web page while KL gets 5G. The irony of spending billions on cutting-edge infrastructure while basic connectivity remains unavailable to millions is not lost on anyone except apparently the government.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government chose to spend billions on a dual 5G network — solving a political problem between telecom companies — while JENDELA, the basic connectivity programme, faces delays. For Orang Asli and indigenous communities in particular, digital exclusion compounds existing educational and economic marginalisation. A government that prioritises 5G headlines over universal basic connectivity has its priorities backwards. A truly “Madani” government would ensure every Malaysian can access basic internet before giving KL residents faster streaming.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Digital infrastructure promises have been made under every Malaysian government. Under BN, the 1Malaysia Internet Centre programme was launched — many centres were built but poorly maintained or unstaffed. Under PH 1.0, digital infrastructure was briefly prioritised before the Sheraton Move. Under PH 2.0, JENDELA has continued but with persistent delays. The pattern is consistent: announce connectivity programmes, build some infrastructure, under-maintain it, and move on to the next headline technology while basic access gaps persist.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You spent billions on 5G so KL can stream faster. Meanwhile, a kid in a Sarawak longhouse can’t submit homework online. That’s not a digital divide — it’s digital apartheid. Prioritise JENDELA completion. Ensure every school in Malaysia has reliable broadband. Fix basic connectivity before chasing 5G headlines. Or keep building luxury infrastructure for urban areas while rural Malaysians — the communities that vote for you in every election — remain disconnected from the digital economy you keep promoting.

Sources: FMT

item.title: Gambling Addiction Crisis — 4D, Toto, and Online Betting Destroy Families While Government Collects Revenue
item.description:
Said by: Health Ministry / NGOs

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia faces a growing gambling addiction crisis driven by legal 4D and Toto outlets, sports betting, and an explosion of illegal online gambling platforms. The government profits from gambling through licensing fees and gaming taxes — collecting billions annually from licensed operators. Meanwhile, gambling addiction destroys lower-income families, with treatment services remaining minimal compared to the scale of the problem. Online gambling has exploded beyond MCMC’s enforcement capacity, with estimates suggesting billions of ringgit flow through illegal platforms annually.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Gambling addiction disproportionately affects B40 families who can least afford losses. The social costs — family breakdown, debt, crime, mental health deterioration — are borne by communities while the government collects revenue. Licensed gambling operators contribute to political campaigns and enjoy regulatory protection. The government has a financial incentive to maintain gambling revenue and a social responsibility to address addiction. It consistently chooses revenue over responsibility.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government simultaneously profits from gambling and claims to combat it. Licensing fees and gaming taxes generate billions for the Treasury. Online gambling enforcement is performative — MCMC blocks a few sites while thousands more operate freely through VPNs and messaging apps. Treatment services for gambling addiction are chronically underfunded. The conflict of interest is structural: the government is both regulator and beneficiary of the industry it’s supposed to control. That’s not governance — it’s a revenue addiction masquerading as regulation.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Gambling regulation in Malaysia has always been politically convenient. Under BN, licensed operators flourished with political connections. Under PH 1.0, no changes were made. Under PH 2.0, the same operators continue with the same political connections, and online gambling has grown unchecked. Each government collects the revenue, underfunds treatment, and claims to be fighting the problem. The Forest City SFZ’s potential for gaming activities has raised further concerns about the government’s relationship with gambling revenue.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You collect billions from gambling while addiction destroys families. Your enforcement against online gambling is performative. Your treatment services are underfunded. The conflict of interest is obvious: you profit from the industry you’re supposed to regulate. Either significantly increase gambling addiction treatment funding — proportional to the revenue you collect — or admit that gambling regulation is a revenue operation, not a public health programme.

Sources: Bernama | FMT | Health Ministry

item.title: Gender Pay Gap Persists at 6-9% — Women Do More Work for Less Pay, Government Does Nothing
item.description:
Said by: DOSM / Talentcorp Data

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Despite Anwar’s government having prominent women in cabinet and occasional rhetoric about gender equality, Malaysia’s gender pay gap remains stubbornly persistent at an estimated 6-9% across sectors, with significantly larger gaps in certain industries. Women bear a disproportionate burden of unpaid care work, face career penalties for motherhood, and are underrepresented in senior management and board positions.

🔥 Why It Matters

The female labour force participation rate, while improving, still lags regional peers. Maternity protection remains inadequate for gig economy workers. Sexual harassment laws, while strengthened, face enforcement challenges.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government has not introduced pay transparency legislation, gender-disaggregated budgeting, or mandatory gender pay gap reporting for companies — measures that have proven effective in other countries. For a PM who claims to champion a civilisational approach to governance, the failure to meaningfully address gender economic inequality represents a significant blind spot.

🎯 PH Callout

Half of Malaysia’s population deserves more than tokenistic representation and empty rhetoric. Put women in cabinet for the photos, then ignore the structural inequality that holds women back economically. Pay transparency costs nothing to legislate. Gender budgeting is best practice globally. If you won’t do the easy things, you certainly won’t do the hard ones.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: Higher Education Budget Keeps Shrinking — From 6.8% in 2010 to Just 4.3% in 2025
item.description:
Said by: New Mandala / Academic Analysis

Category: Education

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s higher education budget has dropped from 6.8% of the total national budget in 2010 to just 4.3% in 2025, with the lowest point at 3.9% in 2023. Meanwhile, tertiary enrollment stands at only 42.57% — far below Singapore’s 93% and the global average of 51.98%. Academic analysis describes a “widening disjuncture between reformist discourse and actual financial commitment” to education under PH. While the government talks about the knowledge economy and human capital development, the budget tells a different story.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A country that reduces education spending while talking about becoming a knowledge economy is lying to itself. The 4.3% allocation means fewer university places, less research funding, lower academic salaries, and deteriorating campus infrastructure. The tertiary enrollment gap — Malaysia at 42.57% vs Singapore at 93% — represents millions of young Malaysians who could be in higher education but aren’t, because the system doesn’t have the capacity to accommodate them. Every percentage point of budget decline translates into fewer graduates, less innovation, and slower economic growth.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The same government that cut higher education to 4.3% allocated RM2.6 billion to JAKIM — the highest allocation in JAKIM’s history. The civil service budget grows every year while education shrinks. When your budget priorities tell students they matter less than bureaucrats and religious administration, students respond by leaving the country. The brain drain — 1.86 million Malaysians abroad — is directly connected to an education system that receives declining investment. You can’t build human capital while cutting the budget for building it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, education received a larger budget share but was plagued by inefficiency and political management. Under PH 1.0, education reform was briefly prioritised. Under PH 2.0, the budget has consistently declined. New Mandala’s analysis noted that PH’s education rhetoric is more progressive than any previous government but its funding commitment is among the worst. The gap between words and money is the gap between aspiration and reality.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You talk about human capital. You talk about the knowledge economy. You talk about innovation and competitiveness. Then you cut higher education to 4.3% of the budget — the lowest in modern Malaysian history. Singapore spends three times as much proportionally and has 93% tertiary enrollment. You have 42.57%. The numbers don’t lie. Reverse the education budget decline. Commit to reaching 6% within three years. Or stop talking about the knowledge economy while defunding the institutions that are supposed to build it.

Sources: New Mandala | Fulcrum | Taylor’s University

item.title: Jho Low STILL Free After 8 Years — Malaysia's Most Wanted Fugitive Living Comfortably Somewhere
item.description:
Said by: Interpol / MACC / DOJ

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of 2025, Low Taek Jho (Jho Low) — the central figure in the 1MDB scandal — remains free despite Interpol red notices, international arrest warrants, and charges in multiple jurisdictions. Three different Malaysian governments have failed to secure his extradition. Anwar made 1MDB accountability his signature promise, yet has made no visible progress in locating or extraditing Jho Low. Reports suggest he has been residing in China or Macau, but the Malaysian government has not disclosed any diplomatic efforts to secure his return.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Jho Low orchestrated the theft of billions from a Malaysian sovereign wealth fund. He is the most wanted fugitive in Malaysian history. His freedom — eight years after charges were filed — represents the single largest accountability failure in Malaysian governance. Every day Jho Low remains free is a day the rakyat services debt he helped create while he lives comfortably beyond the reach of Malaysian law. If Malaysia cannot bring its most-wanted kleptocrat to justice, its entire anti-corruption framework is performative.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent years demanding 1MDB accountability. “Where is Jho Low?” was a PH rallying cry. Three years into government, the answer remains the same: somewhere comfortable, apparently beyond Anwar’s reach. The US DOJ recovered over $1 billion in 1MDB-linked assets. Singapore jailed bankers. Switzerland sanctioned institutions. Malaysia — the country that was robbed — has not managed to bring the principal orchestrator to trial. If the political will existed, diplomatic pressure on China (where Jho Low is believed to reside) would be a priority. It clearly isn’t.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under Najib, the 1MDB investigation was actively suppressed — the AG was fired, MACC officers were transferred, and Jho Low was protected. Under Muhyiddin, investigations continued but Jho Low remained free. Under Anwar, investigations concluded with settlements and Najib’s reduced sentence, but Jho Low’s extradition has not progressed. The pattern suggests that every Malaysian government has found it politically convenient to talk about catching Jho Low while doing nothing that would actually bring him back.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: “Where is Jho Low?” — you asked that question for five years. Now you’re the PM. You control the diplomatic apparatus. You have Interpol red notices. You have international cooperation frameworks. And Jho Low is still free. Disclose what diplomatic steps you’ve taken. Publish the extradition requests. Pressure China publicly if necessary. Or admit that catching Jho Low was always a slogan, never a priority — and that the biggest kleptocrat in Malaysian history will die free because no government cared enough to bring him home.

Sources: Reuters | DOJ | SCMP

item.title: Malaysia Refuses to Ratify ICERD — Anwar Caves to Racial Politics on International Human Rights Treaty
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Malay-Muslim Groups

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia remains one of the few countries in the world that has not ratified the International Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Racial Discrimination (ICERD). In 2018, PH’s attempt to ratify ICERD was abandoned after mass protests led by Malay-Muslim groups who argued it would undermine Bumiputera privileges. Anwar, once a progressive leader committed to equality, has not revisited the issue since becoming PM. The “reformist” Prime Minister has accepted the status quo on racial discrimination — ICERD ratification is not on the agenda.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: ICERD ratification would commit Malaysia to eliminating racial discrimination in law and practice. Malaysia’s refusal to ratify — alongside countries like North Korea and Myanmar — places it in embarrassing company. The refusal signals to the international community that Malaysia is unwilling to subject its race-based policies to international scrutiny. For non-Bumiputera Malaysians, the refusal to ratify confirms that their rights are subordinate to racial politics.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent decades advocating for a “Malaysian Malaysia” — equality regardless of race. He founded the multi-racial PH coalition on the principle that all Malaysians deserve equal treatment. ICERD ratification would be the natural expression of that principle. Instead, Anwar has accepted the 2018 defeat and moved on. The man who was imprisoned partly for challenging racial politics has, as PM, decided that racial politics are too powerful to challenge. The reformist has capitulated to the very forces he built his career opposing.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia signed ICERD in 1966 but never ratified it. Under Mahathir 1.0, ratification was never considered. Under Najib, it was ignored. Under PH 1.0, ratification was announced and then abandoned after protests. Under PH 2.0, it hasn’t even been raised. Each government decides that the political cost of challenging racial discrimination is higher than the cost of maintaining it. ICERD joins the Sedition Act, the PPPA, and SOSMA on the list of reforms PH promised and abandoned.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You built a coalition on multiracial equality. Your manifesto promised a “New Malaysia” where race wouldn’t determine rights. Ratifying ICERD would be the simplest, most direct expression of that promise. Instead, you’ve accepted the 2018 defeat and moved on — just like every government before you. Either ICERD is consistent with the Malaysia you promised, or the Malaysia you promised was a lie. Which is it?

Sources: UN OHCHR | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: Malaysia Tertiary Enrollment at 42.57% — Below Global Average, Far Behind Singapore's 93%
item.description:
Said by: Academic Research / UNESCO Data

Category: Education

Date: 2025-01-01

Only 42.57% of eligible Malaysians enrolled in tertiary education — below global average of 51.98%. Singapore at 93%, US at 88%. Over 10,000 SPM students (2.6%) didn’t even sit for the exam.

item.title: Mental Health Crisis Spiralling — Youth Suicide and Depression Surge While Government Underfunds Services
item.description:
Said by: Health Ministry / WHO Data

Category: Health

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Malaysia is facing a deepening mental health crisis, particularly among young people, with rising rates of depression, anxiety, and suicide. The National Health and Morbidity Survey has consistently shown alarming increases in mental health issues, with nearly 3 in 10 Malaysians experiencing some form of mental health problem.

🔥 Why It Matters

Youth are disproportionately affected: social media pressure, academic stress, economic uncertainty with 12.28% youth unemployment, and the COVID-19 pandemic’s lingering psychological effects have created a perfect storm. Yet government mental health services remain woefully underfunded and understaffed. Public hospitals face severe shortages of psychiatrists and psychologists, with waiting times stretching weeks or months.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government allocated increased budgets for healthcare but mental health remains a fraction of overall health spending. School counsellors are often undertrained and overwhelmed. Helplines like Befrienders are largely volunteer-run. For a government that talks about ‘Madani’ (civilisational) values, allowing an entire generation to suffer in silence is a civilisational failure.

🎯 PH Callout

Three in ten Malaysians are struggling mentally and your response is underfunded. You talk about ‘Madani’ values but won’t fund the psychiatrists, counsellors, and helplines that could actually save lives. Mental health isn’t a soft issue — it’s a crisis. Fund it like one.

Sources

  • CNA — K-Pods and Drug Crisis

item.title: Palm Oil Deforestation Continues Unchecked — Environmental Pledges vs Plantation Profits
item.description:
Said by: Environmental NGOs / SAM

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Despite Malaysia’s commitments to international climate agreements and sustainable development goals, deforestation for palm oil plantations continues to degrade tropical rainforests among the oldest and most biodiverse on Earth. The Malaysian Sustainable Palm Oil (MSPO) certification scheme has been criticised by international NGOs as inadequate greenwashing. Malaysia’s river water quality is declining — clean rivers dropped from 486 to 468 in 2025. The Perak River turned blue from rare earth mining discharge in November 2025. Endangered species including orangutans, pygmy elephants, and Malayan tigers face shrinking habitats.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Malaysia accounts for approximately 25% of global palm oil production, and the economic dependence on this commodity creates enormous political resistance to genuine environmental reform. While the rate of primary forest loss has slowed from its peak, secondary forest clearing and peatland conversion continue. The environmental cost — biodiversity loss, river pollution, carbon emissions from peatland drainage — is not counted in GDP figures but is borne by communities who depend on clean water, healthy forests, and functioning ecosystems.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The MSPO certification scheme is a sticker, not a standard. It allows continued environmental degradation while providing a veneer of sustainability that satisfies international buyers. The government talks about sustainability at COP summits and international conferences while approving new plantation developments at home. When your rivers turn blue from industrial discharge and your forests disappear for palm oil, no amount of international pledges will bring them back. Sustainability isn’t a speech — it’s a policy. Malaysia doesn’t have one.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, Malaysia’s deforestation rate was among the highest in the world. Mahathir’s era saw massive conversion of primary rainforest to plantations. Under PH 1.0, a moratorium on new oil palm licences was briefly discussed. Under PH 2.0, the MSPO was expanded but not strengthened. Each government pays lip service to environmental protection while protecting the plantation industry that funds political parties across the spectrum. The pattern is bipartisan: plantations are more politically valuable than forests.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You sign climate agreements and pose for photos at COP summits. Then you come home and approve more plantations. Your MSPO scheme is window dressing. Your rivers are getting dirtier. Your forests are getting smaller. Strengthen MSPO to meet international standards. Enforce illegal logging laws. Invest in downstream palm oil processing rather than acreage expansion. Or keep giving sustainability speeches while your country’s environmental foundation crumbles — and hope the world doesn’t notice the hypocrisy.

Sources: Asian News Network | FOE Malaysia

item.title: Penang South Islands: $14B Reclamation Project Destroys Fishing Grounds for 6,000 Fishers
item.description:
Said by: Penang State Government / SAM

Category: Other

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Penang South Islands (PSI) project — originally three artificial islands costing approximately $14 billion — threatens the livelihoods of 6,000 fishers, 51,184 tonnes of annual marine catch worth RM525 million, and 511 aquaculture farms generating RM1.67 billion per year. After sustained protests from fishing communities and environmental groups including Sahabat Alam Malaysia (SAM), the project was downsized to one island. However, construction proceeds, and the fundamental impact on fishing grounds and marine ecosystems remains.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Penang’s fishing communities have worked these waters for generations. The reclamation destroys their fishing grounds — permanently. No amount of compensation replaces a livelihood that sustains 6,000 families. The marine catch and aquaculture figures represent hundreds of millions in annual economic output that will be lost. The project benefits property developers; the cost is borne by fishing communities who have no political leverage against DAP’s state government.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP-led Penang has governed for over 16 years. The PSI project epitomises the contradiction at the heart of DAP’s governance: progressive rhetoric, developer-friendly reality. Reclaiming thousands of acres of sea to build luxury property while destroying the livelihoods of 6,000 fishers is not sustainable development — it is displacement development. The “downsizing” to one island was a PR concession, not a policy change. The fundamental trade-off — fishing communities sacrificed for developer profits — remains unchanged.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Land reclamation for development has been contentious in Penang for decades. The Gurney Drive reclamation, Tanjung Tokong development, and SRS Consortium controversies all followed the same pattern: developers propose, fishers protest, the state proceeds. DAP — which championed environmental protection in opposition — has greenlit more reclamation in Penang than BN ever did. The PSI project is the largest and most destructive yet.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You’re destroying the livelihoods of 6,000 fishers for a developer’s dream. RM525 million in annual marine catch. RM1.67 billion in aquaculture. Gone — permanently — so developers can sell luxury apartments on artificial islands. This is not development. It is the displacement of working communities for profit. If you can’t develop Penang without destroying the fishing industry, you can’t develop Penang.

Sources: SAM | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: Proton Still Dependent on Government Protections — 40 Years of 'National Car' and Still Can't Compete
item.description:
Said by: Automotive Industry Analysts

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: After 40 years and billions in public subsidies, tax exemptions, and protectionist trade policies, Proton still cannot compete internationally without government protection. High import duties — excise duties of up to 100% on foreign cars — inflate vehicle prices for 33 million Malaysians. The same car costs half as much in Thailand or Indonesia. Anwar’s government shows no inclination to reform automotive trade policies or phase out protections that cost Malaysian consumers billions annually.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Car ownership is not a luxury in Malaysia — it is a necessity in a country with inadequate public transport outside KL. High import duties mean Malaysian families spend a disproportionate share of their income on vehicles. A Honda City that costs RM65,000 in Thailand costs over RM100,000 in Malaysia. A Toyota Corolla in Indonesia costs about RM80,000; in Malaysia, over RM130,000. The “national car” policy forces 33 million people to subsidise one company’s inability to compete — through higher prices on every vehicle they buy.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Proton was supposed to be a temporary infant industry — protected for a few years until it could stand on its own. Forty years later, it still can’t stand on its own. The company was sold to Geely (China) in 2017 because it couldn’t survive without a foreign partner. Yet import duties remain, protecting a company that is no longer even fully Malaysian-owned. The protection no longer serves a national champion — it serves a joint venture with a Chinese automaker while Malaysian consumers pay inflated prices for every car on the road.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The national car project has been a political sacred cow since Mahathir launched Proton in 1983. Every government has maintained protections because the automotive lobby — dealers, suppliers, workers — represents votes. BN protected Proton for 35 years. PH 1.0 made no changes. PH 2.0 has made no changes. The policy survives not because it works but because dismantling it would anger connected interests. Meanwhile, Thailand and Indonesia — without national car protections — have become major automotive manufacturing hubs. Malaysia has not.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Malaysian families pay double for cars because of a 40-year-old policy that protects a company now half-owned by China. Public transport is inadequate. Car ownership is mandatory. And you maintain import duties that inflate prices for everyone. Phase out automotive protections over 5-10 years. Invest the savings in public transport. Let Malaysian consumers buy cars at regional prices. Or keep forcing 33 million people to subsidise a failed industrial policy — and wonder why cost of living is your biggest political problem.

Sources: Paultan.org | Nikkei Asia | The Edge

item.title: PTPTN Loan Crisis — Millions Owe Billions, Repayment Rates Abysmal, Graduates Trapped in Debt
item.description:
Said by: PTPTN / Auditor General Reports

Category: Education

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) faces a chronic repayment crisis, with billions of ringgit in outstanding loans and repayment rates that consistently fall short of targets. Millions of Malaysian graduates carry PTPTN debt that limits their ability to save, invest, or purchase homes. The fund’s sustainability is questionable without major reform.

🔥 Why It Matters

PTPTN debt creates a generational poverty trap: graduates enter the workforce already indebted, face an economy with 12.28% youth unemployment, earn wages that barely cover living costs (RM1,500 minimum wage), and must somehow repay student loans while building a life. Many can’t — which is why repayment rates are so low. The cycle feeds itself: unaffordable education → debt → low wages → inability to repay → creditworthiness issues → inability to get mortgages → permanent renter status.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH promised various PTPTN reforms including debt relief for low-income graduates, extended repayment flexibility, and making education more affordable to reduce future borrowing. Progress has been marginal. The government talks about education as an investment but treats PTPTN as a debt collection agency. Students who borrowed to get degrees that the economy doesn’t value (because the education system doesn’t produce employable graduates) are being punished for a system-level failure that isn’t their fault.

📜 Previous Cases

BN’s approach was also inadequate — carrot-and-stick approaches to loan recovery without addressing the fundamental issues of education quality and labour market mismatch. PH 1.0 promised PTPTN reform but collapsed before implementation. PH 2.0 has made incremental adjustments but the structural crisis remains. The money lent produces graduates who can’t find matching employment, who then can’t repay loans, which undermines the fund’s ability to lend to future students. It’s a doom loop.

🎯 PH Callout

Your education system produces graduates who can’t find jobs (12.28% youth unemployment). Your economy doesn’t pay graduates enough to repay their loans (RM1,500 minimum wage). And your PTPTN continues lending for degrees that don’t lead to employment. You’ve created a perfectly designed poverty machine: borrow for education → get a degree → find no matching job → can’t repay loan → can’t buy house → stay in poverty. Fix the pipeline or stop pretending the problem is lazy graduates.

Sources

  • Taylor’s University — The Value of Education
  • FMT — Decline of Malaysia’s Schooling System

item.title: Road Deaths at Catastrophic Levels — Malaysia Among Deadliest Roads in ASEAN, Government's Response: 'Speed Cameras'
item.description:
Said by: MIROS / WHO Road Safety Data

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia records over 6,000 road deaths annually — approximately 16 people every day. Motorcyclists make up the largest share of fatalities. WHO data ranks Malaysia among the deadliest roads in ASEAN with a road traffic death rate significantly above the global average. The government’s primary response has been speed cameras, periodic enforcement campaigns, and awareness programmes. No structural reform of road safety infrastructure, licensing standards, or vehicle safety requirements has been implemented under PH.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: 6,000 deaths per year is a public health crisis that receives a fraction of the attention given to lesser issues. For context, Malaysia loses more people to road accidents annually than to most diseases. The economic cost — medical expenses, lost productivity, insurance claims — runs into billions. Motorcyclists, who are disproportionately B40 and young, bear the highest risk. The death toll has remained at catastrophic levels for over a decade with no meaningful decline, suggesting that current interventions are not working.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Speed cameras and enforcement campaigns are band-aid solutions. The structural causes of Malaysia’s road death crisis are well-documented: poor road design (especially rural roads without barriers), lax licensing standards (motorcycle licences are too easy to obtain), inadequate vehicle safety requirements (no mandatory ABS on motorcycles, no crash testing regime), and a culture of enforcement-then-amnesty that undermines deterrence. The government knows what works — segregated motorcycle lanes, mandatory safety equipment, stricter licensing — but implementing these requires investment and political will. Speed cameras are cheap. Structural reform is not.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Road safety has been a declared government priority under every administration since the 1990s. Under Mahathir 1.0, the Road Safety Department was established. Under Najib, the Road Safety Plan was launched. Under PH, the same plans continue with the same results: 6,000+ deaths per year. The UN Decade of Action for Road Safety (2011-2020) set targets Malaysia missed. The new decade (2021-2030) is heading the same direction. Each government announces road safety campaigns. None reduces the death toll.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Sixteen Malaysians die on the roads every day. Your response is speed cameras. The structural interventions that work — segregated motorcycle lanes, mandatory ABS, stricter licensing, road design reform — require investment and political courage you haven’t shown. Allocate a dedicated road safety infrastructure budget. Mandate motorcycle ABS. Reform the licensing system. Or keep installing speed cameras while 6,000 families lose someone every year — and pretend the problem is speed, not infrastructure.

Sources: WHO Road Safety | MIROS | FMT

item.title: Sabah and Sarawak Autonomy Demands Still Unmet — East Malaysia Remains the Forgotten Stepchild
item.description:
Said by: Sabah & Sarawak Leaders / GPS

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Despite 2021 constitutional amendments restoring Sabah and Sarawak’s status as equal partners in the Federation, East Malaysia remains structurally marginalised. Sabah is Malaysia’s poorest state. MA63 (Malaysia Agreement 1963) recommendations are gathering dust. Oil and gas royalty disputes remain unresolved. Equal partnership exists on paper but not in budget allocation, infrastructure investment, or political representation. Sarawak’s GPS maintains leverage through coalition politics, but Sabah — where PH won only 1 of 20 seats in the 2025 state election — has almost no bargaining power.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Sabah and Sarawak contributed the resources — oil, gas, timber, land — that built Malaysia’s modern economy. In return, they received the worst infrastructure, healthcare, education, and connectivity in the country. The MA63 framework guarantees equal treatment that has never been delivered. When Sabah remains the poorest state despite sitting on some of Malaysia’s richest natural resources, the federal system has failed East Malaysia. Every year the disparities persist is another year the federation’s promise is broken.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar promised to honour MA63 and treat Sabah and Sarawak as equal partners. Three years later, the specific demands — increased oil royalties, control over immigration, education autonomy, infrastructure investment — remain unresolved. The constitutional amendments were symbolic victories that changed no budget line items. Sabah still has the highest poverty rate. Sarawak still has the worst road infrastructure. Both still have the weakest healthcare coverage. Symbolic equal partnership without fiscal equal partnership is not partnership — it is a rebrand.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, East Malaysian states were coalition partners who received patronage in exchange for parliamentary seats — but never structural reform. Under PH 1.0, a committee was formed to study MA63. Under PH 2.0, the committee’s recommendations remain largely unimplemented. Under every government, the pattern is the same: promise East Malaysian autonomy, study the issue, delay implementation, and rely on coalition arithmetic to avoid accountability. Sixty years after Malaysia Agreement, the agreement’s terms remain unfulfilled.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You amended the Constitution to restore equal partner status. Then you failed to follow through with equal budgets, equal infrastructure, and equal services. Sabah is still the poorest state. Sarawak still has the worst roads. Both still have the worst healthcare access. Constitutional amendments without fiscal commitments are empty gestures. Increase federal allocation to East Malaysia proportional to their resource contribution. Resolve oil royalty disputes. Deliver infrastructure on par with Peninsular Malaysia. Or keep calling them “equal partners” while treating them like subsidiaries.

Sources: Malaysiakini | Borneo Post | East Asia Forum

item.title: Sedition Act STILL Not Repealed — PH Promised to Abolish It in 2018 and 2022, Then Used It Instead
item.description:
Said by: Lawyers for Liberty / Article 19 / Bar Council

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

The Sedition Act 1948 — a colonial-era law that PH explicitly promised to repeal in both its 2018 and 2022 election manifestos — remains fully operational under the Anwar government. Not only has it not been repealed, it is being actively used against government critics. Activist Chegubard was charged under the Act for his Forest City casino social media post.

🔥 Why It Matters

The Act criminalises speech that is ‘seditious,’ a vaguely defined term that gives authorities enormous discretion to prosecute political expression. Lawyers for Liberty has repeatedly condemned the government’s continued reliance on repressive laws. The Malaysian Bar Council has called for its repeal. International press freedom organisations have flagged its use as incompatible with democratic norms.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Alongside the Sedition Act, other repressive laws PH promised to repeal remain on the books: the Printing Presses and Publications Act 1984, the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 (SOSMA), and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act (used to prosecute online speech). Every single repressive law PH campaigned against is still available for PH to use — and PH is using them. The betrayal is complete.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised to repeal these laws. Twice. In writing. In your manifesto. You didn’t just break a promise — you weaponised the very tools you swore to destroy. When you use the Sedition Act against critics, you’re not governing — you’re doing exactly what BN did, with the added insult of having promised not to.

Sources

  • NST — Chegubard Sedition Case
  • Freedom House — Malaysia

item.title: Single Mothers in Poverty — 300,000+ Families Led by Women Living Below the Poverty Line
item.description:
Said by: Women’s Ministry / DOSM Data

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

An estimated 300,000+ single-mother households in Malaysia live at or below the poverty line, facing inadequate government support, employment discrimination, and insufficient childcare infrastructure. Single mothers are disproportionately represented among B40 households and face barriers including limited access to affordable housing, healthcare, and skills training.

🔥 Why It Matters

Single mothers and their children represent one of the most vulnerable demographics in Malaysia. Children in these households face higher risks of educational dropout, malnutrition, and intergenerational poverty. The lack of affordable childcare forces many single mothers to choose between working (and leaving children unsupervised) or staying home (and being unable to afford basic needs).

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Government programmes for single mothers exist on paper but are fragmented, underfunded, and bureaucratically complex to access. The Bantuan Ibu Tunggal and various state-level programmes reach only a fraction of eligible recipients. Meanwhile, the government finds billions for GLC bailouts, political appointments, and civil service perks. The contrast between how Malaysia treats political elites and how it treats single mothers tells you everything about government priorities.

📜 Previous Cases

Under BN, single mother welfare was also inadequate. PH promised to do better. Under PH 1.0, minimal progress was made before the government collapsed. Under PH 2.0, single mothers remain an afterthought — occasionally mentioned in budget speeches, rarely served by meaningful policy. The gender pay gap (6-9%), lack of affordable childcare, and employment discrimination that compound single mothers’ poverty remain unaddressed.

🎯 PH Callout

You talk about ‘Madani’ values — compassion, civilisation, humanity. There are 300,000+ families led by single mothers living in poverty. These are real women and real children, not statistics. Your RM2.6 billion JAKIM budget could transform single mother welfare overnight. Your RM85.5 million J-Kom propaganda budget could fund childcare centres. But you’d rather fund religious bureaucracy and government PR than feed hungry children. That’s not Madani — that’s malicious.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: SOSMA Still Unrepealed — PH Using the Security Law They Called 'Draconian' to Detain Without Trial
item.description:
Said by: Lawyers for Liberty / Bar Council / Amnesty International

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

The Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 (SOSMA), which allows detention without trial for up to 28 days and restricts judicial oversight of security cases, remains fully operational under the Anwar government. PH explicitly promised to repeal SOSMA in both its 2018 and 2022 manifestos, calling it ‘draconian’ and incompatible with democratic governance.

🔥 Why It Matters

Detention without trial is fundamentally incompatible with the rule of law. SOSMA gives the government the power to detain individuals for nearly a month without charge and without full judicial scrutiny. In the wrong hands, it’s a tool for political persecution. Every day it remains on the books is a day any Malaysian can be detained without the protections that democracy promises.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH called SOSMA ‘draconian’ when BN used it. PH politicians gave speeches condemning it. Lawyers for Liberty documented its abuse. The Bar Council called for its repeal. Amnesty International flagged it annually. Now PH is in power with a supermajority, and SOSMA remains. Not amended. Not reformed. Not repealed. Unchanged and available for use.

📜 Previous Cases

Under BN, SOSMA was used against political activists. Under PH 1.0, no repeal in 22 months. Under PH 2.0, over three years, no repeal. The Sedition Act: same story. PPPA: same story. EVERY repressive law PH promised to abolish: same story. The pattern is not accidental.

🎯 PH Callout

You have the numbers. You have the mandate. You spent YEARS calling SOSMA draconian. Repeal it. You won’t — because having the power to detain without trial is convenient, even for ‘reformists.’ Your refusal proves your opposition was always about who held the power, never about the principle.

Sources

  • Freedom House — Malaysia 2024
  • Malay Mail / Amnesty — Reform Failures

item.title: Teacher Shortage in Rural Schools — Kids in Kampungs Get Second-Class Education
item.description:
Said by: Education Ministry / NUTP

Category: Education

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Rural schools across Malaysia face chronic teacher shortages in mathematics, science, and English — the three subjects most critical for economic competitiveness. Indigenous communities in Sabah, Sarawak, and Orang Asli settlements face the worst gaps, with some schools lacking qualified teachers entirely for key subjects. The National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) has repeatedly raised the issue. The Education Ministry acknowledges the shortage but has not implemented structural reforms to address it.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A child’s postcode determines the quality of education they receive. Urban schools in KL, Penang, and JB have qualified teachers, resources, and facilities. Rural schools — especially in Sabah, Sarawak, and Orang Asli areas — have empty classrooms, unqualified substitute teachers, and crumbling infrastructure. This two-tier system perpetuates generational poverty: rural children receive inferior education, score lower on SPM, access fewer university places, and earn less throughout their lives. The education gap is not a resource problem — it is a political priorities problem.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government spends RM2.6 billion on JAKIM and shrinks the higher education budget to 4.3%. Rural teacher shortages have been documented for decades. The solution is known: incentivise qualified teachers to serve in rural areas with housing, salary bonuses, and career advancement. Singapore does this. Thailand does this. Malaysia talks about it. Every education blueprint mentions rural schools. None solves the problem because solving it requires redirecting money from urban constituencies to rural areas — and urban constituencies have more political leverage.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Rural teacher shortages have been a known problem since the 1980s. Under BN, the “Guru Cemerlang” programme was supposed to attract top teachers to underserved areas — it didn’t. Under PH 1.0, rural education was briefly prioritised before the Sheraton Move. Under PH 2.0, the same shortage persists. Each government launches a programme with a new name. None provides the structural incentives — housing, salary, career progression — that would actually convince qualified teachers to serve in rural Sabah or Orang Asli settlements.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Kids in kampungs deserve the same quality teachers as kids in KL. They don’t get them because your government won’t pay for it. Provide housing and salary incentives for rural teachers. Build partnerships with local universities to train teachers for their own communities. Fund rural school infrastructure to the same standard as urban schools. Or keep producing two classes of Malaysian graduates — one educated, one abandoned — and wonder why the development gap never closes.

Sources: FMT | NUTP | Fulcrum

item.title: [BM] MCA Kepada PH: 'Jangan Salahkan BN Untuk Kegagalan ANDA Mereformasi'
item.description:
Said by: Neow Choo Seong, MCA Youth

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-30

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir Disember 2024, jurucakap Pemuda MCA Neow Choo Seong mengeluarkan kenyataan memberitahu PH supaya berhenti menyalahkan BN atas kegagalan PH melaksanakan reformasi yang dijanjikan. Neow berhujah bahawa “kuasa berada sepenuhnya di tangan PH” dan BN tidak patut dijadikan kambing hitam atas keengganan PH sendiri bertindak. Kenyataan ini ketara kerana MCA adalah parti komponen BN dalam gabungan kerajaan perpaduan — bermakna rakan gabungan PH sendiri sudah jemu PH menyalahkan semua orang lain atas kegagalannya menyampaikan reformasi.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Apabila rakan gabungan anda sendiri secara terbuka memberitahu anda untuk berhenti memberi alasan, alasan itu sudah tamat tempoh. Pembelaan lalai PH untuk setiap janji yang dimungkiri ialah “kekangan gabungan” — khususnya, UMNO dan BN menyekat reformasi. Kenyataan MCA meruntuhkan hujah itu dari dalam gabungan: kuasa adalah milik PH. PM adalah PH. Menteri Kewangan adalah PH. Majoriti jawatan kabinet adalah PH. Jika reformasi tidak berlaku, ia kerana PH memilih untuk tidak melaksanakannya — bukan kerana BN menghalangnya.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: PH telah menggunakan “kekangan gabungan” sebagai alasan serba guna selama tiga tahun. Tidak boleh mansuhkan Akta Hasutan? Kekangan gabungan. Tidak boleh mereformasi Akta Mesin Cetak? Kekangan gabungan. Tidak boleh luluskan had tempoh PM? Kekangan gabungan. Kenyataan MCA mendedahkan penipuan: PH mengawal kerajaan. PH mempunyai majoriti parlimen. PH memegang portfolio utama. Reformasi bukan disekat — ia tidak dicuba. Menyalahkan BN bukan strategi politik; ia pengakuan bahawa PH tidak pernah berniat menyampaikan reformasi yang dijanjikan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Menyalahkan rakan gabungan atas kegagalan dasar adalah tradisi politik Malaysia. Di bawah BN, UMNO menyalahkan MCA dan MIC kerana “tidak memahami sensitiviti Melayu.” Di bawah PH 1.0, PH menyalahkan puak Mahathir atas reformasi yang lambat. Di bawah PH 2.0, PH menyalahkan UMNO. Coraknya sama merentas gabungan: parti dominan menggunakan rakan yang lebih kecil sebagai kambing hitam bagi kegagalan dasarnya sendiri. Apabila kambing hitam itu sendiri berkata “berhenti salahkan kami,” strategi itu telah runtuh.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Rakan gabungan anda sendiri memberitahu anda untuk berhenti memberi alasan. Kuasa ada di tangan anda. Anda memegang pejabat PM, Kementerian Kewangan, dan majoriti kerusi kabinet. Jika reformasi tidak berlaku selepas tiga tahun, ia kerana anda memilih untuk tidak melaksanakannya. Berhenti menyalahkan BN. Berhenti menyalahkan “kekangan gabungan.” Akui rekod anda. Sampaikan reformasi yang anda janjikan — atau akui bahawa janji-janji itu tidak pernah ikhlas, dan hadapi pengundi dengan pengakuan itu.

Sumber: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: MCA to PH: 'Don't Blame BN for YOUR Failure to Reform'
item.description:
Said by: Neow Choo Seong, MCA Youth

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late December 2024, MCA Youth spokesman Neow Choo Seong issued a statement telling PH to stop blaming BN for PH’s failure to implement promised reforms. Neow argued that “power is firmly in PH’s hands” and BN should not be scapegoated for PH’s own inaction. The statement was notable because MCA is a BN component party within the unity government coalition — meaning even PH’s own coalition partners were tired of PH blaming everyone else for its failure to deliver reform.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When your own coalition partner publicly tells you to stop making excuses, the excuse has expired. PH’s default defence for every broken promise has been “coalition constraints” — specifically, UMNO and BN blocking reform. MCA’s statement demolishes that argument from inside the coalition: power is PH’s. The PM is PH. The Finance Minister is PH. The majority of cabinet positions are PH. If reforms haven’t happened, it’s because PH chose not to implement them — not because BN prevented it.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH has used “coalition constraints” as an all-purpose excuse for three years. Can’t repeal the Sedition Act? Coalition constraints. Can’t reform the Printing Presses Act? Coalition constraints. Can’t deliver PM term limits? Coalition constraints. MCA’s statement exposes the fraud: PH controls the government. PH has a parliamentary majority. PH holds the key portfolios. The reforms aren’t blocked — they’re not attempted. Blaming BN is not a political strategy; it’s a confession that PH never intended to deliver the reforms it promised.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Blaming coalition partners for policy failures is a Malaysian political tradition. Under BN, UMNO blamed MCA and MIC for “not understanding Malay sensitivities.” Under PH 1.0, PH blamed Mahathir’s faction for slow reform. Under PH 2.0, PH blames UMNO. The pattern is identical across coalitions: the dominant party uses smaller partners as scapegoats for its own policy failures. When even the scapegoat says “stop blaming us,” the strategy has collapsed.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own coalition partner is telling you to stop making excuses. Power is in your hands. You hold the PM’s office, the Finance Ministry, and the majority of cabinet seats. If reforms haven’t happened after three years, it’s because you chose not to implement them. Stop blaming BN. Stop blaming “coalition constraints.” Own your record. Deliver the reforms you promised — or admit that the promises were never sincere, and face the voters with that admission.

Sources: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian Diluluskan — SKMM Dapat Kuasa Penapisan Menyeluruh, Tiada Pengawasan
item.description:
Said by: Parliament / Fahmi Fadzil

Category: Social

Date: 2024-12-20

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Disember 2024, Parlimen meluluskan Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian dan pindaan AKM yang memberi SKMM kuasa penapisan menyeluruh dengan penalti sehingga RM10 juta bagi platform yang tidak patuh. Perundangan ini memberi kuasa kepada SKMM untuk mengarahkan penyingkiran kandungan, menyekat laman web dan menghukum syarikat media sosial — semuanya tanpa pengawasan kehakiman bebas. Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil menggambarkannya sebagai perlu untuk melindungi kanak-kanak dan membanteras ancaman siber. Freedom House membenderakannya sebagai kemunduran besar dalam kebebasan internet. Tiada mekanisme pengawasan bebas dimasukkan.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian memberi agensi kerajaan — SKMM, yang melaporkan kepada Menteri Komunikasi — kuasa untuk menentukan apa yang rakyat Malaysia boleh lihat dalam talian. Tiada tribunal bebas, tiada keperluan semakan kehakiman sebelum penyingkiran kandungan, dan tiada mekanisme ketelusan untuk kandungan yang disekat. Apabila agensi kerajaan boleh menyingkir kandungan dan menghukum platform sehingga RM10 juta tanpa pengawasan bebas, setiap platform mempunyai insentif untuk mematuhi secara berlebihan — menyingkir kandungan sempadan secara proaktif berbanding mengambil risiko penalti. Hasilnya ialah penapisan melalui insentif, bukan sahaja penapisan melalui perintah.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: PH semasa pembangkang melawan tepat jenis undang-undang ini. Akta Antiberita Tidak Benar adalah bukti utama PH bagi autoritarianisme BN — dan Akta itu lebih ringan daripada apa yang PH kini luluskan. Akta Antiberita Tidak Benar mempunyai denda sehingga RM500,000. Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian mempunyai penalti sehingga RM10 juta. Akta Antiberita Tidak Benar menyasarkan penceramah individu. Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian menyasarkan platform — bermakna SKMM boleh mengancam menutup keseluruhan perkhidmatan melainkan mereka mematuhi. PH telah menggantikan undang-undang buruk dengan yang lebih buruk dan memanggilnya reformasi.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah BN, Akta Antiberita Tidak Benar (2018), Akta Hasutan, dan Seksyen 233 AKM membentuk peralatan penapisan internet. PH memansuhkan Akta Antiberita Tidak Benar pada 2018 dan berjanji mereformasi AKM. Di bawah PH 2.0, AKM diperkuatkan (bukan direformasi), Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian diluluskan (menambah kuasa baharu), dan SKMM diberi alat penguatkuasaan yang melebihi apa yang BN pernah miliki. Infrastruktur penapisan yang PH bina dalam tiga tahun lebih komprehensif daripada apa yang BN bina dalam 60 tahun.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda memansuhkan Akta Antiberita Tidak Benar kerana ia memberi kerajaan terlalu banyak kuasa ke atas ucapan dalam talian. Kemudian anda meluluskan Akta Keselamatan Dalam Talian, yang memberi kerajaan lebih banyak kuasa lagi dengan penalti lebih besar dan tiada pengawasan bebas. Anda tidak mereformasi peraturan internet — anda menguatkannya secara turbo. Masukkan pengawasan kehakiman bebas untuk semua perintah penyingkiran kandungan. Hadkan penalti pada tahap yang tidak mewujudkan insentif untuk pematuhan berlebihan. Terbitkan laporan ketelusan tentang semua permintaan penyingkiran kandungan SKMM. Atau terima bahawa anda telah membina rangka kerja penapisan internet paling komprehensif dalam sejarah Malaysia — dan akui ia.

Sumber: Freedom House | ARTICLE 19 | Malaysiakini

item.title: Online Safety Act Passes — MCMC Gets Sweeping Censorship Powers, No Oversight
item.description:
Said by: Parliament / Fahmi Fadzil

Category: Social

Date: 2024-12-20

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2024, Parliament passed the Online Safety Act and CMA amendments giving MCMC sweeping censorship powers with penalties up to RM10 million for non-compliant platforms. The legislation empowers MCMC to order content removal, block websites, and penalise social media companies — all without independent judicial oversight. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil described it as necessary to protect children and combat cyber threats. Freedom House flagged it as a major regression in internet freedom. No independent oversight mechanism was included.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The Online Safety Act gives a government agency — MCMC, which reports to the Communications Minister — the power to determine what Malaysians can see online.
