Reuters reported the pardon came “amid accusations of backsliding on reforms.” Critics called it a backroom deal to keep UMNO in the coalition — UMNO had made Najib’s release a precondition for continued support of Anwar’s government.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Najib was convicted of the largest financial scandal in Malaysian history — billions stolen from a sovereign wealth fund, laundered through global banks, spent on yachts, jewellery, and Hollywood films. The conviction was PH’s signature achievement under PH 1.0. Halving the sentence under PH 2.0 sent a devastating message: in Malaysia, corruption penalties are negotiable if you have enough political leverage. The pardon was not based on new evidence or legal merit — it was based on coalition arithmetic. UMNO’s support for PH came with an implicit price, and the rakyat is watching that price get paid.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent his entire political career demanding accountability for Najib. “Malu Apa Bossku” was the slogan PH mocked relentlessly. Now Najib’s sentence is halved, his fine reduced by 76%, and his supporters are pushing for house arrest — all under Anwar’s watch. The Pardons Board includes the PM. The decision cannot be separated from the political context: UMNO wanted Najib’s sentence reduced, UMNO is essential to PH’s majority, and the sentence was reduced. Calling this anything other than a political deal requires a level of naivety that no Malaysian possesses.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a history of politically convenient pardons. Anwar himself was pardoned by the King after PH 1.0’s victory in 2018 — a pardon that was celebrated as justice. The difference: Anwar’s conviction was widely seen as politically motivated. Najib’s conviction was upheld by every level of the judiciary based on overwhelming evidence. Pardoning a political prisoner and reducing the sentence of a convicted kleptocrat are fundamentally different acts — but PH’s willingness to facilitate both suggests that the pardons process is a political tool, not a justice mechanism.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You built your movement on holding Najib accountable. Then you halved his sentence to keep UMNO happy. The rakyat marched in Bersih rallies, voted in GE14 and GE15, and waited years for justice — and you traded it for coalition stability. If Najib’s crime was worth 12 years when PH was in opposition, it doesn’t become worth 6 years just because UMNO is in your coalition. Every ringgit Najib stole is still missing. Every year removed from his sentence is a discount on accountability. The rakyat will remember.

Sources: Reuters | Straits Times | Al Jazeera

item.title: [BM] Skor PISA Malaysia Merosot Paling Teruk Di Kalangan Negara Asia Tenggara — Anwar Kata 'Tidak Boleh Nafikan'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2024-01-15

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Keputusan PISA 2022 Malaysia — mengukur kualiti pendidikan pelajar berumur 15 tahun — menunjukkan kemerosotan terbesar di kalangan semua negara Asia Tenggara yang diuji. Skor jatuh merentas matematik, sains, dan bacaan. Anwar merespons dengan mengatakan Malaysia “mesti mengetahui keadaan sebenar dan tidak boleh dalam keadaan menafikan.” Menteri Pendidikan mengakui sistem itu “mengecewakan guru dan pelajar.” CNA melaporkan bahawa politik — terutamanya ketegangan antara keperluan bahasa Melayu dan kecekapan bahasa Inggeris — berada di sebalik kesukaran reformasi pendidikan. Tiga tahun kemudian, pakar mengatakan tiada apa yang berubah di lapangan.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Pendidikan menentukan masa depan sesebuah negara. Sistem pendidikan Malaysia sedang gagal — dan kerajaan tahu. Keputusan PISA telah bertakung selama tiga kitaran penilaian berturut-turut sementara pesaing serantau seperti Vietnam dan Indonesia telah meningkat. FMT memerhatikan: “Setiap beberapa tahun, kementerian melancarkan pelan induk pendidikan baharu. Ia mempunyai logo yang kemas dan halaman penuh jargon. Tetapi di lapangan, tiada apa yang berubah.” Jurang antara Malaysia dan Singapura dalam matematik kini bersamaan kira-kira 8 tahun pembelajaran. Satu generasi pelajar Malaysia sedang ditinggalkan oleh sistem yang mengutamakan pertimbangan politik berbanding hasil pendidikan.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: “Tidak boleh nafikan” adalah pengakuan, bukan dasar. Anwar mengakui masalah pada Januari 2024. Kini sudah Mac 2026. Mana hasilnya? Politik bahasa, agama, dan kaum menjadikan reformasi pendidikan hampir mustahil dalam sistem gabungan Malaysia — dan PH menunjukkan sifar kesediaan untuk menghadapi politik tersebut. Keperluan wajib Bahasa Melayu dan Sejarah merentas semua sistem pendidikan diperluaskan bukannya direformasi. AUKU — yang menyekat penyertaan politik pelajar — masih kekal meskipun ia janji manifesto. Sistem pendidikan gagal kerana membaikinya memerlukan menghadapi kepentingan yang mengakar yang PH bergantung padanya untuk kelangsungan politik.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Kemerosotan pendidikan Malaysia bukan baharu. Skor PISA telah sederhana sejak Malaysia mula menyertai pada 2009. Setiap menteri pendidikan melancarkan pelan induk baharu: Pelan Pembangunan Pendidikan Malaysia 2013-2025, pelbagai inisiatif STEM, pengembangan TVET. Setiap satu menjana laporan dan sidang akhbar. Tiada yang menjana peningkatan yang boleh diukur dalam hasil pelajar. Masalah asas — sistem yang direka berdasarkan pertimbangan politik dan etnik bukannya kecemerlangan pendidikan — kekal tidak disentuh kerana tiada kerajaan yang sanggup membayar kos politik memreformasinya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda berkata Malaysia “tidak boleh nafikan.” Kemudian anda terus menafikan. Skor PISA merosot. Jurang pendidikan dengan Singapura adalah 8 tahun pembelajaran. Respons anda ialah mengakui masalah dan tidak melakukan apa-apa. Reformasi kurikulum untuk mengutamakan pemikiran kritis berbanding hafalan. Laburkan dalam latihan dan pampasan guru. Tangani kesesakan dasar bahasa dengan jujur. Atau terus melancarkan pelan induk pendidikan dengan logo kemas sementara pelajar Malaysia semakin ketinggalan berbanding rakan serantau mereka — dan tertanya-tanya mengapa penghijrahan otak semakin pantas.

Sumber: CNA | FMT

item.title: Malaysia's PISA Scores Decline Most Among Southeast Asian Countries — Anwar Says 'Cannot Be in Denial'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2024-01-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s 2022 PISA results — measuring education quality among 15-year-olds — showed the biggest decline among all Southeast Asian countries tested. Scores dropped across mathematics, science, and reading. Anwar responded by saying Malaysia “must know the state of our affairs and cannot be in a state of denial.” The Education Minister admitted the system “failed both teachers and students.” CNA reported that politics — particularly the tension between Malay language requirements and English proficiency — is behind the difficulty of education reform. Three years later, experts say nothing has changed on the ground.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Education determines a nation’s future. Malaysia’s education system is failing — and the government knows it. PISA results have stagnated for three consecutive assessment cycles while regional competitors like Vietnam and Indonesia have improved. FMT observed: “Every few years, the ministry launches a new education blueprint. It’s got a sleek logo and pages of jargon. But on the ground, nothing changes.” The gap between Malaysia and Singapore in mathematics is now equivalent to approximately 8 years of learning. A generation of Malaysian students is being left behind by a system that prioritises political considerations over educational outcomes.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: “Cannot be in denial” is an admission, not a policy. Anwar acknowledged the problem in January 2024. It is now March 2026. Where are the results? The politics of language, religion, and race make education reform nearly impossible in Malaysia’s coalition system — and PH has shown zero willingness to confront those politics. Mandatory Malay and History requirements across all education systems were expanded rather than reformed. UUCA — which restricts student political participation — remains intact despite being a manifesto promise. The education system is failing because fixing it requires confronting entrenched interests that PH depends on for political survival.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s education decline is not new. PISA scores have been mediocre since Malaysia first participated in 2009. Every education minister launches a new blueprint: the National Education Blueprint 2013-2025, various STEM initiatives, TVET expansions. Each generates reports and press conferences. None generates measurable improvement in student outcomes. The fundamental problem — a system designed around political and ethnic considerations rather than educational excellence — remains untouched because no government has been willing to pay the political cost of reforming it.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You said Malaysia “cannot be in denial.” Then you stayed in denial. PISA scores are declining. The education gap with Singapore is 8 years of learning. Your response was to acknowledge the problem and do nothing about it. Reform the curriculum to prioritise critical thinking over rote memorisation. Invest in teacher training and compensation. Address the language policy bottleneck honestly. Or keep launching education blueprints with sleek logos while Malaysian students fall further behind their regional peers — and wonder why the brain drain accelerates.

Sources: CNA | FMT

item.title: [BM] Amnesty International 2024: Anwar 'Mengkhianati Janji Reformasi Yang Dibuat Kepada Pengundi Malaysia'
item.description:
Said by: Amnesty International / Shayna Bauchner

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Januari 2024, laporan tahunan Amnesty International menyatakan bahawa Anwar Ibrahim telah “mengkhianati janji reformasi yang dibuatnya kepada pengundi Malaysia supaya beliau boleh mengekalkan gabungannya.” Laporan itu, ditulis oleh penyelidik Shayna Bauchner, mengenal pasti tujuh bidang di mana Malaysia gagal memenuhi tanggungjawab hak asasi manusianya, termasuk kebebasan bersuara, kebebasan berhimpun, kebebasan media, hak pendatang, hak LGBTQ+, dan hukuman mati. Amnesty menyatakan bahawa kerajaan bukan sahaja gagal memajukan reformasi tetapi secara aktif menggunakan undang-undang menindas yang dijanjikan untuk dimansuhkan.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Apabila Amnesty International — pertubuhan hak asasi manusia terbesar dunia — menggunakan perkataan “mengkhianati,” ia bukan membuat kritikan biasa. Ia mendokumentasikan kerajaan yang secara eksplisit menjanjikan reformasi tertentu, menerima undi atas dasar itu, dan kemudian berpatah balik. Pilihan perkataan itu penting: “terbantut” membawa makna kelewatan; “mengkhianati” membawa makna niat. Amnesty mengatakan bahawa peninggalan reformasi oleh PH bukan disebabkan kekangan atau kerumitan — ia adalah pilihan sengaja untuk mengutamakan kestabilan gabungan berbanding komitmen hak asasi manusia.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Pembelaan PH — “kekangan gabungan memerlukan kompromi” — adalah tepat-tepat hujah yang digunakan BN selama berdekad. UMNO berkata ia tidak boleh mereformasi kerana rakan gabungan. PAS berkata ia tidak boleh bersederhana kerana asasnya. Kini PH berkata ia tidak boleh menyampaikan hak kerana UMNO ada dalam gabungan. Alasan itu serupa; hanya nama parti yang bertukar. Jika anda tahu UMNO akan menyekat reformasi apabila anda membentuk gabungan, maka janji manifesto anda adalah palsu dengan sengaja. Jika anda tidak menjangkakannya, anda tidak cekap. Walau bagaimanapun, penggunaan “mengkhianati” oleh Amnesty adalah tepat.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Amnesty telah mendokumentasikan kegagalan hak asasi manusia Malaysia merentas setiap pentadbiran. Di bawah Mahathir 1.0, tumpuan adalah penahanan ISA dan tindakan keras terhadap perbezaan pendapat. Di bawah Najib, ia adalah penindasan berkaitan 1MDB dan Akta Hasutan. Di bawah PH 1.0, Amnesty mencatatkan kemajuan separa sebelum Langkah Sheraton. Di bawah PH 2.0, keputusan adalah yang paling keras — kerana jurang antara janji dan penyampaian tidak pernah lebih lebar. PH membuat komitmen hak asasi manusia paling bercita-cita tinggi dalam sejarah Malaysia, dan memungkirinya secara lebih menyeluruh daripada mana-mana pendahulu.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Amnesty International mengatakan anda “mengkhianati” pengundi anda. Bukan “mengecewakan.” Bukan “tidak memenuhi.” Mengkhianati. Perkataan itu akan mengikut kerajaan ini ke setiap forum antarabangsa, setiap pertemuan diplomatik, dan setiap pilihan raya masa depan. Anda masih boleh mengubah trajektori: mansuhkan Akta Hasutan, lindungi hak berhimpun, jamin kebebasan akhbar, dan berhenti menggunakan undang-undang menindas terhadap pengkritik. Atau biarkan “mengkhianati” menjadi ringkasan satu perkataan era Anwar — kerana sekarang, Amnesty dan HRW bersetuju bahawa ia memang begitu.

Sumber: Amnesty International | Malaysiakini

item.title: [BM] HRW 2024: 'Agenda Reformasi Terbantut' — Kerajaan Gagal Tunaikan Janji Dalam Tahun Pertama
item.description:
Said by: Human Rights Watch

Category: Social

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Januari 2024, Human Rights Watch mengeluarkan Laporan Dunia 2024, memberikan keputusan yang dahsyat terhadap tahun pertama Anwar Ibrahim berkuasa: “Agenda Reformasi Terbantut.” HRW menyatakan bahawa Anwar telah “sebahagian besarnya gagal menunaikan janjinya untuk menangani penindasan dan rasuah” dan menyeru kerajaan “mengembalikan komitmen kepada agenda yang menghormati hak dengan menukarkan janji kempen yang diketepikan menjadi tindakan.” Laporan itu mendokumentasikan kegagalan merentas kebebasan bersuara, perhimpunan, kebebasan media, dan reformasi institusi. Setahun kemudian, penilaian HRW 2025 lebih keras lagi: “Kemunduran Hak.”

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Dua tahun berturut-turut kecaman HRW bukan lekuk di jalan — ia adalah trajektori. Tahun 1: terbantut. Tahun 2: kemunduran. Tahun 3: kawalan internet yang diperluaskan, 90+ tangkapan hasutan, dan tangkapan wartawan. Apabila pertubuhan hak asasi manusia paling terkenal dunia mengatakan agenda reformasi anda telah terbantut — dan kemudian mengatakan ia secara aktif mundur — masalahnya bukan persepsi. Ia dasar. PH dipilih berdasarkan mandat reformasi. Komuniti hak asasi manusia antarabangsa mendokumentasikan, dalam masa nyata, peninggalan mandat itu.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Respons PH terhadap penilaian HRW boleh diramal: “Mereka tidak memahami konteks kami.” “Kekangan gabungan.” “Kami perlukan masa.” Tetapi HRW memberikan PH setahun sebelum mengeluarkan keputusan “terbantut.” Kemudian setahun lagi sebelum meningkatkan kepada “kemunduran.” Pertubuhan itu tidak tidak sabar — ia mendokumentasikan kerajaan yang menjanjikan reformasi dan menyampaikan kemunduran. Akta Hasutan bukan sekadar dibiarkan dalam buku — ia diperluaskan. Kebebasan media bukan sekadar diabaikan — wartawan ditangkap. Hak berhimpun bukan sekadar tidak dilindungi — perhimpunan Bersih ditindas. Ini bukan terbantut. Ini pembalikan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: HRW telah mengkritik setiap kerajaan Malaysia. Di bawah Najib, penilaian tertumpu pada 1MDB, undang-undang menindas, dan tindakan keras terhadap masyarakat sivil. Di bawah Muhyiddin dan Ismail Sabri, tumpuan adalah sekatan era pandemik dan kemunduran demokrasi. Tetapi PH sepatutnya berbeza — ia adalah kerajaan yang cadangan HRW sejajar dengannya. Apabila pertubuhan hak asasi manusia yang sepatutnya menjadi sekutu semula jadi anda mengatakan anda telah terbantut dan mundur, masalahnya bukan pengkritik. Ia kerajaan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Human Rights Watch memberikan anda setahun. Kemudian setahun lagi. Keputusan menjadi lebih teruk, bukan lebih baik. “Terbantut” menjadi “kemunduran.” Jika trajektori berterusan, Tahun 4 akan menjadi “kemerosotan.” Laksanakan reformasi yang dikenal pasti HRW: mansuhkan Akta Hasutan, kukuhkan perlindungan kebebasan media, jamin hak berhimpun, dan pulihkan kebebasan SPRM. Atau terus menolak kritikan antarabangsa sebagai “tidak memahami konteks” — sementara konteksnya ialah anda melakukan tepat apa yang BN lakukan, dengan bahasa Inggeris yang lebih baik.

Sumber: HRW

item.title: Amnesty International 2024: Anwar 'Betrayed the Reform Pledges He Made to Malaysian Voters'
item.description:
Said by: Amnesty International / Shayna Bauchner

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2024, Amnesty International’s annual report stated that Anwar Ibrahim had “betrayed the reform pledges he made to Malaysian voters so he could hold his coalition together.” The report, authored by researcher Shayna Bauchner, identified seven areas where Malaysia fell short of its human rights obligations, including freedom of expression, freedom of assembly, media freedom, migrant rights, LGBTQ+ rights, and the death penalty. Amnesty noted that the government had not only failed to advance reforms but had actively used repressive laws it had promised to repeal.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When Amnesty International — the world’s largest human rights organisation — uses the word “betrayed,” it is not making a casual criticism. It is documenting a government that explicitly promised specific reforms, received votes on that basis, and then reversed course. The word choice matters: “stalled” implies delay; “betrayed” implies intent. Amnesty is saying that PH’s abandonment of reform was not due to constraints or complexity — it was a deliberate choice to prioritise coalition stability over human rights commitments.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s defence — “coalition constraints require compromise” — is exactly the argument BN used for decades. UMNO said it couldn’t reform because of coalition partners. PAS said it couldn’t moderate because of its base. Now PH says it can’t deliver rights because UMNO is in the coalition. The excuse is identical; only the party name has changed. If you knew UMNO would block reforms when you formed the coalition, then your manifesto promises were knowingly false. If you didn’t anticipate it, you’re incompetent. Either way, Amnesty’s use of “betrayed” is accurate.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Amnesty has documented Malaysian human rights failures across every administration. Under Mahathir 1.0, the focus was ISA detentions and crackdowns on dissent. Under Najib, it was 1MDB-related repression and the Sedition Act. Under PH 1.0, Amnesty noted partial progress before the Sheraton Move. Under PH 2.0, the verdict is the harshest yet — because the gap between promise and delivery has never been wider. PH made the most ambitious human rights commitments in Malaysian history, and broke them more comprehensively than any predecessor.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Amnesty International says you “betrayed” your voters. Not “disappointed.” Not “fell short.” Betrayed. That word will follow this government into every international forum, every diplomatic meeting, and every future election. You can still change the trajectory: repeal the Sedition Act, protect assembly rights, guarantee press freedom, and stop using repressive laws against critics. Or let “betrayed” become the one-word summary of the Anwar era — because right now, Amnesty and HRW agree that it is.

Sources: Amnesty International | Malaysiakini

item.title: HRW 2024: 'Reform Agenda Stalled' — Government Failed to Uphold Pledges in First Year
item.description:
Said by: Human Rights Watch

Category: Social

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2024, Human Rights Watch released its 2024 World Report, delivering a damning verdict on Anwar Ibrahim’s first year in office: “Reform Agenda Stalled.” HRW stated that Anwar had “largely failed to uphold his pledges to address repression and corruption” and called on the government to “recommit to a rights-respecting agenda by turning its sidelined campaign promises into action.” The report documented failures across freedom of expression, assembly, media freedom, and institutional reform. A year later, HRW’s 2025 assessment was even harsher: “Rights Backslide.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Two consecutive years of HRW condemnation is not a bump in the road — it is a trajectory. Year 1: stalled. Year 2: backslide. Year 3: expanded internet controls, 90+ sedition arrests, and journalist arrests. When the world’s most prominent human rights organisation says your reform agenda has stalled — and then says it’s actively going backwards — the problem is not perception. It is policy. PH was elected on a reform mandate. The international human rights community is documenting, in real time, the abandonment of that mandate.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s response to HRW’s assessment was predictable: “They don’t understand our context.” “Coalition constraints.” “We need time.” But HRW gave PH a year before issuing the “stalled” verdict. Then another year before escalating to “backslide.” The organisation is not being impatient — it is documenting a government that promised reform and delivered regression. The Sedition Act was not just left on the books — it was expanded. Media freedom was not just neglected — journalists were arrested. Assembly rights were not just unprotected — Bersih rallies were suppressed. This is not stalling. This is reversal.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: HRW has criticised every Malaysian government. Under Najib, the assessments focused on 1MDB, repressive laws, and crackdowns on civil society. Under Muhyiddin and Ismail Sabri, the focus was pandemic-era restrictions and democratic backsliding. But PH was supposed to be different — it was the government that HRW’s recommendations aligned with. When the human rights organisation that should be your natural ally says you’ve stalled and backslid, the problem is not the critic. It’s the government.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Human Rights Watch gave you a year. Then another year. The verdict got worse, not better. “Stalled” became “backslide.” If the trajectory continues, Year 4 will be “regression.” Implement the reforms HRW identified: repeal the Sedition Act, strengthen media freedom protections, guarantee assembly rights, and restore MACC independence. Or keep dismissing international criticism as “not understanding context” — while the context is that you’re doing exactly what BN did, with better English.

Sources: HRW

item.title: [BM] Pangkalan Data PADU Dilancar Sebagai Mimpi Ngeri Privasi — Sesiapa Dengan Nombor IC Anda Boleh Akses Data Anda
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2024-01-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 2 Januari 2024, Anwar melancarkan Hab Pangkalan Data Pusat (PADU) — sistem yang bertujuan merevolusikan penyampaian subsidi dengan menyasarkan bantuan kepada mereka yang memerlukan. Lebih 233,000 pengguna mendaftar pada hari pelancaran. Dalam 48 jam, penyelidik keselamatan mendapati pengesahan itu sangat lemah: sesiapa yang mempunyai nombor IC dan poskod rumah orang lain boleh mendaftar dan mengakses data mereka. CybersecurityAsia memanggilnya “mimpi ngeri privasi.” Sarawak menghentikan penyertaannya sepenuhnya pada Mac 2024, memetik kebimbangan mengenai autonomi negeri dan data peribadi rakyat. Menjelang pertengahan 2025, PADU senyap-senyap diketepikan — reformasi subsidi RON95 langsung tidak menggunakannya, menggunakan data pendaftaran kenderaan JPJ sebaliknya.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Kerajaan melancarkan pangkalan data kebangsaan dengan pengesahan yang sangat lemah sehingga ia pada dasarnya akses terbuka. Nombor IC bukan rahsia — ia muncul pada setiap borang kerajaan dan kerap bocor dalam pelanggaran data. Menggunakannya sebagai faktor pengesahan tunggal untuk pangkalan data yang mengandungi data pendapatan, isi rumah, dan pekerjaan bukan kecacatan reka bentuk — ia adalah kegagalan sistemik tadbir urus digital. ISEAS menyatakan PADU “mencetuskan ribut perdebatan mengenai isu privasi, federalisme, dan sifat kontrak sosial antara rakyat dan negara.” Apabila Sarawak enggan menyertai dan membina sistemnya sendiri, mesej tersirat itu dahsyat: kami tidak mempercayai anda dengan data rakyat kami.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: PADU sepatutnya menjadi tulang belakang keseluruhan agenda reformasi subsidi Malaysia. Kerajaan menghabiskan bertahun berhujah bahawa subsidi sejagat perlu digantikan dengan yang bersasar, dan PADU adalah mekanismenya. Apabila ujian terbesar tiba — reformasi RON95 — PADU langsung tidak digunakan. Berbilion dibelanjakan membina sistem yang ditinggalkan tanpa pengakuan atau akauntabiliti awam. Tiada bedah siasat diterbitkan. Tiada akauntabiliti diberikan. Tiada perakaunan kos disediakan. Inilah yang berlaku apabila dasar didorong oleh pengumuman bukannya pelaksanaan — PADU menghasilkan tajuk utama yang hebat pada hari pelancaran, dan hakikat bahawa ia tidak selamat, tidak lengkap, dan akhirnya tidak boleh digunakan dilayan sebagai masalah yang semua orang lupakan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Rekod IT kerajaan Malaysia adalah kubur kegagalan yang mahal. Sistem MyKad telah dibelenggu isu data selama bertahun. Aplikasi COVID MySejahtera dibelitkan kontroversi pemilikan dan privasi data. Laman web kerajaan kerap digodam atau dirosakkan. Setiap projek mengikut corak yang sama: lancar dengan gembar-gembur, temui kecacatan, tampung secara reaktif, dan akhirnya tinggalkan. PADU adalah iterasi paling mahal kitaran ini — pangkalan data kebangsaan yang dibina tanpa keselamatan yang mencukupi, dilancarkan tanpa ujian yang mencukupi, dan diketepikan tanpa penjelasan yang mencukupi.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda membelanjakan ratusan juta membina pangkalan data kebangsaan. Ia dilancarkan dengan keselamatan yang sangat lemah sehingga sesiapa boleh mengakses data sesiapa. Sarawak enggan menyertai. Dan apabila reformasi subsidi terbesar anda tiba, anda langsung tidak menggunakannya. Terbitkan jumlah kos PADU. Jelaskan mengapa ia diketepikan. Jalankan bedah siasat awam mengenai apa yang salah. Atau terus melancarkan inisiatif digital yang menjadi tajuk utama yang berkos berbilion dan tidak menyampaikan apa-apa — sementara data peribadi rakyat duduk dalam pangkalan data yang tidak mampu mengesahkan siapa yang mengaksesnya.

Sumber: CybersecurityAsia | ISEAS/Fulcrum | Malay Mail

item.title: PADU Database Launches as Privacy Nightmare — Anyone With Your IC Number Can Access Your Data
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2024-01-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 2 January 2024, Anwar launched the Central Database Hub (PADU) — a system meant to revolutionise subsidy delivery by targeting aid to those who need it. Over 233,000 users registered on launch day. Within 48 hours, security researchers discovered the authentication was laughably weak: anyone armed with another person’s IC number and home postcode could register and access their data. CybersecurityAsia called it a “privacy nightmare.” Sarawak halted its participation entirely in March 2024, citing concerns over state autonomy and citizens’ personal data. By mid-2025, PADU was quietly sidelined — the RON95 subsidy reform bypassed it entirely, using JPJ vehicle registration data instead.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The government launched a national database with authentication so weak it was effectively open access. IC numbers are not secrets — they appear on every government form and are routinely leaked in data breaches. Using them as the sole authentication factor for a database containing income, household, and employment data is not a design flaw — it is a systemic failure of digital governance. ISEAS noted that PADU “ignited a firestorm of debate over issues of privacy, federalism, and the very nature of the social contract between citizens and the state.” When Sarawak refused to participate and built its own system, the implied message was devastating: we don’t trust you with our citizens’ data.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PADU was meant to be the backbone of Malaysia’s entire subsidy reform agenda. The government spent years arguing that blanket subsidies needed to be replaced with targeted ones, and PADU was the mechanism. When the biggest test came — RON95 reform — PADU was bypassed entirely. Billions were spent building a system that was abandoned without public acknowledgment or accountability. No postmortem was published. No accountability was assigned. No cost accounting was provided. This is what happens when policy is announcement-driven rather than implementation-driven — PADU made a great headline on launch day, and the fact that it was insecure, incomplete, and ultimately unusable was treated as everyone’s forgotten problem.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia’s government IT track record is a graveyard of expensive failures. The MyKad system has been plagued by data issues for years. The MySejahtera COVID app was mired in ownership and data privacy controversies. Government websites are routinely hacked or defaced. Each project follows the same pattern: launch with fanfare, discover flaws, patch reactively, and eventually abandon. PADU is the most expensive iteration of this cycle — a national database built without adequate security, launched without adequate testing, and shelved without adequate explanation.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You spent hundreds of millions building a national database. It launched with security so weak anyone could access anyone’s data. Sarawak refused to participate. And when your biggest subsidy reform came, you didn’t even use it. Publish the total cost of PADU. Explain why it was sidelined. Conduct a public postmortem on what went wrong. Or keep launching headline-grabbing digital initiatives that cost billions and deliver nothing — while the rakyat’s personal data sits in a database that couldn’t even verify who was accessing it.

Sources: CybersecurityAsia | ISEAS/Fulcrum | Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Semakan Penyelesaian Goldman Sachs 1MDB — Anwar Ugut Saman, Tiada Apa Berlaku
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2023-08-21

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Ogos 2023, Anwar Ibrahim memanggil penyelesaian Goldman Sachs 1MDB bernilai $3.9 bilion “dilakukan secara tergesa-gesa” di bawah kerajaan terdahulu dan mengugut untuk membawa Goldman ke mahkamah bagi pemulihan yang lebih besar. Beliau berikrar Malaysia akan “memulihkan setiap sen.” Goldman dilaporkan membuat “tawaran baru” sebagai respons. Bertahun kemudian, semakan itu tidak menghasilkan apa-apa yang konkrit — tiada saman, tiada rundingan semula, tiada pemulihan tambahan. CNA mendedahkan butiran yang mencadangkan perjanjian penyelesaian sulit mungkin merugikan Malaysia, dengan Goldman membayar sebahagian kecil daripada kerugian yang difasilitasinya.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Goldman Sachs membantu mengatur $6.5 bilion dalam bon untuk 1MDB, memperoleh ratusan juta dalam yuran sementara berbilion dicuri. Penyelesaian $3.9 bilion — dirundingkan di bawah Muhyiddin — dikritik sebagai tidak mencukupi memandangkan skala peranan Goldman. Janji Anwar untuk menyemak penyelesaian dan memulihkan lebih banyak menaikkan jangkaan. Tetapi janji tanpa tindakan susulan hanyalah persembahan. Jika penyelesaian itu “dilakukan secara tergesa-gesa” dan merugikan Malaysia, mengapa Anwar tidak memfailkan saman dalam tiga tahun? Jawapannya sama ada kes undang-undang lebih lemah daripada yang didakwa, atau leveraj politik Goldman lebih kuat daripada keazaman Anwar.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Mengugut Goldman Sachs dengan saman menghasilkan tajuk utama yang hebat. Sebenarnya menyaman Goldman Sachs memerlukan sumber, kehendak politik, dan kesediaan untuk menentang institusi Wall Street yang berkuasa dengan kepentingan perniagaan yang luas di Malaysia. Anwar memilih tajuk utama berbanding saman. Tiga tahun “semakan” tanpa hasil bukan usaha wajar — ia penangguhan tanpa had. Rakyat masih membayar hutang 1MDB — RM50 bilion dalam buku — sementara bank yang memudahkan kecurian berlalu dengan penyelesaian yang Anwar sendiri panggil tidak mencukupi.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Penyelesaian Goldman dirundingkan di bawah kerajaan Muhyiddin pada 2020 — pakej $3.9 bilion termasuk $2.5 bilion tunai dan jaminan $1.4 bilion terhadap pemulihan aset 1MDB. PH 1.0 di bawah Mahathir telah memulakan prosiding undang-undang terhadap Goldman tetapi disingkirkan sebelum selesai. Kerajaan Langkah Sheraton menyelesaikan. Anwar berjanji untuk membuka semula kes. Tiga pentadbiran kemudian, liabiliti Goldman telah dihadkan sementara hutang Malaysia terus berkompaun.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda memanggil penyelesaian Goldman “tergesa-gesa” dan berjanji memulihkan “setiap sen.” Itu tiga tahun lalu. Mana saman? Mana rundingan semula? Mana pemulihan tambahan? Goldman memudahkan kecurian berbilion daripada Malaysia. Rakyat masih membayar hutangnya. Sama ada failkan saman atau jelaskan mengapa anda tidak boleh. Tetapi berhenti mengugut tindakan yang anda tiada niat untuk mengambil — kerana setiap hari anda berlengah, peguam Goldman semakin yakin ugutan anda kosong.

Sumber: Reuters | CNBC | CNA

item.title: Goldman Sachs 1MDB Settlement Review — Anwar Threatens Lawsuits, Nothing Happens
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2023-08-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In August 2023, Anwar Ibrahim called the $3.9 billion Goldman Sachs 1MDB settlement “done hastily” under the previous government and threatened to take Goldman to court for a larger recovery. He vowed Malaysia would “recover every single sen.” Goldman reportedly made “fresh overtures” in response. Years later, the review has produced nothing concrete — no lawsuit, no renegotiation, no additional recovery. CNA uncovered details suggesting the confidential settlement agreement may have shortchanged Malaysia, with Goldman paying a fraction of the losses it facilitated.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Goldman Sachs helped arrange $6.5 billion in bonds for 1MDB, earning hundreds of millions in fees while billions were stolen. The $3.9 billion settlement — negotiated under Muhyiddin — was criticised as inadequate given the scale of Goldman’s role. Anwar’s promise to review the settlement and recover more raised expectations. But promises without follow-through are just performance. If the settlement was “done hastily” and shortchanged Malaysia, why hasn’t Anwar filed suit in three years? The answer is either that the legal case is weaker than claimed, or that Goldman’s political leverage is stronger than Anwar’s resolve.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Threatening Goldman Sachs with lawsuits makes for great headlines. Actually suing Goldman Sachs requires resources, political will, and a willingness to antagonise a powerful Wall Street institution with extensive business interests in Malaysia. Anwar chose the headline over the lawsuit. Three years of “review” with no outcome is not due diligence — it’s indefinite deferral. The rakyat is still paying the 1MDB debt — RM50 billion on the books — while the bank that facilitated the theft walks away with a settlement that Anwar himself called inadequate.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The Goldman settlement was negotiated under Muhyiddin’s government in 2020 — a $3.9 billion package including $2.5 billion in cash and a $1.4 billion guarantee on 1MDB asset recovery. PH 1.0 under Mahathir had initiated legal proceedings against Goldman but was removed before completion. The Sheraton Move government settled. Anwar promised to reopen the case. Three administrations later, Goldman’s liability has been capped while Malaysia’s debt continues to compound.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You called the Goldman settlement “hasty” and promised to recover “every single sen.” That was three years ago. Where’s the lawsuit? Where’s the renegotiation? Where’s the additional recovery? Goldman facilitated the theft of billions from Malaysia. The rakyat is still paying the debt. Either file suit or explain why you can’t. But stop threatening action you have no intention of taking — because every day you delay, Goldman’s lawyers grow more confident that your threats were empty.

Sources: Reuters | CNBC | CNA

item.title: [BM] Skandal Gerbang Maritim Selangor RM10 Bilion — Laporan SPRM Difailkan Soal Urus Niaga Tanah Dan Saham
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / Chegubard / MACC

Category: Economy

Date: 2023-08-07

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Aktivis Badrul Hisham Shaharin (Chegubard) memfailkan laporan SPRM pada Ogos 2023 mengenai dakwaan penyelewengan RM10 bilion dalam projek Gerbang Maritim Selangor — pembangunan sepanjang 56km di sepanjang Sungai Selangor. Projek itu dilaporkan melibatkan penjualan saham majoriti dan ratusan ekar tanah milik kerajaan kepada syarikat swasta yang dikaitkan dengan ahli perniagaan Vincent Tan. Amirudin menghantar surat tuntutan kepada MB Kedah Sanusi Md Nor kerana mengulas skandal itu secara terbuka.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: RM10 bilion dalam dakwaan penyelewengan melibatkan pemindahan tanah negeri dan penjualan saham bukan isu tadbir urus kecil — ia adalah jenis skandal yang menamatkan kerjaya politik dalam demokrasi yang berfungsi. Gerbang Maritim Selangor adalah projek infrastruktur terbesar negeri, dan dakwaan bahawa tanah kerajaan dijual kepada entiti swasta di bawah nilai pasaran sementara saham dipindahkan dalam keadaan yang dipersoalkan menyentuh nadi kepercayaan awam.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Respons Amirudin terhadap dakwaan RM10 bilion bukan membuka siasatan atau menuntut audit awam — ia menghantar surat guaman kepada ahli politik pembangkang yang bercakap mengenainya. Itu memberitahu anda segala-galanya mengenai pendekatan PH terhadap akauntabiliti: diamkan pengkritik, abaikan substansi. Jika seseorang menuduh anda menyalah urus RM10 bilion aset negeri, respons yang betul adalah audit bebas, bukan surat peguam.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Ini adalah sebahagian daripada corak kebimbangan pengurusan aset Selangor yang lebih luas di bawah Amirudin — dari rasuah perlombongan pasir kepada siasatan MBI kepada siasatan dana warga emas SMUE. Setiap kes melibatkan sumber negeri, setiap satu melibatkan SPRM, dan setiap satu disambut dengan respons yang sama: nafi, alih perhatian, ugut tindakan undang-undang.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: RM10 bilion aset negeri. Tanah kerajaan dijual kepada kepentingan swasta. Saham dipindahkan. Dan respons anda ialah menyaman orang yang bercakap mengenainya. Jika urusan itu bersih, terbitkan kontrak, penilaian, dan dokumen kelulusan. Jika ia tidak bersih, siasat. Tetapi mengugut saman fitnah terhadap pengkritik sementara SPRM menyiasat negeri anda bukan reformasi — ia penyembunyian dengan langkah tambahan.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow

item.title: RM10 Billion Selangor Maritime Gateway Scandal — MACC Report Filed Over Land and Shares Deal
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / Chegubard / MACC

Category: Economy

Date: 2023-08-07

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin (Chegubard) lodged a MACC report in August 2023 over alleged RM10 billion irregularities in the Selangor Maritime Gateway project — a 56km development along the Selangor River. The project reportedly involved the sale of majority shares and hundreds of acres of government-owned land to a private company linked to businessman Vincent Tan. Amirudin sent a letter of demand to Kedah MB Sanusi Md Nor for commenting on the scandal publicly.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: RM10 billion in alleged irregularities involving state land transfers and share sales is not a minor governance issue — it’s the kind of scandal that ends political careers in functional democracies. The Selangor Maritime Gateway is the state’s largest infrastructure project, and allegations that government land was sold to a private entity below market value while shares were transferred under questionable circumstances strike at the heart of public trust.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Amirudin’s response to the RM10 billion allegation was not to open an investigation or demand a public audit — it was to send a legal letter to the opposition politician who talked about it. That tells you everything about PH’s approach to accountability: silence the critics, ignore the substance. If someone accuses you of mishandling RM10 billion in state assets, the correct response is an independent audit, not a lawyer’s letter.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This is part of a broader pattern of Selangor asset management concerns under Amirudin — from sand mining corruption to MBI investigations to the SMUE elderly fund probe. Each case involves state resources, each involves MACC, and each is met with the same response: deny, deflect, threaten legal action.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: RM10 billion in state assets. Government land sold to private interests. Shares transferred. And your response was to sue the person who talked about it. If the deal is clean, publish the contracts, the valuations, and the approval documents. If it’s not clean, investigate. But threatening defamation suits against critics while MACC investigates your state is not reform — it’s cover-up with extra steps.

Sources: MalaysiaNow

item.title: [BM] Hukuman Mati Masih Kekal Untuk 27 Kesalahan — 'Pemansuhan' Hanya Separuh Jalan
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration

Category: Social

Date: 2023-07-04

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 4 Julai 2023, PH menggembar-gemburkan “pemansuhan” hukuman mati mandatori — menggantikannya dengan hukuman budi bicara untuk kesalahan berat. Walau bagaimanapun, hukuman mati itu sendiri masih kekal untuk 27 kesalahan, termasuk pengedaran dadah, pembunuhan, dan kesalahan senjata api. Hakim kini mempunyai budi bicara untuk menjatuhkan hukuman mati atau hukuman alternatif, tetapi tiang gantung masih tersedia. Amnesty International menyeru pemansuhan penuh. Pada akhir 2025, Malaysia mengumumkan “kajian” mengenai pemansuhan penuh bermula Januari 2026 — tiga tahun selepas reformasi awal.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: PH membingkaikan reformasi hukuman mati mandatori sebagai pencapaian hak asasi manusia yang bersejarah. Pada hakikatnya, ia adalah langkah separuh. Menghapuskan perkataan “mandatori” tidak memansuhkan hukuman mati — ia memindahkan keputusan daripada statut kepada budi bicara kehakiman. Hakim dalam sistem mahkamah Malaysia, yang PH sendiri telah mengkritik kerana campur tangan politik, kini menentukan siapa yang hidup dan siapa yang mati. Bagi kerajaan yang mendakwa memperjuangkan hak asasi manusia, mengekalkan hukuman mati untuk 27 kesalahan sambil mengumumkan “kajian” mengenai pemansuhan — tiga tahun selepas reformasi — bukan kemajuan. Ia penangguhan yang dijenamakan sebagai prinsip.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: “Kajian” yang diumumkan untuk Januari 2026 adalah taktik penangguhan lalai kerajaan Malaysia: umumkan kajian, bentuk jawatankuasa, tunggu kitaran berita bertukar, dan tidak pernah melaksanakan penemuan. Jika PH percaya pada pemansuhan, ia akan memansuhkan hukuman mati sepenuhnya pada 2023 ketika ia mempunyai bilangan parlimen dan momentum awam. Sebaliknya, ia memilih pilihan yang selamat secara politik — buang “mandatori,” kekalkan hukuman, dan tuntut kredit untuk reformasi. Amnesty International nampak terus. Rakyat juga sepatutnya.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia telah menghukum mati banduan di bawah setiap pentadbiran. Di bawah BN, hukuman mati digunakan terutamanya untuk pengedaran dadah — secara tidak seimbang menjejaskan defendan miskin, terpinggir, dan warga asing. PH 1.0 memulakan perbincangan mengenai pemansuhan tetapi runtuh sebelum bertindak. PH 2.0 mempunyai tiga tahun untuk menyelesaikan kerja. Sebaliknya, ia bersetuju dengan hukuman budi bicara dan “kajian” masa depan. Corak ini konsisten: kerajaan Malaysia mengakui hukuman mati bermasalah, membuat perubahan bertahap, dan tidak pernah menyelesaikan tugas.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda memanggilnya pemansuhan. Ia bukan. Hukuman mati masih kekal untuk 27 kesalahan. Anda mengumumkan “kajian” mengenai pemansuhan penuh — tiga tahun selepas reformasi awal anda. Jika anda perlukan tiga tahun untuk mengkaji sama ada negara patut membunuh warganya, jawapannya sudah tidak. Mansuhkan hukuman mati untuk semua kesalahan. Tukar hukuman mati sedia ada. Berhenti mengkaji dan mula bertindak. Atau akui bahawa “pemansuhan” itu hanya kenyataan akhbar, bukan dasar.

Sumber: Amnesty International | Reuters | Bernama

item.title: Death Penalty Still on Books for 27 Offences — 'Abolition' Was Only Half-Done
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration

Category: Social

Date: 2023-07-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 4 July 2023, PH trumpeted the “abolition” of the mandatory death penalty — replacing it with discretionary sentencing for capital offences. However, the death penalty itself remains on the books for 27 offences, including drug trafficking, murder, and firearms offences. Judges now have discretion to impose death or alternative sentences, but the gallows remain available. Amnesty International called for full abolition. In late 2025, Malaysia announced a “study” on full abolition to begin in January 2026 — three years after the initial reform.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: PH framed the mandatory death penalty reform as a landmark human rights achievement. In reality, it was a half-measure. Removing the word “mandatory” does not abolish the death penalty — it transfers the decision from statute to judicial discretion. Judges in Malaysia’s court system, which PH itself has criticised for political interference, now decide who lives and who dies. For a government that claims to champion human rights, maintaining the death penalty for 27 offences while announcing a “study” on abolition — three years after reform — is not progress. It’s procrastination branded as principle.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The “study” announced for January 2026 is the Malaysian government’s default delaying tactic: announce a study, form a committee, wait for the news cycle to move on, and never implement the findings. If PH believed in abolition, it would have abolished the death penalty entirely in 2023 when it had the parliamentary numbers and the public momentum. Instead, it chose the politically safe option — remove “mandatory,” keep the penalty, and claim credit for reform. Amnesty International saw through it immediately. The rakyat should too.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has executed prisoners under every administration. Under BN, the death penalty was used primarily for drug trafficking — disproportionately affecting poor, marginalised, and foreign defendants. PH 1.0 initiated discussions on abolition but collapsed before acting. PH 2.0 had three years to complete the work. Instead, it settled for discretionary sentencing and a future “study.” The pattern is consistent: Malaysian governments acknowledge the death penalty is problematic, make incremental changes, and never finish the job.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You called it abolition. It wasn’t. The death penalty remains for 27 offences. You announced a “study” on full abolition — three years after your initial reform. If you need three years to study whether the state should kill its citizens, the answer is already no. Abolish the death penalty for all offences. Commute existing death sentences. Stop studying and start acting. Or admit that “abolition” was a press release, not a policy.

Sources: Amnesty International | Reuters | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Anwar Lantik Anak Sendiri Nurul Izzah Sebagai Penasihat Kanan Ekonomi — Kemudian Kata Bukan Nepotisme
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-01-31

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 31 Januari 2023 — dua bulan selepas menjadi perdana menteri — Anwar Ibrahim melantik anaknya Nurul Izzah Anwar sebagai Penasihat Kanan Ekonomi dan Kewangan di Jabatan Perdana Menteri. Ini berlaku selepas Nurul Izzah kalah di kerusi Parlimen Permatang Pauh dalam PRU15 kepada Perikatan Nasional dengan hampir 6,000 undi — kerusi yang merupakan kubu tradisional PKR. Pelantikan itu tanpa gaji, yang digunakan Anwar sebagai pembelaan utamanya. Beliau memberitahu wartawan: “Nepotisme adalah apabila ahli keluarga diberi jawatan untuk menyalahguna kuasa, memperkayakan diri, mendapatkan kontrak dan dibayar sejumlah besar. Ini bukan kesnya.”

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Nepotisme tidak ditakrifkan oleh gaji — ia ditakrifkan oleh akses. Penasihat tanpa gaji kepada Perdana Menteri mempunyai lebih banyak pengaruh institusi daripada kebanyakan penjawat awam bergaji. Peranan itu memberikan kedekatan kepada dasar, kontrak, dan pembuatan keputusan di peringkat tertinggi. Melantik calon yang baru ditolak oleh pengundi ke peranan penasihat yang tidak dipilih adalah tamparan kepada hasil demokrasi. Jika PM boleh melantik ahli keluarga ke peranan penasihat yang tidak dipilih dan memanggilnya boleh diterima, maka setiap PM masa depan boleh melakukan perkara yang sama. Preseden sudah ditetapkan.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Anwar menghabiskan 25 tahun menjenamakan dirinya sebagai penawar kepada politik naungan yang mengakar di Malaysia. Dua bulan selepas berkuasa, beliau melantik anak sendiri. Pembelaan — “ia tanpa gaji, oleh itu bukan nepotisme” — adalah pengelakan buku teks. Adakah terdapat panggilan terbuka? Senarai pendek? Jawatankuasa tapisan? Tidak. Beliau dilantik kerana beliau anak PM, dan setiap rakyat Malaysia tahu ini. Nurul Izzah memegang ijazah dari Johns Hopkins, tetapi kelayakan bukan isu — proses yang menjadi isu. Mahathir melantik ahli keluarga ke jawatan perniagaan. Kroni Najib memperkaya saudara-mara melalui urusan berkaitan 1MDB. Anwar berjanji untuk berbeza. Beliau tidak berbeza.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Dinasti politik adalah endemik dalam politik Malaysia. Anak-anak Mahathir memegang empayar perniagaan yang dibina atas hubungan politik. Anak tiri Najib, Riza Aziz, didakwa dalam skandal 1MDB. Rangkaian naungan UMNO memperkaya keluarga selama berdekad. PH mengkritik semua ini tanpa henti. Pelantikan Nurul Izzah oleh Anwar — dalam minggu-minggu memegang jawatan — mengisyaratkan bahawa buku panduan reformasi mempunyai asterisk: tidak terpakai kepada keluarga PM. Nurul Izzah sejak itu dilantik sebagai timbalan presiden PKR, mengukuhkan dinasti Anwar dalam parti.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda menghabiskan 25 tahun berkhutbah menentang naungan. Anda melantik anak anda dalam 60 hari. Beliau kalah kerusinya dan anda berikan peranan penasihat. Beliau kini timbalan presiden PKR. Ini bukan meritokrasi — ini pembinaan dinasti dengan PR yang lebih baik. Semua pelantikan penasihat PM harus melalui proses tapisan telus. Ahli keluarga PM yang sedang berkhidmat harus secara automatik hilang kelayakan daripada peranan yang tidak dipilih dalam JPM. Akui optiknya daripada mentakrifkan semula nepotisme untuk memenuhi keadaan anda.

Sumber: SCMP | Straits Times | CNA

item.title: Anwar Appoints Own Daughter Nurul Izzah as Senior Economic Adviser — Then Calls It Not Nepotism
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-01-31

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 31 January 2023 — two months into his premiership — Anwar Ibrahim appointed his daughter Nurul Izzah Anwar as Senior Adviser on Economics and Finance in the Prime Minister’s Department. This came after Nurul Izzah lost her Permatang Pauh parliamentary seat in GE15 to Perikatan Nasional by nearly 6,000 votes — a seat that was a traditional PKR stronghold. The appointment was unpaid, which Anwar used as his primary defence. He told reporters: “Nepotism is where a family member is given a position to abuse power, enrich themselves, obtain contracts and get paid a huge sum. This is not the case.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Nepotism is not defined by salary — it is defined by access. An unpaid adviser to the Prime Minister has more institutional influence than most paid civil servants. The role grants proximity to policy, contracts, and decision-making at the highest level. Appointing a candidate who was just rejected by voters to an unelected advisory role is a slap in the face of democratic outcomes. If the PM can appoint family members to unelected advisory roles and call it acceptable, then every future PM can do the same. The precedent is set.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent 25 years branding himself as the antidote to Malaysia’s entrenched patronage politics. Two months into office, he appointed his own daughter. The defence — “it’s unpaid, therefore not nepotism” — is a textbook deflection. Was there an open call? A shortlist? A vetting committee? No. She was appointed because she is the PM’s daughter, and every Malaysian knows it. Nurul Izzah holds degrees from Johns Hopkins, but qualification is not the issue — process is. Mahathir appointed family members to business positions. Najib’s circle enriched relatives through 1MDB-adjacent deals. Anwar promised to be different. He wasn’t.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Political dynasties are endemic in Malaysian politics. Mahathir’s sons held business empires built on political connections. Najib’s stepson Riza Aziz was charged in the 1MDB scandal. UMNO’s patronage networks enriched families for decades. PH criticised all of this relentlessly. Anwar’s appointment of Nurul Izzah — within weeks of taking office — signalled that the reformasi playbook had an asterisk: does not apply to the PM’s family. Nurul Izzah has since been installed as PKR deputy president, consolidating the Anwar dynasty within the party.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You spent 25 years preaching against patronage. You appointed your daughter within 60 days. She lost her seat and you gave her an advisory role. She’s now PKR deputy president. This is not meritocracy — it’s dynasty-building with better PR. All PM advisory appointments should go through a transparent vetting process. Family members of the sitting PM should be automatically disqualified from unelected roles in the PMO. Acknowledge the optics instead of redefining nepotism to suit your situation.

Sources: SCMP | Straits Times | CNA

item.title: [BM] Kos ECRL Membengkak Kepada RM75 Bilion — Anwar Teruskan Juga, Panggil Ia 'Dikurangkan'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2022-12-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Anwar Ibrahim mendedahkan bahawa kos asal Laluan Rel Pantai Timur (ECRL) ialah RM86 bilion dan mendakwa kerajaannya “mengurangkan” ia kepada RM75 bilion. Pembinaan sahaja berjumlah RM50.27 bilion, dengan tambahan RM24.69 bilion dalam kos faedah dan pemerolehan tanah. Projek ini — projek mega yang disokong China menghubungkan pantai timur dan barat Semenanjung Malaysia — dirundingkan semula di bawah kerajaan PH 1.0 Mahathir dan diteruskan di bawah Muhyiddin dan Ismail Sabri. Kerajaan Anwar meneruskan pembinaan, membingkaikan “pengurangan” RM11 bilion sebagai pencapaian fiskal.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: RM75 bilion adalah komitmen infrastruktur tunggal terbesar dalam sejarah Malaysia. Komponen faedah sahaja — RM24.69 bilion — melebihi keseluruhan bajet tahunan kebanyakan kementerian kerajaan. Pengkritik, termasuk ahli ekonomi dan AP pembangkang, mempersoalkan sama ada ECRL akan menjana pulangan ekonomi yang mencukupi untuk mewajarkan kos, menyatakan bahawa laluan itu terutamanya melayani kawasan berkepadatan rendah dengan aktiviti komersial terhad. Projek ini dibiayai melalui pinjaman dari Exim Bank China, menimbulkan kebimbangan kebergantungan hutang pada masa nisbah hutang-kepada-KDNK Malaysia sudah melebihi 64%.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Memanggil projek RM75 bilion “dikurangkan” kerana ia asalnya RM86 bilion adalah seperti meraikan diskaun 13% untuk sesuatu yang anda tidak mampu. Analisis kos-manfaat ECRL tidak pernah diterbitkan sepenuhnya kepada umum. Beban faedah sahaja akan memakan hasil kerajaan selama berdekad. Penciptaan pekerjaan tempatan telah dikritik sebagai minimum — kebanyakan tenaga kerja pembinaan dan kepakaran kejuruteraan datang dari China. Anwar meneruskan projek yang dimulakan oleh Najib, diteruskan oleh setiap PM selepas itu, dan dipersoalkan oleh ahli ekonomi merentas spektrum politik — kerana pembatalan akan merosakkan hubungan China dan mencetuskan klausa penalti. Ini bukan keputusan dasar; ini adalah situasi tebusan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: ECRL pada asalnya ditandatangani oleh Najib pada 2016 sebagai sebahagian daripada Inisiatif Jalur dan Laluan China, pada kos yang melambung yang dilihat secara meluas dikaitkan dengan rundingan penyelamatan 1MDB. Mahathir menggantungnya pada 2018, kemudian merundingkan semula pada kos yang lebih rendah. Muhyiddin menyambung semula pembinaan. Setiap kerajaan mewarisi dan meneruskan projek ini kerana kos tenggelam dan akibat diplomatik pembatalan bertambah dengan setiap tahun yang berlalu. ECRL adalah perangkap fiskal Malaysia — setiap PM menyentuhnya dan tidak boleh melepaskan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda mewarisi projek mega kontroversi yang disokong China daripada Najib, melalui Mahathir, melalui Muhyiddin, melalui Ismail Sabri. Bukannya menjalankan semakan kos-manfaat bebas, anda meneruskan dan memanggil kos “dikurangkan.” Terbitkan analisis kos-manfaat penuh. Dedahkan kadar faedah dan jadual pembayaran balik. Tunjukkan berapa banyak pekerjaan tempatan berbanding pekerja import yang dihasilkan projek ini. Jelaskan bagaimana laluan rel RM75 bilion melalui kawasan berkepadatan rendah akan menjana pulangan yang mewajarkan hutang. Atau akui bahawa anda meneruskan projek ini kerana menghentikannya lebih sukar secara politik daripada membayarnya.

Sumber: Reuters | Nikkei Asia | FMT

item.title: ECRL Cost Balloons to RM75 Billion — Anwar Proceeds Anyway, Calls It 'Reduced'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2022-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Anwar Ibrahim revealed that the original East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) cost was RM86 billion and claimed his government “reduced” it to RM75 billion. Construction alone accounts for RM50.27 billion, with an additional RM24.69 billion in interest and land acquisition costs. The project — a China-backed megaproject connecting the east and west coasts of Peninsular Malaysia — was renegotiated under Mahathir’s PH 1.0 government and continued under Muhyiddin and Ismail Sabri. Anwar’s government proceeded with construction, framing the RM11 billion “reduction” as a fiscal achievement.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: RM75 billion is the single largest infrastructure commitment in Malaysian history. The interest component alone — RM24.69 billion — exceeds the entire annual budget of most government ministries. Critics, including economists and opposition MPs, have questioned whether the ECRL will generate sufficient economic returns to justify the cost, noting that the route primarily serves low-density areas with limited commercial activity. The project is funded through a loan from China’s Exim Bank, raising debt dependency concerns at a time when Malaysia’s debt-to-GDP ratio already exceeds 64%.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Calling a RM75 billion project “reduced” because it was originally RM86 billion is like celebrating a 13% discount on something you can’t afford. The ECRL’s cost-benefit analysis has never been publicly released in full. The interest burden alone will consume government revenue for decades. Local job creation has been criticised as minimal — much of the construction workforce and engineering expertise comes from China. Anwar is proceeding with a project initiated by Najib, continued by every subsequent PM, and questioned by economists across the political spectrum — because cancellation would damage the China relationship and trigger penalty clauses. This isn’t a policy decision; it’s a hostage situation.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The ECRL was originally signed by Najib in 2016 as part of China’s Belt and Road Initiative, at an inflated cost that was widely seen as linked to 1MDB bailout negotiations. Mahathir suspended it in 2018, then renegotiated at a lower cost. Muhyiddin resumed construction. Each government has inherited and continued the project because the sunk costs and diplomatic consequences of cancellation grow with each passing year. The ECRL is Malaysia’s fiscal tar baby — every PM touches it and can’t let go.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You inherited a controversial China-backed megaproject from Najib, via Mahathir, via Muhyiddin, via Ismail Sabri. Instead of conducting an independent cost-benefit review, you proceeded and called the cost “reduced.” Publish the full cost-benefit analysis. Disclose the interest rate and repayment schedule. Show how many local jobs versus imported workers the project creates. Explain how a RM75 billion rail line through low-density areas will generate returns that justify the debt. Or admit that you’re continuing the project because stopping it is politically harder than paying for it.

Sources: Reuters | Nikkei Asia | FMT

item.title: [BM] Manifesto PRU15 PH: 60 Janji Dibuat, Majoriti Dimungkiri Atau Ditinggalkan — Penipuan Terbesar Dalam Politik Malaysia
item.description:
Said by: Malaysiakini Manifesto Tracker / Straits Times Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2022-11-19

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Manifesto PRU15 PH — dikeluarkan pada 19 November 2022 — mengandungi kira-kira 60 janji yang boleh diukur. Seperti yang dijejak oleh Penjejak Manifesto Malaysiakini dan dianalisis oleh Straits Times, majoriti telah dimungkiri, ditangguhkan, atau senyap-senyap ditinggalkan. Kegagalan utama termasuk: pemansuhan undang-undang menindas (Akta Hasutan diperluaskan sebaliknya, 90+ tangkapan), pemansuhan tol (kerajaan bayar RM591J untuk bekukan kenaikan), reformasi perkhidmatan awam (bilangan pekerja bertambah), had tempoh PM (rang undang-undang gagal di Parlimen), ratifikasi ICCPR (tidak dilakukan), pemansuhan AUKU (tidak dilakukan), pemisahan PG-PP (dihantar ke jawatankuasa), dan pengukuhan undang-undang antilompat parti (dilemahkan oleh pengecualian).

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Manifesto pilihan raya adalah kontrak antara kerajaan dan pengundi. Apabila majoriti janji dimungkiri, kontrak sosial runtuh. PH tidak kehilangan janji-janji ini kerana keadaan luaran — mereka memilih untuk meninggalkannya. Akta Hasutan tidak dimansuhkan kerana PH mendapatinya berguna. Tol tidak dimansuhkan kerana pemegang konsesi mempunyai hubungan politik. Rang undang-undang had tempoh PM gagal kerana AP PH sendiri tidak hadir. Ini bukan janji yang dimungkiri — ini adalah pembalikan dasar yang disamarkan sebagai pragmatisme.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Pembelaan PH sentiasa “kekangan gabungan” dan “kami perlukan sokongan UMNO.” Tetapi PH menulis manifesto dengan mengetahui ia memerlukan rakan gabungan. Jika anda menjanjikan reformasi yang tidak boleh anda sampaikan kerana aritmetik gabungan anda sendiri, anda berbohong pada ketika janji dibuat, bukan pada ketika pengkhianatan. Manifesto itu bukan aspirasi — ia transaksional. PH menukar janji dengan undi, mengumpul undi, dan mungkiri janji. Dalam konteks lain, ini adalah penipuan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap kerajaan Malaysia telah memungkiri janji manifesto. Manifesto BN adalah senarai impian yang tiada siapa jangka akan ditunaikan. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) mencapai reformasi separa dalam 22 bulan sebelum Langkah Sheraton. Tetapi PH 2.0 unik: ia mempunyai majoriti lebih besar, lebih banyak masa, dan kurang alasan — namun rekod reformasinya lebih teruk daripada PH 1.0. Straits Times menyatakan bahawa kadar pematuhan manifesto PH telah menurun dengan setiap tahun berkuasa, bukan meningkat. Janji-janji bukan ditangguhkan — ia ditinggalkan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda menulis 60 janji. Anda mungkiri kebanyakannya. Anda mempunyai tiga tahun, majoriti besar, dan aritmetik parlimen paling menguntungkan dalam sejarah Malaysia. Jika anda tidak boleh menyampaikan reformasi dalam keadaan ini, anda tidak akan pernah boleh. Terbitkan laporan pematuhan manifesto klausa demi klausa. Jelaskan janji mana yang ditinggalkan dan mengapa. Tetapkan garis masa yang mengikat untuk komitmen yang tinggal. Atau akui apa yang setiap rakyat Malaysia sudah tahu: manifesto itu hanya prop kempen, bukan pelan pemerintahan.

Sumber: Malaysiakini Manifesto Tracker | Straits Times

item.title: PH's GE15 Manifesto: 60 Promises Made, Majority Broken or Abandoned — The Biggest Con Job in Malaysian Politics
item.description:
Said by: Malaysiakini Manifesto Tracker / Straits Times Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2022-11-19

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: PH’s GE15 manifesto — released on 19 November 2022 — contained approximately 60 measurable promises. As tracked by Malaysiakini’s Manifesto Tracker and analysed by the Straits Times, the majority have been broken, delayed, or quietly abandoned. Key failures include: repeal of repressive laws (Sedition Act expanded instead, 90+ arrests), abolition of tolls (government pays RM591M to freeze hikes instead), civil service reform (headcount grew), PM term limits (bill failed in Parliament), ICCPR ratification (not done), UUCA abolition (not done), AG-PP separation (sent to committee), and anti-hopping law strengthening (weakened by exceptions).

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Election manifestos are the contract between a government and voters. When a majority of promises are broken, the social contract collapses. PH did not lose these promises to external circumstances — they chose to abandon them. The Sedition Act wasn’t repealed because PH found it useful. Tolls weren’t abolished because concessionaires have political connections. The PM term limit bill failed because PH’s own MPs didn’t show up. These aren’t broken promises — they’re policy reversals dressed up as pragmatism.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s defence is always “coalition constraints” and “we need UMNO’s support.” But PH wrote the manifesto knowing it would need coalition partners. If you promise reforms you can’t deliver because of your own coalition arithmetic, you were lying at the point of the promise, not at the point of the betrayal. The manifesto wasn’t aspirational — it was transactional. PH traded promises for votes, collected the votes, and defaulted on the promises. In any other context, this is fraud.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian government has broken manifesto promises. BN’s manifestos were wish lists that no one expected to be honoured. PH 1.0 (2018-2020) achieved partial reforms in 22 months before the Sheraton Move. But PH 2.0 is unique: it has a larger majority, more time, and fewer excuses — yet its reform record is worse than PH 1.0’s. The Straits Times noted that PH’s manifesto compliance rate has declined with each year in power, not improved. Promises aren’t being delayed — they’re being abandoned.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You wrote 60 promises. You broke most of them. You had three years, a supermajority, and the most favourable parliamentary arithmetic in Malaysian history. If you couldn’t deliver reform under these conditions, you never will. Publish a clause-by-clause manifesto compliance report. Explain which promises were abandoned and why. Set binding timelines for remaining commitments. Or admit what every Malaysian already knows: the manifesto was a campaign prop, not a governing plan.

Sources: Malaysiakini Manifesto Tracker | Straits Times
